-lc \\\"nl -ba docs/INVARIANTS.md | sed -n '1,220p'\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"     1\\t# INVARIANTS.md\\n     2\\t\\n     3\\tProject-wide constraints. Every workstream must respect these. If a change would violate an invariant, it requires explicit discussion and sign-off before proceeding.\\n     4\\t\\n     5\\t## I-1: Deterministic Output\\n     6\\t\\n     7\\tSame recipe + same variables + same existing files = same output. Always. No randomness, no timestamps, no machine-specific behavior in rendered output. Tests can assert exact equality.\\n     8\\t\\n     9\\t## I-2: Idempotent Operations\\n    10\\t\\n    11\\tEvery file operation *supports* idempotent re-runs via `skip_if_exists` (create) and `skip_if` (inject). When a recipe is designed for idempotent execution (all creates use `skip_if_exists: true` and all injects use `skip_if`), running the same recipe twice with the same variables produces no changes on the second run. JSON output reports `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` with a reason for every skipped operation.\\n    12\\t\\n    13\\t## I-3: JSON In, Files Out\\n    14\\t\\n    15\\tVariables are structured JSON. Output is files on disk. The CLI is non-interactive — no prompts, no wizards, no \\\"press y to continue.\\\" An LLM (or script) can call jig without any human in the loop.\\n    16\\t\\n    17\\t## I-4: Structured Errors with Rendered Content\\n    18\\t\\n    19\\tWhen an operation fails (anchor not found, scope parse fails, file missing), jig still renders the template and includes the rendered content in the error output. The caller never has to re-derive what to insert — only where to put it. Errors include what/where/why + a hint.\\n    20\\t\\n    21\\t## I-5: Stable Exit Codes\\n    22\\t\\n    23\\tExit codes are API. They do not change between minor versions.\\n    24\\t\\n    25\\t| Code | Meaning |\\n    26\\t|------|---------|\\n    27\\t| 0 | Success |\\n    28\\t| 1 | Recipe validation error |\\n    29\\t| 2 | Template rendering error |\\n    30\\t| 3 | File operation error |\\n    31\\t| 4 | Variable validation error |\\n    32\\t\\n    33\\t## I-6: Single Static Binary, Zero Runtime Dependencies\\n    34\\t\\n    35\\tNo Python, no Node, no JVM. One binary, runs anywhere. Startup must be fast — jig may be called dozens of times in a single LLM session.\\n    36\\t\\n    37\\t## I-7: Templates Live With the Consumer\\n    38\\t\\n    39\\tThere is no central template directory. Templates are co-located with the recipe that uses them. A recipe + its templates form a self-contained unit that can be moved, versioned, or shared independently.\\n    40\\t\\n    41\\t## I-8: Dual Output Streams\\n    42\\t\\n    43\\tFor `jig run`: In TTY mode, human-readable colored output to stderr, nothing to stdout. In piped mode, JSON to stdout, nothing to stderr (except errors). `--json` forces JSON to stdout regardless of TTY. `--quiet` suppresses stderr only; stdout behavior is determined by `--json` independently. `--verbose` adds rendered template content to output. Note: `jig vars` and `jig render` write to stdout by default regardless of TTY mode. `jig render --to <path>` redirects output to a file.\\n    44\\t\\n    45\\t## I-9: Operations Are Ordered\\n    46\\t\\n    47\\tFile operations in a recipe execute in declaration order. Later operations can depend on files created or modified by earlier operations in the same recipe run.\\n    48\\t\\n    49\\t## I-10: Graceful Degradation to LLM\\n    50\\t\\n    51\\tjig handles the deterministic part (rendering). When structural analysis fails (can't find anchor, can't parse scope), it fails with enough information for the LLM to fall back to its native Edit tool. jig never silently produces wrong output — it either succeeds or fails clearly.\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/reviews/code-review-codex-20260404-165354.md:97:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_49\",\"type\":\"agent_message\",\"text\":\"**Verdict:** Request Changes\\n\\n**Critical** (must fix before merge):\\n- Patch swallows write failures (`let _ = std::fs::write(...)`) and can still return success, violating the file-op error contract (AC-7.11) and graceful failure invariants. [position.rs:175](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/position.rs:175) [position.rs:179](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/position.rs:179) [SPEC.md:122](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/replace-patch/SPEC.md:122)\\n\\n**Major** (should fix):\\n- Replace fallback append/prepend also ignores write errors and still reports success (AC-2.14 violation). [replace.rs:25](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/replace.rs:25) [patch.rs:26](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/patch.rs:26)\\n\\n\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/reviews/code-review-synthesized-20260404-165354.md:1:{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"success\",\"is_error\":false,\"duration_ms\":103100,\"duration_api_ms\":103083,\"num_turns\":1,\"result\":\"**Verdict: Request Changes**\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Critical** (must fix):\\n\\n- [ ] **`write_back` silently swallows write errors (patch all paths + replace fallback paths).** Both reviewers flagged this. `patch.rs:228` uses `let _ = std::fs::write(...)` for every non-dry-run write, and `replace.rs:278` does the same in the fallback append/prepend path. A permission-denied error returns `OpResult::Success`, violating AC-7.11, AC-2.14, and I-10 (never silently produce wrong output). The main replace path at `replace.rs:106` handles this correctly — apply the same pattern. **Fix:** Make `write_back` in both files return `Result` and propagate `OpResult::Error` with path, OS error, and rendered content.\\n\\n- [ ] **`Position::Sorted` is accepted at parse time but stub-implemented.** Both reviewers flagged this. `position.rs:170-184` just inserts at end-of-scope. AC-5.7 requires alphabetically correct insertion among siblings. A user writing `position: sorted` gets silently wrong output. **Fix:** Either implement sorted insertion (compare trimmed content of sibling lines) or reject `sorted` at parse time with a \\\"not yet implemented\\\" error in `recipe.rs:582`.\\n\\n- [ ] **Byte index used where char index is tracked in delimiter scope detection.** Review A flagged, verified valid. `delimiter.rs:87`: `&lines[open_line][open_col + 1..scanner.col]` — `CharScanner` tracks `col` in character positions (via `chars().nth()`, `chars().count()`), but Rust string slicing uses byte offsets. Any multi-byte UTF-8 character (accented names, CJK comments, emoji) causes a panic at `byte index N is not a char boundary`. **Fix:** Use `char_indices()` to convert char offsets to byte offsets before slicing, or use a byte-based scanner consistently.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Major** (should fix):\\n\\n- [ ] **11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures missing (42% gap).** Both reviewers flagged identical list. Missing: `patch-function-body`, `patch-brackets`, `patch-function-signature`, `patch-find-narrowing`, `patch-sorted`, `patch-indent-matching`, `patch-nested-delimiters`, `patch-string-delimiters`, `error-patch-unbalanced`, `error-patch-find-missing`, `combined-patch-idempotent`. Unit tests cover some behaviors, but the spec (`SPEC.md:504-533`) explicitly requires fixture-level integration tests for each.\\n\\n- [ ] **Verbose scope diagnostics (FR-8) not implemented.** Both reviewers flagged. `SPEC.md:168-172` requires a `scope_diagnostics` object in JSON output with `anchor_line`, `scope_start`, `scope_end`, `insertion_line`, `find_match_line`, `position_fallback`. Currently verbose mode only adds `rendered_content` to the operation result (`output.rs:55-58`). The `ScopeDiagnostics` struct from the spec doesn't exist in code.\\n\\n- [ ] **`find` narrowing doesn't re-anchor when no sub-scope is detected.** Review B flagged, valid per AC-6.1. `patch.rs:119-123`: when `find_result.sub_scope` is `None`, the code falls back to `scope_result` (the original scope). AC-6.1 requires \\\"re-anchor the position resolution to that line\\\" — meaning position should resolve relative to the found line, not the entire original scope. **Fix:** When no sub-scope exists, create a narrowed `ScopeResult` centered on `find_result.found_line`.\\n\\n- [ ] **`scope: line` auto-adjusts indentation, diverging from inject-after behavior.** Review B flagged, valid per AC-7.12. `patch.rs:80-81` calls `detect_anchor_indent` + `adjust_indentation`, while inject's after mode (`inject.rs:178-189`) inserts content verbatim. AC-7.12: \\\"SHALL behave identically to inject's `after` mode for the matched line.\\\" **Fix:** In the `ScopeType::Line` branch, insert `rendered_content` without indentation adjustment.\\n\\n- [ ] **`find_opening` doesn't skip strings/comments when searching for the opening delimiter.** Review B flagged, valid per AC-4.6/4.7. `delimiter.rs:117-129` scans character-by-character for the open delimiter but doesn't call `skip_string_literal`, `skip_line_comment`, or `skip_block_comment`. An opening delimiter inside a string literal or comment before the real one will be selected. The closing-delimiter scan correctly handles these. **Fix:** Apply the same string/comment/escape skipping logic in `find_opening` as in the nesting-depth loop.\\n\\n- [ ] **`AfterLastMethod` regex too narrow for Rust.** Review A flagged. `position.rs:62`: `r\\\"^\\\\s+(pub\\\\s+)?(async\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\"` misses `const fn`, `unsafe fn`, `pub(crate) fn`, `extern \\\"C\\\" fn`. **Fix:** Broaden to `r\\\"^\\\\s+(pub(\\\\s*\\\\([^)]*\\\\))?\\\\s+)?(const\\\\s+)?(unsafe\\\\s+)?(async\\\\s+)?(extern\\\\s+\\\"[^\\\"]*\\\"\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\"`.\\n\\n- [ ] **Location string formatting uses `Debug` instead of snake_case.** Both reviewers flagged. `patch.rs:160-162`: `format!(\\\"{:?}\\\", anchor.scope).to_lowercase()` produces `classbody` not `class_body`, and `afterlastfield` not `after_last_field`. The spec (`SPEC.md:150`) shows `class_body(^class Foo):after_last_field`. **Fix:** Implement `Display` for `ScopeType` and `Position` with snake_case output.\\n\\n- [ ] **Mixed tab/space scope detection uses length comparison, not literal prefix comparison.** Review B flagged, valid per AC-3.8. `indent.rs:106-108` uses `line.len() - line.trim_start().len()` (pure length). AC-3.8 requires \\\"literal prefix comparison — each line's leading whitespace is compared character-by-character against the anchor line's.\\\" **Fix:** Compare the actual whitespace prefix string, not just its length.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Minor** (nice to fix):\\n\\n- [ ] **`AfterLastField` regex requires leading whitespace.** `position.rs:41`: `r\\\"^\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\\s*[:=]\\\"` — `\\\\s+` means it never matches fields at indent level 0 (e.g., Go top-level struct fields). Should be `\\\\s*` if the intent is to work at any indentation. Review A.\\n\\n- [ ] **`peek_next` returns `None` on empty next line.** `delimiter.rs:171`: the `!self.lines[self.line + 1].is_empty()` guard means blank lines break comment detection for `//` and `/*` sequences spanning a line boundary. Review A.\\n\\n- [ ] **`chars().count()` called on every `normalize()`, `peek()`, `advance()`.** `delimiter.rs:146,160,182` — O(n) per call, making the scanner loop O(n^2) per line. Cache the line char count or switch to byte-based indexing (which also fixes the Critical byte/char issue). Review A.\\n\\n- [ ] **Regex recompiled at runtime in replace.** `replace.rs:162-163`: `Regex::new(pattern).expect(...)` compiles the regex fresh for every execution. Pre-compile during recipe parsing for better performance when jig is called repeatedly (I-6). Review A.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Strengths** (confirmed by both reviewers):\\n\\n- **Error-with-rendered-content contract is solid.** Every error path in replace and patch includes the rendered template content, honoring I-4 and I-10. The main replace execution path at `replace.rs:106-117` correctly handles write failures — only the `write_back` helper doesn't.\\n- **Virtual files chaining is clean and consistent.** Dry-run and cross-operation chaining via `virtual_files` HashMap works correctly across create, replace, and patch in both dry and wet modes.\\n- **Recipe parsing and validation is thorough.** All structural validation (mutual exclusivity, regex compile-check, default values, invalid enum rejection) is well-implemented with clear structured errors and exit code 1. This is the strongest part of the implementation.\\n- **Scope detection architecture is well-separated.** `scope/` has zero dependency on the operation pipeline, taking `&[&str]` and returning structs, making it trivially testable.\\n- **284 tests passing, zero failures.** The test suite is substantial and the fixture auto-discovery mechanism means adding new fixtures requires zero test harness changes.\",\"stop_reason\":\"end_turn\",\"session_id\":\"2898bf1d-ffa1-4aed-89d8-31007317ec60\",\"total_cost_usd\":0.9716465,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":3,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":135152,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":11413,\"output_tokens\":4849,\"server_tool_use\":{\"web_search_requests\":0,\"web_fetch_requests\":0},\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":135152,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"},\"modelUsage\":{\"claude-opus-4-6[1m]\":{\"inputTokens\":3,\"outputTokens\":4849,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":11413,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":135152,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":0.9716465,\"contextWindow\":1000000,\"maxOutputTokens\":64000}},\"permission_denials\":[],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"bff53e8d-e1b5-4389-8dc5-b863c0af22c6\"}\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:61:- **`detect_file_type()` as the auto-detection entry point.** A single function (`workflow.rs:151-198`) reads YAML, checks for `steps` vs `files` keys, and returns `FileType::Workflow` or `FileType::Recipe`. Used in three places: `cmd_validate`, `cmd_vars`, and `cmd_workflow` (guard against recipe files). This pattern should be reused by libraries (v0.4) if they add a new file type.\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/iteration-(workstream)-1-20260404-215011.md:1:{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"success\",\"is_error\":false,\"duration_ms\":3716791,\"duration_api_ms\":3700784,\"num_turns\":60,\"result\":\"All **386 tests pass** (359 unit + 2 CLI + 12 integration + 13 library integration). Zero clippy warnings from library code.\\n\\n## Summary of what was implemented\\n\\n### New files\\n- **`src/library/mod.rs`** — Module root exposing manifest, install, discover, conventions\\n- **`src/library/manifest.rs`** — `jig-library.yaml` parsing and validation (name, version, description, framework, language, conventions, recipes, workflows) with 7 unit tests\\n- **`src/library/install.rs`** — Add/remove/update/list operations with project-local (`<project>/.jig/libraries/`) and global (`~/.jig/libraries/`) install locations, project-local takes precedence — 7 unit tests\\n- **`src/library/discover.rs`** — Recipe listing, recipe info (loads actual `recipe.yaml` for variable/operation details), workflow listing, library-qualified path resolution — 7 unit tests\\n- **`src/library/conventions.rs`** — `.jigrc.yaml` project config parsing with per-library convention overrides that merge with library defaults — 6 unit tests\\n- **`tests/library.rs`** — 13 CLI integration tests covering all 7 subcommands + error cases + full lifecycle\\n\\n### Modified files\\n- **`src/main.rs`** — Added `library` module, `Library` command variant with 7 `LibraryAction` subcommands, `cmd_library` handler with JSON and human-readable output for each action\\n\\n### CLI commands added\\n```\\njig library add <path> [--global]     # Install from local directory\\njig library remove <name>             # Uninstall\\njig library update <name> <path>      # Replace with updated source\\njig library list                      # Show installed libraries\\njig library recipes <name>            # List recipes in a library\\njig library info <lib>/<recipe>       # Show recipe details + variables\\njig library workflows <name>          # List workflows in a library\\n```\\n\\nAll commands support `--json` and `--quiet` flags consistent with existing CLI patterns.\\n\\nCOMPLETE\",\"stop_reason\":\"end_turn\",\"session_id\":\"17dd53f2-c239-461c-a519-8660819edf3f\",\"total_cost_usd\":3.16323865,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":56,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":80950,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":3173034,\"output_tokens\":28410,\"server_tool_use\":{\"web_search_requests\":0,\"web_fetch_requests\":0},\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":80950,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"},\"modelUsage\":{\"claude-opus-4-6[1m]\":{\"inputTokens\":57,\"outputTokens\":33812,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":3222236,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":80950,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":2.962640499999999,\"contextWindow\":1000000,\"maxOutputTokens\":64000},\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\":{\"inputTokens\":71,\"outputTokens\":6311,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":955384,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":58747,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":0.20059815,\"contextWindow\":200000,\"maxOutputTokens\":32000}},\"permission_denials\":[],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"fa605e12-0f90-49c2-bc88-b854f69b2d9b\"}\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/NARRATIVE.md:8:The workflows workstream adds multi-recipe orchestration to jig. Where a recipe is a single YAML file that declares variables and file operations (create, inject, replace, patch), a workflow chains multiple recipes into a single invocation. One command — `jig workflow add-field.yaml --vars '{...}'` — can patch a model, update a service, extend a schema, add an admin field, and modify test factories, all in sequence.\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/SPEC.md:1:# SPEC.md\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/SPEC.md:9:The workflows workstream adds multi-recipe orchestration to jig. A workflow is a YAML file that chains multiple recipes into a single invocation with conditional steps, variable mapping between steps, and configurable error handling. This is the composition layer — where individual recipes (create, inject, replace, patch) are combined into meaningful cross-file operations like \"add a field to a model and propagate it through the service, schema, admin, and tests.\"\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/SPEC.md:11:Workflows replace the earlier `includes` concept from the spec. Unlike static includes (which always run all sub-recipes), workflows are conditional, configurable, and report per-step results. They are the prerequisite for libraries (v0.4), which define workflows over library-namespaced recipes.\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/SPEC.md:13:Out of scope for this workstream: library integration (v0.4), cross-library workflows (v0.9), schema-first generation (v0.9), nested workflows (a step cannot reference another workflow), and output variable passing between steps (steps share the filesystem, not variables).\n./docs/workstreams/libraries/reviews/code-review-codex-20260404-225210.md:5:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_0\",\"type\":\"agent_message\",\"text\":\"Reviewing the `libraries` workstream now with a spec-first adversarial pass. I’ll first locate `SPEC.md`, `PLAN.md`, and the implementation/tests tied to this workstream, then map findings to EARS criteria and invariants.\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/exec/claude-plan-20260404-190039.md:1:{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"success\",\"is_error\":false,\"duration_ms\":405759,\"duration_api_ms\":453460,\"num_turns\":12,\"result\":\"Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me produce the execution plan.\\n\\n---\\n\\n# Workflows Execution Plan\\n\\n## Phase 1: Workflow Parsing & CLI Detection\\n\\n### 1.1: Create `src/workflow.rs` with core types and serde deserialization\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs` (new)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Define `OnError` enum with `Stop`, `Continue`, `Report` variants + `Deserialize` impl (case-insensitive: \\\"stop\\\", \\\"continue\\\", \\\"report\\\") with `Default = Stop`\\n2. Define `WorkflowStep` struct with serde: `recipe: String`, `when: Option<String>`, `vars_map: Option<IndexMap<String, String>>`, `vars: Option<IndexMap<String, Value>>`, `on_error: Option<OnError>`\\n3. Define `Workflow` struct with serde: `name: Option<String>`, `description: Option<String>`, `variables: IndexMap<String, VariableDecl>` (default empty), `steps: Vec<WorkflowStep>`, `on_error: OnError` (default Stop), plus non-serde `workflow_dir: PathBuf`\\n4. Define result types: `StepResult` enum (`Success`, `Skipped`, `Error`), `WorkflowResult` struct\\n5. Register module in `src/main.rs` with `mod workflow;`\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN a valid workflow YAML with `steps`, `name`, `description`, `variables`, and `on_error` is deserialized, the system SHALL produce a `Workflow` struct with all fields populated (AC-1.1)\\n- WHEN `on_error` is omitted, the system SHALL default to `OnError::Stop` (AC-1.4)\\n- WHEN `on_error` is `\\\"stop\\\"`, `\\\"continue\\\"`, or `\\\"report\\\"`, the system SHALL parse it successfully (AC-1.5)\\n- WHEN a step has `recipe`, `when`, `vars_map`, `vars`, and `on_error`, the system SHALL parse all into `WorkflowStep` (AC-1.2)\\n- WHEN `variables` is omitted or empty, the system SHALL default to an empty `IndexMap` (AC-1.10)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** None  \\n**Parallel:** With nothing — this is the foundation\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 1.2: Implement `load_workflow()` — parse, resolve, validate\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Implement `pub fn load_workflow(path: &Path) -> Result<Workflow, JigError>`:\\n   - Read file, parse YAML into `Workflow` struct\\n   - Set `workflow_dir` from the file's parent directory\\n   - Resolve each step's `recipe` path relative to `workflow_dir`\\n   - For each step: call `Recipe::load()` on the resolved recipe path — confirm the file exists and is structurally valid\\n   - Return validation errors with step index context\\n2. Implement YAML ambiguity check: reject files with both `steps` and `files` keys. This requires a pre-parse check: deserialize to `serde_yaml::Value` first, check top-level keys, then deserialize to `Workflow`.\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN a step's `recipe` path is relative, the system SHALL resolve it relative to the workflow file's directory (AC-1.3)\\n- IF a step's `recipe` path does not point to an existing file, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting the missing path and resolved location (AC-1.7)\\n- IF the workflow YAML is malformed, the system SHALL exit code 1 with parse failure location (AC-1.8)\\n- WHEN the workflow has empty `steps: []`, the system SHALL accept it as valid (AC-1.9)\\n- IF a YAML file contains both `steps` and `files`, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting ambiguity (AC-1.16)\\n- WHEN validating, the system SHALL also validate each referenced recipe (AC-1.18)\\n- IF a referenced recipe is invalid, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting which step referenced it (AC-1.19)\\n- IF `on_error` is an invalid value, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting allowed options (AC-1.11)\\n- WHEN `when` is specified, the system SHALL store it as a string for later evaluation (AC-1.12)\\n- WHEN `vars_map` is specified, the system SHALL parse it as string-to-string mapping (AC-1.13)\\n- WHEN `vars` is specified, the system SHALL parse it as string-to-Value mapping (AC-1.14)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 1.1  \\n**Parallel:** No — depends on types from 1.1\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 1.3: Implement auto-detection in `jig validate` (P)\\n\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` (modify `cmd_validate`)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Add `pub fn detect_file_type(path: &Path) -> Result<FileType, JigError>` to `workflow.rs` — reads YAML, checks for `steps` vs `files` keys, returns enum `FileType::Workflow` or `FileType::Recipe`\\n2. Modify `cmd_validate()` to call `detect_file_type()` first, then dispatch to either recipe validation (existing) or workflow validation (new `validate_workflow()`)\\n3. Implement `validate_workflow()` — loads workflow, reports: type, name, variable count, step count (unconditional/conditional), per-step recipe validity\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `jig validate` receives a workflow file (has `steps`), the system SHALL validate it as a workflow (AC-7.3)\\n- IF a YAML file contains neither `steps` nor `files`, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting the missing structural key (AC-1.17)\\n- WHEN `jig validate` succeeds on a workflow, the human output SHALL report workflow name, variable summary, step count, and per-recipe status (AC-7.5)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 1.2  \\n\\n### 1.4: Implement auto-detection in `jig vars` (P)\\n\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` (modify `cmd_vars`)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Modify `cmd_vars()` to call `detect_file_type()`, dispatch to workflow or recipe\\n2. For workflows: load workflow, output `variables::vars_json(&workflow.variables)`\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `jig vars` is invoked with a workflow file, the system SHALL output the workflow's variable declarations in JSON format (AC-2.5, AC-7.4)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 1.2  \\n**Parallel:** With 1.3 — both depend on 1.2 but not on each other\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 1.5: Add workflow validation JSON output format (P)\\n\\n**File:** `src/output.rs` (add function), `src/main.rs` (wire up)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Add `pub fn build_workflow_validate_json(workflow: &Workflow) -> Value` — produces JSON with `type`, `valid`, `name`, `variables`, `steps` array (each with `recipe`, `valid`, `conditional`, optional `when`)\\n2. Wire into `cmd_validate` JSON path\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `jig validate` succeeds on a workflow with `--json`, the JSON output SHALL include `type: \\\"workflow\\\"`, `valid: true`, workflow name, variables, and steps array with recipe/valid/conditional for each step (AC-7.6)\\n- WHEN workflow has optional metadata fields, the system SHALL accept workflows with or without them (AC-1.20)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 1.3  \\n**Parallel:** With 1.6\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 1.6: Unit tests for Phase 1\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs` (inline `#[cfg(test)]` module)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Test valid workflow parsing: minimal (just steps), full (all fields), metadata present/absent\\n2. Test empty steps accepted\\n3. Test missing `steps` key → error\\n4. Test both `steps` and `files` → error\\n5. Test bad `on_error` value → error\\n6. Test missing recipe file → error\\n7. Test invalid recipe file → error\\n8. Test no variables accepted\\n9. Test step with `when`, `vars_map`, `vars`, per-step `on_error`\\n10. Test `on_error` case insensitivity (if supported) or exact match\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- The system SHALL have 20+ unit tests covering AC-1.1 through AC-1.20\\n- `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` pass\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 1.2, 1.3, 1.4, 1.5  \\n**Parallel:** With 1.5 (tests can be written alongside)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 1 Validation Criteria\\n- `jig validate workflow.yaml` auto-detects and validates a workflow with 3+ steps, reporting each recipe's validity\\n- `jig vars workflow.yaml` outputs workflow variable declarations as JSON\\n- All parse-time error cases produce exit code 1 with structured errors\\n- 20+ unit tests covering AC-1.1 through AC-1.20\\n- `cargo test` passes, `cargo clippy` clean\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 2: Variable Resolution & Conditional Steps\\n\\n### 2.1: Implement `resolve_step_variables()`\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Implement `pub fn resolve_step_variables(workflow_vars: &Value, step: &WorkflowStep) -> Value`:\\n   - Start with clone of `workflow_vars` (must be an Object)\\n   - Apply `vars_map` simultaneously: snapshot all source values first, then remove sources and insert targets. Handle: nonexistent source (skip silently, AC-4.7), target collision (overwrite, AC-4.8)\\n   - Apply `vars` overrides: merge into result, overwriting existing keys (AC-4.3)\\n   - Return the resolved Value\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `vars_map` is `{source: target}`, the system SHALL rename `source` to `target` and remove `source` (AC-4.1, AC-4.2)\\n- WHEN `vars` overrides are specified, the system SHALL merge them, overriding existing values (AC-4.3)\\n- The resolution order SHALL be: workflow vars → vars_map → vars overrides (AC-4.4)\\n- WHEN `vars_map` references a nonexistent source, the system SHALL silently ignore it (AC-4.7)\\n- WHEN `vars_map` maps to a target that already exists, the renamed value SHALL override (AC-4.8)\\n- WHEN a step has neither `vars_map` nor `vars`, the system SHALL pass workflow vars unchanged (AC-4.9)\\n- WHEN multiple `vars_map` entries are specified, all renamings SHALL be simultaneous (AC-4.10)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 1.1 (needs types)  \\n**Parallel:** With 2.2\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2.2: Implement `evaluate_when()` (P)\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Implement `pub fn evaluate_when(when_expr: &str, vars: &Value) -> Result<bool, JigError>`:\\n   - Create a standalone minijinja Environment (via `renderer::create_standalone_env()`)\\n   - Render `when_expr` as a Jinja2 template with `vars`\\n   - Trim whitespace from result\\n   - Return `false` if result is empty, case-insensitively equals \\\"false\\\", or equals \\\"0\\\"\\n   - Return `true` otherwise\\n   - On render error (undefined variable, syntax error): return `Err(JigError::TemplateRendering(...))`\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `when` is rendered, the system SHALL use workflow-level resolved variables (before vars_map/vars) (AC-3.1)\\n- WHEN rendered result is empty, \\\"false\\\" (any case), or \\\"0\\\", the system SHALL evaluate to false (AC-3.2)\\n- WHEN rendered result is truthy, the system SHALL evaluate to true (AC-3.3)\\n- WHEN step has no `when`, the system SHALL execute unconditionally (AC-3.5)\\n- IF `when` references an undefined variable, the system SHALL treat it as a step failure (AC-3.6)\\n- IF `when` has Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL treat it as a step failure (AC-3.7)\\n- WHEN `when` uses Jinja2 control flow, the system SHALL evaluate correctly (AC-3.8)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 1.1 (types), renderer.rs (existing, no changes needed)  \\n**Parallel:** With 2.1\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2.3: Implement workflow-level variable validation (P)\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. In the workflow execution path (not yet built, but the validation function itself):\\n   - Reuse `variables::validate_variables(&workflow.variables, &provided)` \\n   - This is a call to existing code — no new function needed\\n   - Confirm that extra keys pass through (AC-2.4), and that validation errors accumulate (AC-2.7)\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN workflow variables are declared, the system SHALL validate using same rules as recipe validation (AC-2.1)\\n- WHEN variable sources are provided, the system SHALL merge with same precedence as recipes (AC-2.2)\\n- The system SHALL pass all resolved variables to every step by default (AC-2.3)\\n- The system SHALL accept undeclared variable keys without error (AC-2.4)\\n- WHEN no variables are declared and none provided, the system SHALL pass empty object (AC-2.6)\\n- The system SHALL accumulate validation errors and report with exit code 4 (AC-2.7)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 1.1  \\n**Parallel:** With 2.1 and 2.2 — just confirming existing API works\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2.4: Implement step-level variable validation\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. After `resolve_step_variables()` produces the step's vars, validate against the recipe's `VariableDecl` map using `variables::validate_variables(&recipe.variables, &step_vars)`\\n2. This happens at execution time (D-9), not upfront — conditional steps that are skipped don't get validated\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN the step's resolved variables are passed to the recipe, the system SHALL validate against the recipe's declarations (AC-4.5)\\n- IF a required variable is missing after vars_map and vars, the system SHALL treat it as a step failure subject to on_error (AC-4.6)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 2.1  \\n**Parallel:** No — uses resolve output\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 2.5: Unit tests for Phase 2\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs` (add to `#[cfg(test)]` module)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. `vars_map` rename: `{a: b}` → result has `b`, not `a`\\n2. `vars_map` nonexistent source: `{nonexistent: x}` → silently ignored\\n3. `vars_map` target collision: `{a: b}` when `b` already exists → `a`'s value overwrites\\n4. `vars` override: adds and overwrites\\n5. Combined `vars_map` + `vars`: correct order of application\\n6. Simultaneous `vars_map`: `{a: b, b: c}` → original `a` → `b`, original `b` → `c`\\n7. `when` truthy: \\\"true\\\", \\\"yes\\\", \\\"anything\\\", \\\"1\\\" → true\\n8. `when` falsy: \\\"\\\", \\\"false\\\", \\\"False\\\", \\\"FALSE\\\", \\\"0\\\" → false\\n9. `when` with Jinja2: `{{ flag }}` with `flag: true` → renders \\\"true\\\" → truthy\\n10. `when` with control flow: `{% if items | length > 0 %}yes{% endif %}` → test both truthy and falsy\\n11. `when` with undefined var → error\\n12. No `when` → always execute\\n13. Empty vars + no declarations → empty object passes through\\n14. Step validation catches missing required var\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- 15+ unit tests covering FR-3 (AC-3.1–3.8) and FR-4 (AC-4.1–4.10)\\n- `cargo test` passes\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.4  \\n**Parallel:** Can be written alongside 2.1–2.4\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2 Validation Criteria\\n- `resolve_step_variables()` handles all edge cases (simultaneous rename, collision, nonexistent source)\\n- `evaluate_when()` correctly classifies all falsy/truthy patterns\\n- Workflow-level variable validation halts before execution with exit 4\\n- 15+ unit tests covering FR-3 and FR-4\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 3: Execution Engine\\n\\n### 3.1: Extract reusable `run_recipe()` from `cmd_run`\\n\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` (refactor)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Extract lines 274-366 of `cmd_run` into a new function:\\n   ```rust\\n   pub fn run_recipe(\\n       recipe: &Recipe,\\n       vars: &Value,\\n       ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\n       verbose: bool,\\n   ) -> Result<Vec<OpResult>, JigError>\\n   ```\\n   This function:\\n   - Creates recipe-aware Jinja2 environment (`renderer::create_recipe_env`)\\n   - Renders all templates and paths into `PreparedOp` vec (current lines 280-336)\\n   - Executes operations sequentially with fail-fast (current lines 355-366)\\n   - Returns `Vec<OpResult>`\\n   - On rendering errors: returns `Err(JigError::TemplateRendering(...))`\\n   \\n2. Modify `cmd_run` to call `run_recipe()` — the function still handles variable collection, output formatting, and exit code logic\\n\\n3. **Critical:** `run_recipe` must NOT validate `base_dir` — that stays in `cmd_run`/`cmd_workflow`. It receives an already-configured `ExecutionContext`.\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `run_recipe()` is called, the system SHALL render all templates, prepare operations, and execute them with fail-fast\\n- The refactored `cmd_run` SHALL produce identical behavior to the pre-refactoring version (no functional change)\\n- `run_recipe()` SHALL be callable from workflow step execution with a shared `ExecutionContext`\\n\\n**Dependencies:** None (pure refactor of existing code)  \\n**Parallel:** With 2.1, 2.2 (no dependency on Phase 2 types)\\n\\n> [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]: The `run_recipe` function needs to handle the **fail-fast vs. continue** distinction. For `cmd_run`, it always fails fast (current behavior). For workflows, the caller (`execute_workflow`) needs to decide based on `on_error`. **Proposal:** `run_recipe` always returns `Vec<OpResult>` — it fails fast internally (stops on first error within a recipe), and the caller inspects the result for errors. The fail-fast behavior within a single recipe's operations is preserved regardless of workflow error mode. Only **cross-step** continuation is controlled by `on_error`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 3.2: Implement `execute_workflow()`\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Implement:\\n   ```rust\\n   pub fn execute_workflow(\\n       workflow: &Workflow,\\n       vars: Value,\\n       ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\n       verbose: bool,\\n   ) -> WorkflowResult\\n   ```\\n2. Sequential step loop:\\n   - For each step:\\n     a. Evaluate `when` (if present) against `vars` — if falsy, push `StepResult::Skipped`\\n     b. Resolve step variables: `resolve_step_variables(&vars, &step)`\\n     c. Load the recipe: `Recipe::load(&resolved_recipe_path)`\\n     d. Validate step variables against recipe: `variables::validate_variables(&recipe.variables, &step_vars)`\\n     e. Call `run_recipe(&recipe, &step_vars, ctx, verbose)` \\n     f. Inspect result: if any `OpResult::Error`, create `StepResult::Error`; else `StepResult::Success`\\n     g. Apply `on_error` logic: if error and effective mode is `Stop`, break loop\\n3. Return `WorkflowResult` with all step results\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- The system SHALL execute steps in declaration order (AC-6.1, AC-N2.1)\\n- WHEN a step executes, the system SHALL load the recipe, render templates, and execute operations (AC-6.2)\\n- WHEN a step creates/modifies files, subsequent steps SHALL see those changes (AC-6.3)\\n- WHEN `--force` is specified, the system SHALL pass force to every step (AC-6.5)\\n- WHEN `--base-dir` is specified, the system SHALL resolve all output paths relative to it (AC-6.6)\\n- The system SHALL validate workflow-level variables before executing any step (AC-6.9)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 3.1 (needs `run_recipe()`), 2.1, 2.2, 2.4  \\n**Parallel:** No — sequential dependency chain\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 3.3: Implement dry-run cross-step `virtual_files` carryover\\n\\n**File:** No new file — this is inherent in the design\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Verify that passing a single `&mut ExecutionContext` across steps already handles this — `virtual_files` persists across calls to `run_recipe()`\\n2. Write a test confirming: step 1 creates file (virtual in dry-run), step 2 injects into same path using `virtual_files` lookup\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL carry `virtual_files` state across steps so later steps can read files \\\"created\\\" by earlier steps (AC-6.4)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 3.2  \\n**Parallel:** No\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 3.4: Implement `on_error` handling\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs` (within `execute_workflow`)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. After each step that produces an error:\\n   - **Stop**: push result, break loop immediately\\n   - **Continue**: push result, continue to next step\\n   - **Report**: push result, continue to next step (difference from Continue is exit code)\\n2. Determine effective `on_error` for each step: `step.on_error.unwrap_or(workflow.on_error)`\\n3. Exit code selection (handled by caller in `cmd_workflow`, not in `execute_workflow`):\\n   - All success/skipped → 0\\n   - Stop mode failure → failed step's error exit code\\n   - Continue with failures → 0\\n   - Report with failures → 3\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `on_error` is `stop` and a step fails, the system SHALL stop immediately (AC-5.1)\\n- WHEN `on_error` is `stop` and a step fails, the system SHALL exit with the step's error code (AC-5.2)\\n- WHEN `on_error` is `continue` and a step fails, the system SHALL proceed (AC-5.3)\\n- WHEN `on_error` is `continue` with failures, the system SHALL exit 0 (AC-5.4)\\n- WHEN `on_error` is `report` and a step fails, the system SHALL proceed (AC-5.5)\\n- WHEN `on_error` is `report` with failures, the system SHALL exit 3 (AC-5.6)\\n- WHEN step has its own `on_error`, the system SHALL use it for that step (AC-5.7)\\n- WHEN all steps succeed/skip, the system SHALL exit 0 regardless of mode (AC-5.10)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 3.2  \\n**Parallel:** No — modifies the execution loop\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 3.5: Unit tests for Phase 3\\n\\n**File:** `src/workflow.rs` (add to tests), possibly `tests/cli.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Verify `cmd_run` still works identically after refactor (run existing test suite)\\n2. Test basic two-step execution: both succeed\\n3. Test conditional skip: step with `when: \\\"{{ false }}\\\"` is skipped\\n4. Test chain: step 1 creates file, step 2 injects into it\\n5. Test `on_error: stop`: second step fails, third step doesn't execute\\n6. Test `on_error: continue`: second step fails, third step executes, exit 0\\n7. Test `on_error: report`: second step fails, third step executes, exit 3\\n8. Test per-step `on_error` override\\n9. Test dry-run chaining: step 1 creates virtual, step 2 injects into virtual\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- Existing tests pass (refactor is behavior-preserving)\\n- 20+ unit tests covering FR-5 and FR-6\\n- `cargo test` passes, `cargo clippy` clean\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4  \\n**Parallel:** With 3.4 (some tests can be written alongside)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3 Validation Criteria\\n- `cmd_run` refactor is invisible: all 308+ existing tests pass\\n- Two-step workflow succeeds in normal and dry-run mode with cross-step file sharing\\n- `when: \\\"{{ false }}\\\"` properly skips\\n- All three `on_error` modes behave correctly\\n- Per-step `on_error` overrides workflow default\\n- 20+ new unit tests\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 4: CLI Command & Output Formatting\\n\\n### 4.1: Add `Workflow` command variant to CLI (P)\\n\\n**File:** `src/main.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Add `Workflow { path: PathBuf }` to `Commands` enum\\n2. Add dispatch in `run()`: `Commands::Workflow { path } => cmd_workflow(...)`\\n3. Accept same global options as `Run` (already global on `Cli` struct — no changes needed)\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `jig workflow <path>` is invoked, the system SHALL dispatch to workflow execution (AC-7.1)\\n- The `jig workflow` command SHALL accept `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose` (AC-7.2)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 3 (execution engine)  \\n**Parallel:** With 4.3\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 4.2: Implement `cmd_workflow()` in `main.rs`\\n\\n**File:** `src/main.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Implement `cmd_workflow()` following same pattern as `cmd_run`:\\n   - Detect output mode\\n   - Load workflow via `load_workflow()`\\n   - Collect and validate variables\\n   - Validate and resolve `base_dir`\\n   - Create `ExecutionContext`\\n   - Call `execute_workflow()`\\n   - Format output (JSON or human)\\n   - Return exit code\\n2. Handle edge case: if file has `files` not `steps`, exit 1 with message directing to `jig run`\\n3. Handle file not found: exit 1\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN `jig workflow` is invoked with a valid workflow and variables, the system SHALL execute it (AC-7.1)\\n- IF the path does not exist, the system SHALL exit code 1 (AC-7.7)\\n- IF the file is a recipe (has `files`), the system SHALL exit code 1 with helpful message (AC-7.8)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 4.1, Phase 3  \\n**Parallel:** No — needs CLI variant and execution engine\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 4.3: Implement `format_workflow_json()` (P)\\n\\n**File:** `src/output.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Implement `pub fn format_workflow_json(result: &WorkflowResult, dry_run: bool, verbose: bool) -> Value`:\\n   - Top-level: `workflow` (name or null), `on_error`, `status` (\\\"success\\\"/\\\"partial\\\"/\\\"error\\\"), `dry_run`\\n   - `steps` array: per-step JSON based on `StepResult` variant\\n   - Success step: `recipe`, `status: \\\"success\\\"`, `operations` (reuse existing `op_result_to_json`), `files_written`, `files_skipped`\\n   - Skipped step: `recipe`, `status: \\\"skipped\\\"`, `reason`\\n   - Error step: `recipe`, `status: \\\"error\\\"`, `error` (what/where/why/hint), `rendered_content`, `operations`\\n   - Aggregate `files_written` and `files_skipped` across all steps (AC-8.5 rules)\\n2. Make `op_result_to_json` and `compute_file_summaries` in `output.rs` pub(crate) so workflow formatting can reuse them\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include `steps` array with per-step results (AC-8.1)\\n- Successful step JSON SHALL include recipe, status, operations, files_written, files_skipped (AC-8.2)\\n- Skipped step JSON SHALL include recipe, status, reason (AC-8.3)\\n- Error step JSON SHALL include recipe, status, error, rendered_content, operations (AC-8.4)\\n- The system SHALL include aggregate files_written/files_skipped (AC-8.5)\\n- The system SHALL include top-level `on_error` field (AC-8.6)\\n- The system SHALL include `workflow` field with name or null (AC-8.7)\\n- The system SHALL include `dry_run` boolean (AC-8.10)\\n- Status SHALL be \\\"success\\\", \\\"partial\\\" (report+failures), or \\\"error\\\" (stop+failure) (AC-8.11, AC-8.12, AC-8.13)\\n- WHEN `--verbose`, rendered content SHALL be included (AC-8.9)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 3 (needs `WorkflowResult`, `StepResult` types)  \\n**Parallel:** With 4.1, 4.4\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 4.4: Implement `format_workflow_human()` (P)\\n\\n**File:** `src/output.rs`\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Implement `pub fn format_workflow_human(result: &WorkflowResult, dry_run: bool, verbose: bool)`:\\n   - Print step progress headers: `\\\"Step 1/3: model/add-field/recipe.yaml\\\"`\\n   - For success steps: indent and print each operation result (reuse existing human formatting logic, maybe extract helper)\\n   - For skipped steps: print `\\\"  ⊘ skipped (when condition evaluated to false)\\\"`\\n   - For error steps: print error details indented\\n   - Summary line: `\\\"3 steps: 2 succeeded, 1 skipped, 0 failed\\\"`\\n2. Respect `--quiet` (handled by caller — don't call this function if quiet)\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- WHEN human output is produced, the system SHALL print step headers, nested results, and summary (AC-8.8)\\n- WHILE `--quiet` is active, stderr SHALL be suppressed (AC-8.14)\\n\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 3  \\n**Parallel:** With 4.1, 4.3\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 4.5: CLI integration tests\\n\\n**File:** `tests/cli.rs` (add workflow tests)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Test `jig workflow` with basic two-step workflow → success\\n2. Test `jig workflow --json` → JSON output matches schema\\n3. Test `jig workflow --dry-run` → no files written\\n4. Test `jig workflow --quiet` → no stderr\\n5. Test `jig workflow recipe.yaml` → error directing to `jig run`\\n6. Test `jig validate workflow.yaml` → auto-detect works\\n7. Test `jig vars workflow.yaml` → outputs workflow variables\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- 10+ CLI integration tests covering FR-7 and FR-8\\n- `cargo test` passes\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 4.2, 4.3, 4.4  \\n**Parallel:** No — needs all output functions\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4 Validation Criteria\\n- `jig workflow workflow.yaml --vars '...' --json` produces correct JSON matching SPEC.md schema\\n- Human output shows step headers, nested operations, summary\\n- `jig workflow recipe.yaml` → clear error pointing to `jig run`\\n- 10+ tests covering FR-7 and FR-8\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 5: Integration Test Fixtures\\n\\n### 5.1: Create fixture infrastructure\\n\\n**File:** `tests/cli.rs` (extend existing fixture runner)\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Extend the existing fixture test runner to handle workflow fixtures:\\n   - Look for `workflow.yaml` (instead of `recipe.yaml`) in fixture directory\\n   - If `workflow.yaml` exists, run `jig workflow` instead of `jig run`\\n   - Step recipe subdirectories are within the fixture directory\\n2. Verify infrastructure works with one simple fixture before building the full set\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- The fixture runner SHALL auto-detect workflow vs recipe fixtures\\n- Adding a new workflow test is just adding a directory\\n\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 4  \\n**Parallel:** No\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 5.2: Success-path fixtures (P — all independent of each other)\\n\\n**Directory:** `tests/fixtures/workflow-*`\\n\\n| Fixture | Key ACs | Description |\\n|---------|---------|-------------|\\n| `workflow-basic` | AC-6.1, AC-6.2, AC-8.2 | Two-step workflow, both succeed |\\n| `workflow-three-steps` | AC-6.1, AC-8.1, AC-8.5 | Three steps: create, inject, patch |\\n| `workflow-when-true` | AC-3.3, AC-3.5 | Step with truthy `when` executes |\\n| `workflow-when-false` | AC-3.2, AC-3.4, AC-8.3 | Step with falsy `when` is skipped |\\n| `workflow-when-complex` | AC-3.8 | `when` uses `{% if %}` control flow |\\n| `workflow-vars-map` | AC-4.1, AC-4.2 | Step renames variable |\\n| `workflow-vars-override` | AC-4.3 | Step overrides variable value |\\n| `workflow-vars-map-and-override` | AC-4.4 | Both vars_map and vars |\\n| `workflow-chain-create-inject` | AC-6.3, AC-N2.2 | Step 1 creates, step 2 injects |\\n| `workflow-chain-create-patch` | AC-6.3 | Step 1 creates, step 2 patches |\\n| `workflow-empty-steps` | AC-1.9 | Empty steps array, exits 0 |\\n| `workflow-no-variables` | AC-1.10, AC-2.6 | No variables section |\\n| `workflow-idempotent` | AC-N4.1, AC-N4.2 | Run twice, second run all skips |\\n| `workflow-dry-run-chain` | AC-6.4 | Dry-run with cross-step virtual_files |\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 5.1  \\n**Parallel:** All 14 fixtures can be created in parallel\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 5.3: Error-handling fixtures (P)\\n\\n| Fixture | Key ACs | Description |\\n|---------|---------|-------------|\\n| `workflow-on-error-stop` | AC-5.1, AC-5.2, AC-5.8, AC-8.12 | Step fails, workflow stops |\\n| `workflow-on-error-continue` | AC-5.3, AC-5.4, AC-8.13 | Step fails, continues, exit 0 |\\n| `workflow-on-error-report` | AC-5.5, AC-5.6, AC-8.11 | Step fails, continues, exit 3 |\\n| `workflow-step-on-error` | AC-5.7 | Per-step on_error override |\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 5.1  \\n**Parallel:** All 4 can be created in parallel, and in parallel with 5.2\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 5.4: Error-case fixtures (P)\\n\\n| Fixture | Key ACs | Description |\\n|---------|---------|-------------|\\n| `error-workflow-missing-recipe` | AC-1.7 | Step references nonexistent recipe |\\n| `error-workflow-bad-yaml` | AC-1.8 | Malformed YAML |\\n| `error-workflow-missing-steps` | AC-1.17 | No steps or files key |\\n| `error-workflow-ambiguous` | AC-1.16 | Both steps and files |\\n| `error-workflow-bad-vars` | AC-2.7, AC-6.9 | Variable validation failure |\\n| `error-workflow-when-undef` | AC-3.6 | `when` references undefined variable |\\n| `error-workflow-step-missing-var` | AC-4.6 | Recipe requires var not in step's vars |\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 5.1  \\n**Parallel:** All 7 can be created in parallel, and in parallel with 5.2, 5.3\\n\\n---\\n\\n### 5.5: Final validation\\n\\n**Tasks:**\\n1. Run `cargo test` — all tests pass\\n2. Run `cargo clippy` — clean\\n3. Count total tests, update CLAUDE.md project status\\n\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\n- All 25 spec-required fixtures exist and pass\\n- `cargo test` passes with 0 failures\\n- `cargo clippy` clean\\n- CLAUDE.md updated with new test count\\n\\n**Dependencies:** 5.2, 5.3, 5.4\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5 Validation Criteria\\n- 25 fixture directories created and passing\\n- `cargo test` zero failures\\n- `cargo clippy` clean\\n- CLAUDE.md project status updated\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Dependency Graph\\n\\n```\\nPhase 1:  1.1 → 1.2 → 1.3 (P) → 1.5\\n                    ↘ 1.4 (P) ↗     → 1.6\\n                                    \\nPhase 2:  2.1 (P) → 2.4 → 2.5\\n          2.2 (P) ↗\\n          2.3 (P) ↗\\n\\nPhase 3:  3.1 (refactor, parallel with Phase 2)\\n              ↓\\n          3.2 (needs 3.1 + 2.1 + 2.2)\\n              ↓\\n          3.3 → 3.5\\n          3.4 ↗\\n\\nPhase 4:  4.1 (P) → 4.2 → 4.5\\n          4.3 (P) ↗\\n          4.4 (P) ↗\\n\\nPhase 5:  5.1 → 5.2 (P)\\n              → 5.3 (P) → 5.5\\n              → 5.4 (P) ↗\\n```\\n\\n**Cross-phase parallelism:**\\n- Phase 2 (2.1, 2.2, 2.3) and Phase 3 milestone 3.1 (refactor) can run in parallel\\n- Phase 4 output formatting (4.3, 4.4) can be started once Phase 3 types are defined (after 3.2)\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Summary of Files Changed\\n\\n| File | Phase | Change |\\n|------|-------|--------|\\n| `src/workflow.rs` | 1, 2, 3 | **New.** ~600-800 lines: types, parsing, variable resolution, when evaluation, execution engine |\\n| `src/main.rs` | 1, 3, 4 | **Modified.** Add `mod workflow`, `Workflow` command variant, `cmd_workflow()`, refactor `cmd_run` → extract `run_recipe()`, extend `cmd_validate`/`cmd_vars` with auto-detection |\\n| `src/output.rs` | 4 | **Modified.** Add `format_workflow_json()`, `format_workflow_human()`, make `op_result_to_json`/`compute_file_summaries` pub(crate) |\\n| `tests/cli.rs` | 4, 5 | **Modified.** Extend fixture runner for workflow fixtures, add CLI integration tests |\\n| `tests/fixtures/workflow-*` | 5 | **New.** 25 fixture directories |\\n| `CLAUDE.md` | 5 | **Modified.** Update project status and test count |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Open Decision\\n\\n> [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]: **`run_recipe` fail-fast behavior.** The proposal is that `run_recipe()` always fails fast within a single recipe (stops on first operation error), consistent with current `cmd_run` behavior. The `on_error` mode only controls cross-step behavior in `execute_workflow()`. This means a step with 5 operations where operation 3 fails will report operations 1-3 (with 3 being the error), regardless of `on_error`. The alternative would be to add a `fail_fast: bool` parameter to `run_recipe()` so `on_error: continue` could attempt remaining operations within a failed step — but the spec doesn't require this (AC-5.9 says \\\"operations that completed before the failure within that step\\\"), confirming fail-fast within a step. **Recommendation: keep fail-fast within a recipe.** Does this align with your intent?\",\"stop_reason\":\"end_turn\",\"session_id\":\"9ec76a9a-a50d-49f1-a064-aa015ef5ff05\",\"total_cost_usd\":1.1026950000000002,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":8,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":42276,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":251607,\"output_tokens\":20118,\"server_tool_use\":{\"web_search_requests\":0,\"web_fetch_requests\":0},\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":42276,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"},\"modelUsage\":{\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\":{\"inputTokens\":1811,\"outputTokens\":9254,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":581530,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":82754,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":0.20967649999999996,\"contextWindow\":200000,\"maxOutputTokens\":32000},\"claude-opus-4-6[1m]\":{\"inputTokens\":8,\"outputTokens\":20118,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":251607,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":42276,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":0.8930184999999999,\"contextWindow\":1000000,\"maxOutputTokens\":64000}},\"permission_denials\":[{\"tool_name\":\"Bash\",\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01TxPzCmm3rH23kTYZFmi1gY\",\"tool_input\":{\"command\":\"wc -l src/*.rs src/**/*.rs 2>/dev/null; echo \\\"---\\\"; ls -la tests/ 2>/dev/null; echo \\\"---\\\"; ls tests/fixtures/ 2>/dev/null | head -30\",\"description\":\"Count lines and list test structure\"}},{\"tool_name\":\"Bash\",\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01RTTLoDRRUQdBtNtW3Mcpfx\",\"tool_input\":{\"command\":\"wc -l src/*.rs src/**/*.rs 2>/dev/null\",\"description\":\"Count lines per source file\"}}],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"d469e8c4-a288-4afe-9bed-7c622c938d69\"}\n./docs/workstreams/libraries/exec/execution-summary-20260404-215010.md:3:- **Workstream:** libraries\n./docs/workstreams/libraries/exec/execution-summary-20260404-215010.md:15:- ✓ (workstream)\n./docs/workstreams/libraries/exec/synthesized-20260404-213100.md:1:/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/scripts/ws-plan.sh: line 92: /Users/tylerobriant/.local/bin/claude: Argument list too long\n./Cargo.lock:50: \"windows-sys\",\n./Cargo.lock:61: \"windows-sys\",\n./Cargo.lock:136: \"windows-sys\",\n./Cargo.lock:158: \"windows-sys\",\n./Cargo.lock:422: \"windows-sys\",\n./Cargo.lock:527: \"windows-sys\",\n./Cargo.lock:627:name = \"windows-link\"\n./Cargo.lock:633:name = \"windows-sys\"\n./Cargo.lock:638: \"windows-link\",\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/reviews/plan-review-synthesized-20260403-114234.md:1:{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"success\",\"is_error\":false,\"duration_ms\":157335,\"duration_api_ms\":157304,\"num_turns\":3,\"result\":\"Here's the consolidated review.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Critical (blocks implementation)\\n\\n**C-1: Render-all-upfront vs render-per-operation — three docs, three interpretations** *(Both reviewers)*\\n\\n`jig.md:796` defines render-per-operation. `ARCHITECTURE.md:D-1` says \\\"rendered *before* the operation executes\\\" (per-op). `PLAN.md:202` says \\\"Render ALL templates before ANY file write.\\\" These produce different failure semantics — render-all-upfront means a syntax error in template 3 prevents ops 1-2 from writing; render-per-op lets 1-2 write before 3 fails. The \\\"no partial writes\\\" claim in PLAN only holds for rendering failures, not execution failures (op 1 creates a file, op 2's inject fails — partial state on disk).\\n\\n**Fix:** Pick render-all-upfront (the PLAN's intent). Update `ARCHITECTURE.md:D-1` to say \\\"all templates are rendered before any operation executes.\\\" Add a paragraph to SPEC acknowledging that execution failures can still produce partial writes, and specify recovery behavior (re-run with `--force`, or the user handles it with the rendered content from the error output).\\n\\n---\\n\\n**C-2: Idempotency invariant (I-2) contradicts SPEC defaults** *(Both reviewers — A: Major, B: Critical → Critical)*\\n\\n`INVARIANTS.md:9` says \\\"every file operation is safe to re-run\\\" and \\\"second run produces no changes.\\\" `AC-N2.1` says second run SHALL report all operations as `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"`. But `create` defaults to `skip_if_exists: false` (AC-4.5), meaning a second run ERRORS, not skips. Similarly, `inject` without `skip_if` will duplicate content. I-2 and AC-N2.1 are only true for recipes that explicitly opt in to idempotency.\\n\\n**Fix:** Qualify I-2: \\\"Every file operation *supports* idempotent re-runs via `skip_if_exists` and `skip_if`.\\\" Rewrite AC-N2.1: \\\"WHEN a recipe is designed for idempotent execution (all creates use `skip_if_exists: true` and all injects use `skip_if`), the system SHALL report all operations as `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` on the second run.\\\" AC-N2.2 is already correctly scoped.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**C-3: Structured error contract is unimplementable as specified** *(Codex only — valid)*\\n\\n`INVARIANTS.md:17` and `AC-N4.1` require `what/where/why/hint` fields in every error. But `JigError` in the SPEC data model (`SPEC.md:299`) uses `String` payloads, not structured fields. The JSON output schema (`SPEC.md:237`) shows `\\\"error\\\": \\\"...\\\"` and `\\\"hint\\\": \\\"...\\\"` but not `what`/`where`/`why` as separate keys. An implementer cannot know whether to put structured fields in the JSON output, in stderr messages, or both.\\n\\n**Fix:** Replace `JigError` variants' `String` payloads with a `StructuredError` struct containing `what: String, where_: String, why: String, hint: String`. Update the JSON output schema's error entry to show all four fields. Specify that stderr human output formats these as a multi-line message.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**C-4: `skip_if` template rendering lifecycle unspecified** *(Claude only — valid)*\\n\\n`jig.md:107` shows `skip_if: \\\"{{ class_name }}\\\"` — a Jinja2 expression. `AC-5.7` says \\\"the string exists in the target file\\\" but never specifies that `skip_if` values are rendered through the template engine first. The data model defines `skip_if: Option<String>` — a raw string. If not rendered, the system searches for the literal `{{ class_name }}` instead of `BookingService`. The same ambiguity applies to `inject` path rendering and `to` path rendering — every string field that can contain template expressions needs its rendering point defined.\\n\\n**Fix:** Add to SPEC (FR-5 preamble or a new \\\"Rendering Lifecycle\\\" section): \\\"The following fields are rendered as Jinja2 templates with the recipe's variables before use: `to`, `inject` (path), `skip_if`, `skip_if_exists` (no — boolean, not rendered). All other string fields in FileOp are literal.\\\" This also clarifies what \\\"render all upfront\\\" includes.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**C-5: `jig render` lacks recipe context for validation and hints** *(Codex only — valid)*\\n\\n`AC-7.3` defines `jig render <template> --vars '<json>'` with no recipe argument. But FR-2 variable validation requires recipe declarations for type checking, and `AC-3.17` (\\\"did you mean?\\\" hints) requires declared variable names for edit-distance matching. Without a recipe, `jig render` can't validate types or suggest corrections. Also conflicts with \\\"recipe-relative template loading\\\" — there's no recipe to be relative to.\\n\\n**Fix:** Either (a) `jig render` skips variable validation and \\\"did you mean?\\\" (it's a raw render, no recipe context — document this explicitly), or (b) add an optional `--recipe` flag to `jig render` that enables validation. Option (a) is simpler and aligns with the command's purpose as a quick template preview.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**C-6: Dry-run semantics undefined for dependent operations** *(Codex only — valid)*\\n\\n`AC-6.8` says dry-run produces \\\"identical output format but writes no files.\\\" But `AC-N6.2` says inject can target a file created by an earlier create in the same recipe. In dry-run mode, the create doesn't write the file, so the inject will fail with \\\"target file does not exist\\\" (AC-5.9) — the output is NOT identical. No virtual filesystem or state tracking is defined.\\n\\n**Fix:** Specify in AC-6.8: \\\"In dry-run mode, create operations record their output paths as if written. Inject operations targeting a path created earlier in the same dry-run SHALL behave as if the file exists with the rendered content from the create operation.\\\" This requires the executor to maintain a virtual file state map during dry-run.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Major (should fix before implementation)\\n\\n**M-1: ARCHITECTURE still defines Operation trait; PLAN/SPEC use enum** *(Both reviewers)*\\n\\n`ARCHITECTURE.md:131-139` defines `trait Operation { fn execute(...) }`. `D-4` says \\\"for v0.1, operations can be a simple enum.\\\" PLAN and SPEC use the enum approach. The trait definition will confuse implementers.\\n\\n**Fix:** Remove the `trait Operation` block from ARCHITECTURE's `operations/mod.rs` section. Replace with the `FileOp` enum match dispatch pattern. Keep D-4 as-is (it already says the right thing).\\n\\n---\\n\\n**M-2: Output stream semantics — three different behaviors described** *(Both reviewers)*\\n\\n`AC-6.1` says \\\"output human-readable colored text to stderr\\\" in TTY mode — but what goes to stdout? `I-8` says \\\"JSON to stdout, human-readable to stderr\\\" implying both always. `ARCHITECTURE.md:274` says \\\"if stdout is a TTY, use human mode; if piped, use JSON\\\" — one OR the other. These are three different behaviors.\\n\\n**Fix:** Specify explicitly: In TTY mode, human output goes to stderr, nothing to stdout. In piped mode, JSON goes to stdout, nothing to stderr (except errors). `--json` forces JSON to stdout regardless of TTY. `--quiet` suppresses stderr entirely and suppresses stdout JSON except on error. Update I-8 to match.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**M-3: Flag interaction precedence undefined** *(Both ograms, interpreters, or shared libraries beyond the system C library to run | TEST-N3.2 |\n./docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:1:# PLAN.md\n./docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md:32:- `jig vars recipe.yaml` outputs JSON matching the SPEC schema (type, required, default,
