Complete\",\"replace_all\":false}},{\"tool_name\":\"Write\",\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01JnhzKMVVP8ya3jYxnRMUmS\",\"tool_input\":{\"file_path\":\"/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\",\"content\":\"# PLAN.md\\n\\n> Workstream: core-engine\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Status: Complete\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\n\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\n\\n## Phases\\n\\n### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\n\\nBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror, regex, indexmap)\\n- [x] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — StructuredError struct (what/where/why/hint), JigError enum wrapping StructuredError with exit code mapping (0-4)\\n- [x] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation (missing fields, missing template files); unknown operation type detection with clear error message; custom deserialization for FileOp — use intermediate flat struct with optional fields, then validate and convert to typed enum (reject when more than one of `to`/`inject`/`replace`/`patch` is present, or none is present; if `replace` or `patch` is present, emit AC-1.10 'not supported in v0.1' error, reject when multiple inject modes (after/before/prepend/append) are specified); compile-check regex patterns in after/before fields during recipe validation\\n- [x] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — Variable merging and type-checking scaffolding (types imported from recipe.rs, validation logic added in Phase 2)\\n- [x] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands, `#[command(version)]`; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes; `jig validate` outputs summary to stderr (variable count, operation types); with `--json`, outputs structured JSON to stdout\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `jig validate recipe.yaml` parses the example recipe from jig.md and exits 0\\n- `jig validate bad.yaml` exits 1 with a clear error naming the problem\\n- `jig vars recipe.yaml` outputs JSON matching the SPEC schema (type, required, default, description)\\n- Template paths resolve relative to recipe file, not cwd\\n- AC-1.1 through AC-1.15 have corresponding unit tests (AC-1.11 empty files array tested in Phase 3 when `jig run` exists)\\n- `jig validate` output includes variable names and operation types\\n- AC-N5.1 exit codes are correct for recipe validation errors\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `Cargo.toml`\\n- `src/main.rs`\\n- `src/recipe.rs`\\n- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\n- `src/error.rs`\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- regex crate (needed at parse time for compile-checking after/before patterns)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, FR-7 (partial), NFR-1 (partial)\\n\\nFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence (defaults < file < stdin < inline); type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\n- [x] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\n- [x] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\"did you mean?\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\n- [x] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options. For `jig render`, create a standalone Environment with filters registered but no template directory — load the template file directly by path via `render_str()` or equivalent\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\n- [x] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering (conditionals, loops, comments, raw blocks, undefined vars, syntax errors)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- All 13 filters produce correct output per AC-3.4 through AC-3.14\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\"class_name\\\": \\\"BookingService\\\"}'` renders to stdout\\n- Type mismatch exits 4 with expected vs actual type (AC-2.7)\\n- Missing required variable exits 4 with variable name and hint (AC-2.5)\\n- Merge precedence: inline --vars wins over --vars-stdin wins over --vars-file wins over defaults (AC-2.4)\\n- Undefined template variable exits 2 with \\\"did you mean?\\\" hint (AC-3.17)\\n- Template syntax error exits 2 with file path and line number (AC-3.18)\\n- Same inputs produce byte-identical output across runs (AC-N1.1)\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '...' --to output.txt` writes to file instead of stdout (AC-7.4)\\n- Multiple validation errors accumulated and reported together (AC-2.11)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\n- `src/filters.rs`\\n- `src/renderer.rs`\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- heck crate for case conversions\\n- minijinja crate for template rendering\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\n\\nFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force, virtual_files for dry-run state); OpResult enum (Success with action/path/lines/location, Skip, Error); operation dispatch (create only initially). In dry-run mode, create ops populate virtual_files instead of writing to disk.\\n- [x] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\n- [x] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; track per-path write/skip status for files_written and files_skipped arrays; colored human output to stderr; --verbose includes rendered content\\n- [x] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; render all template contents AND templated path fields (`to`, `inject`, `skip_if`) into a Vec before executing any operation — rendering failures abort before any file is touched; execute operations in declaration order; fail-fast on operation errors — stop execution on first failure\\n- [x] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation — happy path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, templated paths, directory creation, file-already-exists error\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting — JSON schema matches SPEC, human output, quiet mode, verbose mode, dry-run\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `jig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'` creates files at templated paths (AC-4.1, AC-4.2)\\n- Parent directories created automatically (AC-4.3)\\n- skip_if_exists: true skips existing files with action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-4.4)\\n- Default (skip_if_exists: false) errors on existing file without --force (AC-4.5)\\n- --force overwrites regardless (AC-4.6)\\n- --base-dir changes output root (AC-4.7)\\n- --dry-run produces output but writes nothing (AC-6.8)\\n- JSON output when piped, human output when TTY (AC-6.1, AC-6.2)\\n- --json forces JSON (AC-6.3), --quiet suppresses non-errors (AC-6.4)\\n- Operations execute in declaration order (AC-N6.1)\\n- Second run with skip_if_exists: true reports all skips (AC-N2.1)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\n- `src/operations/create.rs`\\n- `src/output.rs`\\n- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- owo-colors crate for terminal coloring\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\n\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search (skip_if string search must check virtual_files content when target was created in same dry-run); regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content for subsequent operations\\n- [x] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n- [x] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode — after (first match), after (last match), before, prepend, append, skip_if, missing target file error, regex no-match error, templated inject path\\n- [x] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run (create file, then inject into it — tests ordered execution AC-N6.2)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- after: content on line after first match (AC-5.1)\\n- before: content on line before first match (AC-5.2)\\n- prepend: content at start of file (AC-5.3)\\n- append: content at end of file (AC-5.4)\\n- at:last uses last match (AC-5.5), at:first (default) uses first (AC-5.6)\\n- skip_if: skips when string found in file, reports action:\\\"skip\\\" (AC-5.7)\\n- Regex no-match exits 3 with pattern, file path, hint (AC-5.8)\\n- Missing target file exits 3 (AC-5.9)\\n- Inject path renders as template (AC-5.11)\\n- Create-then-inject in same recipe works (AC-N6.2)\\n- Second run with skip_if shows all skips — no duplicate content (AC-N2.2)\\n- Inject success reports action:\\\"inject\\\" with path, location, line count (AC-5.10)\\n- at field ignored when prepend/append specified; after/before without regex exits 1 (AC-5.12, AC-5.13)\\n- Invalid regex pattern in after/before exits 1 (AC-5.14)\\n- Multiple inject modes (after+before etc.) specified exits 1 (AC-5.15)\\n- --force has no effect on inject operations (AC-5.16)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- regex crate\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats. This phase validates the entire v0.1 pipeline end-to-end.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert JSON output against expected_output.json; assert exit code against expected_exit_code\\n- [x] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\n- [x] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n- [x] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force\\n- [x] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n- [x] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format, human output format, error message format\\n- [x] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n\\n#### Validation Criteria\\n- `cargo test` runs all unit + integration + snapshot tests green\\n- Every operation mode has at least one fixture\\n- Every error exit code (1-4) has at least one fixture\\n- Adding a new test case requires only a new directory, no code changes. Integration fixtures are auto-discovered from directories (no code changes to add tests). Spec-level unit tests are named functions in `#[cfg(test)]` modules (e.g., `spec::fr1::ac_1_1`). The two layers serve different purposes.\\n- Idempotency fixture: second run produces all skips, no file changes\\n- Binary has no dynamic dependencies beyond system libc (verified via `otool -L` on macOS, `ldd` on Linux)\\n- Every `TEST-*` ID in SPEC.md has a corresponding test function named `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}` or `spec::nfr{N}::ac_n{N}_{M}`\\n- Error fixtures assert that error JSON contains `what`, `where`, `why`, and `hint` fields (at least one fixture per exit code)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `tests/integration.rs`\\n- `tests/fixtures/` (directory tree)\\n\\n#### Dependencies\\n- insta crate for snapshot testing\\n- assert_cmd or similar for subprocess testing (or raw std::process::Command)\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\n| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n\\n## Risks / Open Questions\\n\\nAll original risks resolved during implementation. See Resolved Questions below.\\n\\n## Resolved Questions\\n\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\n- **TTY detection**: `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS. Used in std since Rust 1.70, no external crate needed.\\n- **stdin variable reading**: `--vars-stdin` consumes stdin. Non-interactive by design (I-3), documented as single-purpose per invocation.\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: Reads entire file into memory. Fine for source files (<10K lines). Accepted as known limitation for v0.1.\\n- **pluralizer irregular plurals**: Verified working for standard cases. Acceptable for v0.1 scope.\\n- **minijinja built-in filter override**: Successfully overridden by registering our filters. No conflicts.\\n\\n## Execution Order\\n\\n```\\nPhase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5\\nskeleton     vars+render  create+output inject     integration\\n                                                    tests\\n```\\n\\nEach phase produces a working, testable artifact. No phase depends on \\\"wiring up later.\\\" The CLI grows incrementally:\\n- After Phase 1: `jig validate`, `jig vars`\\n- After Phase 2: + `jig render`\\n- After Phase 3: + `jig run` (create only)\\n- After Phase 4: + `jig run` (create + inject) — **v0.1 complete**\\n- After Phase 5: Full test coverage validates the release\\n\\n## Completion Summary\\n\\nAll 5 phases completed 2026-04-04. 191 tests passing (177 unit + 2 CLI integration + 12 fixture integration). 22 test fixture directories covering success paths, error cases, and combined operations.\\n\\n### Outstanding Review Findings\\n\\nThe final code review identified issues that should be addressed in a follow-up before v0.2 work begins:\\n\\n**Critical:**\\n- Pipeline stage ordering: `--base-dir` existence check (exit 3) runs before template rendering (exit 2), violating AC-N5.2\\n\\n**Major:**\\n- Pre-operation errors bypass output formatting — LLM callers get no JSON on early failures (violates I-4/I-10)\\n- `--vars-file` IO errors discard original error context (permission-denied shows as \\\"not found\\\")\\n- Empty regex pattern `after: \\\"\\\"` silently matches every line instead of failing\\n\\n**Minor:**\\n- Unknown YAML fields on file ops silently ignored instead of reported\\n- `validate` subcommand's local `--json` flag shadows the global flag\\n- `find_undefined_variable` is a best-effort regex heuristic (acceptable for v0.1)\\n- Regex recompiled on every inject execution (perf nit, not functional)\\n\"}},{\"tool_name\":\"Write\",\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01QBHeWXki3iAc8hUEvfPACv\",\"tool_input\":{\"file_path\":\"/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\",\"content\":\"# PLAN.md\\n\\n> Workstream: core-engine\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Status: Complete\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\n\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\n\\n## Phases\\n\\n### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\n\\nBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror, regex, indexmap)\\n- [x] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — StructuredError struct (what/where/why/hint), JigError enum wrapping StructuredError with exit code mapping (0-4)\\n- [x] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation (missing fields, missing template files); unknown operation type detection with clear error message; custom deserialization for FileOp — use intermediate flat struct with optional fields, then validate and convert to typed enum (reject when more than one of `to`/`inject`/`replace`/`patch` is present, or none is present; if `replace` or `patch` is present, emit AC-1.10 'not supported in v0.1' error, reject when multiple inject modes (after/before/prepend/append) are specified); compile-check regex patterns in after/before fields during recipe validation\\n- [x] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — Variable merging and type-checking scaffolding (types imported from recipe.rs, validation logic added in Phase 2)\\n- [x] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands, `#[command(version)]`; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes; `jig validate` outputs summary to stderr (variable count, operation types); with `--json`, outputs structured JSON to stdout\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `Cargo.toml`\\n- `src/main.rs`\\n- `src/recipe.rs`\\n- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\n- `src/error.rs`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, FR-7 (partial), NFR-1 (partial)\\n\\nFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence (defaults < file < stdin < inline); type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\n- [x] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\n- [x] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\"did you mean?\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\n- [x] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\n- [x] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\n- `src/filters.rs`\\n- `src/renderer.rs`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\n\\nFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force, virtual_files for dry-run state); OpResult enum; operation dispatch\\n- [x] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\n- [x] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; colored human output to stderr\\n- [x] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; render-all-then-execute pipeline\\n- [x] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\n- `src/operations/create.rs`\\n- `src/output.rs`\\n- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\n\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search; regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content\\n- [x] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n- [x] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode\\n- [x] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats. This phase validates the entire v0.1 pipeline end-to-end.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert exit code\\n- [x] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\n- [x] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n- [x] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force, template syntax error\\n- [x] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n- [x] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format and error messages\\n- [x] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `tests/integration.rs`\\n- `tests/cli.rs`\\n- `tests/fixtures/` (22 fixture directories)\\n- `tests/snapshots/` (7 snapshot files)\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit until user-defined operations exist, which isn't planned. (ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\n| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n\\n## Resolved Questions\\n\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\n- **TTY detection**: `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS. Used in std since Rust 1.70, no external crate needed.\\n- **stdin variable reading**: `--vars-stdin` consumes stdin. Non-interactive by design (I-3), documented as single-purpose per invocation.\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: Reads entire file into memory. Fine for source files (<10K lines). Accepted as known limitation for v0.1.\\n- **pluralizer irregular plurals**: Verified working for standard cases. Acceptable for v0.1 scope.\\n- **minijinja built-in filter override**: Successfully overridden by registering our filters. No conflicts.\\n\\n## Completion Summary\\n\\nAll 5 phases completed 2026-04-04. 191 tests passing (177 unit + 2 CLI integration + 12 fixture integration). 22 test fixture directories covering success paths, error cases, and combined operations.\\n\\n### Outstanding Review Findings\\n\\nThe final code review (2026-04-04) identified issues to address before v0.2 work begins:\\n\\n**Critical:**\\n- Pipeline stage ordering: `--base-dir` existence check (exit 3) runs before template rendering (exit 2), violating AC-N5.2\\n\\n**Major:**\\n- Pre-operation errors bypass output formatting — LLM callers get no JSON on early failures (violates I-4/I-10)\\n- `--vars-file` IO errors discard original error context (permission-denied shows as \\\"not found\\\")\\n- Empty regex pattern `after: \\\"\\\"` silently matches every line instead of failing\\n\\n**Minor:**\\n- Unknown YAML fields on file ops silently ignored instead of reported\\n- `validate` subcommand's local `--json` flag shadows the global flag\\n- `find_undefined_variable` is a best-effort regex heuristic (acceptable for v0.1)\\n- Regex recompiled on every inject execution (perf nit, not functional)\\n\"}},{\"tool_name\":\"Bash\",\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01NBFkfiT12KkYySPuVGTrJV\",\"tool_input\":{\"command\":\"cat > docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md << 'PLANEOF'\\n# PLAN.md\\n\\n> Workstream: core-engine\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\n> Status: Complete\\n\\n## Objective\\n\\nDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\n\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\n\\n## Phases\\n\\n### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\n\\nBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror, regex, indexmap)\\n- [x] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — StructuredError struct (what/where/why/hint), JigError enum wrapping StructuredError with exit code mapping (0-4)\\n- [x] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation; unknown operation type detection; custom deserialization for FileOp; compile-check regex patterns during recipe validation\\n- [x] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — Variable merging and type-checking scaffolding (types imported from recipe.rs, validation logic added in Phase 2)\\n- [x] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands, `#[command(version)]`; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `Cargo.toml`\\n- `src/main.rs`\\n- `src/recipe.rs`\\n- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\n- `src/error.rs`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, FR-7 (partial), NFR-1 (partial)\\n\\nFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence; type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\n- [x] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja\\n- [x] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\"did you mean?\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\n- [x] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\n- [x] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\n- `src/filters.rs`\\n- `src/renderer.rs`\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\n\\nFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force, virtual_files for dry-run state); OpResult enum; operation dispatch\\n- [x] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\n- [x] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; colored human output to stderr\\n- [x] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; render-all-then-execute pipeline\\n- [x] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\n- `src/operations/create.rs`\\n- `src/output.rs`\\n- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\n\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search; regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content\\n- [x] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\n- [x] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode\\n- [x] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\n\\n---\\n\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\nStatus: Complete\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\n\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats.\\n\\n#### Milestones\\n- [x] 5.1: Test harness in `tests/integration.rs` — fixture discovery; copy existing/ to temp dir; run jig as subprocess; diff output against expected/; assert exit code\\n- [x] 5.2: Fixtures for create operations — simple create, templated path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, directory creation\\n- [x] 5.3: Fixtures for inject operations — after/before/prepend/append, at:first/at:last, skip_if\\n- [x] 5.4: Fixtures for error cases — missing vars, bad type, missing template, missing target file, regex no-match, malformed YAML, file exists without force, template syntax error\\n- [x] 5.5: Fixtures for combined operations — create + inject in one recipe, multi-file recipe, idempotency (run twice)\\n- [x] 5.6: insta snapshot tests for JSON output format and error messages\\n- [x] 5.7: Determinism test — run same recipe twice, assert byte-identical output (AC-N1.1)\\n\\n#### Key Files\\n- `tests/integration.rs`\\n- `tests/cli.rs`\\n- `tests/fixtures/` (22 fixture directories)\\n- `tests/snapshots/` (7 snapshot files)\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\n- **Depends on:** None (first workstream, greenfield)\\n- **Blocks:** replace-operation (v0.2), patch-operation (v0.2), workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4)\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\n| Operations as enum, not trait objects | `FileOp` enum with match dispatch | Only 2 variants in v0.1 (create, inject). Trait abstraction adds complexity for no benefit. (D-4) |\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages. (D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable. (D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism. (D-5, I-1) |\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate |\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling |\\n| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Serves I-10 (graceful degradation). |\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves | We control exact behavior. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. (D-1) |\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness breaks real usage. |\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later ops may depend on earlier ones. (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n\\n## Resolved Questions\\n\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names.\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. No conflicts with builtins.\\n- **TTY detection**: `std::io::IsTerminal` works correctly on macOS. In std since Rust 1.70.\\n- **stdin variable reading**: `--vars-stdin` consumes stdin. Non-interactive by design (I-3).\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: Reads entire file into memory. Fine for source files (<10K lines).\\n- **minijinja built-in filter override**: Successfully overridden by registering our filters.\\n\\n## Completion Summary\\n\\nAll 5 phases completed 2026-04-04. 191 tests passing (177 unit + 2 CLI integration + 12 fixture integration). 22 test fixture directories covering success paths, error cases, and combined operations.\\n\\n### Outstanding Review template rendering (exit 2), violating AC-N5.2\\n\\n**Major:**\\n- Pre-operation errors bypass output formatting — LLM callers get no JSON on early failures (violates I-4/I-10)\\n- `--vars-file` IO errors discard original error context (permission-denied shows as \\\"not found\\\")\\n- Empty regex pattern `after: \\\"\\\"` silently matches every line instead of failing\\n\\n**Minor:**\\n- Unknown YAML fields on file ops silently ignored instead of reported\\n- `validate` subcommand's local `--json` flag shadows the global flag\\n- `find_undefined_variable` is a best-effort regex PLAN.md\n./docs/workstreams/core-engine/tasks/phase-5/CONTEXT.md:31:- Every `TEST-*` ID in SPEC.md has a corresponding test function named `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}` or `spec::nfr{N}::ac_n{N}_{M}`\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:54:This workstream owns the entire `src/` tree for v0.1:\n./docs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:54:This workstream owns the entire `src/` tree for v0.1:\n./docs/workstreams/replace-patch/reviews/code-review-claude-20260404-165354.md:1:{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"success\",\"is_error\":false,\"duration_ms\":262982,\"duration_api_ms\":510728,\"num_turns\":26,\"result\":\"Now I have a comprehensive view of the implementation. Let me compose the review.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Verdict: Request Changes**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Critical (must fix before merge)\\n\\n**C1. `delimiter.rs:87` — Byte index used where char index is tracked: potential panic on non-ASCII**\\n\\n```rust\\nlet between = &lines[open_line][open_col + 1..scanner.col];\\n```\\n\\n`CharScanner` tracks `col` in *character* positions (via `chars().nth()`, `chars().count()`), but this line slices the string with byte indices. For any file containing multi-byte UTF-8 characters (comments in Chinese/Japanese, emoji in strings, accented variable names), this will panic with `byte index N is not a char boundary`. The fix is to use `char_indices()` to convert char offsets to byte offsets before slicing.\\n\\n**C2. `operations/replace.rs:273-281` and `operations/patch.rs:224-231` — `write_back` silently swallows write errors**\\n\\n```rust\\nlet _ = std::fs::write(target, new_content);\\n```\\n\\nIn the fallback paths (replace) and the main path (patch), disk write failures are silently ignored. The spec (AC-2.14, AC-7.11) requires exit code 3 with path, permission error, and rendered content. The main replace path at line 106 correctly returns an error on write failure, but `write_back()` used by fallback-append/prepend and *all* of patch does not. This means a permission-denied error during patch will return `OpResult::Success` — a silent corruption that violates I-10 (never silently produce wrong output).\\n\\n**C3. `scope/position.rs:169-185` — `Position::Sorted` is a stub, not an implementation**\\n\\n```rust\\nfn resolve_sorted(...) -> Result<PositionResult, StructuredError> {\\n    // For sorted, we don't have the content to sort against...\\n    let insertion = scope.closing_line.unwrap_or(scope.end_line + 1);\\n    Ok(PositionResult { insertion_line: insertion, ... })\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe spec (AC-5.7) requires: \\\"find the alphabetically correct insertion point among sibling lines at the same indentation level within the scope.\\\" This just inserts at end-of-scope. The `Sorted` variant is parsed and accepted as valid by the recipe parser, so a user can write `position: sorted` and get silently wrong behavior (appended, not sorted). Either implement it or reject it at parse time with a clear \\\"not yet implemented\\\" error.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Major (should fix)\\n\\n**M1. `scope/position.rs:62` — `AfterLastMethod` regex misses common Rust qualifiers**\\n\\n```rust\\nlet method_re = Regex::new(r\\\"^\\\\s+(pub\\\\s+)?(async\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\").unwrap();\\n```\\n\\nThis misses `const fn`, `unsafe fn`, `pub(crate) fn`, `pub unsafe async fn`, `extern \\\"C\\\" fn`, and `pub const fn`. For a tool targeting Rust as a first-class citizen, this is a significant gap. A more robust pattern: `r\\\"^\\\\s+(pub(\\\\s*\\\\([^)]*\\\\))?\\\\s+)?(const\\\\s+)?(unsafe\\\\s+)?(async\\\\s+)?(extern\\\\s+\\\\\\\"[^\\\\\\\"]*\\\\\\\"\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\"`.\\n\\n**M2. `scope/position.rs:40` — `AfterLastField` regex `^\\\\s+\\\\w+\\\\s*[:=]` requires leading whitespace**\\n\\nThe `\\\\s+` at the start means this never matches fields at indent level 0 (e.g., top-level struct fields in Go `Name string`, module-level Python `CONSTANT = 42`). Should be `^\\\\s*\\\\w+\\\\s*[:=]` if the intent is to work at any indentation, or the assumption should be documented.\\n\\n**M3. 11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures are missing (42% gap)**\\n\\nMissing fixtures:\\n- `patch-function-body`, `patch-brackets`, `patch-function-signature`, `patch-find-narrowing`\\n- `patch-sorted`, `patch-indent-matching`, `patch-nested-delimiters`, `patch-string-delimiters`\\n- `error-patch-unbalanced`, `error-patch-find-missing`\\n- `combined-patch-idempotent`\\n\\nUnit tests cover most of these behaviors, but the spec explicitly requires fixture-level integration tests for each. The replace side is complete (7/7). The patch side is 4/11.\\n\\n**M4. `scope/delimiter.rs:171` — `peek_next` returns `None` when next line is empty**\\n\\n```rust\\n} else if self.line + 1 < self.lines.len() && !self.lines[self.line + 1].is_empty() {\\n```\\n\\nIf the character after the current position is on a blank line (common in real code — blank lines between struct fields), `peek_next` returns `None`. This breaks comment detection (`//`, `/*`) when `//` straddles a line boundary (unlikely but possible) and more importantly causes the scanner to not recognize patterns that span across blank lines correctly.\\n\\n**M5. No `ScopeDiagnostics` struct or verbose scope detail output (FR-8 incomplete)**\\n\\nThe spec (AC-8.1 through AC-8.5) requires verbose mode to include a `scope_diagnostics` object in JSON output with `anchor_line`, `scope_start`, `scope_end`, `insertion_line`, `find_match_line`, `position_fallback`. Currently verbose only adds the `rendered_content` string. The location string encodes some of this information as text, but the structured diagnostic object specified in the JSON Output Extensions section of the spec is absent.\\n\\n**M6. `operations/patch.rs:160-162` — Location string uses `Debug` formatting, producing `classbody` instead of `class_body`**\\n\\n```rust\\nlet scope_name = format!(\\\"{:?}\\\", anchor.scope).to_lowercase();\\n```\\n\\n`#[derive(Debug)]` on `ScopeType::ClassBody` produces `\\\"ClassBody\\\"`, which `.to_lowercase()` turns into `\\\"classbody\\\"`. The spec's JSON Output Extensions show `\\\"class_body(^class Foo):after_last_field\\\"`. Similarly `AfterLastField` becomes `\\\"afterlastfield\\\"` instead of `\\\"after_last_field\\\"`. This affects both machine-parseable output and human readability. Implement `Display` for `ScopeType` and `Position` with snake_case output.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Minor (nice to fix)\\n\\n**m1. `scope/delimiter.rs` — `chars().count()` called on every `normalize()`, `peek()`, `advance()`**\\n\\n`self.lines[self.line].chars().count()` is O(n) per call. In the tight scanner loop, this means O(n^2) over a line. Cache the line length or use `len()` if sticking to ASCII-only files (which the byte-index bug in C1 already assumes).\\n\\n**m2. `operations/patch.rs:48` — `skip_if` uses substring match, not pattern match**\\n\\n```rust\\nif file_content.contains(skip_str) {\\n```\\n\\nA `skip_if` value of `\\\"import\\\"` would match `\\\"important\\\"`. The inject operation uses the same approach, so this is consistent, but it's worth noting that this is a substring check, not a whole-line or word-boundary check. AC-7.2 says \\\"search for the rendered string\\\" so technically compliant, but a rendered value like `\\\"x\\\"` would skip on nearly any file.\\n\\n**m3. `operations/replace.rs:162-163` — `Regex::new().expect()` at runtime instead of using pre-compiled regex**\\n\\n```rust\\nlet start_re = Regex::new(start_pattern).expect(\\\"regex validated at parse time\\\");\\n```\\n\\nThe recipe parser validates regex syntax at parse time, so the `.expect()` should never fire. But this compiles the regex fresh for every replace execution. Pre-compile during recipe parsing and pass the compiled `Regex` through to avoid the overhead (minor perf, but relevant since jig may be called dozens of times per LLM session per I-6).\\n\\n**m4. `scope/indent.rs` — `find_body_start` control flow is confusing**\\n\\nThe nested `if` / `continue` / `break` pattern is hard to follow. It works correctly but a refactor to use `match` or clearer early returns would improve maintainability.\\n\\n**m5. `operations/mod.rs:38-41` — `resolve_path` does no path traversal validation**\\n\\n```rust\\npub fn resolve_path(&self, relative: &str) -> PathBuf {\\n    self.base_dir.join(relative)\\n}\\n```\\n\\nA recipe with `replace: \\\"../../etc/passwd\\\"` would resolve outside `base_dir`. This isn't a security issue per se (jig is a build tool run by trusted input), but it violates the principle of least surprise and could cause hard-to-debug errors.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Strengths\\n\\n- **Error-with-rendered-content contract is solid.** Every error path in both replace and patch includes the rendered template content. The I-10 invariant (never waste rendering work) is well-honored.\\n\\n- **Virtual files chaining is clean.** The virtual_files HashMap for dry-run and cross-operation chaining is simple, correct, and tested. Create → Replace → Patch chains work in both dry and wet modes.\\n\\n- **Recipe parsing is thorough.** All 19 FR-1 acceptance criteria are covered with explicit validation: regex compile-check, mutual exclusivity, default values, invalid enum rejection. The parser is the strongest part of the implementation.\\n\\n- **Scope detection architecture is well-separated.** `scope/` has zero dependency on the operation pipeline. It takes `&[&str]` and returns structs. This makes it trivially unit-testable and easy to reason about in isolation.\\n\\n- **Replace operation is complete.** All 14 AC-2.x criteria are implemented with matching fixtures. The between/pattern/fallback matrix is fully tested. This is merge-ready modulo the `write_back` error swallowing (C2).\\n\\n- **284 tests passing, zero failures.** The test suite is substantial and covers the critical paths well. The fixture auto-discovery mechanism means adding new fixtures requires zero test harness cfg!(target_os = \\\"macos\\\") {\\n   594\\t        let output = Command::new(\\\"otool\\\")\\n   595\\t            .args([\\\"-L\\\", &binary.display().to_string()])\\n   596\\t dependency: {}\\\",\\n   608\\t                line\\n   609\\t            );\\n   610\\t        }\\n   611\\t    } else if cfg!(target_os = \\\"linux\\\") {\\n   612\\t        let output = find the last line within the scope matching `^\\\\s+def \\\\w+` or `^\\\\s+(pub\\\\s+)?(fn\\\\|async fn) \\\\w+` or `^\\\\s+(public\\\\|private\\\\|protected\\\\|static\\\\|async)?\\\\s*\\\\w+\\\\s*\\\\(` and set the insertion point immediately after that method's complete body | TEST-5.5 |\\n   121\\t| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `position: after_last_import` is specified, the system SHALL find the last line within the scope matching `^\\\\s*(from\\\\|import) ` and set the insertion point immediately after it | TEST-5.6 |\\n   122\\t| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN `position: sorted` is specified, the system SHALL find the alphabetically correct insertion point among sibling lines at the same indentation level within the scope, comparing the trimmed content of each line | TEST-5.7 |\\n   123\\t| AC-5.8 | Unwanted | IF `position: after_last_field` finds no matching field lines in the scope, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` position and include a note in verbose output | TEST-5.8 |\\n   124\\t| AC-5.9 | Unwanted | IF `position: after_last_method` finds no matching method lines in the scope, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` position and include a note in verbose output | TEST-5.9 |\\n   125\\t| AC-5.10 | Unwanted | IF `position: after_last_import` finds no matching import lines in the scope, the system SHALL fall back to `before` position (top of scope) and include a note in verbose output | TEST-5.10 |\\n   126\\t| AC-5.11 | Event | WHEN `position: after_last_method` is resolved, the system SHALL identify the method's complete body (using the same scope detection logic as `function_body`) and insert after the body ends, not just after the `def`/`fn` line | TEST-5.11 |\\n   127\\t\\n   128\\t#### FR-6: Find Narrowing\\n   129\\t\\n   130\\tWhen a scope is large (e.g., an entire class body), `find` narrows the operation to a specific element within that scope.\\n   131\\t\\n   132\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   133\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   134\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   135\\t| AC-6.1 | Event | WHEN `anchor.find` is specified as a plain string, the system SHALL search within the detected scope for the first line containing that string and re-anchor the position resolution to that line | TEST-6.1 |\\n   136\\t| AC-6.2 | Event | WHEN `anchor.find` is specified and the found line opens a sub-scope (e.g., `list_display = [` opens a brackets scope), the system SHALL apply the `position` relative to that sub-scope, not the original class/function scope | TEST-6.2 |\\n   137\\t| AC-6.3 | Unwanted | IF `anchor.find` is specified and no line within the scope contains the string, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting: the find string, the scope boundaries (start/end line numbers), and the file path, with the rendered content in the error | TEST-6.3 |\\n   138\\t| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `anchor.find` narrows to a sub-scope and `position: before_close` is specified, the system SHALL insert before the closing delimiter of the sub-scope (e.g., before `]` in `list_display = [...]`) | TEST-6.4 |\\n   139\\t| AC-6.5 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL apply `find` after scope detection but before position resolution — the order is: pattern match → scope detection → find narrowing → position resolution | TEST-6.5 |\\n   140\\t\\n   141\\t#### FR-7: Patch Operation\\n   142\\t\\n   143\\tRender a template and insert the result into an existing file at a structurally-determined location using the anchor system (pattern + scope + find + position). Support idempotency via `skip_if`.\\n   144\\t\\n   145\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   146\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   147\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   148\\t| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN a patch operation executes, the system SHALL: (1) find the anchor pattern in the target file, (2) detect the scope, (3) optionally narrow with find, (4) resolve the position, and (5) insert the rendered content at the resolved position | TEST-7.1 |\\n   149\\t| AC-7.2 | Event | WHEN `skip_if` is specified, the system SHALL render it as a Jinja2 template with the recipe's variables, then search for the rendered string in the target file. If found, the system SHALL skip the operation and report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` with a reason | TEST-7.2 |\\n   150\\t| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN a patch operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"patch\\\"` with the path, location description (including scope type and position, e.g., `\\\"class_body(^class Foo):after_last_field\\\"`), and line count (number of rendered lines inserted) | TEST-7.3 |\\n   151\\t| AC-7.4 | Unwanted | IF the anchor pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the pattern, the file path, and a hint, with the rendered content included | TEST-7.4 |\\n   152\\t| AC-7.5 | Unwanted | IF scope detection fails (e.g., unbalanced delimiters, inconsistent indentation), the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the scope type, the anchor line, the specific failure reason (e.g., \\\"unbalanced braces at line 45\\\"), and include the rendered content in the error | TEST-7.5 |\\n   153\\t| AC-7.6 | Event | WHEN the anchor pattern matches multiple lines, the system SHALL use the first match (consistent with inject's default `at: first` behavior) | TEST-7.6 |\\n   154\\t| AC-7.7 | Event | WHEN a patch operation inserts content, the system SHALL match the indentation of the insertion point — if the surrounding lines at the target position use N-space indentation, the inserted content's base indentation SHALL be adjusted to match | TEST-7.7 |\\n   155\\t| AC-7.8 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL resolve the patch target path relative to `--base-dir` (consistent with create, inject, and replace) | TEST-7.8 |\\n   156\\t| AC-7.9 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL support patch operations in dry-run mode, reading from and updating `virtual_files` for chaining with prior operations in the same run | TEST-7.9 |\\n   157\\t| AC-7.10 | Unwanted | IF the target file for patching does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path with the rendered content | TEST-7.10 |\\n   158\\t| AC-7.11 | Unwanted | IF writing the modified file content fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit with code 3 with the path, permission error, and rendered content | TEST-7.11 |\\n   159\\t| AC-7.12 | Event | WHEN `scope: line` is used (the default), the system SHALL behave identically to inject's `after` mode for the matched line — no structural analysis, just insert after the matched line | TEST-7.12 |\\n   160\\t\\n   161\\t#### FR-8: Verbose Scope Diagnostics\\n   162\\t\\n   163\\tExtend `--verbose` mode to show scope detection details — boundaries, insertion points, and find narrowing — so recipe authors can debug anchor configurations.\\n   164\\t\\n   165\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   166\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   167\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   168\\t| AC-8.1 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified for a patch operation, the system SHALL include in the output: the anchor pattern, the matched line number, the scope type, scope start line, scope end line, and the resolved insertion line | TEST-8.1 |\\n   169\\t| AC-8.2 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified and `find` narrowing is used, the system SHALL include the find string, the line it was found on, and the sub-scope boundaries (if applicable) | TEST-8.2 |\\n   170\\t| AC-8.3 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified for a replace operation, the system SHALL include the match mode (between or pattern), the matched region (start line to end line), and the number of lines replaced | TEST-8.3 |\\n   171\\t| AC-8.4 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified and a semantic position falls back (e.g., `after_last_field` falls back to `before_close` because no fields found), the system SHALL include a note about the fallback in the output | TEST-8.4 |\\n   172\\t| AC-8.5 | Ubiquitous | Verbose scope diagnostics SHALL appear in both JSON output (as additional fields in the operation object) and human-readable output (as indented detail lines below the operation summary) | TEST-8.5 |\\n   173\\t\\n   174\\t### Non-Functional Requirements\\n   175\\t\\n   176\\t#### NFR-1: Language-Agnostic Scope Detection\\n   177\\t\\n   178\\tScope detection must work across languages without language-specific parsers. The same binary handles Python, TypeScript, Rust, Go, Java, YAML, and any other language that uses either indentation or delimiters for structure.\\n   179\\t\\n   180\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   181\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   182\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   183\\t| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL detect indentation-based scopes correctly for Python class bodies, Python function bodies, and YAML nested blocks without any language identifier or configuration | TEST-N1.1 |\\n   184\\t| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL detect delimiter-based scopes correctly for Rust struct bodies, TypeScript interface bodies, Go function bodies, and JSON objects without any language identifier or configuration | TEST-N1.2 |\\n   185\\t| AC-N1.3 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require a language identifier, file extension, or configuration to select between indentation-based and delimiter-based scope detection — the scope type in the recipe determines the detection strategy | TEST-N1.3 |\\n   186\\t\\n   187\\t#### NFR-2: Graceful Degradation for Scope Failures\\n   188\\t\\n   189\\tWhen scope detection or position resolution fails, the system must provide enough information for the caller (typically an LLM) to fall back to manual editing. This extends invariants I-4 and I-10.\\n   190\\t\\n   191\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   192\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   193\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   194\\t| AC-N2.1 | Event | WHEN a patch operation fails due to scope detection, the error output SHALL include the rendered template content so the caller can manually insert it | TEST-N2.1 |\\n   195\\t| AC-N2.2 | Event | WHEN a replace operation fails due to pattern not found (with fallback: error), the error output SHALL include the rendered template content so the caller can manually apply the replacement | TEST-N2.2 |\\n   196\\t| AC-N2.3 | Ubiquitous | Every patch/replace error SHALL include what (the failure), where (file path and line number), why (specific reason: unbalanced delimiter, no match, etc.), and hint (actionable suggestion) | TEST-N2.3 |\\n   197\\t| AC-N2.4 | Event | WHEN scope detection fails, the error hint SHALL suggest using a simpler scope type or falling back to inject with a regex anchor | TEST-N2.4 |\\n   198\\t\\n   199\\t#### NFR-3: Idempotent Patch and Replace Operations\\n   200\\t\\n   201\\tPatch and replace operations must support safe re-runs, consistent with jig's idempotency guarantee (I-2).\\n   202\\t\\n   203\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   204\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   205\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   206\\t| AC-N3.1 | Event | WHEN a patch operation with `skip_if` is run a second time with the same variables on an already-patched file, the system SHALL report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"skip\\\"` and make no changes | TEST-N3.1 |\\n   207\\t| AC-N3.2 | Event | WHEN a replace operation with `between` markers replaces content that is identical to the rendered template (second run), the system SHALL still perform the replacement (replace is inherently idempotent — same content replaces same content) and report `\\\"action\\\": \\\"replace\\\"` | TEST-N3.2 |\\n   208\\t| AC-N3.3 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when patch uses `skip_if` — the idempotency check searches the entire target file, not just the scope | TEST-N3.3 |\\n   209\\t\\n   210\\t#### NFR-4: Deterministic Scope Detection\\n   211\\t\\n   212\\tScope detection must be deterministic — the same file content always produces the same scope boundaries. This extends invariant I-1.\\n   213\\t\\n   214\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\n   215\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\n   216\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\n   217\\t| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce identical scope boundaries when given the same file content and anchor configuration across multiple runs | TEST-N4.1 |\\n   218\\t| AC-N4.2 | Ubiquitous | Scope detection SHALL not depend on file modification time, filesystem metadata, or any external state — only on file content and anchor configuration | TEST-N4.2 |\\n   219\\t\\n   220\\t## Interfaces\\n   221\\t\\n   222\\t### Public API\\n   223\\t\\n   224\\tNo new CLI subcommands. The existing `jig run` and `jig validate` commands gain support for replace and patch operations in recipes. The `--verbose` flag gains additional output for scope diagnostics.\\n   225\\t\\n   226\\t```yaml\\n   227\\t# Replace operation in recipe YAML\\n   228\\tfiles:\\n   229\\t  - template: fragment.j2\\n   230\\t    replace: \\\"path/to/file.py\\\"\\n   231\\t    between:\\n   232\\t      start: \\\"^# region start\\\"\\n   233\\t      end: \\\"^# region end\\\"\\n   234\\t    fallback: append          # append | prepend | skip | error (default)\\n   235\\t\\n   236\\t  - template: fragment.j2\\n   237\\t    replace: \\\"path/to/file.py\\\"\\n   238\\t    pattern: \\\"^old_line.*\\\"\\n   239\\t    fallback: skip\\n   240\\t\\n   241\\t# Patch operation in recipe YAML\\n   242\\tfiles:\\n   243\\t  - template: field.j2\\n   244\\t    patch: \\\"path/to/file.py\\\"\\n   245\\t    anchor:\\n   246\\t      pattern: \\\"^class MyModel\\\\\\\\(\\\"\\n   247\\t      scope: class_body         # line | block | class_body | function_body |\\n   248\\t                                # function_signature | braces | brackets | parens\\n   249\\t      find: \\\"list_display\\\"      # optional narrowing\\n   250\\t      position: before_close    # before | after | before_close | after_last_field |\\n   251\\t                                # after_last_method | after_last_import | sorted\\n   252\\t    skip_if: \\\"{{ field_name }}\\\"\\n   253\\t```\\n   254\\t\\n   255\\t### JSON Output Extensions\\n   256\\t\\n   257\\tReplace and patch operations produce the same `OpResult` structure as create and inject. New `action` values:\\n   258\\t\\n   259\\t```json\\n   260\\t{\\\"action\\\": \\\"replace\\\", \\\"path\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"location\\\": \\\"between:^# start:^# end\\\", \\\"lines\\\": 5}\\n   261\\t{\\\"action\\\": \\\"patch\\\", \\\"path\\\": \\\"...\\\", \\\"location\\\": \\\"class_body(^class Foo):after_last_field\\\", \\\"lines\\\": 1}\\n   262\\t```\\n   263\\t\\n   264\\tVerbose mode adds scope diagnostic fields:\\n   265\\t\\n   266\\t```json\\n   267\\t{\\n   268\\t  \\\"action\\\": \\\"patch\\\",\\n   269\\t  \\\"path\\\": \\\"models/reservation.py\\\",\\n   270\\t  \\\"location\\\": \\\"class_body(^class Reservation):after_last_field\\\",\\n   271\\t  \\\"lines\\\": 1,\\n   272\\t  \\\"scope_diagnostics\\\": {\\n   273\\t    \\\"anchor_line\\\": 12,\\n   274\\t    \\\"scope_start\\\": 13,\\n   275\\t    \\\"scope_end\\\": 45,\\n   276\\t    \\\"insertion_line\\\": 38,\\n   277\\t    \\\"find_match_line\\\": null,\\n   278\\t    \\\"position_fallback\\\": null\\n   279\\t  }\\n file operation errors (exit code 3) include `rendered_content` so the caller can fall back to manual editing. This is always present, independent of `--verbose`.\\n   494\\t\\n   495\\t## Testing Strategy\\n   496\\t\\n   497\\t- **Spec tests:** One or more tests per AC-* criterion above, namespaced as `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}`\\n   498\\t- **Invariant tests:** Deterministic scope detection (same file + anchor = same boundaries), idempotency (patch with skip_if run twice), graceful degradation (error includes rendered content)\\n   499\\t- **Integration fixtures:** Each fixture directory follows the established pattern: `recipe.yaml`, `vars.json`, `templates/`, `existing/`, `expected/`, optionally `expected_output.json` and `expected_exit_code`\\n   500\\t- **Scope detection unit tests:** Dedicated tests per scope type with representative code snippets from Python, Rust, TypeScript, Go, YAML\\n   501\\t- **Position resolution unit tests:** Each semantic position tested against realistic code blocks\\n   502\\t- **Snapshot tests:** `insta` for verbose scope diagnostics output 285\\t```rust\\n   286\\t// ── Extended FileOp enum (recipe.rs) ─────────────────────────────\\n   287\\tenum FileOp {\\n   288\\t    Create { template: String, to: String, skip_if_exists: bool },\\n   289\\t    Inject { template: String, inject: String, mode: InjectMode, skip_if: Option<String> },\\n   290\\t    Replace { template: String, replace: String, spec: ReplaceSpec, fallback: Fallback },\\n   291\\t    Patch { template: String, patch: String, anchor: Anchor, skip_if: Option<String> },\\n   292\\t}\\n   293\\t\\n   294\\t// ── Scope detection (scope/mod.rs) ───────────────────────────────\\n   295\\tfn detect_scope(\\n   296\\t    lines: &[&str],\\n   297\\t    anchor_line: usize,\\n   298\\t    scope_type: &ScopeType,\\n   299\\t) -> Result<ScopeResult, StructuredError>;\\n   300\\t\\n   301\\t// ── Position resolution (scope/position.rs) ──────────────────────\\n   302\\tfn resolve_position(\\n   303\\t    lines: &[&str],\\n   304\\t    scope: &ScopeResult,\\n   305\\t    position: &Position,\\n   306\\t) -> Result<PositionResult, StructuredError>;\\n   307\\t\\n   308\\t// ── Find narrowing (scope/mod.rs or patch.rs) ────────────────────\\n   309\\tfn find_within_scope(\\n   310\\t    lines: &[&str],\\n   311\\t    scope: &ScopeResult,\\n   312\\t    find_str: &str,\\n   313\\t) -> Result<FindResult, StructuredError>;\\n   314\\t\\n   315\\t// ── Operation execution (operations/*.rs) ────────────────────────\\n   316\\t// replace.rs\\n   317\\tfn execute(\\n   318\\t    rendered_path: &str,\\n   319\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\n   320\\t    spec: &ReplaceSpec,\\n   321\\t    fallback: &Fallback,\\n   322\\t    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\n   323\\t    verbose: bool,\\n   324\\t) -> OpResult;\\n   325\\t\\n   326\\t// patch.rs\\n   327\\tfn execute(\\n   328\\t    rendered_path: &str,\\n   329\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\n   330\\t    rendered_skip_if: Option<&str>,\\n   331\\t    anchor: &Anchor,\\n   332\\t    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\n   333\\t    verbose: bool,\\n   334\\t) -> OpResult;\\n   335\\t```\\n   336\\t\\n   337\\t## Component Relationships\\n   338\\t\\n   339\\t```mermaid\\n   340\\tgraph TD\\n   341\\t    Recipe[recipe.rs] -->|parses| Replace[FileOp::Replace]\\n   342\\t    Recipe -->|parses| Patch[FileOp::Patch]\\n   343\\t\\n   344\\t    Dispatch[operations/mod.rs] -->|dispatches| ReplaceOp[operations/replace.rs]\\n   345\\t    Dispatch -->|dispatches| PatchOp[operations/patch.rs]\\n   346\\t\\n   347\\t    PatchOp -->|calls| ScopeMod[scope/mod.rs]\\n   348\\t    ScopeMod -->|indent scopes| Indent[scope/indent.rs]\\n   349\\t    ScopeMod -->|delimiter scopes| Delimiter[scope/delimiter.rs]\\n   350\\t    ScopeMod -->|positions| PositionMod[scope/position.rs]\\n   351\\t\\n   352\\t    PatchOp -->|find narrowing| ScopeMod\\n   353\\t\\n   354\\t    ReplaceOp -->|regex matching| Regex[regex crate]\\n   355\\t    PatchOp -->|anchor matching| Regex\\n   356\\t\\n   357\\t    ReplaceOp -->|results| Output[output.rs]\\n   358\\t    PatchOp -->|results| Output\\n   359\\t```\\n   360\\t\\n   361\\t## Data Model\\n   362\\t\\n   363\\t```rust\\n   364\\t// ── Replace types ──────────────────────────────────────────────\\n   365\\t\\n   366\\t/// How to match the region to replace.\\n   367\\tenum ReplaceSpec {\\n   368\\t    /// Replace content between two marker lines (markers preserved).\\n   369\\t    Between { start: String, end: String },\\n   370\\t    /// Replace all contiguous lines matching this pattern.\\n   371\\t    Pattern(String),\\n   372\\t}\\n   373\\t\\n   374\\t/// What to do when the match pattern is not found.\\n   375\\tenum Fallback {\\n   376\\t    Append,   // append rendered content to end of file\\n   377\\t    Prepend,  // prepend rendered content to start of file\\n   378\\t    Skip,     // skip operation, report as skip\\n   379\\t    Error,    // fail with exit code 3 (default)\\n   380\\t}\\n   381\\t\\n   382\\t// ── Patch types ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n   383\\t\\n   384\\t/// Structural anchor for patch operations.\\n   385\\tstruct Anchor {\\n   386\\t    /// Regex to find the anchor line in the file.\\n   387\\t    pattern: String,\\n   388\\t    /// Structural region to target relative to the match.\\n   389\\t    scope: ScopeType,\\n   390\\t    /// Optional string to find within the scope for narrowing.\\n   391\\t    find: Option<String>,\\n   392\\t    /// Where within the scope to insert.\\n   393\\t    position: Position,\\n   394\\t}\\n   395\\t\\n   396\\t// ── Scope types ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\n   397\\t\\n   398\\t/// Scope type determines how structural boundaries are detected.\\n   399\\tenum ScopeType {\\n   400\\t    Line,               // No scope — anchor line only (default)\\n   401\\t    Block,              // Indentation: deeper-indented block after anchor\\n   402\\t    ClassBody,          // Indentation: class body\\n   403\\t    FunctionBody,       // Indentation: function/method body\\n   404\\t    FunctionSignature,  // Delimiter: ( to ) on the function definition\\n   405\\t    Braces,             // Delimiter: { to }\\n   406\\t    Brackets,           // Delimiter: [ to ]\\n   407\\t    Parens,             // Delimiter: ( to )\\n   408\\t}\\n   409\\t\\n   410\\t/// Result of scope detection.\\n   411\\tstruct ScopeResult {\\n   412\\t    /// First line of scope content (0-indexed into file lines).\\n   413\\t    start_line: usize,\\n   414\\t    /// Last line of scope content (0-indexed, inclusive).\\n   415\\t    end_line: usize,\\n   416\\t    /// Line number of closing delimiter, if applicable.\\n   417\\t    closing_line: Option<usize>,\\n   418\\t    /// Whether the scope is empty (no content between boundaries).\\n   419\\t    is_empty: bool,\\n   420\\t}\\n   421\\t\\n   422\\t// ── Position types ─────────────────────────────────────────────\\n   423\\t\\n   424\\t/// Where within the scope to insert rendered content.\\n   425\\tenum Position {\\n   426\\t    Before,           // Before first line of scope\\n   427\\t    After,            // After last line of scope (default)\\n   428\\t    BeforeClose,      // Before closing delimiter or dedent\\n   429\\t    AfterLastField,   // After last assignment/annotation line\\n   430\\t    AfterLastMethod,  // After last method definition's complete
