(ARCHITECTURE D-4) |\\n| Render before execute | Template rendering happens before operation execution | Errors caught before any file touched; rendered content always available for error messages (ARCHITECTURE D-1, I-4, I-10) |\\n| Recipe-relative template resolution | Template paths relative to recipe file, not cwd | Keeps recipes self-contained and portable (ARCHITECTURE D-2, I-7). --base-dir only affects output paths. |\\n| No global state | Every function takes inputs as arguments | Trivial testing, guaranteed determinism (ARCHITECTURE D-5, I-1) |\\n| thiserror for error types | Use thiserror derive macros for JigError | Idiomatic Rust error handling with minimal boilerplate, good Display/Error trait impls |\\n| IndexMap for variables | Preserve declaration order in variable maps | Deterministic iteration order for vars output and error messages (I-1) |\\n| Inline unit tests + fixture integration tests | #[cfg(test)] modules in each source file; tests/fixtures/ for end-to-end | Unit tests co-located with code for fast iteration; fixture tests for pipeline validation without code changes |\\n| pluralize/singularize approach | Use the `pluralizer` crate | Full English pluralization rules without hand-rolling. Acceptable dependency for correctness. |\\n| \\\"did you mean?\\\" for undefined vars | `strsim` crate (Levenshtein distance) against declared variable names | minijinja doesn't provide hints. Hand-rolling edit distance serves I-10 (graceful degradation) — the error tells the caller exactly what to fix. |\\n| Filter ownership | Register all 13 filters ourselves, even for ones minijinja provides (replace, indent, join) | We control exact behavior. No surprises from upstream minijinja changes. Determinism (I-1) over convenience. |\\n| Render all templates upfront | Render ALL templates before ANY file write | Fail-fast, no partial writes. Templates reference variables not created files. (D-1) |\\n| Extra variables pass through | Don't error or warn on undeclared variables in input | LLMs construct variable objects with extra fields. Strictness here breaks real usage. |\\n| Fail-fast on operation errors | Stop on first operation failure | Later operations may depend on earlier ones (e.g., inject targets a prior create). (I-9, AC-N6.2) |\\n\\n## Risks / Open Questions\\n\\nAll resolved — see Resolved Questions below.\\n\\n## Resolved Questions\\n\\n- **pluralize/singularize**: Using `pluralizer` crate. Full English rules, no hand-rolling.\\n- **\\\"did you mean?\\\"**: Using `strsim` crate for Levenshtein distance against declared var names. minijinja doesn't provide this. Serves I-10.\\n- **minijinja filter compatibility**: Register all 13 filters ourselves. We own the behavior, upstream changes don't affect us. Serves I-1.\\n- **TTY detection**: `std::io::IsTerminal` (Rust 1.70+) works correctly. No external crate needed.\\n- **stdin variable reading**: Documented as single-purpose per invocation. Non-interactive design (I-3) makes this a non-issue.\\n- **Large file handling for inject**: Reads entire file into memory. Acceptable for source files. Documented as known limitation.\\n- **pluralizer irregular plurals**: Works for common irregulars (person→people, child→children). Acceptable coverage.\\n- **minijinja filter override**: Overriding built-in filters works. All 13 registered as custom filters.\\n\\n## Completion Summary\\n\\n- **Completed**: 2026-04-04\\n- **Tests**: 191 total (177 unit + 2 CLI integration + 12 fixture integration)\\n- **Review**: Dual-agent review cycle (Claude + Codex) came back clean — 0 Critical, 0 Major findings\\n- **All 5 phases delivered**: validate, vars, render, run (create + inject), full integration test suite\\n- **CLI commands working**: `jig validate`, `jig vars`, `jig render`, `jig run`\\n\\n## Execution Order\\n\\n```\\nPhase 1 ──► Phase 2 ──► Phase 3 ──► Phase 4 ──► Phase 5\\nskeleton     vars+render  create+output inject     integration\\n                                                    tests\\n```\\n\\nEach phase produces a working, testable artifact. No phase depends on \\\"wiring up later.\\\" The CLI grows incrementally:\\n- After Phase 1: `jig validate`, `jig vars`\\n- After Phase 2: + `jig render`\\n- After Phase 3: + `jig run` (create only)\\n- After Phase 4: + `jig run` (create + inject) — **v0.1 complete**\\n- After Phase 5: Full test coverage validates the release\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:58:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_29\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"sed -n '1,320p' CLAUDE.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"# CLAUDE.md\\n\\nThis file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.\\n\\n## Project Status\\n\\n**v0.3 workflows complete.** All four file operations (create, inject, replace, patch) plus multi-recipe workflows with conditional steps, variable mapping, and error handling. 343 tests passing. Next up: libraries (v0.4).\\n\\n## What jig Is\\n\\njig is a template rendering CLI purpose-built for LLM code generation workflows. It takes a recipe (YAML) + variables (JSON) and produces deterministic file operations (create, inject, patch, replace). It is designed to be called by LLMs, not humans at a terminal.\\n\\nThe full specification lives in `jig.md`. Read it before making architectural decisions.\\n\\n## Language and Build\\n\\nRust.\\n\\n```bash\\ncargo build              # build\\ncargo test               # run all tests\\ncargo test <test_name>   # run a single test\\ncargo run -- <args>      # run the CLI\\ncargo clippy             # lint\\n```\\n\\n### Planned Dependencies\\n\\n| Crate | Purpose |\\n|-------|---------|\\n| `minijinja` | Jinja2 template rendering |\\n| `serde` + `serde_yaml` + `serde_json` | Recipe and variable parsing |\\n| `regex` | Injection/replace pattern matching |\\n| `clap` | CLI argument parsing |\\n| `heck` | Case conversion filters (snake_case, camelCase, etc.) |\\n| `owo-colors` | Terminal coloring |\\n| `insta` | Snapshot testing |\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\nPlanned source layout:\\n\\n```\\nsrc/\\n  main.rs              # CLI entry point (clap)\\n  recipe.rs            # Recipe YAML parsing and validation\\n  variables.rs         # Variable declaration, type checking, JSON merging\\n  renderer.rs          # Jinja2 rendering via minijinja\\n  operations/\\n    mod.rs             # Operation trait and dispatch\\n    create.rs          # Create new files\\n    inject.rs          # Inject into existing files (line-level)\\n    patch.rs           # Patch existing files (scope-aware anchoring)\\n    replace.rs         # Replace regions in existing files\\n  scope/\\n    mod.rs             # Scope detection dispatch\\n    indent.rs          # Indentation-based (Python, YAML)\\n    delimiter.rs       # Delimiter-based (braces, brackets, parens)\\n    position.rs        # Semantic positions (after_last_field, etc.)\\n  workflow.rs          # Multi-recipe orchestration, conditional steps\\n  library/\\n    mod.rs             # Library manifest parsing\\n    install.rs         # Add/remove/update libraries\\n    discover.rs        # Recipe and workflow discovery\\n    conventions.rs     # Convention mapping and overrides\\n  filters.rs           # Custom Jinja2 filters (snakecase, pluralize, etc.)\\n  output.rs            # Human-readable and JSON output formatting\\n  error.rs             # Structured error types with exit codes\\n```\\n\\n## Core Concepts\\n\\n- **Recipe**: YAML file declaring variables and file operations. Lives alongside its templates.\\n- **Operations**: `create` (new file), `inject` (insert at anchor in existing file), `patch` (scope-aware insert using anchor/scope/position), `replace` (swap region between markers).\\n- **Anchor system**: Pattern match + scope (class_body, function_body, braces, etc.) + position (after_last_field, before_close, etc.) for structural code insertion without a full parser.\\n- **Scope detection**: Indentation-based for Python/YAML, delimiter-based for C-family. Lightweight heuristics, not AST.\\n- **Workflows**: Multi-recipe chains with conditional steps (`when`), variable mapping, and error handling modes.\\n- **Libraries**: Versioned recipe collections for a framework (e.g., jig-django). Installed globally or per-project, with convention overrides.\\n\\n## CLI Commands\\n\\n```\\njig run <recipe> --vars '<json>'    # Execute a recipe\\njig render <template> --vars '...'  # Render a single template\\njig validate <recipe>               # Check recipe is well-formed\\njig vars <recipe>                   # Show expected variables (JSON)\\njig workflow <name> --vars '...'    # Run a multi-recipe workflow\\njig scan <recipe> <path>            # Reverse-extract variables from existing code\\njig check <recipe> <path>           # Conformance verification\\njig library add|remove|update|list  # Manage recipe libraries\\n```\\n\\n## Exit Codes\\n\\n| Code | Meaning |\\n|------|---------|\\n| 0 | Success |\\n| 1 | Recipe validation error |\\n| 2 | Template rendering error |\\n| 3 | File operation error |\\n| 4 | Variable validation error |\\n\\n## Design Principles\\n\\n1. JSON in, files out. Non-interactive by default.\\n2. Deterministic: same recipe + same variables + same files = same output.\\n3. Idempotent: every operation safe to re-run (`skip_if`, `skip_if_exists`).\\n4. Transparent failures: errors include what/where/why + rendered content so the LLM can fall back to manual editing.\\n5. JSON to stdout, human-readable to stderr. Auto-detect TTY vs piped.\\n6. Templates live with the consumer, not in a central directory.\\n\\n## Testing Strategy\\n\\n- **Unit tests**: Recipe parsing, variable validation, template rendering, each operation type.\\n- **Integration tests**: Fixture directories with `recipe.yaml`, `vars.json`, `templates/`, `existing/`, and `expected/`. Test runner copies existing/ to temp dir, runs jig, diffs against expected/.\\n- **Snapshot tests**: `insta` crate for template rendering output.\\n- **Agent evals** (`eval/`): End-to-end tests where real LLM agents invoke jig against fixture codebases, scored on assertion pass rate, jig usage, and efficiency vs. baseline.\\n\\n## Slash Commands\\n\\n- `/review` - Adversarial code review (channels Linus, Hickey, Cantrill, Katz, Klabnik)\\n- `/plan-review <path>` - Adversarial review of planning/design docs for contradictions and gaps\\n- `/spacex [path]` - Apply The Algorithm to simplify code (Question, Delete, Simplify, Accelerate, Automate)\\n- `/clean-copy` - Rewrite branch with clean, narrative commit history\\n\\n## Roadmap Reference\\n\\nThe spec defines a phased roadmap (v0.1 through v1.0+). When implementing, follow the milestone order in `jig.md` — MVP core engine first, then patches, workflows, libraries, distribution, and advanced features (scan, infer, check).\\n\",\"exit_code\":0,\"status\":\"completed\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:59:{\"type\":\"item.started\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_30\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"rg -n \\\\\\\"jigrc|libraries|library\\\\\\\" src tests docs | sed -n '1,240p'\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"\",\"exit_code\":null,\"status\":\"in_progress\"}}\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:60:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_30\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\"rg -n \\\\\\\"jigrc|libraries|library\\\\\\\" src tests docs | sed -n '1,240p'\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"docs/workstreams/workflows/PLAN.md:9:Add multi-recipe orchestration to jig (v0.3). A workflow chains multiple recipes into a single invocation with conditional steps (`when`), variable mapping between steps (`vars_map`, `vars`), and configurable error handling (`on_error: stop|continue|report`). This is the prerequisite for libraries (v0.4), which organize workflows over library-namespaced recipes.\\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/PLAN.md:114:- **Blocks:** libraries (v0.4) — libraries define workflows over library-namespaced recipes and need the workflow execution engine.\\ntests/integration.rs:627:        // On macOS, only system libraries should appear.\\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/reviews/code-review-claude-20260404-200636.md:1:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"result\\\",\\\"subtype\\\":\\\"success\\\",\\\"is_error\\\":false,\\\"duration_ms\\\":613439,\\\"duration_api_ms\\\":682224,\\\"num_turns\\\":29,\\\"result\\\":\\\"Good — `Replace` does NOT have a `skip_if` field, so `run_recipe` correctly only handles skip_if for Inject and Patch.\\\\n\\\\nI now have everything I need. Here's the review:\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Verdict: Request Changes**\\\\n\\\\n## Critical (must fix before merge)\\\\n\\\\n**C1: `extract_rendered_from_error` returns the error description, not the rendered template content**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:621-628`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn extract_rendered_from_error(err: &JigError) -> Option<String> {\\\\n    match err {\\\\n        JigError::TemplateRendering(se) | JigError::FileOperation(se) => {\\\\n            Some(se.what.clone())  // ← BUG: \\\\\\\"what\\\\\\\" is \\\\\\\"anchor pattern not found\\\\\\\", not the rendered content\\\\n        }\\\\n        _ => None,\\\\n    }\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis is called at line 519 to populate `StepResult::Error::rendered_content`, which propagates to the JSON output's `rendered_content` field. The spec says (AC-8.4, AC-N3.2): rendered_content should contain the actual rendered template so an LLM caller can fall back to manual editing (I-4, I-10). Instead, it contains the error description string.\\\\n\\\\nThe fix: extract from the last `OpResult::Error` in `partial_results` instead:\\\\n```rust\\\\nErr((e, partial_results)) => {\\\\n    let rendered = partial_results.last().and_then(|r| match r {\\\\n        OpResult::Error { rendered_content, .. } if !rendered_content.is_empty() => {\\\\n            Some(rendered_content.clone())\\\\n        }\\\\n        _ => None,\\\\n    });\\\\n    // ...\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nFor non-operation errors (variable validation, when-template failure), `rendered_content: None` is correct — there is no rendered content to extract. The current code incorrectly returns `Some(\\\\\\\"missing required variable 'name'\\\\\\\")` as rendered_content for those cases too.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Major (should fix)\\\\n\\\\n**M1: `format_workflow_json` status logic breaks with per-step `on_error` overrides**\\\\n`src/output.rs:300-306`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet status = if !has_errors || exit_code == 0 {\\\\n    \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\"\\\\n} else if exit_code == 3 && result.on_error == workflow::OnError::Report {\\\\n    \\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\"\\\\n} else {\\\\n    \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"\\\\n};\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis checks `result.on_error` (the **workflow-level** default). When a step uses `on_error: report` as a per-step override but the workflow default is `stop`, `compute_workflow_exit_code` correctly returns 3, but the status incorrectly becomes `\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"` instead of `\\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\"`. The LLM caller sees exit code 3 + status \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\" — a contradiction since \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\" implies the workflow halted but exit 3 means report-mode partial failure.\\\\n\\\\nFix: don't rely on `result.on_error` for the \\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\" check:\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet status = if !has_errors || exit_code == 0 {\\\\n    \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\"\\\\n} else {\\\\n    // Determine if any errored step used report mode\\\\n    let any_report = result.steps.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, StepResult::Error { .. }))\\\\n        && exit_code == 3;\\\\n    // But also check if we stopped early (stop mode with file-op error also gives exit 3)\\\\n    let stopped_early = result.steps.iter().last().map_or(false, |s| s.is_error())\\\\n        && result.steps.len() < total_steps;  // need total from workflow\\\\n    if any_report && !stopped_early { \\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\" } else { \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\" }\\\\n};\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nOr simpler: pass a flag from `compute_workflow_exit_code` indicating whether report-mode errors were present.\\\\n\\\\n**M2: `resolve_step_variables` panics on non-object JSON via `.unwrap()`**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:369` and `src/workflow.rs:394`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet obj = vars.as_object_mut().unwrap();\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nIf `vars` is ever not a JSON object (e.g., `null`, array, string), this panics with no context. This is a public function. While `variables::collect_vars` should always return an object, defensive code here avoids an opaque panic. Return a `JigError::VariableValidation` instead.\\\\n\\\\n**M3: `cmd_run` and `run_recipe` are divergent copies of the same logic**\\\\n`src/main.rs:335-427` vs `src/workflow.rs:551-618`\\\\n\\\\nThe rendering pipeline (create env → render templates → render paths → render skip_if → execute ops) is duplicated. They're already subtly different:\\\\n- `cmd_run` returns early via `handle_early_error` for template failures (no partial results)\\\\n- `run_recipe` collects into a vec and returns `Err((error, vec![]))` for template failures\\\\n\\\\nIf a new operation type or rendering phase is added (e.g., for libraries v0.4), the change must be made in both places. Refactor `cmd_run` to call `run_recipe` and handle early errors at the CLI layer.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Minor (nice to fix)\\\\n\\\\n**m1: Determinism unit test is trivially weak**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:1215-1233`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nresults.push(format!(\\\\\\\"{:?}\\\\\\\", result.steps.len()));\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis only compares the Debug representation of a `usize` (e.g., `\\\\\\\"1\\\\\\\"` == `\\\\\\\"1\\\\\\\"`). It doesn't compare actual output content, file content, or operation results. A real determinism test should compare the `StepResult` variants and their contents across runs (or use the integration-level determinism test which is much stronger).\\\\n\\\\n**m2: `load_workflow` collects all validation errors but only reports the first**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:303-306`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet (_, _, err) = validation_errors.into_iter().next().unwrap();\\\\nreturn Err(JigError::RecipeValidation(err));\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nIf steps 2 and 5 both reference invalid recipes, the user only sees the error for step 2. They fix it, re-run, and then see step 5. Reporting all errors at once would be more LLM-friendly. The errors are already collected — just use `JigError::VariableValidation(errors)` style or a new multi-error variant.\\\\n\\\\n**m3: No integration test for AC-7.8 (recipe passed to `jig workflow`)**\\\\nThe spec requires (AC-7.8): passing a recipe file to `jig workflow` should exit 1 with a message pointing to `jig run`. The code handles this correctly (`main.rs:510-519`), but there's no test fixture for it. Add an `error-workflow-is-recipe` fixture with a recipe.yaml and expected_exit_code=1.\\\\n\\\\n**m4: Early error handler creates misleading JSON**\\\\n`src/main.rs:473-507`\\\\n\\\\nPre-execution errors produce JSON with `\\\\\\\"steps\\\\\\\": [{\\\\\\\"recipe\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"}]` — an empty recipe string and a fake step entry. An LLM caller might try to interpret this. For pre-execution errors (file not found, bad YAML, variable validation), consider `\\\\\\\"steps\\\\\\\": []` instead, with the error at the top level.\\\\n\\\\n**m5: `validate_workflow` sets `valid: true` for all steps unconditionally**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:342-344`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nvalid: true, // already validated in load_workflow\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis is correct (load_workflow would have returned an error if any step was invalid), but it means `jig validate` never produces a per-step error report — if any recipe is invalid, the whole command fails before reaching the validation output. AC-7.5/AC-7.6 only require per-step output when validation succeeds, so this is spec-compliant, but a validation command that shows \\\\\\\"steps 1, 3 valid / step 2 invalid\\\\\\\" would be more useful.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Strengths\\\\n\\\\n- **Clean architecture**: Parsing → variable resolution → condition evaluation → execution is well-separated. Each concern is testable in isolation.\\\\n- **Simultaneous `vars_map` renaming**: The snapshot-then-remove-then-insert approach at lines 372-389 correctly implements non-chaining rename semantics. The test at line 887 nails the tricky case.\\\\n- **ExecutionContext sharing across steps**: Single context for virtual_files, base_dir, and force flag is the right design. Dry-run chaining works correctly (tested at line 1140).\\\\n- **File type auto-detection**: The `detect_file_type` function cleanly handles all four cases (workflow, recipe, both, neither) and integrates smoothly into `jig validate` and `jig vars`.\\\\n- **Error handling structure**: Every error path through the step execution loop respects the effective `on_error` mode (step override or workflow default). The pattern at lines 440-453 is repeated consistently for all five failure points.\\\\n- **35 unit tests + 25 integration fixtures**: Good coverage of core logic. The fixture auto-discovery pattern is elegant — no test registration needed.\\\\n- **Spec traceability**: The test names and comments reference acceptance criteria consistently.\\\",\\\"stop_reason\\\":\\\"end_turn\\\",\\\"session_id\\\":\\\"8f910b25-063b-4054-a75d-988d5e1469d4\\\",\\\"total_cost_usd\\\":2.344686099999999,\\\"usage\\\":{\\\"input_tokens\\\":20,\\\"cache_creation_input_tokens\\\":111076,\\\"cache_read_input_tokens\\\":1279858,\\\"output_tokens\\\":24985,\\\"server_tool_use\\\":{\\\"web_search_requests\\\":0,\\\"web_fetch_requests\\\":0},\\\"service_tier\\\":\\\"standard\\\",\\\"cache_creation\\\":{\\\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\\\":111076,\\\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\\\":0},\\\"inference_geo\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"iterations\\\":[],\\\"speed\\\":\\\"standard\\\"},\\\"modelUsage\\\":{\\\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":209,\\\"outputTokens\\\":16306,\\\"cacheReadInputTokens\\\":1611206,\\\"cacheCreationInputTokens\\\":114358,\\\"webSearchRequests\\\":0,\\\"costUSD\\\":0.38580709999999996,\\\"contextWindow\\\":200000,\\\"maxOutputTokens\\\":32000},\\\"claude-opus-4-6[1m]\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":20,\\\"outputTokens\\\":24985,\\\"cacheReadInputTokens\\\":1279858,\\\"cacheCreationInputTokens\\\":111076,\\\"webSearchRequests\\\":0,\\\"costUSD\\\":1.958879,\\\"contextWindow\\\":1000000,\\\"maxOutputTokens\\\":64000}},\\\"permission_denials\\\":[{\\\"tool_name\\\":\\\"Bash\\\",\\\"tool_use_id\\\":\\\"toolu_01Buj5C7L5RAzHW4Ejikn4va\\\",\\\"tool_input\\\":{\\\"command\\\":\\\"for dir in /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/error-workflow-* /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/workflow-*; do echo \\\\\\\"=== $(basename $dir) ===\\\\\\\"; ls -la \\\\\\\"$dir\\\\\\\" | grep -E \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\.yaml|\\\\\\\\.yml|recipe\\\\\\\"; done\\\"}},{\\\"tool_name\\\":\\\"Bash\\\",\\\"tool_use_id\\\":\\\"toolu_013zGsGm3sSwYKf76P1oVF11\\\",\\\"tool_input\\\":{\\\"command\\\":\\\"cd /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures && for dir in error-workflow-* workflow-*; do if [ -d \\\\\\\"$dir\\\\\\\" ]; then echo \\\\\\\"=== $dir ===\\\\\\\"; ls -1 \\\\\\\"$dir\\\\\\\"; echo \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"; fi; done\\\"}},{\\\"tool_name\\\":\\\"Bash\\\",\\\"tool_use_id\\\":\\\"toolu_018dEk75WpEnBud2PEaz7nc8\\\",\\\"tool_input\\\":{\\\"command\\\":\\\"find /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures -type d \\\\\\\\( -name \\\\\\\"error-workflow*\\\\\\\" -o -name \\\\\\\"workflow-*\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\\) -exec sh -c 'echo \\\\\\\"=== $(basename \\\\\\\"$1\\\\\\\") ===\\\\\\\"; ls -1 \\\\\\\"$1\\\\\\\" 2>/dev/null' _ {} \\\\\\\\;\\\"}},{\\\"tool_name\\\":\\\"Bash\\\",\\\"tool_use_id\\\":\\\"toolu_01DT748GfevoWkkcFPQ1DT2i\\\",\\\"tool_input\\\":{\\\"command\\\":\\\"ls -1d /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/workflow-* /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/error-workflow-* | wc -l\\\"}}],\\\"terminal_reason\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"fast_mode_state\\\":\\\"off\\\",\\\"uuid\\\":\\\"d44e2820-dc14-4775-8c5d-dd6f6cf3ac39\\\"}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:5:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_0\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"I’ll produce a phase-by-phase implementation plan for workstream `libraries` aligned to your invariants and architecture. First I’m going to scan the repo for existing workstream/planning docs and any current library-related code so the plan maps to actual files and avoids duplicating prior decisions.\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:8:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_2\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"I found the project root and confirmed this branch already contains workflow-related work through Phase H. Next I’ll inspect `docs/` and current `src/library` state to anchor the `libraries` plan to existing structure and naming.\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:15:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_6\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"I located existing workstream plans/specs (`core-engine`, `replace-patch`, `workflows`) and there isn’t a `libraries` directory yet. I’m now reading prior `PLAN.md` style so this new execution plan matches established structure and depth.\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:17:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_7\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/workflows/PLAN.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# PLAN.md\\\\n\\\\n> Workstream: workflows\\\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\\\n> Status: Complete\\\\n\\\\n## Objective\\\\n\\\\nAdd multi-recipe orchestration to jig (v0.3). A workflow chains multiple recipes into a single invocation with conditional steps (`when`), variable mapping between steps (`vars_map`, `vars`), and configurable error handling (`on_error: stop|continue|report`). This is the prerequisite for libraries (v0.4), which organize workflows over library-namespaced recipes.\\\\n\\\\nThe deliverable is: `jig workflow <path> --vars '{...}'` executes an ordered sequence of recipe invocations, with per-step JSON results, shared filesystem state, and predictable error behavior.\\\\n\\\\n## Phases\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 1: Workflow Parsing & CLI Detection\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\n\\\\nBuild the workflow YAML parser and extend `jig validate` / `jig vars` to auto-detect workflow files.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 1.1: Create `src/workflow.rs` with `Workflow`, `WorkflowStep`, `OnError` structs and serde deserialization from YAML\\\\n- [x] 1.2: Implement `load_workflow()` — parse YAML, resolve recipe paths relative to workflow dir, validate recipe files exist and are structurally valid\\\\n- [x] 1.3: Implement auto-detection in `jig validate`: read YAML, check for `steps` vs `files` key, dispatch to workflow or recipe validation\\\\n- [x] 1.4: Implement auto-detection in `jig vars`: same detection logic, output workflow variable declarations\\\\n- [x] 1.5: Add workflow validation JSON and human output formats (type, name, variables, steps with recipe/valid/conditional)\\\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for parsing: valid workflow, empty steps, missing steps key, both steps+files, bad on_error, missing recipe file, invalid recipe, no variables, optional metadata\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- `jig validate workflow.yaml` auto-detects and validates a workflow with 3+ steps, reporting each recipe's validity\\\\n- `jig vars workflow.yaml` outputs the workflow's variable declarations as JSON\\\\n- All parse-time error cases produce exit code 1 with structured error messages\\\\n- 20+ unit tests covering AC-1.1 through AC-1.20\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 2: Variable Resolution & Conditional Steps\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\n\\\\nImplement step variable resolution (shared vars → vars_map → vars overrides) and `when` condition evaluation.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 2.1: Implement `resolve_step_variables()` — start with workflow vars, apply vars_map renaming (simultaneous, not chained), apply vars overrides\\\\n- [x] 2.2: Implement `evaluate_when()` — render Jinja2 template with workflow vars, apply falsy rules (empty, \\\\\\\"false\\\\\\\" case-insensitive, \\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\")\\\\n- [x] 2.3: Implement workflow-level variable validation — reuse `variables::validate_variables()` with workflow's `VariableDecl` map\\\\n- [x] 2.4: Implement step-level variable validation — validate step's resolved vars against the step recipe's `VariableDecl` map\\\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for: vars_map rename semantics, vars_map with nonexistent source, vars_map target collision, vars override, combined vars_map+vars, when truthy/falsy cases, when with Jinja2 control flow, when with undefined variable, simultaneous vars_map\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- `resolve_step_variables()` produces correct output for all documented edge cases (AC-4.1 through AC-4.10)\\\\n- `evaluate_when()` correctly classifies: empty → false, \\\\\\\"false\\\\\\\"/\\\\\\\"False\\\\\\\"/\\\\\\\"FALSE\\\\\\\" → false, \\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\" → false, \\\\\\\"true\\\\\\\" → true, \\\\\\\"yes\\\\\\\" → true, non-empty string → true\\\\n- Variable validation errors at workflow level halt before any execution (exit 4)\\\\n- 15+ unit tests covering FR-3 and FR-4\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 3: Execution Engine\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\n\\\\nBuild the workflow execution loop that iterates steps, evaluates conditions, resolves variables, and runs recipes — sharing filesystem state (and virtual_files in dry-run) across steps.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 3.1: Implement `execute_workflow()` — sequential step loop with condition evaluation, variable resolution, and recipe execution\\\\n- [x] 3.2: Extract `run_recipe()` in `workflow.rs` — reusable recipe execution without CLI concerns (rendering, operation execution, error collection)\\\\n- [x] 3.3: Implement cross-step `virtual_files` carryover for `--dry-run` mode — a single `ExecutionContext` spans all steps\\\\n- [x] 3.4: Implement `on_error` handling: stop (halt on failure), continue (record and proceed, exit 0), report (record and proceed, exit 3)\\\\n- [x] 3.5: Implement per-step `on_error` overrides — step-level value takes precedence over workflow-level default\\\\n- [x] 3.6: Implement exit code selection via `compute_workflow_exit_code()` in `main.rs`\\\\n- [x] 3.7: Unit tests for execution: basic two-step, conditional skip, chain create→inject, chain create→patch, error stop, error continue, error report, step on_error override, dry-run chaining\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- A two-step workflow where step 1 creates a file and step 2 injects into it succeeds in both normal and dry-run mode\\\\n- A workflow with `when: \\\\\\\"{{ false }}\\\\\\\"` skips the step and reports status \\\\\\\"skipped\\\\\\\"\\\\n- `on_error: stop` halts after first failure; `continue` runs all steps; `report` runs all steps but exits 3\\\\n- Per-step on_error correctly overrides workflow default\\\\n- 20+ unit tests covering FR-5 and FR-6\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 4: CLI Command & Output Formatting\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\n\\\\nAdd the `jig workflow` subcommand and implement workflow-specific JSON and human output.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 4.1: Add `Workflow` variant to `Commands` enum in `main.rs` — accepts path + same global options as `Run`\\\\n- [x] 4.2: Implement `cmd_workflow()` in `main.rs` — parse workflow, validate vars, execute, format output, return exit code\\\\n- [x] 4.3: Implement `format_workflow_json()` in `output.rs` — per-step results, aggregate files_written/files_skipped, top-level workflow/on_error/status/dry_run fields\\\\n- [x] 4.4: Implement `format_workflow_human()` in `output.rs` — step headers (\\\\\\\"Step 1/3: ...\\\\\\\"), nested operation lines, summary line\\\\n- [x] 4.5: Handle edge case: `jig workflow` passed a recipe file → exit 1 with helpful message pointing to `jig run`\\\\n- [x] 4.6: Wire `--verbose` to include rendered content across all steps in both JSON and human output\\\\n- [x] 4.7: CLI integration tests: basic workflow run, JSON output format, human output format, --dry-run, --quiet\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- `jig workflow workflow.yaml --vars '...' --json` produces correct per-step JSON output matching the schema in SPEC.md\\\\n- Human output shows step progress headers, nested operation results, and summary line\\\\n- `jig workflow recipe.yaml` produces a clear error directing to `jig run`\\\\n- 10+ tests covering FR-7 and FR-8\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 5: Integration Testing\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\n\\\\nBuild fixture-based integration tests covering the full workflow feature set, including error cases and cross-step chaining.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 5.1: Create fixture infrastructure — workflow fixtures follow the same pattern as recipe fixtures, with a `workflow.yaml` at the root and step recipe subdirectories\\\\n- [x] 5.2: Implement 14 success-path fixtures: basic, three-steps, when-true, when-false, when-complex, vars-map, vars-override, vars-map-and-override, chain-create-inject, chain-create-patch, empty-steps, no-variables, idempotent, dry-run-chain\\\\n- [x] 5.3: Implement 4 error-handling fixtures: on-error-stop, on-error-continue, on-error-report, step-on-error\\\\n- [x] 5.4: Implement 7 error-case fixtures: missing-recipe, bad-yaml, missing-steps, ambiguous, bad-vars, when-undef, step-missing-var\\\\n- [x] 5.5: Run full test suite (`cargo test`), verify all pass, update test count in CLAUDE.md — 343 tests passing\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- All 25 spec-required fixtures exist and pass\\\\n- `cargo test` passes with 0 failures\\\\n- `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- Test count updated in CLAUDE.md project status\\\\n\\\\n## Dependencies\\\\n\\\\n- **Depends on:** core-engine (v0.1, complete), replace-patch (v0.2, complete). Workflows orchestrate all four operation types — they must all work.\\\\n- **Blocks:** libraries (v0.4) — libraries define workflows over library-namespaced recipes and need the workflow execution engine.\\\\n\\\\n## Key Decisions\\\\n\\\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\\\n| Workflow file format | Standalone YAML with `steps` key | Distinguishable from recipes (which have `files`). Clean auto-detection. Libraries (v0.4) will reference these. |\\\\n| Recipe path resolution | Relative to workflow file directory | Consistent with I-7 (templates live with the consumer). Keeps workflow + recipe bundles self-contained. |\\\\n| `when` evaluation | Render Jinja2, check string truthiness | Simple, predictable. Reuses existing renderer. No new expression language. |\\\\n| Falsy values | Empty, \\\\\\\"false\\\\\\\" (case-insensitive), \\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\" | Covers minijinja boolean rendering (\\\\\\\"false\\\\\\\"), zero, and empty. Minimal set — easy to document and remember. |\\\\n| `when` variable scope | Workflow-level variables (before vars_map/vars) | Prevents circular dependency where step vars affect step execution. Condition is about \\\\\\\"should this step run at all\\\\\\\" — a workflow-level concern. |\\\\n| vars_map semantics | Rename (not copy) | Spec says \\\\\\\"rename.\\\\\\\" Avoids ambiguity when recipe has different meaning for original name. Copy achievable via `vars` override. |\\\\n| vars_map application | Simultaneous (not chained) | `{a: b, b: c}` means original `a` → `b` and original `b` → `c`, not `a` → `b` → `c`. Prevents order-dependent surprises. |\\\\n| No new exit codes | Reuse 0-4 | Workflow errors map to existing categories (validation, rendering, file ops, variables). Adding exit code 5+ would break I-5 stability guarantee without clear benefit. |\\\\n| No nested workflows | Steps reference recipes only | Keeps v0.3 scope manageable. Nested workflows can be added later if needed. Flat is better than nested. |\\\\n| No inter-step variable passing | Steps share filesystem, not variables | Variables flow from workflow to steps. Steps communicate through the filesystem (one creates a file, the next reads/patches it). Keeps the model simple. |\\\\n| Execution context sharing | Single ExecutionContext across steps | Enables dry-run virtual_files carryover. One base_dir, one force flag, one set of virtual files. |\\\\n| Step variable validation timing | At execution time (not upfront) | Conditional steps might have variables that only make sense when the step runs. Validating skipped steps would produce false errors. |\\\\n\\\\n## Risks / Open Questions (Resolved)\\\\n\\\\n- **~~Risk: Refactoring `cmd_run` for reuse.~~** Resolved. Created `run_recipe()` in `workflow.rs` (lines 551-619) that handles recipe loading, rendering, and execution without CLI concerns. `cmd_run` in `main.rs` was NOT refactored — both contain similar rendering pipelines. See review finding M4 in Known Issues.\\\\n- **~~Risk: `virtual_files` carryover complexity.~~** Resolved. Single `ExecutionContext` spans all steps. Tested via `workflow-dry-run-chain` fixture (step 1 creates, step 2 injects into virtual file).\\\\n- **~~Open question: `vars` values support Jinja2?~~** Decision: no for v0.3. Literal JSON values only.\\\\n- **~~Open question: vars_map conflict detection?~~** Decision: `load_workflow()` now validates for duplicate targets within a single step's vars_map and rejects them at parse time. Cross-step conflicts are not checked (different steps are independent).\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:20:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_9\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/replace-patch/PLAN.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# PLAN.md\\\\n\\\\n> Workstream: replace-patch\\\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\\\n> Status: Implementation Complete — Review Findings Pending\\\\n\\\\n## Objective\\\\n\\\\nExtend jig from greenfield-only (create + inject) to brownfield operations — replacing regions in existing files and inserting content at structurally-determined locations. This is the v0.2 feature set that makes jig useful for the most common real-world task: extending existing code (adding fields, methods, endpoints) across multiple files in a single recipe.\\\\n\\\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases F (Replace Operation) and G (Patch Operation + Scope Detection).\\\\n\\\\n## Phases\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 1: Recipe Parsing Extensions\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\nTraces to: FR-1\\\\n\\\\nExtend `recipe.rs` to parse `replace` and `patch` file operations into fully typed `FileOp` variants. Currently these are accepted by `RawFileOp` but rejected at conversion time (lines 276-291). This phase removes that rejection, adds the new types to `FileOp`, and validates all fields at parse time.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 1.1: Add new types to `src/recipe.rs` — `ReplaceSpec` (Between, Pattern), `Fallback` (Append, Prepend, Skip, Error), `Anchor` struct (pattern, scope, find, position), `ScopeType` enum (8 variants), `Position` enum (7 variants). Add `FileOp::Replace` and `FileOp::Patch` variants.\\\\n- [x] 1.2: Extend `RawFileOp` deserialization to capture replace-specific fields (`between`, `pattern`, `fallback`) and patch-specific fields (`anchor` with `pattern`, `scope`, `find`, `position`). Add `RawAnchor` intermediate struct for serde.\\\\n- [x] 1.3: Implement `convert_file_op` logic for replace: validate between vs pattern (exactly one), validate fallback value, compile-check regex patterns at parse time. For patch: validate anchor.pattern required, compile-check anchor pattern, validate scope type and position type against known variants, default scope to `line` and position to `after`.\\\\n- [x] 1.4: Extend `FileOp::template()` to cover Replace and Patch variants.\\\\n- [x] 1.5: Update `validate_templates()` to check template existence for replace and patch operations.\\\\n- [x] 1.6: Extend `jig validate` and `jig vars` output to include replace/patch operations in their summaries.\\\\n- [x] 1.7: Unit tests for all parsing paths — valid replace (between, pattern, fallback variants), valid patch (all scope/position combinations, with/without find), and error cases (conflicting fields, missing fields, invalid regex, invalid scope/position/fallback strings).\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- Recipes with replace operations parse without error\\\\n- Recipes with patch operations parse without error\\\\n- All error cases from AC-1.5 through AC-1.16 produce exit code 1 with clear messages\\\\n- Regex patterns in between.start, between.end, pattern, and anchor.pattern are compile-checked at parse time\\\\n- `jig validate` reports replace/patch operation counts alongside create/inject\\\\n- Existing create/inject recipes still parse identically (no regressions)\\\\n\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\n- `src/recipe.rs` (major changes — new types, new conversion logic)\\\\n\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\n- None (builds on existing recipe.rs structure)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 2: Replace Operation\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-8 (partial)\\\\n\\\\nImplement the replace operation: match a region in an existing file and swap it with rendered content. Two modes: `between` (marker-delimited region) and `pattern` (regex-matched lines). Four fallback strategies when the match fails.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 2.1: Create `src/operations/replace.rs` — implement `execute()` function following the same signature pattern as create.rs and inject.rs. Core logic: read target file (or virtual_files), apply between or pattern matching, replace the region, write back.\\\\n- [x] 2.2: Implement `between` mode — find start regex match, then find end regex match after start, replace content between them (exclusive of markers). Handle adjacent markers (empty region = insert between).\\\\n- [x] 2.3: Implement `pattern` mode — find all contiguous lines matching regex, replace them with rendered content.\\\\n- [x] 2.4: Implement fallback behavior — when match not found, dispatch to append/prepend/skip/error. Append and prepend write the content; skip returns OpResult::Skip; error returns OpResult::Error with rendered_content.\\\\n- [x] 2.5: Wire replace dispatch into `operations/mod.rs` — add `FileOp::Replace` match arm in `execute_operation`.\\\\n- [x] 2.6: Wire replace into `main.rs` — extend the template preparation loop to handle replace operations (render template content and target path). Add replace to the `PreparedOp` match arms.\\\\n- [x] 2.7: Add verbose output for replace operations — match mode, matched region line range, lines replaced.\\\\n- [x] 2.8: Unit tests — between (normal, empty region, adjacent markers), pattern (single line, multi-line), all four fallbacks, missing target file, end marker not found, dry-run with virtual_files.\\\\n- [x] 2.9: Integration test fixtures — `replace-between`, `replace-between-empty`, `replace-pattern`, `replace-fallback-append`, `replace-fallback-prepend`, `replace-fallback-skip`, `replace-fallback-error`, `error-replace-no-match`, `error-replace-end-missing`.\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- `between` mode preserves marker lines, replaces content between (AC-2.1, AC-2.7)\\\\n- `pattern` mode replaces matched lines entirely (AC-2.2)\\\\n- All four fallbacks work correctly (AC-2.3 through AC-2.6)\\\\n- Missing target file exits 3 with rendered content (AC-2.10)\\\\n- Missing end marker exits 3 with rendered content (AC-2.11)\\\\n- Dry-run mode uses and updates virtual_files (AC-2.13)\\\\n- Replace works in a recipe alongside create and inject operations\\\\n- All integration fixtures pass\\\\n\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\n- `src/operations/replace.rs` (new)\\\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\\\n- `src/main.rs` (preparation update)\\\\n- `src/output.rs` (minor — replace action formatting, verbose diagnostics)\\\\n- `tests/fixtures/replace-*` (new)\\\\n\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\n- Phase 1 (recipe parsing for FileOp::Replace)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 3: Scope Detection\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\nTraces to: FR-3, FR-4, FR-5\\\\n\\\\nImplement the scope detection module — the structural analysis layer that makes patch operations \\\\\\\"smart.\\\\\\\" Two detection strategies (indentation-based, delimiter-based) and seven semantic position heuristics.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 3.1: Create `src/scope/mod.rs` — `ScopeResult` struct, `detect_scope()` dispatch function that routes to indent or delimiter based on `ScopeType`. Export the public API.\\\\n- [x] 3.2: Create `src/scope/indent.rs` — indentation-based scope detection for `Block`, `ClassBody`, `FunctionBody`. Algorithm: find anchor indentation, walk forward collecting deeper-indented lines, stop when indentation returns to anchor level or shallower. Handle blank lines (don't terminate scope), multi-line class declarations (find the colon), empty scopes.\\\\n- [x] 3.3: Create `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — delimiter-based scope detection for `Braces`, `Brackets`, `Parens`, `FunctionSignature`. Algorithm: find opening delimiter on/after anchor, count nesting depth with string literal and comment awareness, close when depth returns to zero. Handle nested delimiters, string escaping, empty scopes.\\\\n- [x] 3.4: Create `src/scope/position.rs` — semantic position resolution within a scope. Implement all 7 positions: `Before`, `After`, `BeforeClose`, `AfterLastField`, `AfterLastMethod`, `AfterLastImport`, `Sorted`. Include fallback logic (field→before_close, method→before_close, import→before). Return `PositionResult` with insertion line, detected indentation, and fallback note.\\\\n- [x] 3.5: Implement `find_within_scope()` — search for a string within scope boundaries, detect if the found line opens a sub-scope (heuristic: line contains `[`, `{`, or `(` as the last non-whitespace character, or assignment with delimiter), return `FindResult` with sub-scope if applicable.\\\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for indentation scopes — Python class body, Python function body, YAML nested block, empty scope, blank lines within scope, nested classes, nested functions, multi-line class declaration, decorator handling.\\\\n- [x] 3.7: Unit tests for delimiter scopes — Rust struct braces, TypeScript interface braces, Go function braces, Python list brackets, function signature parens, nested delimiters, delimiters in strings, delimiters in comments, escaped delimiters, empty scope, missing delimiter errors.\\\\n- [x] 3.8: Unit tests for position resolution — each of the 7 positions tested against realistic code blocks, fallback behavior when pattern not found, sorted insertion order.\\\\n- [x] 3.9: Unit tests for find narrowing — find within class body, find with sub-scope detection (list_display = [...]), find not found error.\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- Python class body correctly identified (AC-3.2): `class Foo:` through dedent\\\\n- Python function body correctly identified (AC-3.3): `def bar():` through dedent\\\\n- Blank lines don't terminate indentation scope (AC-3.4)\\\\n- Nested delimiters tracked correctly (AC-4.5)\\\\n- String literals don't affect delimiter counting (AC-4.6)\\\\n- Comments don't affect delimiter counting (AC-4.7)\\\\n- `after_last_field` finds `^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` pattern (AC-5.4)\\\\n- `after_last_method` finds complete method body end (AC-5.5, AC-5.11)\\\\n- `sorted` inserts in alphabetical order (AC-5.7)\\\\n- Fallbacks work: field→before_close, method→before_close, import→before (AC-5.8-5.10)\\\\n- Find narrows to sub-scope when found line opens one (AC-6.2)\\\\n- All scope types work across languages without configuration (AC-N1.3)\\\\n- Deterministic: same input = same scope boundaries (AC-N4.1)\\\\n\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\n- `src/scope/mod.rs` (new)\\\\n- `src/scope/indent.rs` (new)\\\\n- `src/scope/delimiter.rs` (new)\\\\n- `src/scope/position.rs` (new)\\\\n\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\n- None (scope module is independent — tested in isolation before wiring to patch)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 4: Patch Operation\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\nTraces to: FR-6, FR-7, FR-8 (complete)\\\\n\\\\nImplement the patch operation: anchor-based, scope-aware content insertion. This wires together the scope detection module (Phase 3) with the file operation pipeline, adds indentation matching, and completes verbose diagnostics.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 4.1: Create `src/operations/patch.rs` — implement `execute()` function. Core pipeline: read target file → skip_if check → find anchor via regex → detect scope → apply find narrowing (if any) → resolve position → match indentation → insert rendered content → write back.\\\\n- [x] 4.2: Implement indentation matching — detect the indentation level at the insertion point from surrounding lines, adjust the rendered content's base indentation to match. Handle the case where the rendered template already has its own indentation (preserve relative indentation within the template, adjust only the base level).\\\\n- [x] 4.3: Implement `scope: line` as the degenerate case — just insert after the anchor line, identical to inject's `after` mode. This ensures patch can always fall back to the simplest behavior.\\\\n- [x] 4.4: Wire patch dispatch into `operations/mod.rs` — add `FileOp::Patch` match arm in `execute_operation`.\\\\n- [x] 4.5: Wire patch into `main.rs` — extend the template preparation loop to handle patch operations (render template content, target path, and skip_if). Add patch to the `PreparedOp` match arms.\\\\n- [ ] 4.6: Complete verbose diagnostics for patch and replace — `ScopeDiagnostics` struct in output, displayed in both JSON and human modes. Show anchor line, scope range, insertion point, find match, position fallback.\\\\n- [x] 4.7: Unit tests — full pipeline (anchor→scope→position→insert), skip_if, scope: line, indentation matching, anchor not found, scope detection failure, find narrowing, dry-run with virtual_files.\\\\n- [x] 4.8: Integration test fixtures — `patch-class-body-field`, `patch-braces`, `patch-skip-if`, `patch-scope-line`, `error-patch-no-anchor` implemented. Missing: `patch-function-body`, `patch-brackets`, `patch-function-signature`, `patch-find-narrowing`, `patch-sorted`, `patch-indent-matching`, `patch-nested-delimiters`, `patch-string-delimiters`, `error-patch-unbalanced`, `error-patch-find-missing`.\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- Full patch pipeline works end-to-end: anchor→scope→position→insert (AC-7.1)\\\\n- skip_if prevents duplicate content (AC-7.2, AC-N3.1, AC-N3.3)\\\\n- Indentation matching adjusts inserted content (AC-7.7)\\\\n- scope: line behaves like inject after (AC-7.12)\\\\n- Scope detection errors include rendered content (AC-7.5, AC-N2.1)\\\\n- Find narrowing works with sub-scopes (AC-6.2, AC-6.4)\\\\n- Verbose output shows complete scope diagnostics (AC-8.1, AC-8.2, AC-8.5)\\\\n- All integration fixtures pass\\\\n- Existing create/inject tests still pass (no regressions)\\\\n\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\n- `src/operations/patch.rs` (new)\\\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\\\n- `src/main.rs` (preparation update)\\\\n- `src/output.rs` (verbose scope diagnostics)\\\\n- `tests/fixtures/patch-*` (new)\\\\n- `tests/fixtures/error-patch-*` (new)\\\\n\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\n- Phase 1 (recipe parsing for FileOp::Patch)\\\\n- Phase 3 (scope detection module)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 5: Integration Testing + Full Validation\\\\nStatus: Partial — core passing, fixture gaps remain\\\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\\\n\\\\nComprehensive integration testing across all four operation types. Multi-operation recipes, idempotency, determinism, cross-language scope detection, and the full fixture suite.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 5.1: Create `combined-all-ops` fixture — a single recipe that uses create, inject, replace, and patch in sequence, demonstrating all four operation types working together.\\\\n- [ ] 5.2: Create `combined-patch-idempotent` fixture — a patch-heavy recipe run twice, second run should show all skips. (existing `combined-idempotency` covers create+inject but not patch-specific idempotency)\\\\n- [ ] 5.3: Cross-language scope detection fixtures — Python class body and Rust struct braces covered. Missing: TypeScript interface braces, Go function braces, YAML nested block.\\\\n- [x] 5.4: Determinism test — integration tests confirm same inputs produce same outputs.\\\\n- [x] 5.5: Error recovery test — `error-replace-no-match`, `error-replace-end-missing`, `error-patch-no-anchor` all produce structured errors with rendered_content.\\\\n- [ ] 5.6: Snapshot tests — insta snapshots for verbose scope diagnostics not implemented (FR-8 verbose diagnostics not yet built).\\\\n- [x] 5.7: Verify `cargo clippy` passes clean and `cargo test` passes all tests. 298 tests passing (284 unit + 2 CLI + 12 integration).\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- All four operation types work in a single recipe (ordered execution preserved)\\\\n- Idempotent recipes produce all skips on second run\\\\n- Same inputs = same output across runs (determinism)\\\\n- Every error case from the Error Handling table in SPEC.md is covered by at least one fixture\\\\n- All scope types tested against at least two different languages\\\\n- `cargo test` green, `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- Total test count significantly above 191 (target: 300+)\\\\n\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\n- `tests/fixtures/combined-*` (new)\\\\n- `tests/fixtures/patch-*-python`, `patch-*-rust`, etc. (new, language-specific)\\\\n- `tests/integration.rs` (may need minor updates for new fixture types)\\\\n\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\n- Phase 2 (replace operation)\\\\n- Phase 4 (patch operation)\\\\n\\\\n## Dependencies\\\\n\\\\n- **Depends on:** core-engine workstream (v0.1 — complete). Specifically: recipe.rs parsing infrastructure, operations/mod.rs dispatch, output.rs formatting, error.rs types, the existing integration test harness.\\\\n- **Blocks:** workflows (v0.3) — workflows chain recipes that include patch/replace operations. Libraries (v0.4) — library recipes use patch operations extensively (the \\\\\\\"add-model-field\\\\\\\" example from the spec).\\\\n\\\\n## Key Decisions\\\\n\\\\n| Decision | Choice | Rationale |\\\\n|----------|--------|-----------|\\\\n| Scope type in recipe, not auto-detected from file extension | Recipe author specifies `scope: class_body` or `scope: braces` explicitly | Language detection is fragile and adds complexity. The recipe author knows the target language. Scope type is the right abstraction level. (ARCHITECTURE D-3, NFR-1 AC-N1.3) |\\\\n| Indentation vs delimiter are separate code paths | `scope/indent.rs` and `scope/delimiter.rs` as distinct modules | They share no logic. Indentation walks lines measuring whitespace. Delimiters count balanced pairs. Forcing a common abstraction would be artificial. |\\\\n| Semantic positions are heuristic, not parsed | Regex patterns like `^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` for field detection | Full parsing (tree-sitter) is out of scope. Heuristics cover 90%+ of real code. When they fail, the error includes rendered content for fallback. (ARCHITECTURE D-3, I-10) |\\\\n| Position fallback rather than error | `after_last_field` falls back to `before_close` if no fields found | A recipe that works on a class with 5 fields should also work on an empty class. Erroring on empty scopes would make recipes fragile. The fallback is noted in verbose output. |\\\\n| Indentation matching on insert | Patch auto-adjusts rendered content indentation to match insertion context | Template authors shouldn't need to know the exact indentation of every insertion point. Templates define relative indentation; jig adjusts the base level. |\\\\n| `find` narrowing auto-detects sub-scopes | If the `find` line contains an opening delimiter, jig detects the sub-scope | This enables the `list_display = [...]` pattern from the spec without requiring the recipe author to specify a second scope type. Keeps recipe YAML simple. |\\\\n| Replace preserves markers (between mode) | Start and end marker lines are kept; only content between is swapped | Markers serve as stable anchors for future re-runs. Removing them would break idempotency. The spec is explicit about this. |\\\\n| First match for anchor pattern | Patch uses first regex match (like inject's `at: first` default) | Predictable behavior. If the user needs a specific match, they write a more specific regex. Adding `at: first/last` to patch is a future option if needed. |\\\\n\\\\n## Risks / Open Questions\\\\n\\\\n- **Risk: Indentation heuristics fail on unusual code.** Python with mixed indentation, or code with unusual formatting, may confuse the indentation-based scope detection. Mitigation: clear error messages with rendered content, verbose mode showing scope boundaries, and the fallback-to-LLM design (I-10). Accept that edge cases exist and fail gracefully rather than trying to handle every case.\\\\n\\\\n- **Risk: String literal detection in delimiter scopes is imperfect.** Multi-line strings (Python triple-quotes, Rust raw strings, JavaScript template literals with nested expressions) are hard to detect without full parsing. Mitigation: handle the common cases (single/double quoted, backtick), document the limitation, rely on the error-with-rendered-content fallback for edge cases.\\\\n\\\\n- **Risk: `after_last_method` body detection.** Finding the end of a method's body (not just the `def` line) requires running scope detection recursively. This is correct but adds complexity. Mitigation: implement it — the alternative (inserting after the `def` line) would be wrong in most cases.\\\\n\\\\n- **~~Open question~~ Resolved: Should `pattern` mode in replace match a single contiguous block or all matches?** Decision: match the first contiguous block of lines matching the pattern. If users need all occurrences, they can use multiple replace operations. This keeps behavior predictable.\\\\n\\\\n- **~~Open question~~ Resolved: Indentation matching precision.** Decision: match the immediate context (indentation of the line at the insertion point). This is simpler and always correct for the local context, even in files with mixed indentation.\\\\n\\\\n## Review Findings (2026-04-04)\\\\n\\\\nCode review completed (1 round, clean). The review identified issues across three severity levels. **None have been fixed in code yet** — the fix commit (6f9b69b) only added review artifacts.\\\\n\\\\n### Critical (must fix before merge)\\\\n\\\\n1. **`write_back` silently swallows write errors.** `patch.rs:228` uses `let _ = std::fs::write(...)` for every non-dry-run write; `replace.rs:278` does the same in fallback append/prepend. Permission-denied returns `OpResult::Success`, violating AC-7.11, AC-2.14, I-10. The main replace path (`replace.rs:106`) handles this correctly — apply the same pattern.\\\\n2. **`Position::Sorted` is stub-implemented.** `position.rs:170-184` just inserts at end-of-scope. AC-5.7 requires alphabetically correct insertion. Either implement or reject at parse time.\\\\n3. **Byte index / char index mismatch in delimiter scope detection.** `delimiter.rs:87` slices with `CharScanner.col` (char position) but Rust string slicing uses byte offsets. Multi-byte UTF-8 causes panic.\\\\n\\\\n### Major (should fix)\\\\n\\\\n4. **11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures missing (42% gap).** Missing: `patch-function-body`, `patch-brackets`, `patch-function-signature`, `patch-find-narrowing`, `patch-sorted`, `patch-indent-matching`, `patch-nested-delimiters`, `patch-string-delimiters`, `error-patch-unbalanced`, `error-patch-find-missing`, `combined-patch-idempotent`.\\\\n5. **Verbose scope diagnostics (FR-8) not implemented.** No `ScopeDiagnostics` struct, no `scope_diagnostics` JSON field. Verbose only adds `rendered_content`.\\\\n6. **`find` narrowing doesn't re-anchor when no sub-scope is detected.** AC-6.1 requires position resolution relative to the found line, not the original scope.\\\\n7. **`scope: line` auto-adjusts indentation, diverging from inject-after behavior.** AC-7.12 says \\\\\\\"SHALL behave identically to inject's `after` mode.\\\\\\\"\\\\n8. **`find_opening` doesn't skip strings/comments when searching for the opening delimiter.** AC-4.6/4.7 — only the nesting-depth loop has string/comment awareness.\\\\n9. **`AfterLastMethod` regex too narrow for Rust.** Misses `const fn`, `unsafe fn`, `pub(crate) fn`, `extern \\\\\\\"C\\\\\\\" fn`.\\\\n10. **Location string uses `Debug` not `Display`.** Produces `classbody` instead of `class_body`.\\\\n11. **Mixed tab/space scope detection uses length, not literal prefix comparison.** AC-3.8 requires character-by-character whitespace comparison.\\\\n\\\\n### Minor\\\\n\\\\n12. **`AfterLastField` regex requires leading whitespace (`\\\\\\\\s+` not `\\\\\\\\s*`).** Fails on top-level fields (Go structs).\\\\n13. **Regex recompiled at runtime in replace.** Pre-compile during recipe parsing.\\\\n14. **`chars().count()` called on every scanner step.** O(n^2) per line in delimiter detection.\\\\n\\\\n## Execution Order\\\\n\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:21:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_8\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/workflows/SPEC.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# SPEC.md\\\\n\\\\n> Workstream: workflows\\\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\\\n> Scope: v0.3 (Phase H from ARCHITECTURE.md)\\\\n\\\\n## Overview\\\\n\\\\nThe workflows workstream adds multi-recipe orchestration to jig. A workflow is a YAML file that chains multiple recipes into a single invocation with conditional steps, variable mapping between steps, and configurable error handling. This is the composition layer — where individual recipes (create, inject, replace, patch) are combined into meaningful cross-file operations like \\\\\\\"add a field to a model and propagate it through the service, schema, admin, and tests.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\nWorkflows replace the earlier `includes` concept from the spec. Unlike static includes (which always run all sub-recipes), workflows are conditional, configurable, and report per-step results. They are the prerequisite for libraries (v0.4), which define workflows over library-namespaced recipes.\\\\n\\\\nOut of scope for this workstream: library integration (v0.4), cross-library workflows (v0.9), schema-first generation (v0.9), nested workflows (a step cannot reference another workflow), and output variable passing between steps (steps share the filesystem, not variables).\\\\n\\\\n## Requirements\\\\n\\\\n### Functional Requirements\\\\n\\\\n#### FR-1: Workflow Definition Parsing\\\\n\\\\nParse a workflow YAML file into a validated internal representation. A workflow declares shared variables, an ordered list of steps (each referencing a recipe), and a default error handling mode. Resolve recipe paths relative to the workflow file location. Distinguish workflow files from recipe files by structural content.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-1.1 | Event | WHEN a valid workflow YAML is provided with `steps` (required), and optionally `name`, `description`, `variables`, and `on_error`, the system SHALL parse it into a Workflow struct | TEST-1.1 |\\\\n| AC-1.2 | Event | WHEN a workflow step declares `recipe` (required), and optionally `when`, `vars_map`, `vars`, and `on_error`, the system SHALL parse all fields into a WorkflowStep struct | TEST-1.2 |\\\\n| AC-1.3 | Event | WHEN a workflow step's `recipe` path is relative, the system SHALL resolve it relative to the workflow file's directory (consistent with recipe-relative template resolution, I-7) | TEST-1.3 |\\\\n| AC-1.4 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is omitted from the workflow, the system SHALL default to `stop` | TEST-1.4 |\\\\n| AC-1.5 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is specified at the workflow level, the system SHALL accept one of: `stop`, `continue`, or `report` | TEST-1.5 |\\\\n| AC-1.6 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is specified at the step level, the system SHALL use the step-level value as an override for that step only | TEST-1.6 |\\\\n| AC-1.7 | Unwanted | IF a step's `recipe` path (resolved relative to the workflow file) does not point to an existing file, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the missing recipe path and the resolved location | TEST-1.7 |\\\\n| AC-1.8 | Unwanted | IF the workflow YAML is malformed (invalid YAML syntax), the system SHALL exit with code 1 and an error message identifying the parse failure location | TEST-1.8 |\\\\n| AC-1.9 | Event | WHEN the workflow has an empty `steps: []` array, the system SHALL exit 0 with an empty steps result and no files written | TEST-1.9 |\\\\n| AC-1.10 | Event | WHEN the workflow YAML has no `variables` key or an empty `variables` map, the system SHALL accept the workflow as valid (no workflow-level variable declarations) | TEST-1.10 |\\\\n| AC-1.11 | Unwanted | IF a step specifies an invalid `on_error` value (not one of stop/continue/report), the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the invalid value and the allowed options | TEST-1.11 |\\\\n| AC-1.12 | Event | WHEN a step's `when` field is specified, the system SHALL store it as a Jinja2 template string for evaluation at execution time | TEST-1.12 |\\\\n| AC-1.13 | Event | WHEN a step's `vars_map` is specified, the system SHALL parse it as a string-to-string mapping (workflow variable name to recipe variable name) | TEST-1.13 |\\\\n| AC-1.14 | Event | WHEN a step's `vars` is specified, the system SHALL parse it as a string-to-JSON-value mapping of variable overrides | TEST-1.14 |\\\\n| AC-1.15 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL distinguish workflow YAML from recipe YAML by the presence of `steps` (workflow) vs `files` (recipe) when auto-detecting in `jig validate` and `jig vars` | TEST-1.15 |\\\\n| AC-1.16 | Unwanted | IF a YAML file contains both `steps` and `files` top-level keys, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the ambiguous file type | TEST-1.16 |\\\\n| AC-1.17 | Unwanted | IF a YAML file passed to `jig validate` or `jig vars` contains neither `steps` nor `files`, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the missing structural key | TEST-1.17 |\\\\n| AC-1.18 | Event | WHEN validating a workflow, the system SHALL also parse and validate each referenced recipe file (confirming they are structurally valid recipes) | TEST-1.18 |\\\\n| AC-1.19 | Unwanted | IF a referenced recipe file is structurally invalid (malformed YAML, missing template files, etc.), the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the recipe validation error and which step referenced it | TEST-1.19 |\\\\n| AC-1.20 | Event | WHEN the workflow YAML has optional metadata fields (`name`, `description`), the system SHALL accept workflows with or without them | TEST-1.20 |\\\\n\\\\n#### FR-2: Workflow Variable Handling\\\\n\\\\nAccept variables from the same sources as recipe execution (--vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin). Validate against workflow-level declarations. Pass all workflow-level variables to every step by default (shared variables).\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-2.1 | Event | WHEN workflow-level variables are declared, the system SHALL validate provided variables against declarations using the same rules as recipe variable validation (type checking, required fields, defaults, enum validation) | TEST-2.1 |\\\\n| AC-2.2 | Event | WHEN `--vars`, `--vars-file`, and/or `--vars-stdin` are provided, the system SHALL merge with the same precedence as recipes: workflow defaults < vars-file < vars-stdin < inline --vars | TEST-2.2 |\\\\n| AC-2.3 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL pass all workflow-level resolved variables to every step by default (shared variables) | TEST-2.3 |\\\\n| AC-2.4 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accept variable input containing keys not declared in the workflow's variables section without error or warning (consistent with recipe behavior) | TEST-2.4 |\\\\n| AC-2.5 | Event | WHEN `jig vars` is invoked with a workflow file, the system SHALL output the workflow's variable declarations in the same JSON format as recipe variable output | TEST-2.5 |\\\\n| AC-2.6 | Event | WHEN a workflow declares no variables and no variable input is provided, the system SHALL pass an empty object to all steps | TEST-2.6 |\\\\n| AC-2.7 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL accumulate all workflow-level variable validation errors and report them together with exit code 4 (consistent with recipe behavior) | TEST-2.7 |\\\\n\\\\n#### FR-3: Conditional Step Evaluation\\\\n\\\\nEvaluate `when` expressions to determine whether a step should execute. The `when` field is a Jinja2 template rendered with workflow-level variables. The rendered result determines truthiness.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-3.1 | Event | WHEN a step has a `when` field, the system SHALL render it as a Jinja2 template using the workflow-level resolved variables (before vars_map and vars are applied) | TEST-3.1 |\\\\n| AC-3.2 | Event | WHEN the rendered `when` result, after stripping leading and trailing whitespace, is empty, case-insensitively equals \\\\\\\"false\\\\\\\", or equals \\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\", the system SHALL skip the step | TEST-3.2 |\\\\n| AC-3.3 | Event | WHEN the rendered `when` result is truthy (does not match any falsy pattern in AC-3.2), the system SHALL execute the step | TEST-3.3 |\\\\n| AC-3.4 | Event | WHEN a step is skipped due to a `when` condition evaluating to falsy, the system SHALL report the step with status \\\\\\\"skipped\\\\\\\" and include the reason \\\\\\\"when condition evaluated to false\\\\\\\" | TEST-3.4 |\\\\n| AC-3.5 | Event | WHEN a step has no `when` field, the system SHALL execute it unconditionally | TEST-3.5 |\\\\n| AC-3.6 | Unwanted | IF the `when` template references an undefined variable, the system SHALL treat it as a step failure (template rendering error), subject to the applicable on_error mode | TEST-3.6 |\\\\n| AC-3.7 | Unwanted | IF the `when` template has a Jinja2 syntax error, the system SHALL treat it as a step failure (template rendering error), subject to the applicable on_error mode | TEST-3.7 |\\\\n| AC-3.8 | Event | WHEN a `when` expression uses Jinja2 control flow (e.g., `{% if methods | length > 0 %}yes{% endif %}`), the system SHALL evaluate it correctly — the rendered output determines truthiness, not the expression syntax | TEST-3.8 |\\\\n\\\\n#### FR-4: Variable Mapping and Overrides\\\\n\\\\nAllow steps to rename and override variables when passing them to a recipe. This enables workflow variables to be mapped to different names expected by different recipes, and allows step-specific value overrides.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-4.1 | Event | WHEN a step specifies `vars_map` with entries `{source: target}`, the system SHALL rename workflow variable `source` to `target` in the variables passed to the step's recipe | TEST-4.1 |\\\\n| AC-4.2 | Event | WHEN `vars_map` renames a variable, the original name SHALL NOT be present in the step's resolved variables (rename semantics, not copy) | TEST-4.2 |\\\\n| AC-4.3 | Event | WHEN a step specifies `vars`, the system SHALL merge these values into the step's variables, overriding any existing values (including values resulting from vars_map renaming) | TEST-4.3 |\\\\n| AC-4.4 | Ubiquitous | The variable resolution order for each step SHALL be: (1) start with all workflow-level resolved variables, (2) apply vars_map renaming, (3) apply vars overrides | TEST-4.4 |\\\\n| AC-4.5 | Event | WHEN the step's resolved variables are passed to the recipe, the system SHALL validate them against the recipe's variable declarations (type checking, required fields, defaults) | TEST-4.5 |\\\\n| AC-4.6 | Unwanted | IF a step's recipe declares a required variable that is not present in the step's resolved variables (after vars_map and vars), the system SHALL treat it as a step failure (variable validation error), subject to the applicable on_error mode | TEST-4.6 |\\\\n| AC-4.7 | Event | WHEN `vars_map` references a source variable that does not exist in the workflow variables, the system SHALL silently ignore that mapping entry (no error — the variable simply is not in the set) | TEST-4.7 |\\\\n| AC-4.8 | Event | WHEN `vars_map` maps to a target name that already exists in the workflow variables, the renamed value SHALL override the existing value for that target name | TEST-4.8 |\\\\n| AC-4.9 | Event | WHEN a step has neither `vars_map` nor `vars`, the system SHALL pass the workflow-level variables to the recipe unchanged | TEST-4.9 |\\\\n| AC-4.10 | Event | WHEN multiple vars_map entries are specified, all renamings SHALL be applied simultaneously (not sequentially — renaming `a` to `b` and `b` to `c` does not chain; the original `a` becomes `b` and the original `b` becomes `c`) | TEST-4.10 |\\\\n\\\\n#### FR-5: Error Handling Modes\\\\n\\\\nControl workflow behavior when a step fails. Three modes determine whether the workflow stops, continues silently, or continues with partial failure reporting.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-5.1 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `stop` (default) and a step fails, the system SHALL stop the workflow immediately and not execute subsequent steps | TEST-5.1 |\\\\n| AC-5.2 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `stop` and a step fails, the system SHALL exit with the failed step's exit code (1, 2, 3, or 4 depending on the failure type) | TEST-5.2 |\\\\n| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `continue` and a step fails, the system SHALL record the failure and proceed to execute the next step | TEST-5.3 |\\\\n| AC-5.4 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `continue` and one or more steps failed, the system SHALL exit with code 0 | TEST-5.4 |\\\\n| AC-5.5 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `report` and a step fails, the system SHALL record the failure and proceed to execute the next step | TEST-5.5 |\\\\n| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `report` and one or more steps failed, the system SHALL exit with code 3 (file operation error) | TEST-5.6 |\\\\n| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN a step has its own `on_error` value, the system SHALL use the step-level value for that step instead of the workflow-level default | TEST-5.7 |\\\\n| AC-5.8 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `stop` and a step fails, the JSON output SHALL include results for all steps up to and including the failed step, with subsequent steps absent from the output | TEST-5.8 |\\\\n| AC-5.9 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `continue` or `report` and a step fails, the step's result SHALL include the error details and any operations that completed before the failure within that step | TEST-5.9 |\\\\n| AC-5.10 | Event | WHEN all steps succeed (or are skipped via `when`), the system SHALL exit with code 0 regardless of the on_error mode | TEST-5.10 |\\\\n\\\\n#### FR-6: Workflow Execution\\\\n\\\\nExecute workflow steps sequentially, sharing filesystem state between steps. Each step loads and runs a complete recipe with the step's resolved variables.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-6.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute workflow steps in the order they appear in the `steps` array | TEST-6.1 |\\\\n| AC-6.2 | Event | WHEN a step executes, the system SHALL load the referenced recipe, render all templates with the step's resolved variables, and execute all operations in the recipe in declaration order | TEST-6.2 |\\\\n| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN a step creates or modifies files, subsequent steps SHALL see those changes on the filesystem (steps share real filesystem state in normal execution mode) | TEST-6.3 |\\\\n| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `--dry-run` is specified, the system SHALL carry `virtual_files` state across steps so that a later step can read files \\\\\\\"created\\\\\\\" by an earlier step's create operation | TEST-6.4 |\\\\n| AC-6.5 | Event | WHEN `--force` is specified, the system SHALL pass the force flag to every step's recipe execution | TEST-6.5 |\\\\n| AC-6.6 | Event | WHEN `--base-dir` is specified, the system SHALL resolve all recipe output paths (to, inject, replace, patch) relative to the base directory across all steps | TEST-6.6 |\\\\n| AC-6.7 | Event | WHEN a recipe within a step has template paths (to, inject, replace, patch fields containing Jinja2 expressions), the system SHALL render those paths with the step's resolved variables | TEST-6.7 |\\\\n| AC-6.8 | Event | WHEN a recipe within a step has `skip_if` guards, the system SHALL evaluate them in the context of the current filesystem state (which may include changes from earlier steps) | TEST-6.8 |\\\\n| AC-6.9 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL validate workflow-level variables before executing any step. If workflow-level validation fails (exit code 4), no steps execute | TEST-6.9 |\\\\n\\\\n#### FR-7: CLI Workflow Command\\\\n\\\\nAdd a `jig workflow` subcommand to execute workflows. Extend `jig validate` and `jig vars` to auto-detect and handle workflow files.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN `jig workflow <path>` is invoked with a path to a workflow YAML file and variable sources, the system SHALL parse and execute the workflow | TEST-7.1 |\\\\n| AC-7.2 | Ubiquitous | The `jig workflow` command SHALL accept the same global options as `jig run`: `--vars`, `--vars-file`, `--vars-stdin`, `--dry-run`, `--json`, `--quiet`, `--force`, `--base-dir`, `--verbose` | TEST-7.2 |\\\\n| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN `jig validate` is invoked with a workflow YAML file (auto-detected by presence of `steps`), the system SHALL validate the workflow structure, all step configurations, and all referenced recipes | TEST-7.3 |\\\\n| AC-7.4 | Event | WHEN `jig vars` is invoked with a workflow YAML file (auto-detected by presence of `steps`), the system SHALL output the workflow's variable declarations in JSON format | TEST-7.4 |\\\\n| AC-7.5 | Event | WHEN `jig validate` succeeds on a workflow, the human output SHALL report: workflow name (if present), variable summary, step count (unconditional vs conditional), and recipe validation status for each step | TEST-7.5 |\\\\n| AC-7.6 | Event | WHEN `jig validate` succeeds on a workflow, the JSON output SHALL include: `type: \\\\\\\"workflow\\\\\\\"`, `valid: true`, workflow name, variables, and a steps array with recipe path, valid status, and conditional flag for each step | TEST-7.6 |\\\\n| AC-7.7 | Unwanted | IF the path passed to `jig workflow` does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting the missing file | TEST-7.7 |\\\\n| AC-7.8 | Unwanted | IF the file passed to `jig workflow` is a recipe (has `files` not `steps`), the system SHALL exit with code 1 reporting \\\\\\\"expected a workflow file (with `steps`), got a recipe file (with `files`).
