Use `jig run` to execute recipes.\\\\\\\" | TEST-7.8 |\\\\n\\\\n#### FR-8: Workflow Output Formatting\\\\n\\\\nReport workflow execution results as JSON (stdout) or human-readable text (stderr) with per-step detail. Follow the same dual-stream output conventions as recipe execution (I-8).\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-8.1 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced for a workflow, the system SHALL include a `steps` array where each element contains the step's recipe path, status, and (for executed steps) the operations array | TEST-8.1 |\\\\n| AC-8.2 | Event | WHEN a step succeeds, its JSON result SHALL include: `recipe` (path), `status: \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\"`, `operations` (array of OpResult), `files_written` (array), `files_skipped` (array) | TEST-8.2 |\\\\n| AC-8.3 | Event | WHEN a step is skipped due to a `when` condition, its JSON result SHALL include: `recipe` (path), `status: \\\\\\\"skipped\\\\\\\"`, `reason` (string explaining the skip) | TEST-8.3 |\\\\n| AC-8.4 | Event | WHEN a step fails (in continue/report modes), its JSON result SHALL include: `recipe` (path), `status: \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"`, `error` (structured error with what/where/why/hint), `rendered_content` (if available), and `operations` (any that completed before the failure) | TEST-8.4 |\\\\n| AC-8.5 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include aggregate `files_written` and `files_skipped` arrays combining results across all executed steps. A path appears in aggregate `files_written` if any step wrote to it. A path appears in aggregate `files_skipped` only if every operation targeting it across all steps was skipped | TEST-8.5 |\\\\n| AC-8.6 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include a top-level `on_error` field showing the workflow-level error handling mode | TEST-8.6 |\\\\n| AC-8.7 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include a top-level `workflow` field with the workflow name (or `null` if unnamed) | TEST-8.7 |\\\\n| AC-8.8 | Event | WHEN human output is produced, the system SHALL print step progress headers (e.g., \\\\\\\"Step 1/3: model/add-field/recipe.yaml\\\\\\\"), nested operation results indented under each step, and a summary line reporting steps executed, skipped, and failed | TEST-8.8 |\\\\n| AC-8.9 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified, the system SHALL include rendered template content for all operations across all steps (consistent with recipe --verbose behavior) | TEST-8.9 |\\\\n| AC-8.10 | Event | WHEN JSON output is produced, the system SHALL include a top-level `dry_run` boolean field | TEST-8.10 |\\\\n| AC-8.11 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `report` and one or more steps failed, the JSON output SHALL include a top-level `status` field of `\\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\"`. WHEN all steps succeed (or are skipped via `when`), the `status` field SHALL be `\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\"` | TEST-8.11 |\\\\n| AC-8.12 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `stop` and a step fails, the JSON output `status` field SHALL be `\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"` | TEST-8.12 |\\\\n| AC-8.13 | Event | WHEN `on_error` is `continue`, the JSON output `status` field SHALL be `\\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\"` regardless of step failures | TEST-8.13 |\\\\n| AC-8.14 | State | WHILE `--quiet` is active, the system SHALL suppress all stderr output for workflow execution (same as recipe behavior). JSON stdout is unaffected | TEST-8.14 |\\\\n\\\\n### Non-Functional Requirements\\\\n\\\\n#### NFR-1: Deterministic Workflow Execution\\\\n\\\\nWorkflow execution must be deterministic — same inputs always produce the same outputs.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce identical output when given the same workflow, variables, and existing files across multiple runs (extends I-1 to workflows) | TEST-N1.1 |\\\\n| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | Conditional step evaluation (`when`) SHALL be deterministic — the same variables always produce the same skip/execute decision | TEST-N1.2 |\\\\n| AC-N1.3 | Ubiquitous | Variable mapping and override resolution SHALL be deterministic — the same workflow variables, vars_map, and vars always produce the same step variables | TEST-N1.3 |\\\\n\\\\n#### NFR-2: Ordered Step Execution\\\\n\\\\nSteps must execute in declaration order, and later steps must see filesystem changes from earlier steps.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-N2.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL execute workflow steps in declaration order, never in parallel or out of order (extends I-9 to workflows) | TEST-N2.1 |\\\\n| AC-N2.2 | Event | WHEN step N creates a file and step N+1 injects into that file, step N+1 SHALL find and operate on the file created by step N | TEST-N2.2 |\\\\n\\\\n#### NFR-3: Graceful Step Failure Reporting\\\\n\\\\nWhen a step fails, the error must include enough context for the caller (typically an LLM) to diagnose and fall back.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-N3.1 | Event | WHEN a step fails, the error output SHALL include the step index (1-based), the recipe path, and the underlying structured error (what/where/why/hint) | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\n| AC-N3.2 | Event | WHEN a step's recipe operation fails, the rendered content SHALL be included in the error output so the caller can manually apply the change (extends I-4 and I-10 to workflows) | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\n| AC-N3.3 | Ubiquitous | Every workflow error SHALL map to the standard exit codes (1 for validation, 2 for rendering, 3 for file ops, 4 for variables) — no new exit codes are introduced | TEST-N3.3 |\\\\n\\\\n#### NFR-4: Idempotent Workflow Execution\\\\n\\\\nWorkflows must support safe re-runs when all constituent recipes use idempotency guards.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-N4.1 | Event | WHEN a workflow where all steps use `skip_if` guards is run a second time with the same variables on already-modified files, the system SHALL report all operations across all steps as `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\"` | TEST-N4.1 |\\\\n| AC-N4.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when workflow steps use `skip_if` idempotency guards (extends I-2 to workflows) | TEST-N4.2 |\\\\n\\\\n## Interfaces\\\\n\\\\n### Public API (CLI)\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\n# New subcommand\\\\njig workflow <path> [options]\\\\n\\\\n# Extended commands (auto-detect workflow files)\\\\njig validate <path>     # auto-detects recipe vs workflow\\\\njig vars <path>         # auto-detects recipe vs workflow\\\\n\\\\n# Options (same as jig run)\\\\n  --vars <json>        Inline variables as JSON string\\\\n  --vars-file <path>   Variables from a JSON file\\\\n  --vars-stdin         Read variables from stdin\\\\n  --dry-run            Preview without writing files\\\\n  --json               Force JSON output to stdout\\\\n  --quiet              Suppress stderr output\\\\n  --force              Overwrite existing files\\\\n  --base-dir <path>    Resolve output paths from this directory\\\\n  --verbose            Include rendered content and diagnostics in output\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n### Workflow YAML Format\\\\n\\\\n```yaml\\\\n# workflow.yaml\\\\nname: add-field                              # optional\\\\ndescription: \\\\\\\"Add a field across the stack\\\\\\\"  # optional\\\\n\\\\nvariables:                                   # optional\\\\n  app:\\\\n    type: string\\\\n    required: true\\\\n  model:\\\\n    type: string\\\\n    required: true\\\\n  field_name:\\\\n    type: string\\\\n    required: true\\\\n\\\\non_error: stop                               # optional, default: stop\\\\n\\\\nsteps:\\\\n  - recipe: ./model/add-field/recipe.yaml\\\\n  - recipe: ./service/add-method/recipe.yaml\\\\n    when: \\\\\\\"{{ update_service }}\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:24:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_11\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"I’ve confirmed the local workflow conventions and existing plan style. I’m now checking `docs/development` guidance and roadmap notes for `libraries` so the execution plan includes the right milestones, dependencies, and decision points.\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:28:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_13\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/ROADMAP.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# ROADMAP.md\\\\n\\\\nFull delivery plan for jig, bridging the product spec (`jig.md`) to workstream execution. Every feature in the spec is accounted for here — either assigned to a milestone, explicitly deferred, or marked as cross-cutting infrastructure.\\\\n\\\\nLast updated: 2026-04-04\\\\n\\\\n## Current State\\\\n\\\\n| Milestone | Status | Tests | Docs |\\\\n|-----------|--------|-------|------|\\\\n| v0.1 core-engine | **Done** | 191 | PLAN, SPEC, SHARED-CONTEXT, NARRATIVE |\\\\n| v0.2 replace-patch | **Done** | 308 total | PLAN, SPEC, SHARED-CONTEXT, NARRATIVE |\\\\n| v0.3 workflows | **Done** | 343 total | PLAN, SPEC, SHARED-CONTEXT, NARRATIVE |\\\\n| v0.4–v1.0 | Described in jig.md | — | Nothing beyond one-line mentions in ARCHITECTURE.md |\\\\n\\\\nThe engine works. The planning infrastructure works. What's missing is the roadmap connecting the two through the remaining milestones.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Two Tracks\\\\n\\\\njig has two parallel development tracks with dependencies between them:\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nENGINE TRACK (the tool itself)          AGENT TRACK (how agents use the tool)\\\\n─────────────────────────────           ──────────────────────────────────────\\\\nv0.1 core-engine ✅\\\\nv0.2 replace-patch ✅\\\\nv0.3 workflows ──────────────────────── agent evals (basic scenarios)\\\\nv0.4 libraries ──────────────────────── MCP server\\\\nv0.5 distribution                       project instructions template\\\\nv0.6 Claude Code plugin ◄────────────── requires MCP + libraries\\\\nv0.7 scan & check                       agent evals (full scenario suite)\\\\nv0.8 infer\\\\nv0.9 polyglot / schema-first\\\\nv1.0 stable\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe engine track is sequential — each milestone builds on the previous. The agent track runs in parallel once the engine is usable (after v0.3), and the two converge at v0.6 (Claude Code plugin).\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Milestone Details\\\\n\\\\n### v0.3 — Workflows (next up)\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** Complete (PLAN, SPEC, SHARED-CONTEXT)\\\\n**Depends on:** v0.2 (done)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 1223–1253, roadmap line 1920\\\\n\\\\nMulti-recipe orchestration. Chain recipes into a single `jig workflow` invocation with conditional steps (`when`), variable mapping (`vars_map`, `vars`), and error handling modes (`stop`, `continue`, `report`).\\\\n\\\\n**Scope (from SPEC):**\\\\n- Workflow YAML parsing (distinct from recipe format)\\\\n- `jig workflow` CLI command\\\\n- Conditional steps via Jinja2 template truthiness\\\\n- Variable mapping and overrides between steps\\\\n- Three error handling modes\\\\n- Per-step result reporting in JSON output\\\\n- `jig validate` and `jig vars` auto-detect workflow files\\\\n- 25 integration test fixtures\\\\n\\\\n**What this unlocks:** Workflows are the composition primitive. Without them, every multi-file change requires the agent to call `jig run` N times. With them, one `jig workflow` call handles the entire cascade. This is the minimum viable surface for meaningful agent evals.\\\\n\\\\n**Known blockers from v0.2:**\\\\n- `Position::Sorted` is a stub (panics) — any workflow step using it will fail\\\\n- `write_back` in replace.rs/patch.rs may swallow I/O errors\\\\n- 11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures still missing\\\\n- `cmd_run` needs refactoring to extract reusable `run_recipe()` for workflow steps\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### v0.4 — Libraries\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None (needs PLAN + SPEC)\\\\n**Depends on:** v0.3 (workflows reference library recipes)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 1059–1376, roadmap line 1929\\\\n\\\\nLibraries are versioned recipe collections for a framework. This is where framework opinions live — jig itself stays agnostic.\\\\n\\\\n**Scope (from jig.md):**\\\\n\\\\n*Library manifest:*\\\\n- `jig-library.yaml` format: name, version, description, framework, language\\\\n- `conventions` block: path templates mapping concerns to file locations\\\\n- `recipes` block: flat list of recipe paths + descriptions\\\\n- `workflows` block: multi-recipe workflow definitions (uses v0.3 workflow format)\\\\n\\\\n*CLI commands:*\\\\n- `jig library add <url|path>` — install from git repo or local directory\\\\n- `jig library remove <name>` — uninstall\\\\n- `jig library update <name>` — pull latest\\\\n- `jig library list` — show installed libraries\\\\n- `jig library recipes <name>` — list all recipes in a library\\\\n- `jig library info <name>/<recipe>` — show recipe details\\\\n- `jig library workflows <name>` — list workflows\\\\n\\\\n*Installation:*\\\\n- Global: `~/.jig/libraries/`\\\\n- Project-local: `.jig/libraries/` (takes precedence)\\\\n\\\\n*Convention overrides:*\\\\n- `.jigrc.yaml` per-project convention remapping\\\\n- Override just the path patterns, keep everything else\\\\n\\\\n*Project extensions:*\\\\n- `.jig/overrides/<library>/<recipe>/templates/` — replace a single template without forking\\\\n- `.jig/extensions/<library>/<recipe>/` — add new recipes namespaced under a library\\\\n\\\\n**What this unlocks:** Libraries make jig useful beyond a single project. They're also a prerequisite for the MCP server (which needs `jig_library_recipes` to work), the Claude Code plugin (which wraps a library), and meaningful agent evals (which need a library like jig-django to test against).\\\\n\\\\n**Open questions for SPEC:**\\\\n- Does `jig library add` from git clone the whole repo or just the jig-library.yaml + recipe dirs?\\\\n- Versioning strategy: git tags? semver in manifest? lock file?\\\\n- How does `jig run` resolve a library recipe path (e.g., `jig run django/model/add-field`)? Is it `jig run --library django model/add-field` or path-based?\\\\n- Does `jig workflow` accept library-qualified workflow names directly?\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### v0.5 — Distribution\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None (needs PLAN)\\\\n**Depends on:** v0.4 (distribution should include library install capability)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 806–950, roadmap line 1937\\\\n\\\\nPackage and ship jig so others can install it.\\\\n\\\\n**Scope (from jig.md):**\\\\n\\\\n| Channel | Mechanism |\\\\n|---------|-----------|\\\\n| GitHub Releases | Cross-compiled binaries (macOS arm64/x86_64, Linux arm64/x86_64/musl, Windows x86_64) |\\\\n| Homebrew | Tap first (`brew tap <org>/tools`), graduate to homebrew-core at 50+ stars |\\\\n| Cargo | `cargo install jig-cli` on crates.io |\\\\n| Nix | Flake with `buildRustPackage` |\\\\n| npm | Thin binary wrapper (`npx @<org>/jig`), platform-specific postinstall (esbuild pattern) |\\\\n| Shell installer | `curl -fsSL .../install.sh \\\\\\\\| sh` — detects platform, downloads binary |\\\\n\\\\n*CI/CD:*\\\\n- GitHub Actions workflow for cross-compilation\\\\n- Release automation on git tag push\\\\n- Binary signing (optional)\\\\n- SHA256 checksums in release notes\\\\n\\\\n**What this unlocks:** External users. Everything before v0.5 is author-only usage. This is also when the npm wrapper becomes available, which matters because the MCP server ships as `npx @jig/mcp-server` and needs to find the `jig` binary.\\\\n\\\\n**Open questions for PLAN:**\\\\n- Minimum viable distribution: just GitHub Releases + Homebrew tap? Or all channels at once?\\\\n- Does the npm wrapper ship in this milestone or with the MCP server?\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### v0.6 — Claude Code Plugin\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None (needs PLAN + SPEC)\\\\n**Depends on:** v0.4 (libraries), MCP server (agent track)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 650–730, 1307–1376, roadmap line 1946\\\\n\\\\nThe deepest integration: jig as a Claude Code plugin bundling MCP tools, skills, and hooks.\\\\n\\\\n**Scope (from jig.md):**\\\\n\\\\n*jig plugin itself:*\\\\n- `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` manifest\\\\n- `/jig:init` skill — scaffold recipe + templates dir inside a skill\\\\n- `/jig:doctor` skill — validate all recipes in a plugin\\\\n\\\\n*jig-django as reference library + plugin:*\\\\n- First community library: recipes for model, service, view, schema, admin, test, factory\\\\n- Workflows: `add-field`, `add-endpoint`, `scaffold-resource`\\\\n- Claude Code skills wrapping each workflow: read code → extract variables → call jig\\\\n- Dual-publish: installable as `jig library add` AND as a Claude Code plugin\\\\n\\\\n*Skill structure (from jig.md):*\\\\n```\\\\njig-django/\\\\n  jig-library.yaml              # library manifest\\\\n  .claude-plugin/plugin.json    # Claude Code plugin manifest\\\\n  skills/\\\\n    add-field/SKILL.md           # reads model, extracts context, runs jig workflow\\\\n    add-endpoint/SKILL.md\\\\n    scaffold-resource/SKILL.md\\\\n  model/add-field/recipe.yaml    # actual recipes\\\\n  service/add-method/recipe.yaml\\\\n  ...\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**What this unlocks:** The full vision — a developer says \\\\\\\"add a loyalty_tier field to Reservation\\\\\\\" and gets a consistent, multi-file, team-compliant change. The skill handles the intelligence (reading code, extracting variables), jig handles the mechanics (rendering, patching).\\\\n\\\\n**Open questions for SPEC:**\\\\n- Does the jig plugin provide the MCP server, or is it registered separately?\\\\n- Hook integration: what triggers `jig check` automatically? Post-edit on model files?\\\\n- How does dual-publish versioning work? Library version matches plugin version?\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### v0.7 — Scan & Check\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None (needs PLAN + SPEC)\\\\n**Depends on:** v0.4 (needs library recipes to scan/check against)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 1435–1720, roadmap line 1961\\\\n\\\\nReverse operations that close the loop: instead of variables → files, go files → variables (scan) and files → conformance report (check).\\\\n\\\\n**Scope (from jig.md):**\\\\n\\\\n*`jig scan`:*\\\\n- Reverse a recipe: given an existing file, extract the variables that would have produced it\\\\n- File-level scan: `jig scan django/model ./hotels/models/reservation.py`\\\\n- Directory-level scan: `jig scan django ./hotels/` — project map showing coverage\\\\n- Output: JSON with `variables`, `confidence`, `unrecognized` (lines the recipe can't explain)\\\\n- Enables the workflow: scan → LLM modifies variables → run recipe\\\\n\\\\n*`jig check`:*\\\\n- Conformance verification: `jig check django/model ./hotels/models/*.py`\\\\n- Issue reporting with severity levels (error, warn)\\\\n- `fix_recipe` references: when an issue has a known recipe fix, jig tells you\\\\n- Directory-level checking across all library concerns\\\\n- `--strict` and `--exit-on-failure` flags for CI gating\\\\n- JSON output for LLM consumption\\\\n\\\\n**What this unlocks:** Scan → Check → Fix is a self-healing loop. The LLM can run `jig check`, read the issues, and automatically invoke the fix recipes. Check as CI gate turns team conventions into enforceable rules — the recipe IS the source of truth.\\\\n\\\\n**Open questions for SPEC:**\\\\n- Scan algorithm: template reverse-matching is complex. What's the minimum viable approach? Regex extraction from anchor patterns? Or full template inversion?\\\\n- Confidence scoring: what's the threshold for \\\\\\\"matched\\\\\\\"?\\\\n- How does scan handle files that deviate significantly from the recipe?\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### v0.8 — Infer\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None (needs PLAN + SPEC)\\\\n**Depends on:** v0.7 (scan provides the foundation for pattern recognition)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 1526–1625, roadmap line 1968\\\\n\\\\nLearn recipes from examples instead of writing them by hand.\\\\n\\\\n**Scope (from jig.md):**\\\\n- `jig infer --before <file> --after <file>` — learn from a single before/after pair\\\\n- `jig infer --example <file>:before,after` (multiple) — variable detection improves with more examples\\\\n- `jig infer --from-git --pattern \\\\\\\"Add * field to *\\\\\\\"` — learn from commit history\\\\n- `jig infer --from-commit <sha>` — learn multi-file workflow from a single commit\\\\n- Draft/review/promote workflow: inferred recipes go to `_drafts/`, reviewed, then promoted\\\\n- Inferred recipes are expected to be 70-90% correct (the last 10-30% is human judgment)\\\\n\\\\n**What this unlocks:** The biggest friction reduction. Instead of hand-writing recipes, developers (or the LLM) infer them from the team's actual behavior. Combined with the observation engine (post-1.0), this makes recipe creation nearly zero-effort.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### v0.9 — Polyglot & Schema-First\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None (needs PLAN + SPEC)\\\\n**Depends on:** v0.4 (libraries), v0.3 (workflows)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 1722–1851, roadmap line 1975\\\\n\\\\nCross the framework boundary. A single command touches Django backend + Vue frontend.\\\\n\\\\n**Scope (from jig.md):**\\\\n- Cross-library workflows: steps reference different libraries (`library: django`, `library: vue`)\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:30:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_14\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '260,620p' docs/ROADMAP.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"- Cross-library workflows: steps reference different libraries (`library: django`, `library: vue`)\\\\n- `jig from-schema openapi|sql|proto|graphql` command: derive variables from schema definitions\\\\n- Type mapping definitions in library manifests (`openapi_to_django`, `openapi_to_typescript`)\\\\n- Schema-to-variables resolution: read the spec, resolve types, pass to recipes\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### v1.0 — Stable\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None\\\\n**Depends on:** All previous milestones at a quality bar\\\\n**jig.md reference:** roadmap line 1981\\\\n\\\\nSemver stability guarantees on:\\\\n- Recipe YAML format\\\\n- Anchor/scope system\\\\n- Library manifest format\\\\n- CLI interface and JSON output\\\\n- Exit codes\\\\n\\\\nAlso: comprehensive documentation site, homebrew-core publication, 3+ community libraries.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Agent Track\\\\n\\\\nThese are not milestones in the engine sense — they're infrastructure that develops in parallel with the engine track.\\\\n\\\\n### MCP Server\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None (needs PLAN)\\\\n**Can start after:** v0.2 (core engine exists), but full value requires v0.4 (libraries)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 2042–2243\\\\n\\\\nA thin stdio wrapper (~100-200 lines) that exposes jig commands as typed MCP tools. Agents get automatic tool discovery instead of parsing `--help`.\\\\n\\\\n**Architecture:**\\\\n```\\\\nAgent (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.)\\\\n  ↕ MCP stdio transport (JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout)\\\\njig-mcp-server\\\\n  ↕ spawns subprocess\\\\njig CLI binary\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**6 tools defined in jig.md:**\\\\n\\\\n| MCP Tool | Maps to CLI |\\\\n|----------|------------|\\\\n| `jig_run` | `jig run <recipe> --vars '<json>' --json` |\\\\n| `jig_vars` | `jig vars <recipe>` |\\\\n| `jig_scan` | `jig scan <recipe> <path> --json` |\\\\n| `jig_check` | `jig check <recipe> <path> --json` |\\\\n| `jig_workflow` | `jig workflow <workflow> --vars '<json>' --json` |\\\\n| `jig_library_recipes` | `jig library recipes <library> --json` |\\\\n\\\\n**Implementation options (from jig.md):**\\\\n1. TypeScript via `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (~100 lines, distributed via npm as `npx @jig/mcp-server`) — most pragmatic\\\\n2. Rust binary alongside jig (~200 lines) — shares release pipeline\\\\n3. Python via `mcp` SDK — minimal code\\\\n\\\\n**Phasing:**\\\\n- Phase 1 (after v0.3): `jig_run`, `jig_vars`, `jig_workflow` — enough for agents to use existing recipes\\\\n- Phase 2 (after v0.4): add `jig_library_recipes` — agents can discover library recipes\\\\n- Phase 3 (after v0.7): add `jig_scan`, `jig_check` — full tool surface\\\\n\\\\n**Per-tool config (from jig.md):**\\\\n- Claude Code: `.mcp.json` or `~/.claude/settings.json`\\\\n- Codex: `~/.codex/config.toml`\\\\n- Cursor: `.cursor/mcp.json`\\\\n- Windsurf: `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json`\\\\n\\\\n**Open questions:**\\\\n- Ship as separate npm package or bundled with jig binary?\\\\n- Version independently or lock to jig version?\\\\n- Does Phase 1 ship with v0.3 or v0.4?\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Project Instructions Template\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None (trivial — just a markdown snippet)\\\\n**Can start after:** v0.3 (when `jig workflow` exists)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 2244–2278\\\\n\\\\nA CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursorrules snippet telling agents about available jig commands:\\\\n\\\\n```markdown\\\\n## Code Generation with jig\\\\n\\\\nThis project uses jig for template-based code generation. When creating or extending\\\\nmodels, services, views, or tests, prefer jig recipes over manual code generation.\\\\n\\\\nAvailable workflows:\\\\n- `jig workflow django/add-field --vars '...'`\\\\n- `jig workflow django/add-endpoint --vars '...'`\\\\n\\\\nTo see what variables a recipe needs: `jig vars <recipe>`\\\\nAlways use --json flag and review the output before proceeding.\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis costs nothing, works immediately with 6/11 agentic tools, and doesn't need its own workstream. Ship it as part of v0.6 (plugin) or earlier as a docs artifact.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Agent Eval System\\\\n\\\\n**Workstream docs:** None (needs PLAN + SPEC — this is substantial infrastructure)\\\\n**Can start after:** v0.3 (needs workflows for meaningful scenarios), full value at v0.4+ (needs libraries)\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 2315–2904 (590 lines — the most detailed section in the spec)\\\\n\\\\nThe eval system measures whether agents can actually *use* jig. It's the scientific method applied to CLI ergonomics.\\\\n\\\\n**What it tests (axis 2 — agent usability, not mechanical correctness):**\\\\n- Can agents discover the right recipe?\\\\n- Can agents extract variables from existing code?\\\\n- Do agents construct valid `--vars` JSON?\\\\n- Do agents recover from jig errors?\\\\n- Do agents know when to fall back to manual editing?\\\\n- How does success vary across agent models?\\\\n\\\\n**Architecture (from jig.md):**\\\\n```\\\\neval/\\\\n  scenarios/          # fixture codebases + prompts + assertions\\\\n  harness/            # TypeScript: run.ts, agents.ts, score.ts, report.ts\\\\n  results/            # results.jsonl (append-only trial log)\\\\n  log/                # experiments.md (hypothesis journal)\\\\n  lib/                # sandbox.ts, diff.ts, normalize.ts\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Scoring dimensions:**\\\\n1. **Assertion pass rate** (primary) — weighted structural checks, 0-1\\\\n2. **File correctness** — structural diff with Jaccard fallback, 0-1\\\\n3. **Negative assertions** — no syntax errors / duplicates, binary 0 or 1\\\\n4. **Jig usage** — did the agent use jig or bypass it?\\\\n5. **Efficiency** — tool calls, tokens, wall-clock time\\\\n\\\\n**Key design features:**\\\\n- Baseline comparison: same scenarios with jig vs. without jig (isolates tool value)\\\\n- Experiment loop: hypothesis → change → run → score → log → decide\\\\n- Holdout set: training scenarios (iterated freely) vs. holdout scenarios (periodic generalization check)\\\\n- Scenario tiers: easy, medium, hard, discovery, error-recovery\\\\n- MCP vs. CLI mode comparison\\\\n- LLM-as-judge for soft criteria\\\\n- Cost tracking per trial\\\\n\\\\n**Phasing:**\\\\n\\\\n| Phase | When | Scope |\\\\n|-------|------|-------|\\\\n| Eval-1 | After v0.3 | Harness skeleton + 3-5 basic scenarios (single-recipe, no library). Tests: can agents call `jig run` and `jig workflow` correctly? Baseline comparison. |\\\\n| Eval-2 | After v0.4 | Add library scenarios (10-15 total). Tests: recipe discovery, variable extraction from existing code, workflow invocation. Full scoring. |\\\\n| Eval-3 | After v0.6 | Add MCP mode, plugin scenarios, discovery tier. Holdout set. Full experiment loop operational. |\\\\n| Eval-4 | After v0.7 | Add scan/check scenarios. Error-recovery tier. The eval now covers the full tool surface. |\\\\n\\\\n**Why start early:** The eval system doesn't just measure — it drives design decisions. Discovering that agents fail to construct `--vars` JSON correctly *before* v1.0 means we can fix it. Discovering it after v1.0 means we're stuck with a semver-locked interface.\\\\n\\\\n**Open questions for PLAN:**\\\\n- TypeScript is specified for the harness. Confirm this vs. Rust/Python.\\\\n- Where do fixture codebases come from? Hand-written? Extracted from real projects?\\\\n- Agent cost budget per eval run. At $0.04/trial, 450 trials = ~$18/run.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Observation Engine (post-1.0)\\\\n\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 1852–1897\\\\n\\\\nA Claude Code hook that logs edit patterns across sessions and proposes recipes from repeated behavior. `/jig:discover` analyzes the log and suggests what to automate.\\\\n\\\\nNot on the critical path. Depends on v0.8 (infer) to be useful — the observation engine detects patterns, infer creates the recipe drafts.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Cross-Cutting Concerns\\\\n\\\\nThese don't belong to a single milestone but span multiple:\\\\n\\\\n### `.jigrc.yaml` Configuration\\\\n\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 1023–1055\\\\n\\\\nProject-wide defaults: `base_dir`, `vars_file`, custom shell filters. Convention overrides for libraries land in the same file.\\\\n\\\\n| Feature | Lands in |\\\\n|---------|----------|\\\\n| `base_dir`, `vars_file` defaults | v0.3 or v0.4 (whenever config file loading is added) |\\\\n| Convention overrides (`libraries.django.conventions`) | v0.4 (libraries) |\\\\n| Custom filters via shell commands | Post-1.0 or v0.4 (low priority) |\\\\n\\\\n### `includes` → Workflows Migration\\\\n\\\\n**jig.md reference:** lines 1246–1252\\\\n\\\\nThe spec explicitly states that the `includes` concept from the patches section is \\\\\\\"subsumed by workflows\\\\\\\" and removed. Workflows are the composition primitive. No `includes` support should be built.\\\\n\\\\n### Verbose Scope Diagnostics\\\\n\\\\n**Spec reference:** v0.2 SPEC, currently incomplete\\\\n\\\\n`--verbose` showing scope boundaries, insertion points, and indentation detection is spec'd in v0.2 but implementation is partial. Should be completed as tech debt before v0.3 exercises it through workflows.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Dependency Graph\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nv0.1 ✅ ─► v0.2 ✅ ─► v0.3 workflows ─► v0.4 libraries ─► v0.5 distribution\\\\n                           │                   │                  │\\\\n                           │                   ├─► MCP server ────┤\\\\n                           │                   │                  │\\\\n                           ├─► eval (basic) ───┤                  │\\\\n                           │                   │                  │\\\\n                           │                   ├─► eval (full) ◄──┤\\\\n                           │                   │                  │\\\\n                           │                   └─► v0.6 plugin ◄──┘\\\\n                           │                         │\\\\n                           │                   v0.7 scan/check ─► v0.8 infer\\\\n                           │                         │\\\\n                           │                   eval (complete) ◄──┘\\\\n                           │\\\\n                           └─► project instructions template (trivial)\\\\n\\\\nv0.9 polyglot ◄── v0.4 libraries + v0.3 workflows\\\\nv1.0 stable ◄── everything above at quality bar\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nKey dependencies:\\\\n- **MCP server** needs v0.4 (libraries) for `jig_library_recipes` tool, but Phase 1 (`jig_run`, `jig_vars`, `jig_workflow`) can ship with v0.3\\\\n- **Agent evals** need v0.3 (workflows) minimum, v0.4 (libraries) for meaningful scenarios\\\\n- **Plugin (v0.6)** needs MCP server + v0.4 libraries + first library (jig-django)\\\\n- **Scan/Check (v0.7)** needs v0.4 libraries to know what to scan against\\\\n- **Infer (v0.8)** needs v0.7 scan as foundation\\\\n- **Distribution (v0.5)** is technically independent but should ship after v0.4 so the distributed binary includes library support\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## What Needs Docs Before Work Starts\\\\n\\\\n| Next workstream | Needs | Priority |\\\\n|----------------|-------|----------|\\\\n| v0.3 workflows | **Ready.** PLAN + SPEC exist. | Now |\\\\n| Agent evals (basic) | PLAN needed. Scope: harness skeleton, 3-5 scenarios, scoring, baseline comparison. | After v0.3 starts |\\\\n| MCP server (Phase 1) | PLAN needed. Scope: TypeScript stdio wrapper, 3 tools (run, vars, workflow). | After v0.3 done |\\\\n| v0.4 libraries | PLAN + SPEC needed. Most complex remaining spec work — manifest format, install logic, convention overrides, extensions. | After v0.3 done |\\\\n| v0.5 distribution | PLAN needed. Mostly CI/packaging, not code design. | After v0.4 done |\\\\n| v0.6 plugin | PLAN + SPEC needed. Depends on MCP + libraries existing first. | After v0.4 + MCP done |\\\\n| v0.7 scan/check | PLAN + SPEC needed. Algorithm design for template inversion. | After v0.4 done |\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Post-1.0 Features (Captured, Not Planned)\\\\n\\\\nFrom jig.md, for completeness. None of these have workstream docs or timeline commitments:\\\\n\\\\n| Feature | jig.md ref | Notes |\\\\n|---------|-----------|-------|\\\\n| Observation engine | lines 1852–1897 | Claude Code hook + `/jig:discover` skill |\\\\n| Recipe hooks (`pre_run`, `post_run`) | line 1991 | Run formatters/linters on generated files |\\\\n| Interactive mode | line 1992 | Human-prompted variable input (low priority) |\\\\n| Watch mode | line 1993 | Re-render on template change (authoring tool) |\\\\n| Tree-sitter integration | line 1994 | Optional AST-aware scoping (precision upgrade) |\\\\n| Library registry | line 1995 | Searchable index like crates.io for jig libraries |\\\\n| Diff preview (`--diff`) | line 1996 | Unified diff output instead of writing files |\\\\n| Undo (`jig undo`) | line 1997 | Revert last run via `.jig/history/` log |\\\\n| Template linting (`jig lint`) | line 1998 | Catch unused variables, unreachable conditionals, missing skip_if |\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:31:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_15\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"`docs/development` doesn’t exist despite AGENTS pointing to it, so I’ll proceed from the actual docs in repo (`ARCHITECTURE`, `ROADMAP`, existing workstream docs). Next I’m checking current CLI/module layout in `main.rs` to ensure the task list references concrete insertion points for the new `library` command.\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:46:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_23\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"I confirmed there is no `src/library` module yet and no library subcommands in the CLI, so this is net-new implementation planning. I’m pulling the `jig.md` v0.4 libraries section now to ground milestones and acceptance criteria against the product spec rather than only architecture summaries.\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:48:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_24\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba jig.md | sed -n '1030,1415p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"  1030\\\\tvars_file: .jig/defaults.json  # always merge these variables first\\\\n  1031\\\\tfilters:                     # register custom filters (as shell commands)\\\\n  1032\\\\t  copyright_header: \\\\\\\"cat .jig/copyright.txt\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1033\\\\t```\\\\n  1034\\\\t\\\\n  1035\\\\tThis is purely optional. jig works without any config file — recipes are fully self-contained.\\\\n  1036\\\\t\\\\n  1037\\\\t---\\\\n  1038\\\\t\\\\n  1039\\\\t## Custom Filters (Extensibility)\\\\n  1040\\\\t\\\\n  1041\\\\tBeyond the built-in filters, users can register custom filters as shell commands:\\\\n  1042\\\\t\\\\n  1043\\\\t```yaml\\\\n  1044\\\\t# In .jigrc.yaml or recipe.yaml\\\\n  1045\\\\tfilters:\\\\n  1046\\\\t  license_header: \\\\\\\"cat ./HEADER.txt\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1047\\\\t  format_date: \\\\\\\"date -d '{{ value }}' '+%Y-%m-%d'\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1048\\\\t```\\\\n  1049\\\\t\\\\n  1050\\\\tIn a template:\\\\n  1051\\\\t```jinja2\\\\n  1052\\\\t{{ \\\\\\\"2024-01-15\\\\\\\" | format_date }}\\\\n  1053\\\\t```\\\\n  1054\\\\t\\\\n  1055\\\\tThis runs the shell command with `{{ value }}` replaced by the filter input. Simple, composable, no plugin API to learn.\\\\n  1056\\\\t\\\\n  1057\\\\t---\\\\n  1058\\\\t\\\\n  1059\\\\t## Libraries: Reusable Recipe Collections\\\\n  1060\\\\t\\\\n  1061\\\\tIndividual recipes are useful. But the real leverage comes when someone packages a set of recipes for a specific framework and shares them. That's a **jig library** — a versioned collection of recipes, templates, and patch definitions for a domain.\\\\n  1062\\\\t\\\\n  1063\\\\t### What a Library Is\\\\n  1064\\\\t\\\\n  1065\\\\tA library is a directory (or published package) of recipes organized by concern:\\\\n  1066\\\\t\\\\n  1067\\\\t```\\\\n  1068\\\\tjig-django/\\\\n  1069\\\\t  jig-library.yaml            # library manifest\\\\n  1070\\\\t  model/\\\\n  1071\\\\t    add-field/\\\\n  1072\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1073\\\\t      templates/\\\\n  1074\\\\t        model_field.j2\\\\n  1075\\\\t        migration_hint.j2\\\\n  1076\\\\t    add-model/\\\\n  1077\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1078\\\\t      templates/\\\\n  1079\\\\t        model.py.j2\\\\n  1080\\\\t        admin.py.j2\\\\n  1081\\\\t        factories.py.j2\\\\n  1082\\\\t    add-index/\\\\n  1083\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1084\\\\t      templates/\\\\n  1085\\\\t        index_migration.j2\\\\n  1086\\\\t  service/\\\\n  1087\\\\t    add-method/\\\\n  1088\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1089\\\\t      templates/\\\\n  1090\\\\t        service_method.j2\\\\n  1091\\\\t        service_test.j2\\\\n  1092\\\\t    add-service/\\\\n  1093\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1094\\\\t      templates/\\\\n  1095\\\\t        service.py.j2\\\\n  1096\\\\t        service_test.py.j2\\\\n  1097\\\\t  view/\\\\n  1098\\\\t    add-endpoint/\\\\n  1099\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1100\\\\t      templates/\\\\n  1101\\\\t        view_method.j2\\\\n  1102\\\\t        url_pattern.j2\\\\n  1103\\\\t        schema_request.j2\\\\n  1104\\\\t        schema_response.j2\\\\n  1105\\\\t    add-view/\\\\n  1106\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1107\\\\t      templates/\\\\n  1108\\\\t        view.py.j2\\\\n  1109\\\\t        urls.py.j2\\\\n  1110\\\\t        schemas.py.j2\\\\n  1111\\\\t  schema/\\\\n  1112\\\\t    add-field/\\\\n  1113\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1114\\\\t      templates/\\\\n  1115\\\\t        msgspec_field.j2\\\\n  1116\\\\t        marshmallow_field.j2\\\\n  1117\\\\t  test/\\\\n  1118\\\\t    add-unit-test/\\\\n  1119\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1120\\\\t      templates/ ...\\\\n  1121\\\\t    add-integration-test/\\\\n  1122\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1123\\\\t      templates/ ...\\\\n  1124\\\\t    add-factory-field/\\\\n  1125\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1126\\\\t      templates/ ...\\\\n  1127\\\\t  admin/\\\\n  1128\\\\t    add-field/\\\\n  1129\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1130\\\\t      templates/ ...\\\\n  1131\\\\t    add-admin/\\\\n  1132\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1133\\\\t      templates/ ...\\\\n  1134\\\\t```\\\\n  1135\\\\t\\\\n  1136\\\\t### Library Manifest\\\\n  1137\\\\t\\\\n  1138\\\\t```yaml\\\\n  1139\\\\t# jig-library.yaml\\\\n  1140\\\\tname: django\\\\n  1141\\\\tversion: 0.3.0\\\\n  1142\\\\tdescription: Recipes for Django model/service/view development\\\\n  1143\\\\tframework: django\\\\n  1144\\\\tlanguage: python\\\\n  1145\\\\t\\\\n  1146\\\\t# Conventions this library assumes — the LLM reads these to map a project's\\\\n  1147\\\\t# actual layout to the library's expectations\\\\n  1148\\\\tconventions:\\\\n  1149\\\\t  models: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/models/{{ model | snakecase }}.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1150\\\\t  services: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/services/{{ model | snakecase }}_service.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1151\\\\t  views: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/views/{{ model | snakecase }}_view.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1152\\\\t  schemas: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/schemas/{{ model | snakecase }}.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1153\\\\t  tests: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/tests/test_{{ model | snakecase }}.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1154\\\\t  factories: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/tests/factories.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1155\\\\t  admin: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/admin.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1156\\\\t  urls: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/urls.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1157\\\\t\\\\n  1158\\\\t# Recipes in this library\\\\n  1159\\\\trecipes:\\\\n  1160\\\\t  model/add-field: \\\\\\\"Add a field to an existing Django model\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1161\\\\t  model/add-model: \\\\\\\"Scaffold a new Django model with admin, factory, and tests\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1162\\\\t  model/add-index: \\\\\\\"Add a database index to an existing model\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1163\\\\t  service/add-method: \\\\\\\"Add a method to an existing service class\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1164\\\\t  service/add-service: \\\\\\\"Scaffold a new service class with tests\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1165\\\\t  view/add-endpoint: \\\\\\\"Add an endpoint to an existing view\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1166\\\\t  view/add-view: \\\\\\\"Scaffold a new view with URL config and schemas\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1167\\\\t  schema/add-field: \\\\\\\"Add a field to an existing request/response schema\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1168\\\\t  test/add-unit-test: \\\\\\\"Generate a unit test for a service method\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1169\\\\t  test/add-integration-test: \\\\\\\"Generate an integration test for an endpoint\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1170\\\\t  test/add-factory-field: \\\\\\\"Add a field to a factory class\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1171\\\\t  admin/add-field: \\\\\\\"Add a field to admin list_display/fieldsets\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1172\\\\t  admin/add-admin: \\\\\\\"Scaffold admin registration for a model\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1173\\\\t\\\\n  1174\\\\t# Composable workflows — multi-recipe operations\\\\n  1175\\\\tworkflows:\\\\n  1176\\\\t  add-field:\\\\n  1177\\\\t    description: \\\\\\\"Add a field across the full stack (model → service → schema → view → admin → tests)\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1178\\\\t    steps:\\\\n  1179\\\\t      - recipe: model/add-field\\\\n  1180\\\\t      - recipe: service/add-method\\\\n  1181\\\\t        when: \\\\\\\"{{ update_service }}\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1182\\\\t      - recipe: schema/add-field\\\\n  1183\\\\t      - recipe: view/add-endpoint\\\\n  1184\\\\t        when: \\\\\\\"{{ update_view }}\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1185\\\\t      - recipe: test/add-factory-field\\\\n  1186\\\\t      - recipe: admin/add-field\\\\n  1187\\\\t\\\\n  1188\\\\t  add-endpoint:\\\\n  1189\\\\t    description: \\\\\\\"Add a new API endpoint (view → URL → schema → test)\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1190\\\\t    steps:\\\\n  1191\\\\t      - recipe: schema/add-field\\\\n  1192\\\\t        vars_map: { field_name: \\\\\\\"request_field\\\\\\\" }\\\\n  1193\\\\t      - recipe: view/add-endpoint\\\\n  1194\\\\t      - recipe: test/add-integration-test\\\\n  1195\\\\t\\\\n  1196\\\\t  scaffold-resource:\\\\n  1197\\\\t    description: \\\\\\\"Create a new model + service + view + admin from scratch\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1198\\\\t    steps:\\\\n  1199\\\\t      - recipe: model/add-model\\\\n  1200\\\\t      - recipe: service/add-service\\\\n  1201\\\\t      - recipe: view/add-view\\\\n  1202\\\\t      - recipe: admin/add-admin\\\\n  1203\\\\t```\\\\n  1204\\\\t\\\\n  1205\\\\t### Convention Mapping\\\\n  1206\\\\t\\\\n  1207\\\\tThe `conventions` block is crucial. It tells jig (and the LLM) where this library expects files to live. But real projects aren't always organized exactly the same way.\\\\n  1208\\\\t\\\\n  1209\\\\tA project can override conventions in its `.jigrc.yaml`:\\\\n  1210\\\\t\\\\n  1211\\\\t```yaml\\\\n  1212\\\\t# .jigrc.yaml\\\\n  1213\\\\tlibraries:\\\\n  1214\\\\t  django:\\\\n  1215\\\\t    conventions:\\\\n  1216\\\\t      models: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/models.py\\\\\\\"              # single-file models, not per-model files\\\\n  1217\\\\t      services: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/domain/services.py\\\\\\\"    # different service location\\\\n  1218\\\\t      schemas: \\\\\\\"{{ app }}/api/schemas.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1219\\\\t```\\\\n  1220\\\\t\\\\n  1221\\\\tThis lets the same library work across projects with different directory structures. The override is simple key-value — remap the path pattern, keep everything else.\\\\n  1222\\\\t\\\\n  1223\\\\t### Workflows: Multi-Recipe Operations\\\\n  1224\\\\t\\\\n  1225\\\\tA workflow chains multiple recipes together. This is the answer to \\\\\\\"I want to add a field and have it cascade through the whole stack.\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1226\\\\t\\\\n  1227\\\\t```bash\\\\n  1228\\\\t# Run a full-stack field addition\\\\n  1229\\\\tjig workflow django/add-field \\\\\\\\\\\\n  1230\\\\t  --vars '{\\\\\\\"app\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"model\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"Reservation\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"field_name\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"loyalty_tier\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"field_type\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"CharField\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"field_args\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"max_length=50\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"nullable\\\\\\\": true, \\\\\\\"update_service\\\\\\\": true, \\\\\\\"update_view\\\\\\\": false}'\\\\n  1231\\\\t```\\\\n  1232\\\\t\\\\n  1233\\\\tWorkflows support:\\\\n  1234\\\\t\\\\n  1235\\\\t| Feature | Syntax | Behavior |\\\\n  1236\\\\t|---------|--------|----------|\\\\n  1237\\\\t| **Conditional steps** | `when: \\\\\\\"{{ expr }}\\\\\\\"` | Skip this recipe if the expression is falsy |\\\\n  1238\\\\t| **Variable mapping** | `vars_map: {a: b}` | Rename variables when passing to a sub-recipe |\\\\n  1239\\\\t| **Variable overrides** | `vars: {key: value}` | Override specific variables for a sub-recipe |\\\\n  1240\\\\t| **Shared variables** | (default) | All top-level variables are passed through to every step |\\\\n  1241\\\\t| **Stop on error** | `on_error: stop` (default) | Stop the workflow if any recipe fails |\\\\n  1242\\\\t| **Continue on error** | `on_error: continue` | Skip the failed recipe and continue |\\\\n  1243\\\\t| **Report partial** | `on_error: report` | Continue but mark the workflow as partial in output |\\\\n  1244\\\\t\\\\n  1245\\\\t### Workflows vs. Includes\\\\n  1246\\\\t\\\\n  1247\\\\tThe earlier `includes` concept (in the Patches section) is now subsumed by workflows. The difference:\\\\n  1248\\\\t\\\\n  1249\\\\t- **`includes`** was static — always runs all sub-recipes\\\\n  1250\\\\t- **Workflows** are conditional, configurable, and report per-step results\\\\n  1251\\\\t\\\\n  1252\\\\tWorkflows are the right abstraction. `includes` is removed.\\\\n  1253\\\\t\\\\n  1254\\\\t### Installing Libraries\\\\n  1255\\\\t\\\\n  1256\\\\t```bash\\\\n  1257\\\\t# From a git repository\\\\n  1258\\\\tjig library add https://github.com/someone/jig-django\\\\n  1259\\\\t\\\\n  1260\\\\t# From a local directory (development / private libraries)\\\\n  1261\\\\tjig library add ./path/to/jig-django\\\\n  1262\\\\t\\\\n  1263\\\\t# List installed libraries\\\\n  1264\\\\tjig library list\\\\n  1265\\\\t\\\\n  1266\\\\t# Update a library\\\\n  1267\\\\tjig library update django\\\\n  1268\\\\t\\\\n  1269\\\\t# Remove a library\\\\n  1270\\\\tjig library remove django\\\\n  1271\\\\t```\\\\n  1272\\\\t\\\\n  1273\\\\tLibraries are installed to `~/.jig/libraries/` (global) or `.jig/libraries/` (project-local). Project-local takes precedence.\\\\n  1274\\\\t\\\\n  1275\\\\t### Library Discovery\\\\n  1276\\\\t\\\\n  1277\\\\t```bash\\\\n  1278\\\\t# List all recipes in an installed library\\\\n  1279\\\\tjig library recipes django\\\\n  1280\\\\t\\\\n  1281\\\\t# Show details for a specific recipe\\\\n  1282\\\\tjig library info django/model/add-field\\\\n  1283\\\\t\\\\n  1284\\\\t# List all workflows\\\\n  1285\\\\tjig library workflows django\\\\n  1286\\\\t```\\\\n  1287\\\\t\\\\n  1288\\\\t### Built-In vs. Community Libraries\\\\n  1289\\\\t\\\\n  1290\\\\tjig ships with zero built-in libraries. The tool is framework-agnostic. Libraries are where framework opinions live:\\\\n  1291\\\\t\\\\n  1292\\\\t| Library | Domain |\\\\n  1293\\\\t|---------|--------|\\\\n  1294\\\\t| `jig-django` | Django models, views, services, admin, DRF, msgspec |\\\\n  1295\\\\t| `jig-fastapi` | FastAPI routes, Pydantic models, dependency injection |\\\\n  1296\\\\t| `jig-rails` | Rails models, controllers, views, migrations, RSpec |\\\\n  1297\\\\t| `jig-nextjs` | Next.js pages, API routes, components, server actions |\\\\n  1298\\\\t| `jig-vue` | Vue 3 components, composables, Pinia stores, Vitest |\\\\n  1299\\\\t| `jig-react` | React components, hooks, tests, Storybook stories |\\\\n  1300\\\\t| `jig-express` | Express routes, middleware, controllers, Joi schemas |\\\\n  1301\\\\t| `jig-rust` | Rust structs, traits, impls, tests, Cargo features |\\\\n  1302\\\\t| `jig-go` | Go structs, handlers, middleware, table-driven tests |\\\\n  1303\\\\t| `jig-flutter` | Dart widgets, BLoC providers, repository pattern |\\\\n  1304\\\\t\\\\n  1305\\\\tAnyone can publish a library. There's no registry — it's just a git repo with a `jig-library.yaml`.\\\\n  1306\\\\t\\\\n  1307\\\\t### Libraries as Claude Code Plugins\\\\n  1308\\\\t\\\\n  1309\\\\tA jig library can simultaneously be a Claude Code plugin. The plugin provides skills that know how to use the library's recipes:\\\\n  1310\\\\t\\\\n  1311\\\\t```\\\\n  1312\\\\tjig-django/\\\\n  1313\\\\t  jig-library.yaml              # jig library manifest\\\\n  1314\\\\t  .claude-plugin/\\\\n  1315\\\\t    plugin.json                  # Claude Code plugin manifest\\\\n  1316\\\\t  skills/\\\\n  1317\\\\t    add-field/\\\\n  1318\\\\t      SKILL.md                   # \\\\\\\"Read the model, extract context, run jig workflow django/add-field\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1319\\\\t    add-endpoint/\\\\n  1320\\\\t      SKILL.md                   # \\\\\\\"Read the view, extract context, run jig workflow django/add-endpoint\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1321\\\\t    scaffold-resource/\\\\n  1322\\\\t      SKILL.md                   # \\\\\\\"Ask the user for model name + fields, run jig workflow django/scaffold-resource\\\\\\\"\\\\n  1323\\\\t  model/\\\\n  1324\\\\t    add-field/\\\\n  1325\\\\t      recipe.yaml\\\\n  1326\\\\t      templates/ ...\\\\n  1327\\\\t  service/ ...\\\\n  1328\\\\t  view/ ...\\\\n  1329\\\\t```\\\\n  1330\\\\t\\\\n  1331\\\\tThe skills wrap the recipes with LLM intelligence:\\\\n  1332\\\\t- The **skill** reads code, understands context, extracts variables, and decides which workflow to run\\\\n  1333\\\\t- The **recipe/workflow** does the deterministic file generation and patching\\\\n  1334\\\\t- The **library** provides the full collection, installable as one unit\\\\n  1335\\\\t\\\\n  1336\\\\tThis means a team can `jig library add jig-django` to get the CLI recipes, AND install the Claude Code plugin to get the LLM-powered skills that invoke them.\\\\n  1337\\\\t\\\\n  1338\\\\t### Project-Specific Overrides and Extensions\\\\n  1339\\\\t\\\\n  1340\\\\tTeams can extend a library with their own recipes without forking it:\\\\n  1341\\\\t\\\\n  1342\\\\t```\\\\n  1343\\\\tmy-project/\\\\n  1344\\\\t  .jig/\\\\n  1345\\\\t    libraries/\\\\n  1346\\\\t      django/          # installed library (read-only)\\\\n  1347\\\\t    overrides/\\\\n  1348\\\\t      django/\\\\n  1349\\\\t        model/\\\\n  1350\\\\t          add-field/\\\\n  1351\\\\t            templates/\\\\n  1352\\\\t              model_field.j2    # override just this one template\\\\n  1353\\\\t    extensions/\\\\n  1354\\\\t      django/\\\\n  1355\\\\t        model/\\\\n  1356\\\\t          add-audit-fields/\\\\n  1357\\\\t            recipe.yaml         # new recipe that extends the library\\\\n  1358\\\\t            templates/ ...\\\\n  1359\\\\t```\\\\n  1360\\\\t\\\\n  1361\\\\t- **Overrides** replace a single template in a library recipe (your team uses a different field style)\\\\n  1362\\\\t- **Extensions** add new recipes namespaced under the library (your team has audit field patterns unique to your codebase)\\\\n  1363\\\\t\\\\n  1364\\\\tBoth are project-local and version-controlled. The upstream library can be updated without conflicts.\\\\n  1365\\\\t\\\\n  1366\\\\t### The Vision: Framework Ecosystem\\\\n  1367\\\\t\\\\n  1368\\\\tThe end state is:\\\\n  1369\\\\t\\\\n  1370\\\\t1. **jig** is the engine — small, fast, framework-agnostic\\\\n  1371\\\\t2. **Libraries** are where the framework knowledge lives — community-maintained, versioned, overridable\\\\n  1372\\\\t3. **Claude Code plugins** wrap libraries with LLM intelligence — the skill reads code and decides what to do, the recipe handles the mechanical work\\\\n  1373\\\\t4. **Project overrides** customize libraries for your team's conventions — no forks, no divergence\\\\n  1374\\\\t\\\\n  1375\\\\tA new developer joins a Django team. They install the jig-django Claude Code plugin. Now they can say \\\\\\\"add a field called loyalty_tier to Reservation\\\\\\\" and get a consistent, team-compliant, multi-file change — without the LLM having to learn the team's patterns from scratch every time.\\\\n  1376\\\\t\\\\n  1377\\\\t---\\\\n  1378\\\\t\\\\n  1379\\\\t## Thinking Bigger: What Else Composes?\\\\n  1380\\\\t\\\\n  1381\\\\tThe Django example is the most obvious case, but the pattern applies anywhere you have layered architecture with predictable cross-cutting changes:\\\\n  1382\\\\t\\\\n  1383\\\\t### Frontend (Vue/React/Angular)\\\\n  1384\\\\t\\\\n  1385\\\\tAdding a new prop to a component:\\\\n  1386\\\\t- Component file (add prop declaration)\\\\n  1387\\\\t- Parent components (pass the new prop at call sites)\\\\n  1388\\\\t- Types file (add to interface)\\\\n  1389\\\\t- Tests (add to mock props, add assertions)\\\\n  1390\\\\t- Storybook (add to stories, add knob)\\\\n  1391\\\\t\\\\n  1392\\\\tAdding a new store field:\\\\n  1393\\\\t- Store (add state, getter, action, mutation)\\\\n  1394\\\\t- Components (add computed property or composable usage)\\\\n  1395\\\\t- API layer (add to request/response types)\\\\n  1396\\\\t- Tests (add to store tests, component tests)\\\\n  1397\\\\t\\\\n  1398\\\\t### API Layer\\\\n  1399\\\\t\\\\n  1400\\\\tAdding a new endpoint:\\\\n  1401\\\\t- Route definition\\\\n  1402\\\\t- Controller/handler\\\\n  1403\\\\t- Request validation schema\\\\n  1404\\\\t- Response serialization schema\\\\n  1405\\\\t- Middleware registration\\\\n  1406\\\\t- OpenAPI/Swagger docs\\\\n  1407\\\\t- Client SDK regeneration\\\\n  1408\\\\t- Integration tests\\\\n  1409\\\\t\\\\n  1410\\\\t### Infrastructure\\\\n  1411\\\\t\\\\n  1412\\\\tAdding a new service:\\\\n  1413\\\\t- Dockerfile\\\\n  1414\\\\t- Docker Compose service\\\\n  1415\\\\t- Process Compose config\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:51:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_26\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/workflows/SHARED-CONTEXT.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# SHARED-CONTEXT.md\\\\n\\\\n> Workstream: workflows\\\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\\\n\\\\n## Purpose\\\\n\\\\nAdd multi-recipe orchestration to jig (v0.3). Workflows chain multiple recipes into a single `jig workflow` invocation with conditional steps, variable mapping, and configurable error handling. This is the composition layer that enables cross-file operations like \\\\\\\"add a field and propagate it through model, service, schema, admin, and tests.\\\\\\\"\\\\n\\\\n## Current State\\\\n\\\\n- **Complete** (2026-04-04)\\\\n- All 5 phases implemented: parsing, variable resolution, execution engine, CLI command, integration tests\\\\n- 343 tests passing (329 unit + 2 CLI + 12 integration), `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- 25 integration fixtures (18 success-path, 7 error-case)\\\\n- 37 unit tests in `src/workflow.rs` covering parsing, variable resolution, when evaluation, execution\\\\n- Code review completed — 1 critical, 4 major, 6 minor findings identified. **Review findings not yet fixed in code** (commit 76d826f only added review artifacts)\\\\n\\\\n## Decisions Made\\\\n\\\\n### D-1: Workflow files are standalone YAML, distinct from recipes\\\\nWorkflows have `steps:`, recipes have `files:`. Auto-detection in `jig validate` and `jig vars` checks for these keys. A file with both is an error. This keeps the format clean and avoids overloading the recipe concept.\\\\n\\\\n### D-2: Recipe paths resolve relative to the workflow file\\\\nConsistent with I-7 (templates live with the consumer). A workflow + its recipe subdirectories form a self-contained bundle that can be moved or shared.\\\\n\\\\n### D-3: `when` uses Jinja2 rendering + string truthiness\\\\nNo new expression language. Render the `when` template with workflow-level variables. The rendered string is falsy if it's empty, \\\\\\\"false\\\\\\\" (case-insensitive), or \\\\\\\"0\\\\\\\". Everything else is truthy. This covers minijinja's boolean rendering (\\\\\\\"false\\\\\\\") and zero.\\\\n\\\\n### D-4: `when` evaluates against workflow-level variables (not step-level)\\\\nThe condition determines \\\\\\\"should this step run at all\\\\\\\" — a workflow-level concern. vars_map and vars modify variables for the recipe, not for the condition. This prevents circular dependencies.\\\\n\\\\n### D-5: vars_map is rename, not copy\\\\n`{field_name: request_field}` means the step sees `request_field` but NOT `field_name`. The spec says \\\\\\\"rename.\\\\\\\" If you need both, add the original to `vars` explicitly.\\\\n\\\\n### D-6: vars_map renamings are simultaneous\\\\n`{a: b, b: c}` means original `a` → `b` and original `b` → `c`. Not chained (`a` → `b` → `c`). Prevents order-dependent surprises.\\\\n\\\\n### D-7: No new exit codes\\\\nWorkflow errors map to existing codes: 1 (validation), 2 (rendering), 3 (file ops), 4 (variables). No exit code 5+. Respects I-5 (stable exit codes).\\\\n\\\\n### D-8: Single ExecutionContext spans all steps\\\\nOne `base_dir`, one `force` flag, one `virtual_files` map across the entire workflow. Enables dry-run chaining where step N+1 reads files \\\\\\\"created\\\\\\\" by step N.\\\\n\\\\n### D-9: Step variable validation happens at execution time, not upfront\\\\nConditional steps might reference variables that only exist when the condition is true. Upfront validation would produce false errors for skipped steps.\\\\n\\\\n### D-10: No nested workflows, no inter-step variable passing\\\\nSteps reference recipes only (not other workflows). Variables flow from workflow to steps, not between steps. Steps communicate through the filesystem. Keeps v0.3 scope manageable.\\\\n\\\\n### D-11: Duplicate `run_recipe()` rather than refactor `cmd_run` (implementation decision)\\\\nRather than refactoring the working `cmd_run` pipeline in `main.rs`, implementation created a parallel `run_recipe()` in `workflow.rs` that duplicates the rendering logic. This avoided risk of breaking the existing recipe execution path during workflow development, but creates maintenance burden (review finding M4). Should be consolidated before v0.4.\\\\n\\\\n### D-12: Duplicate vars_map targets rejected at parse time (implementation decision)\\\\n`load_workflow()` validates that a single step's `vars_map` doesn't map two source keys to the same target. This was an open question in planning (what happens with `{a: c, b: c}`?). Rather than last-write-wins, it's now a validation error at parse time.\\\\n\\\\n## Patterns Established\\\\n\\\\n- **`run_recipe()` extracted as independent function in `workflow.rs`.** Rather than refactoring `cmd_run`, the implementation created a parallel `run_recipe()` function (`workflow.rs:551-619`) that handles recipe loading, template rendering, and operation execution without CLI concerns. This avoids touching the working `cmd_run` code path but creates code duplication (see review finding M4).\\\\n\\\\n- **`detect_file_type()` as the auto-detection entry point.** A single function (`workflow.rs:151-198`) reads YAML, checks for `steps` vs `files` keys, and returns `FileType::Workflow` or `FileType::Recipe`. Used in three places: `cmd_validate`, `cmd_vars`, and `cmd_workflow` (guard against recipe files). This pattern should be reused by libraries (v0.4) if they add a new file type.\\\\n\\\\n- **`compute_workflow_exit_code()` centralizes exit code logic.** Moved exit code computation out of the execution loop into a dedicated function in `main.rs` (lines 582-607). Takes the workflow's on_error mode and the step results, returns the correct exit code. Prevents exit-code logic from leaking into the execution engine.\\\\n\\\\n- **Workflow fixtures use step subdirectories.** Each workflow fixture has `step1/`, `step2/`, etc. directories containing full recipe bundles (recipe.yaml + templates/). The workflow.yaml references these via relative paths. This mirrors the intended real-world layout.\\\\n\\\\n- **`RawWorkflow` intermediate struct for deserialization.** Serde deserializes into `RawWorkflow` (unresolved paths), then `load_workflow()` resolves paths and validates recipes to produce the final `Workflow` struct. This separates parsing from validation — same pattern as `Recipe::load()`.\\\\n\\\\n- **Simultaneous vars_map via snapshot-remove-insert.** `resolve_step_variables()` (lines 361-401) snapshots original values, removes mapped keys, then inserts renamed values. This prevents chaining without needing a separate temporary map.\\\\n\\\\n## Known Issues / Tech Debt\\\\n\\\\n### From v0.3 code review (unfixed)\\\\n\\\\n**Critical:**\\\\n- **C1: `extract_rendered_from_error` returns error description, not rendered template content.** `workflow.rs:621-628` — `se.what.clone()` returns the human-readable error message as `rendered_content` in JSON output. The spec requires the actual rendered template so LLM callers can fall back to manual editing. Fix: extract from `OpResult::Error` in `partial_results` instead.\\\\n\\\\n**Major:**\\\\n- **M1: `format_workflow_json` status logic breaks with per-step `on_error` overrides.** `output.rs:300-302` — Only checks workflow-level `on_error`, not step-level. When a step uses `on_error: report` but workflow default is `stop`, JSON status becomes `\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"` instead of `\\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\"` despite exit code 3. Fix: derive status from exit_code, not on_error mode.\\\\n- **M2: Workflow steps silently accept workflow YAML files as recipes.** `workflow.rs:279` — `Recipe::load` succeeds on workflow files because `files` defaults to `[]` via `#[serde(default)]` and `steps` is silently ignored. A typo referencing another workflow succeeds with 0 operations. Fix: call `detect_file_type` on each step's recipe and reject `FileType::Workflow`.\\\\n- **M3: Workflow early-error JSON drops multi-error variable validation.** `main.rs:477` — `e.structured_error()` returns only the first error. JSON output loses all but the first validation error; human stderr correctly shows all. Fix: use `e.structured_errors()` and emit all.\\\\n- **M4: `cmd_run` and `run_recipe` are divergent copies of the same rendering pipeline.** `main.rs:335-427` vs `workflow.rs:551-618` — Template rendering, path rendering, and skip_if rendering logic is duplicated with subtly different error handling. Fix: refactor `cmd_run` to call `run_recipe`.\\\\n\\\\n**Minor:**\\\\n- **m1: Determinism unit test is trivially weak.** `workflow.rs:1215-1233` — Compares `steps.len()` stringified, not actual output. Integration test is much stronger; this unit test adds no value.\\\\n- **m2: `load_workflow` reports only first validation error.** `workflow.rs:303-306` — Errors are collected but only the first is returned. Should report all.\\\\n- **m3: No integration test for AC-7.8** (recipe passed to `jig workflow`). Code handles it but no regression fixture exists.\\\\n- **m4: Early error JSON creates misleading output.** `main.rs:473-507` — Pre-execution errors produce `\\\\\\\"steps\\\\\\\": [{\\\\\\\"recipe\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"}]` with empty recipe string.\\\\n- **m5: Dry-run workflow human output says \\\\\\\"wrote\\\\\\\" instead of \\\\\\\"would write\\\\\\\".** `output.rs:406,436` — `format_human(operations, false, verbose)` hardcodes `false` for `dry_run` instead of forwarding actual value.\\\\n- **m6: `resolve_step_variables` panics on non-object JSON.** `workflow.rs:369,394` — `.unwrap()` on `as_object_mut()`. Safe in practice (internal callers always provide objects) but the function is `pub`.\\\\n\\\\n### Pre-existing (from v0.2, still open)\\\\n- `write_back` silently swallows write errors in `patch.rs` and `replace.rs` — **Critical**\\\\n- `Position::Sorted` is a stub (`todo!()` panic) — **Critical**\\\\n- Byte/char index mismatch in `delimiter.rs:87` (multi-byte UTF-8 panics) — **Critical**\\\\n- 11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures from replace-patch still missing\\\\n\\\\n## File Ownership\\\\n\\\\n| File | Phase | What Changed |\\\\n|------|-------|--------------|\\\\n| `src/workflow.rs` | 1-3 | **New** (1234 lines). Workflow parsing, variable resolution, when evaluation, execution engine, `run_recipe()` |\\\\n| `src/main.rs` | 1, 4 | **Modified.** Added `Workflow` command variant, `cmd_workflow()`, `compute_workflow_exit_code()`, auto-detection in `cmd_validate` and `cmd_vars` |\\\\n| `src/output.rs` | 4 | **Modified.** Added `format_workflow_json()`, `format_workflow_human()`, `build_workflow_validate_json()` |\\\\n| `src/recipe.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** `Recipe::load()` used directly from `workflow.rs`; no public API changes needed |\\\\n| `src/variables.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** Reused as-is for workflow variable validation |\\\\n| `src/renderer.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** Reused for `when` expression rendering |\\\\n| `src/operations/mod.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** Reused for operation execution within each step |\\\\n| `src/error.rs` | — | **Unchanged.** No new error types needed |\\\\n| `tests/fixtures/workflow-*` | 5 | **New.** 18 success-path fixture directories |\\\\n| `tests/fixtures/error-workflow-*` | 5 | **New.** 7 error-case fixture directories |\\\\n| `tests/integration.rs` | 5 | **Modified.** Added workflow fixture support, determinism and idempotency tests |\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/libraries/exec/codex-plan-20260404-213100.md:52:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_25\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"sed -n '1,260p' docs/workstreams/core-engine/PLAN.md\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"# PLAN.md\\\\n\\\\n> Workstream: core-engine\\\\n> Last updated: 2026-04-04\\\\n> Status: Complete\\\\n\\\\n## Objective\\\\n\\\\nDeliver the minimum viable jig CLI: parse recipes, validate variables, render Jinja2 templates, execute create and inject file operations, and report structured results. This is the v0.1 feature set — the smallest thing that lets an LLM call `jig run` to produce files from a recipe instead of re-deriving boilerplate.\\\\n\\\\nCovers ARCHITECTURE.md Phases A through E (skeleton, rendering, create, inject, integration tests).\\\\n\\\\n## Phases\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 1: Skeleton + Recipe Parsing\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\nTraces to: FR-1, FR-7 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-5\\\\n\\\\nBootstrap the Rust crate, wire up clap, and make recipe parsing work end-to-end. After this phase, `jig validate` and `jig vars` are functional commands.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 1.1: Cargo.toml with dependencies (serde, serde_yaml, serde_json, clap, thiserror, regex, indexmap)\\\\n- [x] 1.2: `src/error.rs` — StructuredError struct (what/where/why/hint), JigError enum wrapping StructuredError with exit code mapping (0-4)\\\\n- [x] 1.3: `src/recipe.rs` — Recipe, VariableDecl, VarType, FileOp structs with serde deserialization; template path resolution relative to recipe location; structural validation (missing fields, missing template files); unknown operation type detection with clear error message; custom deserialization for FileOp — use intermediate flat struct with optional fields, then validate and convert to typed enum (reject when more than one of `to`/`inject`/`replace`/`patch` is present, or none is present; if `replace` or `patch` is present, emit AC-1.10 'not supported in v0.1' error, reject when multiple inject modes (after/before/prepend/append) are specified); compile-check regex patterns in after/before fields during recipe validation\\\\n- [x] 1.4: `src/variables.rs` — Variable merging and type-checking scaffolding (types imported from recipe.rs, validation logic added in Phase 2)\\\\n- [x] 1.5: `src/main.rs` — clap CLI with `validate` and `vars` subcommands, `#[command(version)]`; wire recipe parsing; map errors to exit codes; `jig validate` outputs summary to stderr (variable count, operation types); with `--json`, outputs structured JSON to stdout\\\\n- [x] 1.6: Unit tests for recipe parsing — valid recipe, missing fields, malformed YAML, missing template files, optional metadata\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- `jig validate recipe.yaml` parses the example recipe from jig.md and exits 0\\\\n- `jig validate bad.yaml` exits 1 with a clear error naming the problem\\\\n- `jig vars recipe.yaml` outputs JSON matching the SPEC schema (type, required, default, description)\\\\n- Template paths resolve relative to recipe file, not cwd\\\\n- AC-1.1 through AC-1.15 have corresponding unit tests (AC-1.11 empty files array tested in Phase 3 when `jig run` exists)\\\\n- `jig validate` output includes variable names and operation types\\\\n- AC-N5.1 exit codes are correct for recipe validation errors\\\\n\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\n- `Cargo.toml`\\\\n- `src/main.rs`\\\\n- `src/recipe.rs`\\\\n- `src/variables.rs` (types only)\\\\n- `src/error.rs`\\\\n\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\n- regex crate (needed at parse time for compile-checking after/before patterns)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 2: Variable Validation + Template Rendering\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\nTraces to: FR-2, FR-3, FR-7 (partial), NFR-1 (partial)\\\\n\\\\nFull variable pipeline (parse, merge, type-check) and template rendering with all 13 built-in filters. After this phase, `jig render` works.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 2.1: `src/variables.rs` — full validation: parse JSON from --vars/--vars-file/--vars-stdin; merge with precedence (defaults < file < stdin < inline); type-check against declarations; required field enforcement; enum validation; array item type validation\\\\n- [x] 2.2: `src/filters.rs` — all 13 built-in filters registered with minijinja: snakecase, camelcase, pascalcase, kebabcase, upper, lower, capitalize, replace, pluralize, singularize, quote, indent, join\\\\n- [x] 2.3: `src/renderer.rs` — minijinja Environment setup; template loading from recipe-relative paths; filter registration; render with variables context; structured error on undefined variable (with \\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\" via edit distance) and syntax errors (with file + line)\\\\n- [x] 2.4: Wire `render` subcommand in main.rs with --vars, --vars-file, --vars-stdin, --to options. For `jig render`, create a standalone Environment with filters registered but no template directory — load the template file directly by path via `render_str()` or equivalent\\\\n- [x] 2.5: Unit tests for variable validation — every VarType, required missing, default fallback, enum rejection, array item mismatch, merge precedence\\\\n- [x] 2.6: Unit tests + insta snapshot tests for all 13 filters and template rendering (conditionals, loops, comments, raw blocks, undefined vars, syntax errors)\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- All 13 filters produce correct output per AC-3.4 through AC-3.14\\\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '{\\\\\\\"class_name\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"BookingService\\\\\\\"}'` renders to stdout\\\\n- Type mismatch exits 4 with expected vs actual type (AC-2.7)\\\\n- Missing required variable exits 4 with variable name and hint (AC-2.5)\\\\n- Merge precedence: inline --vars wins over --vars-stdin wins over --vars-file wins over defaults (AC-2.4)\\\\n- Undefined template variable exits 2 with \\\\\\\"did you mean?\\\\\\\" hint (AC-3.17)\\\\n- Template syntax error exits 2 with file path and line number (AC-3.18)\\\\n- Same inputs produce byte-identical output across runs (AC-N1.1)\\\\n- `jig render template.j2 --vars '...' --to output.txt` writes to file instead of stdout (AC-7.4)\\\\n- Multiple validation errors accumulated and reported together (AC-2.11)\\\\n\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\n- `src/variables.rs` (full implementation)\\\\n- `src/filters.rs`\\\\n- `src/renderer.rs`\\\\n\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\n- heck crate for case conversions\\\\n- minijinja crate for template rendering\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 3: Create Operation + Output Formatting\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\nTraces to: FR-4, FR-6, FR-7 (complete), NFR-2 (partial), NFR-4, NFR-6\\\\n\\\\nFile creation with templated output paths, directory auto-creation, skip_if_exists, and dual-stream output (JSON stdout / human stderr). After this phase, `jig run` works for create-only recipes.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 3.1: `src/operations/mod.rs` — ExecutionContext struct (base_dir, dry_run, force, virtual_files for dry-run state); OpResult enum (Success with action/path/lines/location, Skip, Error); operation dispatch (create only initially). In dry-run mode, create ops populate virtual_files instead of writing to disk.\\\\n- [x] 3.2: `src/operations/create.rs` — render `to` path as template; create parent directories; write rendered content; skip_if_exists logic; --force override; --base-dir path resolution\\\\n- [x] 3.3: `src/output.rs` — OutputMode enum (Json/Human/Quiet); TTY auto-detection; JSON serialization of operations array + files_written/files_skipped; track per-path write/skip status for files_written and files_skipped arrays; colored human output to stderr; --verbose includes rendered content\\\\n- [x] 3.4: Wire `run` subcommand in main.rs with --dry-run, --json, --quiet, --force, --base-dir, --verbose; render all template contents AND templated path fields (`to`, `inject`, `skip_if`) into a Vec before executing any operation — rendering failures abort before any file is touched; execute operations in declaration order; fail-fast on operation errors — stop execution on first failure\\\\n- [x] 3.5: Unit tests for create operation — happy path, skip_if_exists, force overwrite, templated paths, directory creation, file-already-exists error\\\\n- [x] 3.6: Unit tests for output formatting — JSON schema matches SPEC, human output, quiet mode, verbose mode, dry-run\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- `jig run recipe.yaml --vars '...'` creates files at templated paths (AC-4.1, AC-4.2)\\\\n- Parent directories created automatically (AC-4.3)\\\\n- skip_if_exists: true skips existing files with action:\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\" (AC-4.4)\\\\n- Default (skip_if_exists: false) errors on existing file without --force (AC-4.5)\\\\n- --force overwrites regardless (AC-4.6)\\\\n- --base-dir changes output root (AC-4.7)\\\\n- --dry-run produces output but writes nothing (AC-6.8)\\\\n- JSON output when piped, human output when TTY (AC-6.1, AC-6.2)\\\\n- --json forces JSON (AC-6.3), --quiet suppresses non-errors (AC-6.4)\\\\n- Operations execute in declaration order (AC-N6.1)\\\\n- Second run with skip_if_exists: true reports all skips (AC-N2.1)\\\\n\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs`\\\\n- `src/operations/create.rs`\\\\n- `src/output.rs`\\\\n- `src/main.rs` (run subcommand)\\\\n\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\n- owo-colors crate for terminal coloring\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 4: Inject Operation\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\nTraces to: FR-5, NFR-2 (complete), NFR-6\\\\n\\\\nContent injection into existing files via regex anchoring. All injection modes: after, before, prepend, append, with at:first/last match selection and skip_if idempotency. This completes the v0.1 operation set.\\\\n\\\\n#### Milestones\\\\n- [x] 4.1: `src/operations/inject.rs` — read target file (or from virtual_files if target created in same dry-run); skip_if string search (skip_if string search must check virtual_files content when target was created in same dry-run); regex anchor matching (after/before); at:first/at:last match selection; prepend/append modes; render inject path as template; write modified content; inject ops update virtual_files with post-injection content for subsequent operations\\\\n- [x] 4.2: Wire inject dispatch into operations/mod.rs\\\\n- [x] 4.3: Unit tests for every injection mode — after (first match), after (last match), before, prepend, append, skip_if, missing target file error, regex no-match error, templated inject path\\\\n- [x] 4.4: Integration test: recipe with create + inject in same run (create file, then inject into it — tests ordered execution AC-N6.2)\\\\n\\\\n#### Validation Criteria\\\\n- after: content on line after first match (AC-5.1)\\\\n- before: content on line before first match (AC-5.2)\\\\n- prepend: content at start of file (AC-5.3)\\\\n- append: content at end of file (AC-5.4)\\\\n- at:last uses last match (AC-5.5), at:first (default) uses first (AC-5.6)\\\\n- skip_if: skips when string found in file, reports action:\\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\" (AC-5.7)\\\\n- Regex no-match exits 3 with pattern, file path, hint (AC-5.8)\\\\n- Missing target file exits 3 (AC-5.9)\\\\n- Inject path renders as template (AC-5.11)\\\\n- Create-then-inject in same recipe works (AC-N6.2)\\\\n- Second run with skip_if shows all skips — no duplicate content (AC-N2.2)\\\\n- Inject success reports action:\\\\\\\"inject\\\\\\\" with path, location, line count (AC-5.10)\\\\n- at field ignored when prepend/append specified; after/before without regex exits 1 (AC-5.12, AC-5.13)\\\\n- Invalid regex pattern in after/before exits 1 (AC-5.14)\\\\n- Multiple inject modes (after+before etc.) specified exits 1 (AC-5.15)\\\\n- --force has no effect on inject operations (AC-5.16)\\\\n\\\\n#### Key Files\\\\n- `src/operations/inject.rs`\\\\n- `src/operations/mod.rs` (dispatch update)\\\\n\\\\n#### Dependencies\\\\n- regex crate\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 5: Integration Test Framework\\\\nStatus: Complete\\\\nTraces to: All FRs and NFRs (validation layer)\\\\n\\\\nFixture-based integration test harness that makes adding new test cases a matter of adding a directory. Snapshot tests for all output formats.
