PLAN.md\\n./docs/workstreams/core-engine/tasks/phase-5/CONTEXT.md:31:- Every `TEST-*` ID in SPEC.md has a corresponding test function named `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}` or `spec::nfr{N}::ac_n{N}_{M}`\\ndocs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:54:This workstream owns the entire `src/` tree for v0.1:\\n./docs/workstreams/core-engine/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:54:This workstream owns the entire `src/` tree for v0.1:\\n./docs/workstreams/replace-patch/reviews/code-review-claude-20260404-165354.md:1:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"result\\\",\\\"subtype\\\":\\\"success\\\",\\\"is_error\\\":false,\\\"duration_ms\\\":262982,\\\"duration_api_ms\\\":510728,\\\"num_turns\\\":26,\\\"result\\\":\\\"Now I have a comprehensive view of the implementation. Let me compose the review.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Verdict: Request Changes**\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Critical (must fix before merge)\\\\n\\\\n**C1. `delimiter.rs:87` — Byte index used where char index is tracked: potential panic on non-ASCII**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet between = &lines[open_line][open_col + 1..scanner.col];\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n`CharScanner` tracks `col` in *character* positions (via `chars().nth()`, `chars().count()`), but this line slices the string with byte indices. For any file containing multi-byte UTF-8 characters (comments in Chinese/Japanese, emoji in strings, accented variable names), this will panic with `byte index N is not a char boundary`. The fix is to use `char_indices()` to convert char offsets to byte offsets before slicing.\\\\n\\\\n**C2. `operations/replace.rs:273-281` and `operations/patch.rs:224-231` — `write_back` silently swallows write errors**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet _ = std::fs::write(target, new_content);\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nIn the fallback paths (replace) and the main path (patch), disk write failures are silently ignored. The spec (AC-2.14, AC-7.11) requires exit code 3 with path, permission error, and rendered content. The main replace path at line 106 correctly returns an error on write failure, but `write_back()` used by fallback-append/prepend and *all* of patch does not. This means a permission-denied error during patch will return `OpResult::Success` — a silent corruption that violates I-10 (never silently produce wrong output).\\\\n\\\\n**C3. `scope/position.rs:169-185` — `Position::Sorted` is a stub, not an implementation**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn resolve_sorted(...) -> Result<PositionResult, StructuredError> {\\\\n    // For sorted, we don't have the content to sort against...\\\\n    let insertion = scope.closing_line.unwrap_or(scope.end_line + 1);\\\\n    Ok(PositionResult { insertion_line: insertion, ... })\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe spec (AC-5.7) requires: \\\\\\\"find the alphabetically correct insertion point among sibling lines at the same indentation level within the scope.\\\\\\\" This just inserts at end-of-scope. The `Sorted` variant is parsed and accepted as valid by the recipe parser, so a user can write `position: sorted` and get silently wrong behavior (appended, not sorted). Either implement it or reject it at parse time with a clear \\\\\\\"not yet implemented\\\\\\\" error.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Major (should fix)\\\\n\\\\n**M1. `scope/position.rs:62` — `AfterLastMethod` regex misses common Rust qualifiers**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet method_re = Regex::new(r\\\\\\\"^\\\\\\\\s+(pub\\\\\\\\s+)?(async\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\").unwrap();\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis misses `const fn`, `unsafe fn`, `pub(crate) fn`, `pub unsafe async fn`, `extern \\\\\\\"C\\\\\\\" fn`, and `pub const fn`. For a tool targeting Rust as a first-class citizen, this is a significant gap. A more robust pattern: `r\\\\\\\"^\\\\\\\\s+(pub(\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\([^)]*\\\\\\\\))?\\\\\\\\s+)?(const\\\\\\\\s+)?(unsafe\\\\\\\\s+)?(async\\\\\\\\s+)?(extern\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"[^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]*\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\"`.\\\\n\\\\n**M2. `scope/position.rs:40` — `AfterLastField` regex `^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` requires leading whitespace**\\\\n\\\\nThe `\\\\\\\\s+` at the start means this never matches fields at indent level 0 (e.g., top-level struct fields in Go `Name string`, module-level Python `CONSTANT = 42`). Should be `^\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` if the intent is to work at any indentation, or the assumption should be documented.\\\\n\\\\n**M3. 11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures are missing (42% gap)**\\\\n\\\\nMissing fixtures:\\\\n- `patch-function-body`, `patch-brackets`, `patch-function-signature`, `patch-find-narrowing`\\\\n- `patch-sorted`, `patch-indent-matching`, `patch-nested-delimiters`, `patch-string-delimiters`\\\\n- `error-patch-unbalanced`, `error-patch-find-missing`\\\\n- `combined-patch-idempotent`\\\\n\\\\nUnit tests cover most of these behaviors, but the spec explicitly requires fixture-level integration tests for each. The replace side is complete (7/7). The patch side is 4/11.\\\\n\\\\n**M4. `scope/delimiter.rs:171` — `peek_next` returns `None` when next line is empty**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\n} else if self.line + 1 < self.lines.len() && !self.lines[self.line + 1].is_empty() {\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nIf the character after the current position is on a blank line (common in real code — blank lines between struct fields), `peek_next` returns `None`. This breaks comment detection (`//`, `/*`) when `//` straddles a line boundary (unlikely but possible) and more importantly causes the scanner to not recognize patterns that span across blank lines correctly.\\\\n\\\\n**M5. No `ScopeDiagnostics` struct or verbose scope detail output (FR-8 incomplete)**\\\\n\\\\nThe spec (AC-8.1 through AC-8.5) requires verbose mode to include a `scope_diagnostics` object in JSON output with `anchor_line`, `scope_start`, `scope_end`, `insertion_line`, `find_match_line`, `position_fallback`. Currently verbose only adds the `rendered_content` string. The location string encodes some of this information as text, but the structured diagnostic object specified in the JSON Output Extensions section of the spec is absent.\\\\n\\\\n**M6. `operations/patch.rs:160-162` — Location string uses `Debug` formatting, producing `classbody` instead of `class_body`**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet scope_name = format!(\\\\\\\"{:?}\\\\\\\", anchor.scope).to_lowercase();\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n`#[derive(Debug)]` on `ScopeType::ClassBody` produces `\\\\\\\"ClassBody\\\\\\\"`, which `.to_lowercase()` turns into `\\\\\\\"classbody\\\\\\\"`. The spec's JSON Output Extensions show `\\\\\\\"class_body(^class Foo):after_last_field\\\\\\\"`. Similarly `AfterLastField` becomes `\\\\\\\"afterlastfield\\\\\\\"` instead of `\\\\\\\"after_last_field\\\\\\\"`. This affects both machine-parseable output and human readability. Implement `Display` for `ScopeType` and `Position` with snake_case output.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Minor (nice to fix)\\\\n\\\\n**m1. `scope/delimiter.rs` — `chars().count()` called on every `normalize()`, `peek()`, `advance()`**\\\\n\\\\n`self.lines[self.line].chars().count()` is O(n) per call. In the tight scanner loop, this means O(n^2) over a line. Cache the line length or use `len()` if sticking to ASCII-only files (which the byte-index bug in C1 already assumes).\\\\n\\\\n**m2. `operations/patch.rs:48` — `skip_if` uses substring match, not pattern match**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nif file_content.contains(skip_str) {\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nA `skip_if` value of `\\\\\\\"import\\\\\\\"` would match `\\\\\\\"important\\\\\\\"`. The inject operation uses the same approach, so this is consistent, but it's worth noting that this is a substring check, not a whole-line or word-boundary check. AC-7.2 says \\\\\\\"search for the rendered string\\\\\\\" so technically compliant, but a rendered value like `\\\\\\\"x\\\\\\\"` would skip on nearly any file.\\\\n\\\\n**m3. `operations/replace.rs:162-163` — `Regex::new().expect()` at runtime instead of using pre-compiled regex**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet start_re = Regex::new(start_pattern).expect(\\\\\\\"regex validated at parse time\\\\\\\");\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe recipe parser validates regex syntax at parse time, so the `.expect()` should never fire. But this compiles the regex fresh for every replace execution. Pre-compile during recipe parsing and pass the compiled `Regex` through to avoid the overhead (minor perf, but relevant since jig may be called dozens of times per LLM session per I-6).\\\\n\\\\n**m4. `scope/indent.rs` — `find_body_start` control flow is confusing**\\\\n\\\\nThe nested `if` / `continue` / `break` pattern is hard to follow. It works correctly but a refactor to use `match` or clearer early returns would improve maintainability.\\\\n\\\\n**m5. `operations/mod.rs:38-41` — `resolve_path` does no path traversal validation**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn resolve_path(&self, relative: &str) -> PathBuf {\\\\n    self.base_dir.join(relative)\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nA recipe with `replace: \\\\\\\"../../etc/passwd\\\\\\\"` would resolve outside `base_dir`. This isn't a security issue per se (jig is a build tool run by trusted input), but it violates the principle of least surprise and could cause hard-to-debug errors.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Strengths\\\\n\\\\n- **Error-with-rendered-content contract is solid.** Every error path in both replace and patch includes the rendered template content. The I-10 invariant (never waste rendering work) is well-honored.\\\\n\\\\n- **Virtual files chaining is clean.** The virtual_files HashMap for dry-run and cross-operation chaining is simple, correct, and tested. Create → Replace → Patch chains work in both dry and wet modes.\\\\n\\\\n- **Recipe parsing is thorough.** All 19 FR-1 acceptance criteria are covered with explicit validation: regex compile-check, mutual exclusivity, default values, invalid enum rejection. The parser is the strongest part of the implementation.\\\\n\\\\n- **Scope detection architecture is well-separated.** `scope/` has zero dependency on the operation pipeline. It takes `&[&str]` and returns structs. This makes it trivially unit-testable and easy to reason about in isolation.\\\\n\\\\n- **Replace operation is complete.** All 14 AC-2.x criteria are implemented with matching fixtures. The between/pattern/fallback matrix is fully tested. This is merge-ready modulo the `write_back` error swallowing (C2).\\\\n\\\\n- **284 tests passing, zero failures.** The test suite is substantial and covers the critical paths well. The fixture auto-discovery mechanism means adding new fixtures requires zero test harness -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba docs/workstreams/replace-patch/SPEC.md | sed -n '120,280p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"   120\\\\t| AC-5.5 | Event | WHEN `position: after_last_method` is specified, the system SHALL find the last line within the scope matching `^\\\\\\\\s+def \\\\\\\\w+` or `^\\\\\\\\s+(pub\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn\\\\\\\\|async fn) \\\\\\\\w+` or `^\\\\\\\\s+(public\\\\\\\\|private\\\\\\\\|protected\\\\\\\\|static\\\\\\\\|async)?\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\(` and set the insertion point immediately after that method's complete body | TEST-5.5 |\\\\n   121\\\\t| AC-5.6 | Event | WHEN `position: after_last_import` is specified, the system SHALL find the last line within the scope matching `^\\\\\\\\s*(from\\\\\\\\|import) ` and set the insertion point immediately after it | TEST-5.6 |\\\\n   122\\\\t| AC-5.7 | Event | WHEN `position: sorted` is specified, the system SHALL find the alphabetically correct insertion point among sibling lines at the same indentation level within the scope, comparing the trimmed content of each line | TEST-5.7 |\\\\n   123\\\\t| AC-5.8 | Unwanted | IF `position: after_last_field` finds no matching field lines in the scope, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` position and include a note in verbose output | TEST-5.8 |\\\\n   124\\\\t| AC-5.9 | Unwanted | IF `position: after_last_method` finds no matching method lines in the scope, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` position and include a note in verbose output | TEST-5.9 |\\\\n   125\\\\t| AC-5.10 | Unwanted | IF `position: after_last_import` finds no matching import lines in the scope, the system SHALL fall back to `before` position (top of scope) and include a note in verbose output | TEST-5.10 |\\\\n   126\\\\t| AC-5.11 | Event | WHEN `position: after_last_method` is resolved, the system SHALL identify the method's complete body (using the same scope detection logic as `function_body`) and insert after the body ends, not just after the `def`/`fn` line | TEST-5.11 |\\\\n   127\\\\t\\\\n   128\\\\t#### FR-6: Find Narrowing\\\\n   129\\\\t\\\\n   130\\\\tWhen a scope is large (e.g., an entire class body), `find` narrows the operation to a specific element within that scope.\\\\n   131\\\\t\\\\n   132\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   133\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   134\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   135\\\\t| AC-6.1 | Event | WHEN `anchor.find` is specified as a plain string, the system SHALL search within the detected scope for the first line containing that string and re-anchor the position resolution to that line | TEST-6.1 |\\\\n   136\\\\t| AC-6.2 | Event | WHEN `anchor.find` is specified and the found line opens a sub-scope (e.g., `list_display = [` opens a brackets scope), the system SHALL apply the `position` relative to that sub-scope, not the original class/function scope | TEST-6.2 |\\\\n   137\\\\t| AC-6.3 | Unwanted | IF `anchor.find` is specified and no line within the scope contains the string, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting: the find string, the scope boundaries (start/end line numbers), and the file path, with the rendered content in the error | TEST-6.3 |\\\\n   138\\\\t| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `anchor.find` narrows to a sub-scope and `position: before_close` is specified, the system SHALL insert before the closing delimiter of the sub-scope (e.g., before `]` in `list_display = [...]`) | TEST-6.4 |\\\\n   139\\\\t| AC-6.5 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL apply `find` after scope detection but before position resolution — the order is: pattern match → scope detection → find narrowing → position resolution | TEST-6.5 |\\\\n   140\\\\t\\\\n   141\\\\t#### FR-7: Patch Operation\\\\n   142\\\\t\\\\n   143\\\\tRender a template and insert the result into an existing file at a structurally-determined location using the anchor system (pattern + scope + find + position). Support idempotency via `skip_if`.\\\\n   144\\\\t\\\\n   145\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   146\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   147\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   148\\\\t| AC-7.1 | Event | WHEN a patch operation executes, the system SHALL: (1) find the anchor pattern in the target file, (2) detect the scope, (3) optionally narrow with find, (4) resolve the position, and (5) insert the rendered content at the resolved position | TEST-7.1 |\\\\n   149\\\\t| AC-7.2 | Event | WHEN `skip_if` is specified, the system SHALL render it as a Jinja2 template with the recipe's variables, then search for the rendered string in the target file. If found, the system SHALL skip the operation and report `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\"` with a reason | TEST-7.2 |\\\\n   150\\\\t| AC-7.3 | Event | WHEN a patch operation succeeds, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\"` with the path, location description (including scope type and position, e.g., `\\\\\\\"class_body(^class Foo):after_last_field\\\\\\\"`), and line count (number of rendered lines inserted) | TEST-7.3 |\\\\n   151\\\\t| AC-7.4 | Unwanted | IF the anchor pattern matches zero lines in the target file, the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the pattern, the file path, and a hint, with the rendered content included | TEST-7.4 |\\\\n   152\\\\t| AC-7.5 | Unwanted | IF scope detection fails (e.g., unbalanced delimiters, inconsistent indentation), the system SHALL exit with code 3 reporting the scope type, the anchor line, the specific failure reason (e.g., \\\\\\\"unbalanced braces at line 45\\\\\\\"), and include the rendered content in the error | TEST-7.5 |\\\\n   153\\\\t| AC-7.6 | Event | WHEN the anchor pattern matches multiple lines, the system SHALL use the first match (consistent with inject's default `at: first` behavior) | TEST-7.6 |\\\\n   154\\\\t| AC-7.7 | Event | WHEN a patch operation inserts content, the system SHALL match the indentation of the insertion point — if the surrounding lines at the target position use N-space indentation, the inserted content's base indentation SHALL be adjusted to match | TEST-7.7 |\\\\n   155\\\\t| AC-7.8 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL resolve the patch target path relative to `--base-dir` (consistent with create, inject, and replace) | TEST-7.8 |\\\\n   156\\\\t| AC-7.9 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL support patch operations in dry-run mode, reading from and updating `virtual_files` for chaining with prior operations in the same run | TEST-7.9 |\\\\n   157\\\\t| AC-7.10 | Unwanted | IF the target file for patching does not exist, the system SHALL exit with code 3 and report the missing file path with the rendered content | TEST-7.10 |\\\\n   158\\\\t| AC-7.11 | Unwanted | IF writing the modified file content fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit with code 3 with the path, permission error, and rendered content | TEST-7.11 |\\\\n   159\\\\t| AC-7.12 | Event | WHEN `scope: line` is used (the default), the system SHALL behave identically to inject's `after` mode for the matched line — no structural analysis, just insert after the matched line | TEST-7.12 |\\\\n   160\\\\t\\\\n   161\\\\t#### FR-8: Verbose Scope Diagnostics\\\\n   162\\\\t\\\\n   163\\\\tExtend `--verbose` mode to show scope detection details — boundaries, insertion points, and find narrowing — so recipe authors can debug anchor configurations.\\\\n   164\\\\t\\\\n   165\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   166\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   167\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   168\\\\t| AC-8.1 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified for a patch operation, the system SHALL include in the output: the anchor pattern, the matched line number, the scope type, scope start line, scope end line, and the resolved insertion line | TEST-8.1 |\\\\n   169\\\\t| AC-8.2 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified and `find` narrowing is used, the system SHALL include the find string, the line it was found on, and the sub-scope boundaries (if applicable) | TEST-8.2 |\\\\n   170\\\\t| AC-8.3 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified for a replace operation, the system SHALL include the match mode (between or pattern), the matched region (start line to end line), and the number of lines replaced | TEST-8.3 |\\\\n   171\\\\t| AC-8.4 | Event | WHEN `--verbose` is specified and a semantic position falls back (e.g., `after_last_field` falls back to `before_close` because no fields found), the system SHALL include a note about the fallback in the output | TEST-8.4 |\\\\n   172\\\\t| AC-8.5 | Ubiquitous | Verbose scope diagnostics SHALL appear in both JSON output (as additional fields in the operation object) and human-readable output (as indented detail lines below the operation summary) | TEST-8.5 |\\\\n   173\\\\t\\\\n   174\\\\t### Non-Functional Requirements\\\\n   175\\\\t\\\\n   176\\\\t#### NFR-1: Language-Agnostic Scope Detection\\\\n   177\\\\t\\\\n   178\\\\tScope detection must work across languages without language-specific parsers. The same binary handles Python, TypeScript, Rust, Go, Java, YAML, and any other language that uses either indentation or delimiters for structure.\\\\n   179\\\\t\\\\n   180\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   181\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   182\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   183\\\\t| AC-N1.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL detect indentation-based scopes correctly for Python class bodies, Python function bodies, and YAML nested blocks without any language identifier or configuration | TEST-N1.1 |\\\\n   184\\\\t| AC-N1.2 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL detect delimiter-based scopes correctly for Rust struct bodies, TypeScript interface bodies, Go function bodies, and JSON objects without any language identifier or configuration | TEST-N1.2 |\\\\n   185\\\\t| AC-N1.3 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not require a language identifier, file extension, or configuration to select between indentation-based and delimiter-based scope detection — the scope type in the recipe determines the detection strategy | TEST-N1.3 |\\\\n   186\\\\t\\\\n   187\\\\t#### NFR-2: Graceful Degradation for Scope Failures\\\\n   188\\\\t\\\\n   189\\\\tWhen scope detection or position resolution fails, the system must provide enough information for the caller (typically an LLM) to fall back to manual editing. This extends invariants I-4 and I-10.\\\\n   190\\\\t\\\\n   191\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   192\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   193\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   194\\\\t| AC-N2.1 | Event | WHEN a patch operation fails due to scope detection, the error output SHALL include the rendered template content so the caller can manually insert it | TEST-N2.1 |\\\\n   195\\\\t| AC-N2.2 | Event | WHEN a replace operation fails due to pattern not found (with fallback: error), the error output SHALL include the rendered template content so the caller can manually apply the replacement | TEST-N2.2 |\\\\n   196\\\\t| AC-N2.3 | Ubiquitous | Every patch/replace error SHALL include what (the failure), where (file path and line number), why (specific reason: unbalanced delimiter, no match, etc.), and hint (actionable suggestion) | TEST-N2.3 |\\\\n   197\\\\t| AC-N2.4 | Event | WHEN scope detection fails, the error hint SHALL suggest using a simpler scope type or falling back to inject with a regex anchor | TEST-N2.4 |\\\\n   198\\\\t\\\\n   199\\\\t#### NFR-3: Idempotent Patch and Replace Operations\\\\n   200\\\\t\\\\n   201\\\\tPatch and replace operations must support safe re-runs, consistent with jig's idempotency guarantee (I-2).\\\\n   202\\\\t\\\\n   203\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   204\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   205\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   206\\\\t| AC-N3.1 | Event | WHEN a patch operation with `skip_if` is run a second time with the same variables on an already-patched file, the system SHALL report `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\"` and make no changes | TEST-N3.1 |\\\\n   207\\\\t| AC-N3.2 | Event | WHEN a replace operation with `between` markers replaces content that is identical to the rendered template (second run), the system SHALL still perform the replacement (replace is inherently idempotent — same content replaces same content) and report `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"` | TEST-N3.2 |\\\\n   208\\\\t| AC-N3.3 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL not produce duplicate content when patch uses `skip_if` — the idempotency check searches the entire target file, not just the scope | TEST-N3.3 |\\\\n   209\\\\t\\\\n   210\\\\t#### NFR-4: Deterministic Scope Detection\\\\n   211\\\\t\\\\n   212\\\\tScope detection must be deterministic — the same file content always produces the same scope boundaries. This extends invariant I-1.\\\\n   213\\\\t\\\\n   214\\\\t**Acceptance Criteria (EARS):**\\\\n   215\\\\t| ID | Type | Criterion | Traces To |\\\\n   216\\\\t|----|------|-----------|-----------|\\\\n   217\\\\t| AC-N4.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce identical scope boundaries when given the same file content and anchor configuration across multiple runs | TEST-N4.1 |\\\\n   218\\\\t| AC-N4.2 | Ubiquitous | Scope detection SHALL not depend on file modification time, filesystem metadata, or any external state — only on file content and anchor configuration | TEST-N4.2 |\\\\n   219\\\\t\\\\n   220\\\\t## Interfaces\\\\n   221\\\\t\\\\n   222\\\\t### Public API\\\\n   223\\\\t\\\\n   224\\\\tNo new CLI subcommands. The existing `jig run` and `jig validate` commands gain support for replace and patch operations in recipes. The `--verbose` flag gains additional output for scope diagnostics.\\\\n   225\\\\t\\\\n   226\\\\t```yaml\\\\n   227\\\\t# Replace operation in recipe YAML\\\\n   228\\\\tfiles:\\\\n   229\\\\t  - template: fragment.j2\\\\n   230\\\\t    replace: \\\\\\\"path/to/file.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n   231\\\\t    between:\\\\n   232\\\\t      start: \\\\\\\"^# region start\\\\\\\"\\\\n   233\\\\t      end: \\\\\\\"^# region end\\\\\\\"\\\\n   234\\\\t    fallback: append          # append | prepend | skip | error (default)\\\\n   235\\\\t\\\\n   236\\\\t  - template: fragment.j2\\\\n   237\\\\t    replace: \\\\\\\"path/to/file.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n   238\\\\t    pattern: \\\\\\\"^old_line.*\\\\\\\"\\\\n   239\\\\t    fallback: skip\\\\n   240\\\\t\\\\n   241\\\\t# Patch operation in recipe YAML\\\\n   242\\\\tfiles:\\\\n   243\\\\t  - template: field.j2\\\\n   244\\\\t    patch: \\\\\\\"path/to/file.py\\\\\\\"\\\\n   245\\\\t    anchor:\\\\n   246\\\\t      pattern: \\\\\\\"^class MyModel\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\(\\\\\\\"\\\\n   247\\\\t      scope: class_body         # line | block | class_body | function_body |\\\\n   248\\\\t                                # function_signature | braces | brackets | parens\\\\n   249\\\\t      find: \\\\\\\"list_display\\\\\\\"      # optional narrowing\\\\n   250\\\\t      position: before_close    # before | after | before_close | after_last_field |\\\\n   251\\\\t                                # after_last_method | after_last_import | sorted\\\\n   252\\\\t    skip_if: \\\\\\\"{{ field_name }}\\\\\\\"\\\\n   253\\\\t```\\\\n   254\\\\t\\\\n   255\\\\t### JSON Output Extensions\\\\n   256\\\\t\\\\n   257\\\\tReplace and patch operations produce the same `OpResult` structure as create and inject. New `action` values:\\\\n   258\\\\t\\\\n   259\\\\t```json\\\\n   260\\\\t{\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"location\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"between:^# start:^# end\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\": 5}\\\\n   261\\\\t{\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"...\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"location\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"class_body(^class Foo):after_last_field\\\\\\\", \\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\": 1}\\\\n   262\\\\t```\\\\n   263\\\\t\\\\n   264\\\\tVerbose mode adds scope diagnostic fields:\\\\n   265\\\\t\\\\n   266\\\\t```json\\\\n   267\\\\t{\\\\n   268\\\\t  \\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\",\\\\n   269\\\\t  \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"models/reservation.py\\\\\\\",\\\\n   270\\\\t  \\\\\\\"location\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"class_body(^class Reservation):after_last_field\\\\\\\",\\\\n   271\\\\t  \\\\\\\"lines\\\\\\\": 1,\\\\n   272\\\\t  \\\\\\\"scope_diagnostics\\\\\\\": {\\\\n   273\\\\t    \\\\\\\"anchor_line\\\\\\\": 12,\\\\n   274\\\\t    \\\\\\\"scope_start\\\\\\\": 13,\\\\n   275\\\\t    \\\\\\\"scope_end\\\\\\\": 45,\\\\n   276\\\\t    \\\\\\\"insertion_line\\\\\\\": 38,\\\\n   277\\\\t    \\\\\\\"find_match_line\\\\\\\": null,\\\\n   278\\\\t    \\\\\\\"position_fallback\\\\\\\": null\\\\n   279\\\\t  }\\\\n 466\\\\t}\\\\n   467\\\\t```\\\\n   468\\\\t\\\\n   469\\\\t## Error Handling\\\\n   470\\\\t\\\\n   471\\\\t| Error Scenario | Exit Code | Error Content |\\\\n   472\\\\t|----------------|-----------|---------------|\\\\n   473\\\\t| Replace target file missing | 3 | path, \\\\\\\"file not found\\\\\\\", rendered content |\\\\n   474\\\\t| `between.start` not found | 3 | pattern, file, \\\\\\\"start marker matched 0 lines\\\\\\\", rendered content |\\\\n   475\\\\t| `between.end` not found after start | 3 | end pattern, file, \\\\\\\"end marker not found after line N\\\\\\\", rendered content |\\\\n   476\\\\t| `pattern` not found (fallback: error) | 3 | pattern, file, \\\\\\\"matched 0 lines\\\\\\\", rendered content |\\\\n   477\\\\t| Patch anchor pattern not found | 3 | pattern, file, \\\\\\\"anchor matched 0 lines\\\\\\\", rendered content |\\\\n   478\\\\t| Scope detection: unbalanced delimiter | 3 | scope type, anchor line, \\\\\\\"unbalanced { at line N\\\\\\\", rendered content |\\\\n   479\\\\t| Scope detection: indentation inconsistency | 3 | scope type, anchor line, \\\\\\\"could not determine end of scope — indentation inconsistent at line N\\\\\\\", rendered content |\\\\n   480\\\\t| Scope detection: opening delimiter not found | 3 | scope type, anchor line, \\\\\\\"no { found on or after anchor\\\\\\\", rendered content |\\\\n   481\\\\t| `find` string not found in scope | 3 | find string, scope range (lines M-N), file, rendered content |\\\\n   482\\\\t| Patch target file missing | 3 | path, \\\\\\\"file not found\\\\\\\", rendered content |\\\\n   483\\\\t| Write permission denied | 3 | path, OS error, rendered content |\\\\n   484\\\\t| Invalid regex in between/pattern/anchor | 1 | pattern, compilation error (recipe validation time — no rendered content) |\\\\n   485\\\\t| Invalid scope type string | 1 | scope value, valid options list (recipe validation) |\\\\n   486\\\\t| Invalid position type string | 1 | position value, valid options list (recipe validation) |\\\\n   487\\\\t| Invalid fallback value string | 1 | fallback value, valid options list (recipe validation) |\\\\n   488\\\\t| Both `between` and `pattern` specified | 1 | conflicting fields (recipe validation) |\\\\n   489\\\\t| Neither `between` nor `pattern` specified | 1 | missing match specification (recipe validation) |\\\\n   490\\\\t| `between` missing start or end | 1 | missing field name (recipe validation) |\\\\n   491\\\\t| `anchor` missing pattern | 1 | missing field name (recipe validation) |\\\\n   492\\\\t\\\\n   493\\\\tAll file operation errors (exit code 3) include `rendered_content` so the caller can fall back to manual editing. This is always present, independent of `--verbose`.\\\\n   494\\\\t\\\\n   495\\\\t## Testing Strategy\\\\n   496\\\\t\\\\n   497\\\\t- **Spec tests:** One or more tests per AC-* criterion above, namespaced as `spec::fr{N}::ac_{N}_{M}`\\\\n   498\\\\t- **Invariant tests:** Deterministic scope detection (same file + anchor = same boundaries), idempotency (patch with skip_if run twice), graceful degradation (error includes rendered content)\\\\n   499\\\\t- **Integration fixtures:** Each fixture directory follows the established pattern: `recipe.yaml`, `vars.json`, `templates/`, `existing/`, `expected/`, optionally `expected_output.json` and `expected_exit_code`\\\\n   500\\\\t- **Scope detection unit tests:** Dedicated tests per scope type with representative code snippets from Python, Rust, TypeScript, Go, YAML\\\\n   501\\\\t- **Position resolution unit tests:** Each semantic position tested against realistic code blocks\\\\n   502\\\\t- **Snapshot tests:** `insta` for verbose scope diagnostics output (JSON and human formats)\\\\n   503\\\\t\\\\n   504\\\\t### Required Test Fixtures\\\\n   505\\\\t\\\\n   506\\\\t| Fixture | Operations | Tests |\\\\n   507\\\\t|---------|-----------|-------|\\\\n   508\\\\t| `replace-between` | replace with between markers | AC-2.1, AC-2.7 |\\\\n   509\\\\t| `replace-between-empty` | replace between adjacent markers | AC-2.8 |\\\\n   510\\\\t| `replace-pattern` | replace with single pattern | AC-2.2 |\\\\n   511\\\\t| `replace-fallback-append` | replace with fallback: append | AC-2.3 |\\\\n   512\\\\t| `replace-fallback-prepend` | replace with fallback: prepend | AC-2.4 |\\\\n   513\\\\t| `replace-fallback-skip` | replace with fallback: skip | AC-2.5 |\\\\n   514\\\\t| `replace-fallback-error` | replace with fallback: error (default) | AC-2.6 |\\\\n   515\\\\t| `patch-class-body-field` | patch into Python class body, after_last_field | AC-7.1, AC-3.2, AC-5.4 |\\\\n   516\\\\t| `patch-function-body` | patch into Python function body | AC-3.3, AC-7.1 |\\\\n   517\\\\t| `patch-braces` | patch into Rust/TS braces scope | AC-4.1, AC-7.1 |\\\\n   518\\\\t| `patch-brackets` | patch into brackets scope | AC-4.2 |\\\\n   519\\\\t| `patch-function-signature` | patch into function_signature (parens) | AC-4.4 |\\\\n   520\\\\t| `patch-find-narrowing` | patch with find + Interfaces\\\\n   284\\\\t\\\\n   285\\\\t```rust\\\\n   286\\\\t// ── Extended FileOp enum (recipe.rs) ─────────────────────────────\\\\n   287\\\\tenum FileOp {\\\\n   288\\\\t    Create { template: String, to: String, skip_if_exists: bool },\\\\n   289\\\\t    Inject { template: String, inject: String, mode: InjectMode, skip_if: Option<String> },\\\\n   290\\\\t    Replace { template: String, replace: String, spec: ReplaceSpec, fallback: Fallback },\\\\n   291\\\\t    Patch { template: String, patch: String, anchor: Anchor, skip_if: Option<String> },\\\\n   292\\\\t}\\\\n   293\\\\t\\\\n   294\\\\t// ── Scope detection (scope/mod.rs) ───────────────────────────────\\\\n   295\\\\tfn detect_scope(\\\\n   296\\\\t    lines: &[&str],\\\\n   297\\\\t    anchor_line: usize,\\\\n   298\\\\t    scope_type: &ScopeType,\\\\n   299\\\\t) -> Result<ScopeResult, StructuredError>;\\\\n   300\\\\t\\\\n   301\\\\t// ── Position resolution (scope/position.rs) ──────────────────────\\\\n   302\\\\tfn resolve_position(\\\\n   303\\\\t    lines: &[&str],\\\\n   304\\\\t    scope: &ScopeResult,\\\\n   305\\\\t    position: &Position,\\\\n   306\\\\t) -> Result<PositionResult, StructuredError>;\\\\n   307\\\\t\\\\n   308\\\\t// ── Find narrowing (scope/mod.rs or patch.rs) ────────────────────\\\\n   309\\\\tfn find_within_scope(\\\\n   310\\\\t    lines: &[&str],\\\\n   311\\\\t    scope: &ScopeResult,\\\\n   312\\\\t    find_str: &str,\\\\n   313\\\\t) -> Result<FindResult, StructuredError>;\\\\n   314\\\\t\\\\n   315\\\\t// ── Operation execution (operations/*.rs) ────────────────────────\\\\n   316\\\\t// replace.rs\\\\n   317\\\\tfn execute(\\\\n   318\\\\t    rendered_path: &str,\\\\n   319\\\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n   320\\\\t    spec: &ReplaceSpec,\\\\n   321\\\\t    fallback: &Fallback,\\\\n   322\\\\t    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n   323\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n   324\\\\t) -> OpResult;\\\\n   325\\\\t\\\\n   326\\\\t// patch.rs\\\\n   327\\\\tfn execute(\\\\n   328\\\\t    rendered_path: &str,\\\\n   329\\\\t    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n   330\\\\t    rendered_skip_if: Option<&str>,\\\\n   331\\\\t    anchor: &Anchor,\\\\n   332\\\\t    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n   333\\\\t    verbose: bool,\\\\n   334\\\\t) -> OpResult;\\\\n   335\\\\t```\\\\n   336\\\\t\\\\n   337\\\\t## Component Relationships\\\\n   338\\\\t\\\\n   339\\\\t```mermaid\\\\n   340\\\\tgraph TD\\\\n   341\\\\t    Recipe[recipe.rs] -->|parses| Replace[FileOp::Replace]\\\\n   342\\\\t    Recipe -->|parses| Patch[FileOp::Patch]\\\\n   343\\\\t\\\\n   344\\\\t    Dispatch[operations/mod.rs] -->|dispatches| ReplaceOp[operations/replace.rs]\\\\n   345\\\\t    Dispatch -->|dispatches| PatchOp[operations/patch.rs]\\\\n   346\\\\t\\\\n   347\\\\t    PatchOp -->|calls| ScopeMod[scope/mod.rs]\\\\n   348\\\\t    ScopeMod -->|indent scopes| Indent[scope/indent.rs]\\\\n   349\\\\t    ScopeMod -->|delimiter scopes| Delimiter[scope/delimiter.rs]\\\\n   350\\\\t    ScopeMod -->|positions| PositionMod[scope/position.rs]\\\\n   351\\\\t\\\\n   352\\\\t    PatchOp -->|find narrowing| ScopeMod\\\\n   353\\\\t\\\\n   354\\\\t    ReplaceOp -->|regex matching| Regex[regex crate]\\\\n   355\\\\t    PatchOp -->|anchor matching| Regex\\\\n   356\\\\t\\\\n   357\\\\t    ReplaceOp -->|results| Output[output.rs]\\\\n   358\\\\t    PatchOp -->|results| Output\\\\n   359\\\\t```\\\\n   360\\\\t\\\\n   361\\\\t## Data Model\\\\n   362\\\\t\\\\n   363\\\\t```rust\\\\n   364\\\\t// ── Replace types ──────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   365\\\\t\\\\n   366\\\\t/// How to match the region to replace.\\\\n   367\\\\tenum ReplaceSpec {\\\\n   368\\\\t    /// Replace content between two marker lines (markers preserved).\\\\n   369\\\\t    Between { start: String, end: String },\\\\n   370\\\\t    /// Replace all contiguous lines matching this pattern.\\\\n   371\\\\t    Pattern(String),\\\\n   372\\\\t}\\\\n   373\\\\t\\\\n   374\\\\t/// What to do when the match pattern is not found.\\\\n   375\\\\tenum Fallback {\\\\n   376\\\\t    Append,   // append rendered content to end of file\\\\n   377\\\\t    Prepend,  // prepend rendered content to start of file\\\\n   378\\\\t    Skip,     // skip operation, report as skip\\\\n   379\\\\t    Error,    // fail with exit code 3 (default)\\\\n   380\\\\t}\\\\n   381\\\\t\\\\n   382\\\\t// ── Patch types ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   383\\\\t\\\\n   384\\\\t/// Structural anchor for patch operations.\\\\n   385\\\\tstruct Anchor {\\\\n   386\\\\t    /// Regex to find the anchor line in the file.\\\\n   387\\\\t    pattern: String,\\\\n   388\\\\t    /// Structural region to target relative to the match.\\\\n   389\\\\t    scope: ScopeType,\\\\n   390\\\\t    /// Optional string to find within the scope for narrowing.\\\\n   391\\\\t    find: Option<String>,\\\\n   392\\\\t    /// Where within the scope to insert.\\\\n   393\\\\t    position: Position,\\\\n   394\\\\t}\\\\n   395\\\\t\\\\n   396\\\\t// ── Scope types ────────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   397\\\\t\\\\n   398\\\\t/// Scope type determines how structural boundaries are detected.\\\\n   399\\\\tenum ScopeType {\\\\n   400\\\\t    Line,               // No scope — anchor line only (default)\\\\n   401\\\\t    Block,              // Indentation: deeper-indented block after anchor\\\\n   402\\\\t    ClassBody,          // Indentation: class body\\\\n   403\\\\t    FunctionBody,       // Indentation: function/method body\\\\n   404\\\\t    FunctionSignature,  // Delimiter: ( to ) on the function definition\\\\n   405\\\\t    Braces,             // Delimiter: { to }\\\\n   406\\\\t    Brackets,           // Delimiter: [ to ]\\\\n   407\\\\t    Parens,             // Delimiter: ( to )\\\\n   408\\\\t}\\\\n   409\\\\t\\\\n   410\\\\t/// Result of scope detection.\\\\n   411\\\\tstruct ScopeResult {\\\\n   412\\\\t    /// First line of scope content (0-indexed into file lines).\\\\n   413\\\\t    start_line: usize,\\\\n   414\\\\t    /// Last line of scope content (0-indexed, inclusive).\\\\n   415\\\\t    end_line: usize,\\\\n   416\\\\t    /// Line number of closing delimiter, if applicable.\\\\n   417\\\\t    closing_line: Option<usize>,\\\\n   418\\\\t    /// Whether the scope is empty (no content between boundaries).\\\\n   419\\\\t    is_empty: bool,\\\\n   420\\\\t}\\\\n   421\\\\t\\\\n   422\\\\t// ── Position types ─────────────────────────────────────────────\\\\n   423\\\\t\\\\n   424\\\\t/// Where within the scope to insert rendered content.\\\\n   425\\\\tenum Position {\\\\n   426\\\\t    Before,           // Before first line of scope\\\\n   427\\\\t    After,            // After last line of scope (default)\\\\n   428\\\\t    BeforeClose,      // Before closing delimiter or dedent\\\\n   429\\\\t    AfterLastField,   // After last assignment/annotation line\\\\n   430\\\\t    AfterLastMethod,  // After last method definition's complete |\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n./docs/workstreams/replace-patch/reviews/code-review-codex-20260404-165354.md:96:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_48\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"command_execution\\\",\\\"command\\\":\\\"/bin/zsh -lc \\\\\\\"nl -ba docs/INVARIANTS.md | sed -n '1,220p'\\\\\\\"\\\",\\\"aggregated_output\\\":\\\"     1\\\\t# INVARIANTS.md\\\\n     2\\\\t\\\\n     3\\\\tProject-wide constraints. Every workstream must respect these. If a change would violate an invariant, it requires explicit discussion and sign-off before proceeding.\\\\n     4\\\\t\\\\n     5\\\\t## I-1: Deterministic Output\\\\n     6\\\\t\\\\n     7\\\\tSame recipe + same variables + same existing files = same output. Always. No randomness, no timestamps, no machine-specific behavior in rendered output. Tests can assert exact equality.\\\\n     8\\\\t\\\\n     9\\\\t## I-2: Idempotent Operations\\\\n    10\\\\t\\\\n    11\\\\tEvery file operation *supports* idempotent re-runs via `skip_if_exists` (create) and `skip_if` (inject). When a recipe is designed for idempotent execution (all creates use `skip_if_exists: true` and all injects use `skip_if`), running the same recipe twice with the same variables produces no changes on the second run. JSON output reports `\\\\\\\"action\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\"` with a reason for every skipped operation.\\\\n    12\\\\t\\\\n    13\\\\t## I-3: JSON In, Files Out\\\\n    14\\\\t\\\\n    15\\\\tVariables are structured JSON. Output is files on disk. The CLI is non-interactive — no prompts, no wizards, no \\\\\\\"press y to continue.\\\\\\\" An LLM (or script) can call jig without any human in the loop.\\\\n    16\\\\t\\\\n    17\\\\t## I-4: Structured Errors with Rendered Content\\\\n    18\\\\t\\\\n    19\\\\tWhen an operation fails (anchor not found, scope parse fails, file missing), jig still renders the template and includes the rendered content in the error output. The caller never has to re-derive what to insert — only where to put it. Errors include what/where/why + a hint.\\\\n    20\\\\t\\\\n    21\\\\t## I-5: Stable Exit Codes\\\\n    22\\\\t\\\\n    23\\\\tExit codes are API. They do not change between minor versions.\\\\n    24\\\\t\\\\n    25\\\\t| Code | Meaning |\\\\n    26\\\\t|------|---------|\\\\n    27\\\\t| 0 | Success |\\\\n    28\\\\t| 1 | Recipe validation error |\\\\n    29\\\\t| 2 | Template rendering error |\\\\n    30\\\\t| 3 | File operation error |\\\\n    31\\\\t| 4 | Variable validation error |\\\\n    32\\\\t\\\\n    33\\\\t## I-6: Single Static Binary, Zero Runtime Dependencies\\\\n    34\\\\t\\\\n    35\\\\tNo Python, no Node, no JVM. One binary, runs anywhere. Startup must be fast — jig may be called dozens of times in a single LLM session.\\\\n    36\\\\t\\\\n    37\\\\t## I-7: Templates Live With the Consumer\\\\n    38\\\\t\\\\n    39\\\\tThere is no central template directory. Templates are co-located with the recipe that uses them. A recipe + its templates form a self-contained unit that can be moved, versioned, or shared independently.\\\\n    40\\\\t\\\\n    41\\\\t## I-8: Dual Output Streams\\\\n    42\\\\t\\\\n    43\\\\tFor `jig run`: In TTY mode, human-readable colored output to stderr, nothing to stdout. In piped mode, JSON to stdout, nothing to stderr (except errors). `--json` forces JSON to stdout regardless of TTY. `--quiet` suppresses stderr only; stdout behavior is determined by `--json` independently. `--verbose` adds rendered template content to output. Note: `jig vars` and `jig render` write to stdout by default regardless of TTY mode. `jig render --to <path>` redirects output to a file.\\\\n    44\\\\t\\\\n    45\\\\t## I-9: Operations Are Ordered\\\\n    46\\\\t\\\\n    47\\\\tFile operations in a recipe execute in declaration order. Later operations can depend on files created or modified by earlier operations in the same recipe run.\\\\n    48\\\\t\\\\n    49\\\\t## I-10: Graceful Degradation to LLM\\\\n    50\\\\t\\\\n    51\\\\tjig handles the deterministic part (rendering). When structural analysis fails (can't find anchor, can't parse scope), it fails with enough information for the LLM to fall back to its native Edit tool. jig never silently produces wrong output — it either succeeds or fails clearly.\\\\n\\\",\\\"exit_code\\\":0,\\\"status\\\":\\\"completed\\\"}}\\n./docs/workstreams/replace-patch/reviews/code-review-codex-20260404-165354.md:97:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"item.completed\\\",\\\"item\\\":{\\\"id\\\":\\\"item_49\\\",\\\"type\\\":\\\"agent_message\\\",\\\"text\\\":\\\"**Verdict:** Request Changes\\\\n\\\\n**Critical** (must fix before merge):\\\\n- Patch swallows write failures (`let _ = std::fs::write(...)`) and can still return success, violating the file-op error contract (AC-7.11) and graceful failure invariants. [patch.rs:224](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/patch.rs:224) [patch.rs:228](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/patch.rs:228) [SPEC.md:158](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/replace-patch/SPEC.md:158) [INVARIANTS.md:17](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/INVARIANTS.md:17)\\\\n- `position: sorted` is explicitly unimplemented and currently defaults to end-of-scope insertion, so AC-5.7 is not met. [recipe.rs:370](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/recipe.rs:370) [recipe.rs:522](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/recipe.rs:522)\\\\n- Dry-run chaining via `virtual_files` is consistently implemented across replace/patch execution paths. [replace.rs:25](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/replace.rs:25) [patch.rs:26](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/patch.rs:26)\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}}\\n./docs/workstreams/replace-patch/reviews/code-review-synthesized-20260404-165354.md:1:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"result\\\",\\\"subtype\\\":\\\"success\\\",\\\"is_error\\\":false,\\\"duration_ms\\\":103100,\\\"duration_api_ms\\\":103083,\\\"num_turns\\\":1,\\\"result\\\":\\\"**Verdict: Request Changes**\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Critical** (must fix):\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **`write_back` silently swallows write errors (patch all paths + replace fallback paths).** Both reviewers flagged this. `patch.rs:228` uses `let _ = std::fs::write(...)` for every non-dry-run write, and `replace.rs:278` does the same in the fallback append/prepend path. A permission-denied error returns `OpResult::Success`, violating AC-7.11, AC-2.14, and I-10 (never silently produce wrong output). The main replace path at `replace.rs:106` handles this correctly — apply the same pattern. **Fix:** Make `write_back` in both files return `Result` and propagate `OpResult::Error` with path, OS error, and rendered content.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **`Position::Sorted` is accepted at parse time but stub-implemented.** Both reviewers flagged this. `position.rs:170-184` just inserts at end-of-scope. AC-5.7 requires alphabetically correct insertion among siblings. A user writing `position: sorted` gets silently wrong output. **Fix:** Either implement sorted insertion (compare trimmed content of sibling lines) or reject `sorted` at parse time with a \\\\\\\"not yet implemented\\\\\\\" error in `recipe.rs:582`.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **Byte index used where char index is tracked in delimiter scope detection.** Review A flagged, verified valid. `delimiter.rs:87`: `&lines[open_line][open_col + 1..scanner.col]` — `CharScanner` tracks `col` in character positions (via `chars().nth()`, `chars().count()`), but Rust string slicing uses byte offsets. Any multi-byte UTF-8 character (accented names, CJK comments, emoji) causes a panic at `byte index N is not a char boundary`. **Fix:** Use `char_indices()` to convert char offsets to byte offsets before slicing, or use a byte-based scanner consistently.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Major** (should fix):\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures missing (42% gap).** Both reviewers flagged identical list. Missing: `patch-function-body`, `patch-brackets`, `patch-function-signature`, `patch-find-narrowing`, `patch-sorted`, `patch-indent-matching`, `patch-nested-delimiters`, `patch-string-delimiters`, `error-patch-unbalanced`, `error-patch-find-missing`, `combined-patch-idempotent`. Unit tests cover some behaviors, but the spec (`SPEC.md:504-533`) explicitly requires fixture-level integration tests for each.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **Verbose scope diagnostics (FR-8) not implemented.** Both reviewers flagged. `SPEC.md:168-172` requires a `scope_diagnostics` object in JSON output with `anchor_line`, `scope_start`, `scope_end`, `insertion_line`, `find_match_line`, `position_fallback`. Currently verbose mode only adds `rendered_content` to the operation result (`output.rs:55-58`). The `ScopeDiagnostics` struct from the spec doesn't exist in code.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **`find` narrowing doesn't re-anchor when no sub-scope is detected.** Review B flagged, valid per AC-6.1. `patch.rs:119-123`: when `find_result.sub_scope` is `None`, the code falls back to `scope_result` (the original scope). AC-6.1 requires \\\\\\\"re-anchor the position resolution to that line\\\\\\\" — meaning position should resolve relative to the found line, not the entire original scope. **Fix:** When no sub-scope exists, create a narrowed `ScopeResult` centered on `find_result.found_line`.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **`scope: line` auto-adjusts indentation, diverging from inject-after behavior.** Review B flagged, valid per AC-7.12. `patch.rs:80-81` calls `detect_anchor_indent` + `adjust_indentation`, while inject's after mode (`inject.rs:178-189`) inserts content verbatim. AC-7.12: \\\\\\\"SHALL behave identically to inject's `after` mode for the matched line.\\\\\\\" **Fix:** In the `ScopeType::Line` branch, insert `rendered_content` without indentation adjustment.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **`find_opening` doesn't skip strings/comments when searching for the opening delimiter.** Review B flagged, valid per AC-4.6/4.7. `delimiter.rs:117-129` scans character-by-character for the open delimiter but doesn't call `skip_string_literal`, `skip_line_comment`, or `skip_block_comment`. An opening delimiter inside a string literal or comment before the real one will be selected. The closing-delimiter scan correctly handles these. **Fix:** Apply the same string/comment/escape skipping logic in `find_opening` as in the nesting-depth loop.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **`AfterLastMethod` regex too narrow for Rust.** Review A flagged. `position.rs:62`: `r\\\\\\\"^\\\\\\\\s+(pub\\\\\\\\s+)?(async\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\"` misses `const fn`, `unsafe fn`, `pub(crate) fn`, `extern \\\\\\\"C\\\\\\\" fn`. **Fix:** Broaden to `r\\\\\\\"^\\\\\\\\s+(pub(\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\([^)]*\\\\\\\\))?\\\\\\\\s+)?(const\\\\\\\\s+)?(unsafe\\\\\\\\s+)?(async\\\\\\\\s+)?(extern\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\"[^\\\\\\\"]*\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\"`.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **Location string formatting uses `Debug` instead of snake_case.** Both reviewers flagged. `patch.rs:160-162`: `format!(\\\\\\\"{:?}\\\\\\\", anchor.scope).to_lowercase()` produces `classbody` not `class_body`, and `afterlastfield` not `after_last_field`. The spec (`SPEC.md:150`) shows `class_body(^class Foo):after_last_field`. **Fix:** Implement `Display` for `ScopeType` and `Position` with snake_case output.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **Mixed tab/space scope detection uses length comparison, not literal prefix comparison.** Review B flagged, valid per AC-3.8. `indent.rs:106-108` uses `line.len() - line.trim_start().len()` (pure length). AC-3.8 requires \\\\\\\"literal prefix comparison — each line's leading whitespace is compared character-by-character against the anchor line's.\\\\\\\" **Fix:** Compare the actual whitespace prefix string, not just its length.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Minor** (nice to fix):\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **`AfterLastField` regex requires leading whitespace.** `position.rs:41`: `r\\\\\\\"^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]\\\\\\\"` — `\\\\\\\\s+` means it never matches fields at indent level 0 (e.g., Go top-level struct fields). Should be `\\\\\\\\s*` if the intent is to work at any indentation. Review A.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **`peek_next` returns `None` on empty next line.** `delimiter.rs:171`: the `!self.lines[self.line + 1].is_empty()` guard means blank lines break comment detection for `//` and `/*` sequences spanning a line boundary. Review A.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **`chars().count()` called on every `normalize()`, `peek()`, `advance()`.** `delimiter.rs:146,160,182` — O(n) per call, making the scanner loop O(n^2) per line. Cache the line char count or switch to byte-based indexing (which also fixes the Critical byte/char issue). Review A.\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] **Regex recompiled at runtime in replace.** `replace.rs:162-163`: `Regex::new(pattern).expect(...)` compiles the regex fresh for every execution. Pre-compile during recipe parsing for better performance when jig is called repeatedly (I-6). Review A.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Strengths** (confirmed by both reviewers):\\\\n\\\\n- **Error-with-rendered-content contract is solid.** Every error path in replace and patch includes the rendered template content, honoring I-4 and I-10. The main replace execution path at `replace.rs:106-117` correctly handles write failures — only the `write_back` helper doesn't.\\\\n- **Virtual files chaining is clean and consistent.** Dry-run and cross-operation chaining via `virtual_files` HashMap works correctly across create, replace, and patch in both dry and wet modes.\\\\n- **Recipe parsing and validation is thorough.** All structural validation (mutual exclusivity, regex compile-check, default values, invalid enum rejection) is well-implemented with clear structured errors and exit code 1. This is the strongest part of the implementation.\\\\n- **Scope detection architecture is well-separated.** `scope/` has zero dependency on the operation pipeline, taking `&[&str]` and returning structs, making it trivially testable.\\\\n- **284 tests passing, zero failures.** The test suite is substantial and the fixture auto-discovery mechanism means adding new fixtures requires zero test harness changes.\\\",\\\"stop_reason\\\":\\\"end_turn\\\",\\\"session_id\\\":\\\"2898bf1d-ffa1-4aed-89d8-31007317ec60\\\",\\\"total_cost_usd\\\":0.9716465,\\\"usage\\\":{\\\"input_tokens\\\":3,\\\"cache_creation_input_tokens\\\":135152,\\\"cache_read_input_tokens\\\":11413,\\\"output_tokens\\\":4849,\\\"server_tool_use\\\":{\\\"web_search_requests\\\":0,\\\"web_fetch_requests\\\":0},\\\"service_tier\\\":\\\"standard\\\",\\\"cache_creation\\\":{\\\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\\\":135152,\\\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\\\":0},\\\"inference_geo\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"iterations\\\":[],\\\"speed\\\":\\\"standard\\\"},\\\"modelUsage\\\":{\\\"claude-opus-4-6[1m]\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":3,\\\"outputTokens\\\":4849,\\\"cacheReadInputTokens\\\":11413,\\\"cacheCreationInputTokens\\\":135152,\\\"webSearchRequests\\\":0,\\\"costUSD\\\":0.9716465,\\\"contextWindow\\\":1000000,\\\"maxOutputTokens\\\":64000}},\\\"permission_denials\\\":[],\\\"terminal_reason\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"fast_mode_state\\\":\\\"off\\\",\\\"uuid\\\":\\\"bff53e8d-e1b5-4389-8dc5-b863c0af22c6\\\"}\\n./docs/workstreams/replace-patch/NARRATIVE.md:8:Adds two new file operations to jig — **replace** and **patch** — that let recipes modify existing code, not just create new files or inject at regex anchors. Phase 3 → Phase 4 → Phase 5\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 1: Recipe Parsing Extensions\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-1 (AC-1.1 through AC-1.19)\\\\n**Dependencies:** None\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~35\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.1: Define new types in `src/recipe.rs`\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` (after line 89, below existing `FileOp` enum)\\\\n\\\\nAdd types:\\\\n- `ReplaceSpec` enum — `Between { start: String, end: String }`, `Pattern(String)`\\\\n- `Fallback` enum — `Append`, `Prepend`, `Skip`, `Error` (default)\\\\n- `Anchor` struct — `pattern: String`, `scope: ScopeType`, `find: Option<String>`, `position: Position`\\\\n- `ScopeType` enum — 8 variants: `Line` (default), `Block`, `ClassBody`, `FunctionBody`, `FunctionSignature`, `Braces`, `Brackets`, `Parens`\\\\n- `Position` enum — 7 variants: `Before`, `After` (default), `BeforeClose`, `AfterLastField`, `AfterLastMethod`, `AfterLastImport`, `Sorted`\\\\n\\\\nExtend `FileOp` enum (line 76) with:\\\\n```rust\\\\nReplace { template: String, replace: String, spec: ReplaceSpec, fallback: Fallback },\\\\nPatch { template: String, patch: String, anchor: Anchor, skip_if: Option<String> },\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nUpdate `op_type_str()` (line 92) and `template()` (line 99) for new variants.\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL derive `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq` for all new types.\\\\n- Event: WHEN `FileOp::Replace` is constructed, the system SHALL expose `op_type_str() == \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"` and `template()` returning the template field.\\\\n- Event: WHEN `FileOp::Patch` is constructed, the system SHALL expose `op_type_str() == \\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\"` and `template()` returning the template field.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Nothing\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.2: Add `RawAnchor`/`RawBetween` serde structs, extend `RawFileOp`\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` (near line 118, alongside `RawFileOp`)\\\\n\\\\nAdd intermediate serde structs:\\\\n- `RawAnchor` — `pattern: Option<String>`, `scope: Option<String>`, `find: Option<String>`, `position: Option<String>`\\\\n- `RawBetween` — `start: Option<String>`, `end: Option<String>`\\\\n\\\\nModify `RawFileOp`:\\\\n- Change `replace`/`patch` from `Option<serde_yaml::Value>` to `Option<String>` (target path strings)\\\\n- Add fields: `between: Option<RawBetween>`, `pattern: Option<String>`, `fallback: Option<String>`, `anchor: Option<RawAnchor>`\\\\n- Update hint message (line 239) to list new valid field names\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN a recipe YAML contains `replace: \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\"` with `between:` or `pattern:`, the system SHALL deserialize without error.\\\\n- Event: WHEN a recipe YAML contains `patch: \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\"` with `anchor:`, the system SHALL deserialize without error.\\\\n- Event: WHEN unknown fields appear alongside replace/patch, the system SHALL report them via the `extra` catch-all.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.3: Implement `convert_file_op` logic for Replace and Patch\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` — replace rejection block at lines 276-291\\\\n\\\\n**Replace conversion:**\\\\n1. Validate exactly one of `between`/`pattern` is present (not both, not neither)\\\\n2. If `between`: validate both `start` and `end` present; compile-check both as regex via `validate_regex()`\\\\n3. If `pattern`: compile-check as regex\\\\n4. Parse `fallback` string (default: `Error`): `append` | `prepend` | `skip` | `error`\\\\n5. Reject invalid values with exit code 1, listing valid options\\\\n\\\\n**Patch conversion:**\\\\n1. Validate `anchor` present with required `pattern` field\\\\n2. Compile-check `anchor.pattern` as regex\\\\n3. Parse `anchor.scope` into `ScopeType` (default: `Line`); invalid → exit 1 listing valid types\\\\n4. Parse `anchor.position` into `Position` (default: `After`); invalid → exit 1 listing valid types\\\\n5. Pass through `anchor.find` as `Option<String>`\\\\n\\\\nAdd helpers: `parse_scope_type()`, `parse_position()`, `parse_fallback()`.\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN a replace op has `between` with valid `start`/`end`, the system SHALL parse as `ReplaceSpec::Between` (AC-1.1, AC-1.2).\\\\n- Event: WHEN a replace op has `pattern`, the system SHALL parse as `ReplaceSpec::Pattern` (AC-1.3).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `fallback` is omitted, the system SHALL default to `error` (AC-1.4).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF both `between` and `pattern` present, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting conflicting fields (AC-1.5).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF neither `between` nor `pattern`, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting missing match spec (AC-1.6).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `between` missing `start` or `end`, the system SHALL exit code 1 naming the missing field (AC-1.7).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF regex fails to compile, the system SHALL exit code 1 with pattern and compilation error (AC-1.8).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF invalid `fallback`/`scope`/`position` value, the system SHALL exit code 1 listing valid options (AC-1.9, AC-1.15, AC-1.16).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `anchor.scope` omitted, the system SHALL default to `Line` (AC-1.17).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `anchor.position` omitted, the system SHALL default to `After` (AC-1.18).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1, Task 1.2\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.4: Update `jig validate`/`jig vars` output\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — `build_validate_json()` at line 382\\\\n\\\\nExtend match on `FileOp` variants to include Replace and Patch in validation JSON output. Template existence validation already works via `op.template()` (extended in Task 1.1).\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `jig validate` runs on a recipe with replace/patch ops, the system SHALL report operation counts including replace/patch.\\\\n- Event: WHEN `jig vars` runs on a recipe with replace/patch ops, the system SHALL succeed and display variables.\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL validate template file existence for replace and patch operations.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.3\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.5: Unit tests for all Phase 1 parsing paths\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n**Valid parsing tests (~10):**\\\\n- `parse_replace_between` — between.start + between.end → `ReplaceSpec::Between`\\\\n- `parse_replace_pattern` — pattern → `ReplaceSpec::Pattern`\\\\n- `parse_replace_fallback_variants` — all four values parse correctly\\\\n- `parse_replace_fallback_default` — omitted → `Fallback::Error`\\\\n- `parse_patch_full_anchor` — all anchor fields → `FileOp::Patch`\\\\n- `parse_patch_minimal` — just anchor.pattern → defaults scope=Line, position=After\\\\n- `parse_patch_with_skip_if` — skip_if captured\\\\n- `parse_patch_all_scope_types` — all 8 scope type strings\\\\n- `parse_patch_all_position_types` — all 7 position type strings\\\\n- `parse_replace_path_is_string` — `replace:` field is a path string\\\\n\\\\n**Error case tests (~13, each asserts exit code 1):**\\\\n- `reject_replace_both_between_and_pattern` (AC-1.5)\\\\n- `reject_replace_neither_between_nor_pattern` (AC-1.6)\\\\n- `reject_replace_between_missing_start` (AC-1.7)\\\\n- `reject_replace_between_missing_end` (AC-1.7)\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_regex_start` (AC-1.8)\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_regex_end` (AC-1.8)\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_regex_pattern` (AC-1.8)\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_fallback` (AC-1.9)\\\\n- `reject_patch_missing_anchor` (AC-1.13)\\\\n- `reject_patch_missing_anchor_pattern` (AC-1.13)\\\\n- `reject_patch_invalid_anchor_regex` (AC-1.14)\\\\n- `reject_patch_invalid_scope_type` (AC-1.15)\\\\n- `reject_patch_invalid_position_type` (AC-1.16)\\\\n\\\\n**Regression:** `existing_create_inject_still_parse`. Update old v0.1 rejection tests (lines 627-655).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.3\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 1 Validation\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- [ ] All 13 error cases AC-1.5 through AC-1.16 produce exit 1 with clear messages listing valid options\\\\n- [ ] Regex patterns compile-checked at parse time\\\\n- [ ] `jig validate` reports replace/patch operation counts\\\\n- [ ] Existing 191 tests unaffected\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 2: Replace Operation\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-2 (AC-2.1 through AC-2.14), FR-8 (partial), NFR-2\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 1. **Can run in parallel with Phase 3.**\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~25\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.1: Create `src/operations/replace.rs` — executor skeleton (P with 2.5)\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\n\\\\nSignature follows inject.rs pattern:\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn execute(\\\\n    rendered_path: &str,\\\\n    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n    spec: &ReplaceSpec,\\\\n    fallback: &Fallback,\\\\n    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n    verbose: bool,\\\\n) -> OpResult\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nCore pipeline: resolve path → read file (virtual_files then disk) → dispatch to match mode → handle no-match via fallback → replace matched region → write back (virtual_files always; disk if not dry-run) → return `OpResult::Success` with `action: \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"`.\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN replace succeeds, the system SHALL report `action: \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"` with path, location description, and line count (AC-2.9).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF target file missing, the system SHALL exit code 3 with rendered content (AC-2.10).\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL resolve target path relative to `--base-dir` (AC-2.12).\\\\n- State: WHILE `--dry-run` is enabled, the system SHALL read/write through `virtual_files` for chaining (AC-2.13).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF write fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-2.14).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.2: Implement `match_between()` (P with 2.3)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn match_between(lines: &[&str], start_pattern: &str, end_pattern: &str) -> Result<(usize, usize), NoMatch>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n- Find first line matching `start` regex\\\\n- No start match → `NoMatch` (for fallback)\\\\n- Find first line matching `end` regex **after** start\\\\n- No end after start → `OpResult::Error` with \\\\\\\"end marker not found after line N\\\\\\\" (AC-2.11)\\\\n- Return `(start_idx, end_idx)` — content between is exclusive (markers preserved, AC-2.7)\\\\n- Adjacent markers (start_idx + 1 == end_idx) → empty range, content inserted between (AC-2.8)\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `between.start` and `between.end` match, the system SHALL replace content between markers exclusively, preserving marker lines (AC-2.1, AC-2.7).\\\\n- Event: WHEN markers are adjacent, the system SHALL insert content between them (AC-2.8).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `between.end` not found after start, the system SHALL exit code 3 with start line number (AC-2.11).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.3: Implement `match_pattern()` (P with 2.2)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn match_pattern(lines: &[&str], pattern: &str) -> Result<(usize, usize), NoMatch>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n- Find first contiguous block of matching lines\\\\n- Return `(first_match, last_match)` — inclusive range, matched lines replaced entirely (AC-2.2)\\\\n- No match → `NoMatch` (for fallback)\\\\n\\\\n**Consensus:** Both plans agree: first contiguous block, not all matches (predictable, documented in SPEC).\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `pattern` matches, the system SHALL replace all contiguous matched lines with rendered content (AC-2.2).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.4: Implement fallback dispatch\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn apply_fallback(content: &str, rendered: &str, fallback: &Fallback, ...) -> OpResult\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n- `Append` → append content to end, return Success with location `\\\\\\\"fallback:append\\\\\\\"` (AC-2.3)\\\\n- `Prepend` → prepend content to start, return Success with location `\\\\\\\"fallback:prepend\\\\\\\"` (AC-2.4)\\\\n- `Skip` → return `OpResult::Skip` with reason \\\\\\\"pattern not found\\\\\\\" (AC-2.5)\\\\n- `Error` → return `OpResult::Error` with rendered content included (AC-2.6)\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: append`, the system SHALL append content (AC-2.3).\\\\n- Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: prepend`, the system SHALL prepend content (AC-2.4).\\\\n- Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: skip`, the system SHALL skip with reason (AC-2.5).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF no match and `fallback: error`, the system SHALL exit code 3 with rendered content (AC-2.6).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.2, Task 2.3\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.5: Wire replace dispatch\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/mod.rs` — add `pub mod replace;` and match arm in `execute_operation()`.\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — add match arm for `FileOp::Replace` in rendered_path block to render target path as Jinja2.\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN a recipe with replace operations is run, the system SHALL render the replace target path as Jinja2 (AC-1.19).\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL render template content before execution (D-1: render-then-execute).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.4\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.6: Unit tests for replace operation (P with 2.1)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n**Between mode (5):** replaces_exclusively, adjacent_markers_inserts, multiline_content, end_not_found, start_not_found.\\\\n**Pattern mode (3):** replaces_matched_lines, single_line, no_match.\\\\n**Fallback (5):** append, prepend, skip, error, error_is_default.\\\\n**Edge (5):** missing_target_file, dry_run_virtual_files, dry_run_chains_with_create, success_reports_action, verbose_includes_content.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.4\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.7: Integration test fixtures\\\\n\\\\n**Directory:** `tests/fixtures/` (9 new subdirectories)\\\\n\\\\n| Fixture | Purpose | Key ACs |\\\\n|---------|---------|---------|\\\\n| `replace-between` | Replace content between `# START` and `# END` markers | AC-2.1, AC-2.7 |\\\\n| `replace-between-empty` | Adjacent markers (insert into empty region) | AC-2.8 |\\\\n| `replace-pattern` | Replace lines matching regex pattern | AC-2.2 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-append` | Pattern not found, fallback appends | AC-2.3 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-prepend` | Pattern not found, fallback prepends | AC-2.4 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-skip` | Pattern not found, fallback skips | AC-2.5 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-error` | Pattern not found, fallback errors | AC-2.6 |\\\\n| `error-replace-no-match` | Between start not found, default fallback | AC-2.6, AC-N2.2 |\\\\n| `error-replace-end-missing` | Start found, end not found | AC-2.11 |\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.5\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 2 Validation\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- [ ] `between` mode preserves markers, replaces content between\\\\n- [ ] `pattern` mode replaces first contiguous matched block\\\\n- [ ] All four fallback behaviors work correctly\\\\n- [ ] Missing target file exits 3 with rendered content\\\\n- [ ] Dry-run mode uses virtual_files correctly\\\\n- [ ] Replace works alongside create and inject\\\\n- [ ] All 9 integration fixtures pass\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 3: Scope Detection\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-3 (AC-3.1-3.10), FR-4 (AC-4.1-4.12), FR-5 (AC-5.1-5.11), FR-6 (AC-6.1-6.5)\\\\n**Dependencies:** Task 1.1 only (ScopeType enum). **Can run in parallel with Phase 2.**\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~60\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.1: Create `src/scope/mod.rs` — dispatch and types (P with Phase 2)\\\\n\\\\n**New directory:** `src/scope/`\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/mod.rs`\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — add `mod scope;`\\\\n\\\\nDefine types:\\\\n- `ScopeResult` — `start_line: usize`, `end_line: usize`, `closing_line: Option<usize>`, `is_empty: bool`\\\\n- `PositionResult` — `insertion_line: usize`, `indent: String`, `fallback: Option<(String, String)>`\\\\n- `FindResult` — `found_line: usize`, `sub_scope: Option<ScopeResult>`\\\\n\\\\nImplement:\\\\n- `detect_scope(lines, anchor_line, scope_type)` — dispatches by ScopeType: `Line` → single-line scope; `Block/ClassBody/FunctionBody` → indent.rs; `Braces/Brackets/Parens/FunctionSignature` → delimiter.rs\\\\n- `find_within_scope(lines, scope, find_str)` — search within scope, auto-detect sub-scopes\\\\n- `detect_sub_scope(lines, line_idx)` — heuristic: trailing `[`, `{`, `(` or assignment with delimiter\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope_type` is `Line`, the system SHALL return a scope containing only the anchor line.\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL dispatch to indent or delimiter module based on scope type without language identifiers (AC-N1.3).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `find_within_scope` finds the string, the system SHALL return the line index (AC-6.1).\\\\n- Event: WHEN the found line opens a sub-scope, the system SHALL detect and return it (AC-6.2).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF find string not found in scope, the system SHALL return structured error with scope boundaries (AC-6.3).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.2: Create `src/scope/indent.rs` — indentation-based scope (P with 3.3, 3.4)\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/indent.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn detect_indent_scope(lines: &[&str], anchor_line: usize, scope_type: &ScopeType) -> Result<ScopeResult, StructuredError>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Algorithm:**\\\\n1. Measure anchor line indentation (raw whitespace prefix length)\\\\n2. For `ClassBody`/`FunctionBody`: body starts after declaration. Handle multi-line class declarations (scan for colon).\\\\n3. Walk forward from anchor+1: skip blank lines (AC-3.4), include deeper-indented lines, stop at same/shallower indentation\\\\n4. Empty scope (immediate dedent): `is_empty: true` (AC-3.5)\\\\n5. Nested classes/functions included in outer scope (AC-3.6, AC-3.7)\\\\n\\\\n**Edge cases:** mixed tabs/spaces (literal prefix comparison, AC-3.8), multi-line class declaration (AC-3.9), decorator before def/class (AC-3.10).\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: block`, the system SHALL include all lines indented deeper than anchor (AC-3.1).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: class_body`, the system SHALL identify body from first indented line to dedent (AC-3.2).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: function_body`, the system SHALL identify body from first indented line to dedent (AC-3.3).\\\\n- Event: WHEN blank lines appear within scope, the system SHALL include them (AC-3.4).\\\\n- Event: WHEN scope is empty, the system SHALL report `is_empty: true` (AC-3.5).\\\\n- Event: WHEN nested classes/functions appear, the system SHALL include them in outer scope (AC-3.6, AC-3.7).\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL measure indent by raw whitespace prefix length (AC-3.8).\\\\n- Event: WHEN Python class has multi-line declaration, the system SHALL start body after colon (AC-3.9).\\\\n- Event: WHEN decorator precedes def/class, the system SHALL use def/class indentation (AC-3.10).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.3: Create `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — delimiter-based scope (P with 3.2, 3.4)\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/delimiter.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn detect_delimiter_scope(lines: &[&str], anchor_line: usize, open: char, close: char) -> Result<ScopeResult, StructuredError>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Algorithm:**\\\\n1. Scan from `anchor_line` for first `open` delimiter (use it if on anchor line, AC-4.11)\\\\n2. Not found → error (AC-4.8)\\\\n3. Track nesting depth (start at 1 after opener)\\\\n4. Walk forward character-by-character via `CharScanner`:\\\\n   - **String literals:** `\\\\\\\"`, `'`, `` ` `` — skip to matching close, handle escape sequences (AC-4.6)\\\\n   - **Escaped delimiters:** `\\\\\\\\{`, `\\\\\\\\}`, etc. — skip (AC-4.12)\\\\n   - **Single-line comments:** `//` or `#` — skip rest of line (AC-4.7)\\\\n   - **Multi-line comments:** `/*` → skip until `*/` (AC-4.7)\\\\n   - Open → depth++; Close → depth--; depth=0 → found closing\\\\n5. Depth never reaches 0 → error with line number (AC-4.9)\\\\n6. Scope content between delimiters (exclusive, AC-4.1-4.4)\\\\n7. Empty scope (adjacent delimiters): `is_empty: true` (AC-4.10)\\\\n\\\\n**CharScanner struct:**\\\\n```rust\\\\nstruct CharScanner<'a> { lines: &'a [&'a str], line: usize, col: usize }\\\\n// Methods: advance(), peek(), skip_string_literal(), skip_line_comment(), skip_block_comment()\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: braces`, the system SHALL find `{`…`}` scope (AC-4.1).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: brackets`, the system SHALL find `[`…`]` scope (AC-4.2).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: parens`/`function_signature`, the system SHALL find `(`…`)` scope (AC-4.3, AC-4.4).\\\\n- Event: WHEN nested delimiters appear, the system SHALL track depth correctly (AC-4.5).\\\\n- Event: WHEN delimiters appear in strings, the system SHALL ignore them (AC-4.6).\\\\n- Event: WHEN delimiters appear in comments, the system SHALL ignore them (AC-4.7).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF opening delimiter not found, the system SHALL return error (AC-4.8).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF closing delimiter never found, the system SHALL return error with line number (AC-4.9).\\\\n- Event: WHEN scope is empty, the system SHALL report `is_empty: true` (AC-4.10).\\\\n- Event: WHEN opener is on anchor line, the system SHALL use it (AC-4.11).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.4: Create `src/scope/position.rs` — semantic position resolution (P with 3.2, 3.3)\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/position.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn resolve_position(lines: &[&str], scope: &ScopeResult, position: &Position) -> Result<PositionResult, StructuredError>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n| Position | Heuristic | Fallback |\\\\n|----------|-----------|----------|\\\\n| `Before` | insertion_line = scope.start_line | N/A |\\\\n| `After` | insertion_line = scope.end_line + 1 | N/A |\\\\n| `BeforeClose` | closing_line if exists, else end_line + 1 | N/A |\\\\n| `AfterLastField` | Last line matching `^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` | → `BeforeClose` (AC-5.8) |\\\\n| `AfterLastMethod` | Last line matching `^\\\\\\\\s+(pub\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn\\\\\\\\|async fn) \\\\\\\\w+` or `^\\\\\\\\s+def \\\\\\\\w+` etc., find complete body end via recursive scope detection | → `BeforeClose` (AC-5.9) |\\\\n| `AfterLastImport` | Last line matching `^\\\\\\\\s*(from\\\\\\\\|import) ` | → `Before` (AC-5.10) |\\\\n| `Sorted` | Alphabetically correct insertion among siblings at same indent | N/A |\\\\n\\\\nDetect indent from non-blank line within scope. Empty scope: anchor indent + one level (default 4 spaces).\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: before`, the system SHALL insert before scope start (AC-5.1).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: after`, the system SHALL insert after scope end (AC-5.2).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: before_close`, the system SHALL insert before closing delimiter (AC-5.3).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: after_last_field`, the system SHALL find last `^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` (AC-5.4).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: after_last_method`, the system SHALL find method's complete body end (AC-5.5, AC-5.11).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: after_last_import`, the system SHALL find last `^\\\\\\\\s*(from\\\\\\\\|import) ` (AC-5.6).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: sorted`, the system SHALL insert alphabetically (AC-5.7).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `after_last_field` finds no fields, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` with fallback note (AC-5.8).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `after_last_method` finds no methods, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` with fallback note (AC-5.9).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `after_last_import` finds no imports, the system SHALL fall back to `before` with fallback note (AC-5.10).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.5: Unit tests for indentation scope (P with 3.6, 3.7, 3.8)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/indent.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n11 tests: `python_class_body`, `python_function_body`, `blank_lines_within_scope`, `empty_scope`, `nested_class`, `nested_function`, `mixed_tabs_spaces`, `multiline_class_declaration`, `decorator_before_class`, `yaml_nested_block`, `deeply_nested_indent`.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.2\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.6: Unit tests for delimiter scope (P with 3.5, 3.7, 3.8)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n16 tests: `rust_struct_braces`, `typescript_interface_braces`, `go_function_braces`, `python_list_brackets`, `function_signature_parens`, `nested_braces`, `delimiters_in_double_quotes`, `delimiters_in_single_quotes`, `delimiters_in_backticks`, `delimiters_in_line_comments`, `delimiters_in_block_comments`, `escaped_delimiters`, `missing_opening_delimiter`, `unbalanced_delimiter`, `empty_braces`, `opener_on_anchor_line`.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.3\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.7: Unit tests for position resolution (P with 3.5, 3.6, 3.8)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/position.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n13 tests: `position_before`, `position_after`,
