The LLM wastes context window and latency *re-deriving* patterns that should be fixed.\njig.md:780:    mod.rs             # Library manifest parsing\njig.md:781:    install.rs         # Add/remove/update libraries\njig.md:783:    conventions.rs     # Convention mapping and overrides\njig.md:850:  def install\njig.md:851:    bin.install \"jig\"\njig.md:866:brew install <org>/tools/jig\njig.md:876:cargo install jig-cli\njig.md:925:This would be a thin npm package that downloads the correct platform binary on postinstall (same pattern as `esbuild`, `turbo`, `biome`). The npm package itself contains no logic — just a binary redirect.\njig.md:943:One-line install for README convenience:\njig.md:946:curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/<org>/jig/main/install.sh | sh\njig.md:1069:  jig-library.yaml            # library manifest\njig.md:1148:conventions:\njig.md:1207:The `conventions` block is crucial. It tells jig (and the LLM) where this library expects files to live. But real projects aren't always organized exactly the same way.\njig.md:1209:A project can override conventions in its `.jigrc.yaml`:\njig.md:1213:libraries:\njig.md:1215:    conventions:\njig.md:1258:jig library add https://github.com/someone/jig-django\njig.md:1260:# From a local directory (development / private libraries)\njig.md:1261:jig library add ./path/to/jig-django\njig.md:1263:# List installed libraries\njig.md:1264:jig library list\njig.md:1270:jig library remove django\njig.md:1273:Libraries are installed to `~/.jig/libraries/` (global) or `.jig/libraries/` (project-local). Project-local takes precedence.\njig.md:1278:# List all recipes in an installed library\njig.md:1290:jig ships with zero built-in libraries. The tool is framework-agnostic. Libraries are where framework opinions live:\njig.md:1313:  jig-library.yaml              # jig library manifest\njig.md:1315:    plugin.json                  # Claude Code plugin manifest\njig.md:1334:- The **library** provides the full collection, installable as one unit\njig.md:1336:This means a team can `jig library add jig-django` to get the CLI recipes, AND install the Claude Code plugin to get the LLM-powered skills that invoke them.\njig.md:1345:    libraries/\njig.md:1346:      django/          # installed library (read-only)\njig.md:1372:3. **Claude Code plugins** wrap libraries with LLM intelligence — the skill reads code and decides what to do, the recipe handles the mechanical work\njig.md:1373:4. **Project overrides** customize libraries for your team's conventions — no forks, no divergence\njig.md:1375:A new developer joins a Django team. They install the jig-django Claude Code plugin. Now they can say \"add a field called loyalty_tier to Reservation\" and get a consistent, team-compliant, multi-file change — without the LLM having to learn the team's patterns from scratch every time.\njig.md:1431:Every one of these is a **workflow** — a predictable cascade of small, mechanical changes across files. jig libraries capture these patterns once. The LLM provides the judgment (what to call it, what type it should be, where it fits). jig provides the execution (render, patch, create, inject).\njig.md:1720:This turns your team's conventions into enforceable rules — not through lint config files, but through the same recipe templates that generate the code. The source of truth is the recipe.\njig.md:1726:Polyglot workflows span multiple libraries:\njig.md:1887:The developer never has to decide \"I should create a recipe for this.\" They just work normally. jig observes, detects patterns, and proposes recipes when it has enough examples. The team's conventions emerge from their actual behavior, not from a spec document someone has to write and maintain.\njig.md:1929:- Library manifest format (`jig-library.yaml`)\njig.md:1930:- `jig library add/remove/update/list` commands\njig.md:1939:- `cargo install jig-cli`\njig.md:1941:- npm binary wrapper (platform-specific postinstall, like esbuild)\njig.md:1942:- Shell installer script\njig.md:1953:- Semver stability guarantee on recipe format, anchor/scope system, and library manifest\njig.md:1958:- At least 3 community libraries (django, vue/react, rails or fastapi)\njig.md:1977:- Type mapping definitions in library manifests\njig.md:1981:- Semver stability guarantee on recipe format, anchor/scope system, and library manifest\njig.md:1986:- At least 3 community libraries (django, vue/react, rails or fastapi)\njig.md:1995:- **Library registry** — a searchable index of community libraries (like crates.io or npm, but for jig libraries)\njig.md:2040:7. **`--help` with noun-verb structure.** Agents explore CLIs by running `--help`. A tree structure (`jig run`, `jig scan`, `jig check`, `jig library list`) is navigable by an LLM in 1-2 `--help` calls.\njig.md:2145:      \"description\": \"List all available recipes in an installed library\",\njig.md:2295:1. **Templates live with the consumer.** No central template directory. A skill, a project, a team — each owns their templates alongside their code. Libraries distribute collections, but they install locally.\njig.md:2307:7. **Layered ecosystem.** The engine is framework-agnostic. Libraries add framework knowledge. Claude Code plugins add LLM intelligence. Project overrides add team conventions. Each layer is optional, composable, and independently versioned.\njig.md:2311:9. **Small.** One binary. Twenty crates. Five-second install. If jig itself needs a tutorial, it's too complicated.\njig.md:2406:  Use jig to make these changes. The jig-django library is installed.\njig.md:2411:  You are working in a Django project. The `jig` CLI is installed and\njig.md:2501:3. A working `jig` binary on PATH with the relevant library installed\njig.md:2735:1. **`jig` mode** — the prompt tells the agent to use jig, the library is installed, recipes are available.\njig.md:2857:2. **Conventions match the library.** The fixture codebase follows jig-django's expected conventions (file paths, class naming, import style). This is what jig is designed for.\njig.md:2861:4. **Frozen dependencies.** No real Django installation. The fixture is just Python files with the right structure. I’ll first locate `SPEC.md`, `PLAN.md`, and the implementation/tests tied to this workstream, then map findings to EARS criteria and invariants.\\\"}}\\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/reviews/code-review-claude-20260404-165354.md:1:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"result\\\",\\\"subtype\\\":\\\"success\\\",\\\"is_error\\\":false,\\\"duration_ms\\\":262982,\\\"duration_api_ms\\\":510728,\\\"num_turns\\\":26,\\\"result\\\":\\\"Now I have a comprehensive view of the implementation. Let me compose the review.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Verdict: Request Changes**\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Critical (must fix before merge)\\\\n\\\\n**C1. `delimiter.rs:87` — Byte index used where char index is tracked: potential panic on non-ASCII**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet between = &lines[open_line][open_col + 1..scanner.col];\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n`CharScanner` tracks `col` in *character* positions (via `chars().nth()`, `chars().count()`), but this line slices the string with byte indices. For any file containing multi-byte UTF-8 characters (comments in Chinese/Japanese, emoji in strings, accented variable names), this will panic with `byte index N is not a char boundary`. The fix is to use `char_indices()` to convert char offsets to byte offsets before slicing.\\\\n\\\\n**C2. `operations/replace.rs:273-281` and `operations/patch.rs:224-231` — `write_back` silently swallows write errors**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet _ = std::fs::write(target, new_content);\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nIn the fallback paths (replace) and the main path (patch), disk write failures are silently ignored. The spec (AC-2.14, AC-7.11) requires exit code 3 with path, permission error, and rendered content. The main replace path at line 106 correctly returns an error on write failure, but `write_back()` used by fallback-append/prepend and *all* of patch does not. This means a permission-denied error during patch will return `OpResult::Success` — a silent corruption that violates I-10 (never silently produce wrong output).\\\\n\\\\n**C3. `scope/position.rs:169-185` — `Position::Sorted` is a stub, not an implementation**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn resolve_sorted(...) -> Result<PositionResult, StructuredError> {\\\\n    // For sorted, we don't have the content to sort against...\\\\n    let insertion = scope.closing_line.unwrap_or(scope.end_line + 1);\\\\n    Ok(PositionResult { insertion_line: insertion, ... })\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe spec (AC-5.7) requires: \\\\\\\"find the alphabetically correct insertion point among sibling lines at the same indentation level within the scope.\\\\\\\" This just inserts at end-of-scope. The `Sorted` variant is parsed and accepted as valid by the recipe parser, so a user can write `position: sorted` and get silently wrong behavior (appended, not sorted). Either implement it or reject it at parse time with a clear \\\\\\\"not yet implemented\\\\\\\" error.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Major (should fix)\\\\n\\\\n**M1. `scope/position.rs:62` — `AfterLastMethod` regex misses common Rust qualifiers**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet method_re = Regex::new(r\\\\\\\"^\\\\\\\\s+(pub\\\\\\\\s+)?(async\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\").unwrap();\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis misses `const fn`, `unsafe fn`, `pub(crate) fn`, `pub unsafe async fn`, `extern \\\\\\\"C\\\\\\\" fn`, and `pub const fn`. For a tool targeting Rust as a first-class citizen, this is a significant gap. A more robust pattern: `r\\\\\\\"^\\\\\\\\s+(pub(\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\([^)]*\\\\\\\\))?\\\\\\\\s+)?(const\\\\\\\\s+)?(unsafe\\\\\\\\s+)?(async\\\\\\\\s+)?(extern\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"[^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"]*\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn|def)\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\"`.\\\\n\\\\n**M2. `scope/position.rs:40` — `AfterLastField` regex `^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` requires leading whitespace**\\\\n\\\\nThe `\\\\\\\\s+` at the start means this never matches fields at indent level 0 (e.g., top-level struct fields in Go `Name string`, module-level Python `CONSTANT = 42`). Should be `^\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` if the intent is to work at any indentation, or the assumption should be documented.\\\\n\\\\n**M3. 11 of 26 spec-required integration fixtures are missing (42% gap)**\\\\n\\\\nMissing fixtures:\\\\n- `patch-function-body`, `patch-brackets`, `patch-function-signature`, `patch-find-narrowing`\\\\n- `patch-sorted`, `patch-indent-matching`, `patch-nested-delimiters`, `patch-string-delimiters`\\\\n- `error-patch-unbalanced`, `error-patch-find-missing`\\\\n- `combined-patch-idempotent`\\\\n\\\\nUnit tests cover most of these behaviors, but the spec explicitly requires fixture-level integration tests for each. The replace side is complete (7/7). The patch side is 4/11.\\\\n\\\\n**M4. `scope/delimiter.rs:171` — `peek_next` returns `None` when next line is empty**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\n} else if self.line + 1 < self.lines.len() && !self.lines[self.line + 1].is_empty() {\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nIf the character after the current position is on a blank line (common in real code — blank lines between struct fields), `peek_next` returns `None`. This breaks comment detection (`//`, `/*`) when `//` straddles a line boundary (unlikely but possible) and more importantly causes the scanner to not recognize patterns that span across blank lines correctly.\\\\n\\\\n**M5. No `ScopeDiagnostics` struct or verbose scope detail output (FR-8 incomplete)**\\\\n\\\\nThe spec (AC-8.1 through AC-8.5) requires verbose mode to include a `scope_diagnostics` object in JSON output with `anchor_line`, `scope_start`, `scope_end`, `insertion_line`, `find_match_line`, `position_fallback`. Currently verbose only adds the `rendered_content` string. The location string encodes some of this information as text, but the structured diagnostic object specified in the JSON Output Extensions section of the spec is absent.\\\\n\\\\n**M6. `operations/patch.rs:160-162` — Location string uses `Debug` formatting, producing `classbody` instead of `class_body`**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet scope_name = format!(\\\\\\\"{:?}\\\\\\\", anchor.scope).to_lowercase();\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n`#[derive(Debug)]` on `ScopeType::ClassBody` produces `\\\\\\\"ClassBody\\\\\\\"`, which `.to_lowercase()` turns into `\\\\\\\"classbody\\\\\\\"`. The spec's JSON Output Extensions show `\\\\\\\"class_body(^class Foo):after_last_field\\\\\\\"`. Similarly `AfterLastField` becomes `\\\\\\\"afterlastfield\\\\\\\"` instead of `\\\\\\\"after_last_field\\\\\\\"`. This affects both machine-parseable output and human readability. Implement `Display` for `ScopeType` and `Position` with snake_case output.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Minor (nice to fix)\\\\n\\\\n**m1. `scope/delimiter.rs` — `chars().count()` called on every `normalize()`, `peek()`, `advance()`**\\\\n\\\\n`self.lines[self.line].chars().count()` is O(n) per call. In the tight scanner loop, this means O(n^2) over a line. Cache the line length or use `len()` if sticking to ASCII-only files (which the byte-index bug in C1 already assumes).\\\\n\\\\n**m2. `operations/patch.rs:48` — `skip_if` uses substring match, not pattern match**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nif file_content.contains(skip_str) {\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nA `skip_if` value of `\\\\\\\"import\\\\\\\"` would match `\\\\\\\"important\\\\\\\"`. The inject operation uses the same approach, so this is consistent, but it's worth noting that this is a substring check, not a whole-line or word-boundary check. AC-7.2 says \\\\\\\"search for the rendered string\\\\\\\" so technically compliant, but a rendered value like `\\\\\\\"x\\\\\\\"` would skip on nearly any file.\\\\n\\\\n**m3. `operations/replace.rs:162-163` — `Regex::new().expect()` at runtime instead of using pre-compiled regex**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet start_re = Regex::new(start_pattern).expect(\\\\\\\"regex validated at parse time\\\\\\\");\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe recipe parser validates regex syntax at parse time, so the `.expect()` should never fire. But this compiles the regex fresh for every replace execution. Pre-compile during recipe parsing and pass the compiled `Regex` through to avoid the overhead (minor perf, but relevant since jig may be called dozens of times per LLM session per I-6).\\\\n\\\\n**m4. `scope/indent.rs` — `find_body_start` control flow is confusing**\\\\n\\\\nThe nested `if` / `continue` / `break` pattern is hard to follow. It works correctly but a refactor to use `match` or clearer early returns would improve maintainability.\\\\n\\\\n**m5. `operations/mod.rs:38-41` — `resolve_path` does no path traversal validation**\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn resolve_path(&self, relative: &str) -> PathBuf {\\\\n    self.base_dir.join(relative)\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nA recipe with `replace: \\\\\\\"../../etc/passwd\\\\\\\"` would resolve outside `base_dir`. This isn't a security issue per se (jig is a build tool run by trusted input), but it violates the principle of least surprise and could cause hard-to-debug errors.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Strengths\\\\n\\\\n- **Error-with-rendered-content contract is solid.** Every error path in both replace and patch includes the rendered template content. The I-10 invariant (never waste rendering work) is well-honored.\\\\n\\\\n- **Virtual files chaining is clean.** The virtual_files HashMap for dry-run and cross-operation chaining is simple, correct, and tested. Create → Replace → Patch chains work in both dry and wet modes.\\\\n\\\\n- **Recipe parsing is thorough.** All 19 FR-1 acceptance criteria are covered with explicit validation: regex compile-check, mutual exclusivity, default values, invalid enum rejection. The parser is the strongest part of the implementation.\\\\n\\\\n- **Scope detection architecture is well-separated.** `scope/` has zero dependency on the operation pipeline. It takes `&[&str]` and returns structs. This makes it trivially unit-testable and easy to reason about in isolation.\\\\n\\\\n- **Replace operation is complete.** All 14 AC-2.x criteria are implemented with matching fixtures. The between/pattern/fallback matrix is fully tested. This is merge-ready modulo the `write_back` error swallowing (C2).\\\\n\\\\n- **284 tests passing, zero failures.** The test suite is substantial and covers the critical paths well. The fixture auto-discovery mechanism means adding new fixtures requires zero test harness {\\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:8:Deliver the v0.2 jig operations: `replace` (swap regions in existing files) and `patch` (scope-aware insertion using anchors). This extends jig from greenfield scaffolding to brownfield code extension — the most common real-world use case. After this workstream, a recipe can add a field to a Django model class, a parameter to a service method, an entry to an admin list_display, and a factory attribute — all in one `jig run` invocation.\\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:16:- Code review completed — identified 3 critical, 8 major, 3 minor issues (see PLAN.md \\\"Review Findings\\\")\\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/SHARED-CONTEXT.md:25:| Scope type specified in recipe, not auto-detected from file extension | Language detection is fragile. The recipe author knows the target language. `scope: class_body` vs `scope: braces` is the right abstraction. |\\\\n| P1-T6 | 1.6 | Update `validate`/`vars` summaries to include replace/patch operation counts | [src/main.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/main.rs), [src/output.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/output.rs) | P1-T3 | (P) |\\\\n| P1-T7 | 1.7 | Add parser unit tests for success/failure matrix | [src/recipe.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/recipe.rs) | P1-T3 | - |\\\\n| P1-T8 | 1.7 | Add FR-1 spec tests (`spec::fr1::*`) for traceability | [tests/](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/) | P1-T7 | (P) |\\\\n\\\\nEARS acceptance criteria:\\\\n1. Ubiquitous: The system SHALL parse and validate `replace` and `patch` operations during recipe validation.\\\\n2. Event: WHEN a replace op provides `between.start` and `between.end`, the system SHALL parse `FileOp::Replace` with `ReplaceSpec::Between`.\\\\n3. Event: WHEN a replace op provides `pattern`, the system SHALL parse `FileOp::Replace` with `ReplaceSpec::Pattern`.\\\\n4. Event: WHEN `fallback` is omitted, the system SHALL default to `error`.\\\\n5. Unwanted: IF both `between` and `pattern` are provided, the system SHALL exit with code `1` and report conflicting fields.\\\\n6. Unwanted: IF neither `between` nor `pattern` is provided, the system SHALL exit with code `1` and report missing match specification.\\\\n7. Unwanted: IF `between`/`pattern`/`anchor.pattern` regex compilation fails, the system SHALL exit with code `1` and include the regex compilation error.\\\\n8. Event: WHEN `anchor.scope` is omitted, the system SHALL default to `line`.\\\\n9. Event: WHEN `anchor.position` is omitted, the system SHALL default to `after`.\\\\n10. Unwanted: IF `scope`/`position`/`fallback` values are invalid, the system SHALL exit with code `1` and list allowed values.\\\\n\\\\nPhase validation:\\\\n- `cargo test spec::fr1`\\\\n- `jig validate` passes for valid replace/patch recipes and fails with exit `1` for AC-1.5..1.16 cases\\\\n- Existing create/inject recipe tests remain green\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Phase 2: Replace Operation (FR-2, FR-8 partial)**\\\\n\\\\n| Task ID | Milestone | Concrete Task | Files | Depends On | Parallel |\\\\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\\\\n| P2-T1 | 2.1 | Implement replace executor skeleton (file load/write + dry-run virtual file support) | [src/operations/replace.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/replace.rs) | Phase 1 | - |\\\\n| P2-T2 | 2.2 | Implement `between` mode (exclusive content replacement, markers preserved) | [src/operations/replace.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/replace.rs) | P2-T1 | - |\\\\n| P2-T3 | 2.3 | Implement `pattern` mode (contiguous matched block replacement) | [src/operations/replace.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/replace.rs) | P2-T1 | (P) |\\\\n| P2-T4 | 2.4 | Implement fallback behaviors: append/prepend/skip/error | [src/operations/replace.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/replace.rs) | P2-T2, P2-T3 | - |\\\\n| P2-T5 | 2.5 | Wire replace dispatch | [src/operations/mod.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/mod.rs) | P2-T1 | (P) |\\\\n| P2-T6 | 2.6 | Wire prepared op rendering for `replace` target path/content in pipeline | [src/main.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/main.rs) | P2-T5 | - |\\\\n| P2-T7 | 2.7 | Add verbose replace diagnostics (mode, region lines, replaced count) in JSON + human output | [src/output.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/output.rs) | P2-T4 | (P) |\\\\n| P2-T8 | 2.8 | Unit tests for replace algorithm + dry-run chaining | [src/operations/replace.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/replace.rs) | P2-T4 | - |\\\\n| P2-T9 | 2.9 | Add replace integration fixtures (`replace-*`, `error-replace-*`) | [tests/fixtures/](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/) | P2-T4 | (P) |\\\\n\\\\nEARS acceptance criteria:\\\\n1. Event: WHEN `between.start` and `between.end` match, the system SHALL replace only the lines between them and preserve marker lines.\\\\n2. Event: WHEN between markers are adjacent, the system SHALL insert rendered content between them.\\\\n3. Event: WHEN `pattern` mode matches, the system SHALL replace the matched contiguous lines with rendered content.\\\\n4. Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: append` or `fallback: prepend`, the system SHALL write content at file end/start and report action `replace`.\\\\n5. Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: skip`, the system SHALL return action `skip` with reason.\\\\n6. Unwanted: IF no match and `fallback: error`, the system SHALL exit with code `3` and include rendered content.\\\\n7. Unwanted: IF target file is missing or write fails, the system SHALL exit with code `3` including path and rendered content.\\\\n8. State: WHILE `--dry-run` is enabled, the system SHALL read/write through `virtual_files` for operation chaining.\\\\n9. Option: WHERE `--verbose` is enabled, the system SHALL include replace diagnostics in JSON and human output.\\\\n\\\\nPhase validation:\\\\n- `cargo test spec::fr2`\\\\n- Fixture suite: `replace-between`, `replace-between-empty`, `replace-pattern`, `replace-fallback-*`, `error-replace-*`\\\\n- Dry-run chaining verified with create/inject preceding replace\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Phase 3: Scope Detection + Position Resolution (FR-3/4/5/6 core)**\\\\n\\\\n| Task ID | Milestone | Concrete Task | Files | Depends On | Parallel |\\\\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\\\\n| P3-T1 | 3.1 | Create scope public API (`ScopeResult`, `detect_scope`, `find_within_scope`) | [src/scope/mod.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/mod.rs) | Phase 1 | - |\\\\n| P3-T2 | 3.2 | Implement indentation scopes (`block`, `class_body`, `function_body`) including multiline class-header handling | [src/scope/indent.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/indent.rs) | P3-T1 | (P) |\\\\n| P3-T3 | 3.3 | Implement delimiter scopes (`braces`, `brackets`, `parens`, `function_signature`) with nesting/string/comment escaping | [src/scope/delimiter.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/delimiter.rs) | P3-T1 | (P) |\\\\n| P3-T4 | 3.4 | Implement semantic positions + fallback notes | [src/scope/position.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/position.rs) | P3-T2, P3-T3 | - |\\\\n| P3-T5 | 3.5 | Implement `find` narrowing and optional sub-scope rebasing | [src/scope/mod.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/mod.rs), [src/scope/delimiter.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/delimiter.rs) | P3-T2, P3-T3 | - |\\\\n| P3-T6 | 3.6 | Unit tests for indentation scopes | [src/scope/indent.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/indent.rs) | P3-T2 | (P) |\\\\n| P3-T7 | 3.7 | Unit tests for delimiter scopes | [src/scope/delimiter.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/delimiter.rs) | P3-T3 | (P) |\\\\n| P3-T8 | 3.8 | Unit tests for position heuristics and fallbacks | [src/scope/position.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/position.rs) | P3-T4 | (P) |\\\\n| P3-T9 | 3.9 | Unit tests for find narrowing and sub-scope resolution | [src/scope/mod.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/scope/mod.rs) | P3-T5 | (P) |\\\\n\\\\nEARS acceptance criteria:\\\\n1. Ubiquitous: The system SHALL determine scope boundaries from file content plus explicit `scope` type only.\\\\n2. Event: WHEN `scope: block/class_body/function_body` is requested, the system SHALL compute indentation-based boundaries using anchor indentation.\\\\n3. Event: WHEN blank lines occur inside indentation scope, the system SHALL include them and not terminate scope.\\\\n4. Event: WHEN `scope: braces/brackets/parens/function_signature` is requested, the system SHALL compute balanced-delimiter boundaries.\\\\n5. Event: WHEN nested delimiters appear, the system SHALL track depth and close at depth zero.\\\\n6. Event: WHEN delimiters appear in strings/comments/escaped form, the system SHALL ignore them for depth counting.\\\\n7. Unwanted: IF opening delimiter is missing or closing delimiter is unbalanced, the system SHALL return structured scope detection error.\\\\n8. Event: WHEN position is `before/after/before_close`, the system SHALL resolve insertion line accordingly.\\\\n9. Event: WHEN position is `after_last_field/after_last_method/after_last_import`, the system SHALL resolve to last semantic match in scope.\\\\n10. Unwanted: IF semantic match is absent, the system SHALL apply documented fallback (`before_close` or `before`) and record fallback note.\\\\n11. Event: WHEN `find` is present, the system SHALL re-anchor inside detected scope before position resolution.\\\\n12. Unwanted: IF `find` has no match in scope, the system SHALL return code `3` error context (scope range + find string).\\\\n\\\\nPhase validation:\\\\n- `cargo test spec::fr3 spec::fr4 spec::fr5 spec::fr6`\\\\n- Determinism check: repeat `detect_scope` test vectors and assert identical boundaries\\\\n- Cross-language unit snippets: Python, YAML, Rust, TS, Go, JSON\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Phase 4: Patch Operation + Verbose Diagnostics Completion (FR-6/7/8)**\\\\n\\\\n| Task ID | Milestone | Concrete Task | Files | Depends On | Parallel |\\\\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\\\\n| P4-T1 | 4.1 | Implement patch executor pipeline: anchor -> scope -> find -> position -> insert | [src/operations/patch.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/patch.rs) | Phase 1, Phase 3 | - |\\\\n| P4-T2 | 4.2 | Implement insertion indentation normalization (preserve relative indentation) | [src/operations/patch.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/patch.rs) | P4-T1 | - |\\\\n| P4-T3 | 4.3 | Implement `scope: line` degenerate behavior matching inject-after semantics | [src/operations/patch.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/patch.rs) | P4-T1 | (P) |\\\\n| P4-T4 | 4.4 | Wire patch dispatch | [src/operations/mod.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/mod.rs) | P4-T1 | (P) |\\\\n| P4-T5 | 4.5 | Wire prepared op rendering for `patch` target path + rendered `skip_if` | [src/main.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/main.rs) | P4-T4 | - |\\\\n| P4-T6 | 4.6 | Add `scope_diagnostics` output in JSON + human output | [src/output.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/output.rs) | P4-T1 | (P) |\\\\n| P4-T7 | 4.7 | Unit tests for patch pipeline, failures, dry-run virtual chaining | [src/operations/patch.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/operations/patch.rs) | P4-T2, P4-T3 | - |\\\\n| P4-T8 | 4.8 | Add patch integration fixtures (`patch-*`, `error-patch-*`) | [tests/fixtures/](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/) | P4-T1 | (P) |\\\\n\\\\nEARS acceptance criteria:\\\\n1. Event: WHEN a patch operation runs, the system SHALL execute anchor match, scope detection, optional find narrowing, position resolution, then insertion.\\\\n2. Event: WHEN `skip_if` is defined, the system SHALL render it with recipe variables and skip if found in target file.\\\\n3. Event: WHEN patch succeeds, the system SHALL report action `patch`, location summary, and inserted line count.\\\\n4. Event: WHEN anchor regex has multiple matches, the system SHALL use the first match.\\\\n5. Event: WHEN `scope: line` is used, the system SHALL insert after anchor line without structural scope analysis.\\\\n6. Event: WHEN inserting content, the system SHALL align base indentation to insertion context.\\\\n7. Unwanted: IF anchor/scope/find resolution fails, the system SHALL exit with code `3` and include rendered content plus what/where/why/hint.\\\\n8. State: WHILE `--dry-run` is enabled, the system SHALL update virtual files to preserve ordered operation chaining.\\\\n9. Option: WHERE `--verbose` is enabled, the system SHALL emit scope diagnostics in both JSON and human output.\\\\n\\\\nPhase validation:\\\\n- `cargo test spec::fr7 spec::fr8`\\\\n- Fixtures: `patch-class-body-field`, `patch-function-body`, `patch-braces`, `patch-find-narrowing`, `patch-skip-if`, `patch-scope-line`, `error-patch-*`\\\\n- Regression: all create/inject/replace tests remain green\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Phase 5: Full Validation, Integration, and Hardening (All FR/NFR)**\\\\n\\\\n| Task ID | Milestone | Concrete Task | Files | Depends On | Parallel |\\\\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|\\\\n| P5-T1 | 5.1 | Add `combined-all-ops` fixture (create+inject+replace+patch) | [tests/fixtures/combined-all-ops](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/combined-all-ops) | Phases 2, 4 | - |\\\\n| P5-T2 | 5.2 | Add `combined-patch-idempotent` two-run fixture | [tests/fixtures/combined-patch-idempotent](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/combined-patch-idempotent) | Phase 4 | (P) |\\\\n| P5-T3 | 5.3 | Add cross-language fixtures for scope behavior | [tests/fixtures/](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/) | Phase 4 | (P) |\\\\n| P5-T4 | 5.4 | Add deterministic rerun assertions (byte-identical output) | [tests/integration.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/integration.rs) | P5-T1 | - |\\\\n| P5-T5 | 5.5 | Add structured error + `rendered_content` assertions for every failure class | [tests/integration.rs](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/integration.rs) | P5-T1 | (P) |\\\\n| P5-T6 | 5.6 | Add/refresh `insta` snapshots for verbose JSON/human output | [tests/](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/) | P5-T1, P5-T3 | (P) |\\\\n| P5-T7 | 5.7 | Run final quality gates (`cargo test`, `cargo clippy`) and close traceability table status | [PLAN.md](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/PLAN.md), [SPEC.md](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/SPEC.md) | P5-T4, P5-T5, P5-T6 | - |\\\\n\\\\nEARS acceptance criteria:\\\\n1. Ubiquitous: The system SHALL preserve operation declaration order across mixed operation recipes.\\\\n2. Event: WHEN an idempotent patch recipe is run twice with same inputs, the system SHALL produce skips and no second-run file changes.\\\\n3. State: WHILE inputs are unchanged, the system SHALL produce byte-identical outputs across repeated runs.\\\\n4. Ubiquitous: The system SHALL include rendered content in all exit-code-3 replace/patch failures.\\\\n5. Option: WHERE `--verbose` is enabled, the system SHALL produce stable diagnostics suitable for snapshot tests in JSON and human modes.\\\\n6. Unwanted: IF any invariant regression occurs (I-1..I-10), the system SHALL block release of this workstream.\\\\n\\\\nPhase validation:\\\\n- `cargo test` full suite (unit + integration + snapshot)\\\\n- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -D warnings`\\\\n- Requirement traceability table moved from `PENDING` to `PASS` per FR/NFR\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Cross-Phase Dependencies and Parallel Lanes**\\\\n\\\\n1. Critical path: `Phase 1 -> Phase 4 -> Phase 5`.\\\\n2. Parallel lane A: `Phase 2` can run after Phase 1.\\\\n3. Parallel lane B: `Phase 3` can run after Phase 1.\\\\n4. Phase 4 merges outputs of lanes A/B only for final wiring/tests.\\\\n5. Fixture authoring tasks flagged `(P)` should be scheduled in parallel with algorithm implementation once interfaces stabilize.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]**\\\\n\\\\n1. [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED] Confirm replace `pattern` semantics: first contiguous block (current plan) vs all matches in file.\\\\n2. [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED] Confirm whether `scope_diagnostics` should be emitted only on success or also on `skip` and `error`.\\\\n3. [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED] Confirm supported delimiter parser edge cases for v0.2 (e.g., JS template literals with `${}` nesting, Rust raw strings) vs explicit documented limitation.\\\\n4. [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED] Confirm strictness of indentation normalization in mixed tab/space files (literal prefix model vs conversion to cargo build, cargo test, cargo clippy\\\"},{\\\"tool\\\":\\\"Bash\\\",\\\"prompt\\\":\\\"run git commands for status and diff\\\"},{\\\"tool\\\":\\\"Bash\\\",\\\"prompt\\\":\\\"list directory contents with ls\\\"}],\\\"plan\\\":\\\"# Libraries (v0.4) Implementation Plan\\\\n\\\\n> Workstream: libraries\\\\n> Status: Planning\\\\n> Depends on: workflows (v0.3, complete — 343 tests passing)\\\\n\\\\n## Context\\\\n\\\\njig is a template rendering CLI for LLM code generation. v0.1-v0.3 built the core engine (recipes, operations, workflows). v0.4 adds **libraries**: versioned recipe collections for a framework (e.g., `jig-django`), installable globally or per-project, with convention overrides and template customization. Libraries are the ecosystem layer — they're where framework opinions live, separate from the framework-agnostic engine.\\\\n\\\\nThe spec defines libraries in `jig.md` lines 1059-1376. The architecture slots them into `src/library/` (ARCHITECTURE.md Phase I).\\\\n\\\\n## Scope\\\\n\\\\n**In:** Manifest parsing, local + git install, remove, update, list, recipe/workflow discovery, namespaced resolution in `jig run`/`jig workflow`/`jig validate`/`jig vars`, convention system with `.jigrc.yaml` overrides, template overrides, project-local extensions.\\\\n\\\\n**Out:** Claude Code plugin integration (v0.6), cross-library workflows (v0.9), custom filters in `.jigrc.yaml` (future), `jig library link` for dev mode (future).\\\\n\\\\n## Key Decisions\\\\n\\\\n| ID | Decision | Rationale |\\\\n|----|----------|-----------|\\\\n| D-L1 | Shell out to `git` CLI for clone/pull | No new binary deps; git is ubiquitous on dev machines; only needed at install-time, not runtime. Clear error when git is absent. |\\\\n| D-L2 | Local install copies directory (not symlink) | Immutable after install — no surprise mutations. Symlink dev mode can be added later as `jig library link`. |\\\\n| D-L3 | Conventions injected as `{{ conventions.models }}` variable namespace | Reuses existing variable/template pipeline. Two-pass: render convention templates with recipe vars, then inject rendered paths into template context. |\\\\n| D-L4 | Library-namespaced resolution via slash syntax | `jig run django/model/add-field` — first segment is library name, rest is recipe path. Filesystem paths take precedence for backward compat. |\\\\n| D-L5 | No new error types or exit codes | Library errors map to existing JigError: exit 1 (manifest/validation), exit 3 (install/remove I/O), exit 4 (convention variable errors). Respects I-5. |\\\\n| D-L6 | `src/library/` module with submodules | Follows `src/operations/` and `src/scope/` patterns. Self-contained surface area. |\\\\n| D-L7 | `dirs` crate for home directory resolution | Tiny crate, zero transitive deps on macOS/Linux, handles platform differences. |\\\\n| D-L8 | `.jigrc.yaml` scoped to convention overrides only in v0.4 | Only feature libraries need. `base_dir`, `vars_file`, `filters` deferred. Unknown keys ignored. |\\\\n| D-L9 | Do NOT extend `detect_file_type()` for manifests | Library manifests are only processed by `jig library` commands, never by `jig run`/`workflow`. Different concept. |\\\\n| D-L10 | Inline manifest workflows reuse `Workflow` struct | Maximum reuse. Only difference is path resolution (relative to library root, not workflow file). |\\\\n\\\\n### [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]\\\\n\\\\n1. **Phase 0 scope**: The v0.3 review found critical/major bugs (C1, M1-M4). M4 (duplicated `run_recipe`) directly impacts v0.4 integration. Should Phase 0 fix all of C1+M1-M4, or just M4?\\\\n2. **`--global` flag on add/remove**: Spec says project-local by default. Should `jig library add --global` install to `~/.jig/libraries/`? Or should default be global (like npm -g pattern)?\\\\n3. **Convention rendering**: Two-pass (render `{{ app }}/models/{{ model }}.py` with vars, inject result as string) vs pass-through (inject raw template string, let recipe template render it inline)? Two-pass is cleaner for template authors but adds complexity.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 0: Tech Debt Consolidation\\\\n\\\\n**Goal:** Fix v0.3 review findings that impact library development. Clean foundation.\\\\n**Blocks:** All subsequent phases.\\\\n\\\\n### Tasks\\\\n\\\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\\\n| T-0.1 | **Consolidate `run_recipe()`** — refactor `cmd_run()` (`main.rs:269-455`) to call `workflow::run_recipe()` (`workflow.rs:551-619`) instead of duplicating the rendering pipeline. `cmd_run` keeps CLI concerns (var collection, output formatting); rendering moves entirely to `run_recipe`. | `src/main.rs`, `src/workflow.rs` | — |\\\\n| T-0.2 | **Fix `extract_rendered_from_error`** (C1) — `workflow.rs:621-628` returns `se.what.clone()` (error description) instead of actual rendered template content. Extract from `OpResult::Error.rendered_content` in partial results instead. | `src/workflow.rs` | (P) |\\\\n| T-0.3 | **Fix `format_workflow_json` status logic** (M1) — `output.rs:300-302` derives status from `result.on_error` but ignores step-level overrides. Derive from `exit_code` parameter instead. | `src/output.rs` | (P) |\\\\n| T-0.4 | **Fix workflow early-error JSON** (M3) — `main.rs:477` calls `e.structured_error()` losing all but first validation error. Use `e.structured_errors()` to emit all. | `src/main.rs` | (P) |\\\\n\\\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\\\n\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-0.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce byte-identical output for all 343 existing tests after `run_recipe` consolidation |\\\\n| AC-0.2 | Event | WHEN a workflow step fails with a rendering error AND partial results contain rendered content, the JSON `rendered_content` field SHALL contain the actual rendered template, not the error description |\\\\n| AC-0.3 | Event | WHEN a workflow step has `on_error: report` overriding workflow-level `stop`, AND the step fails, JSON status SHALL be `\\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\"` (not `\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"`) |\\\\n| AC-0.4 | Event | WHEN workflow variable validation produces N errors, the JSON error output SHALL include all N errors |\\\\n\\\\n### Gate\\\\nAll 343 tests pass. `cargo clippy` clean. No behavioral changes visible through existing fixtures.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 1: Library Manifest & Storage\\\\n\\\\n**Goal:** Parse `jig-library.yaml`, define storage paths, resolve home dir.\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 0.\\\\n\\\\n### Tasks\\\\n\\\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\\\n| T-1.1 | Add `dirs = \\\\\\\"6\\\\\\\"` to `Cargo.toml` | `Cargo.toml` | (P) |\\\\n| T-1.2 | **Create `src/library/mod.rs`** — module root with `LibraryPaths` struct: `global_dir` (`~/.jig/libraries/`), `project_dir` (`.jig/libraries/`). `LibraryPaths::resolve(project_root)` finds both. `LibraryPaths::find_library(name) -> Option<PathBuf>` checks project-local first, then global. Register `mod library` in `main.rs`. | `src/library/mod.rs`, `src/main.rs` | — |\\\\n| T-1.3 | **Create `src/library/manifest.rs`** — `LibraryManifest` struct with serde: name (required), version (required), description, framework, language, conventions (`IndexMap<String, String>`), recipes (`IndexMap<String, String>`), workflows (`IndexMap<String, RawLibraryWorkflow>`). `LibraryManifest::load(path) -> Result<Self, JigError>`. Validates required fields, checks declared recipe directories exist. | `src/library/manifest.rs` | — (after T-1.2) |\\\\n| T-1.4 | **Unit tests** — valid manifest, minimal manifest, missing name/version, invalid YAML, missing recipe dir, path resolution precedence, `find_library` returns None when not installed | `src/library/mod.rs`, `src/library/manifest.rs` | — (after T-1.3) |\\\\n\\\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\\\n\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-1.1 | Event | WHEN a valid `jig-library.yaml` is loaded, the system SHALL parse all fields into `LibraryManifest` |\\\\n| AC-1.2 | Unwanted | IF `jig-library.yaml` is missing `name`, the system SHALL return exit 1 with hint \\\\\\\"add a 'name' field\\\\\\\" |\\\\n| AC-1.3 | Unwanted | IF `jig-library.yaml` is missing `version`, the system SHALL return exit 1 with hint \\\\\\\"add a 'version' field\\\\\\\" |\\\\n| AC-1.4 | Unwanted | IF a declared recipe path does not exist as a subdirectory with `recipe.yaml`, the system SHALL return exit 1 identifying the missing recipe |\\\\n| AC-1.5 | Event | WHEN a library exists in both project-local and global, `find_library` SHALL return the project-local path |\\\\n| AC-1.6 | Unwanted | IF `dirs::home_dir()` returns None, the system SHALL skip global search without error |\\\\n\\\\n### Gate\\\\n`cargo test` passes with ~12 new unit tests. Manifest parses all spec-defined fields.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 2: Local Install & Management CLI\\\\n\\\\n**Goal:** `jig library add <path>`, `jig library remove <name>`, `jig library list`.\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1.\\\\n\\\\n### Tasks\\\\n\\\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\\\n| T-2.1 | **Create `src/library/install.rs`** — `install_from_path(source, target_dir) -> Result<PathBuf, JigError>`: validate manifest, copy directory recursively, error if already exists. `remove_library(name, storage)`: find and delete. `list_libraries(storage) -> Vec<InstalledLibrary>`: scan both dirs, parse manifests, dedup (project-local wins). | `src/library/install.rs` | — |\\\\n| T-2.2 | **Add `Library` subcommand** — nested `LibraryCommands` enum with `Add { source: String, #[arg(long)] global: bool }`, `Remove { name: String }`, `List`. Implement `cmd_library()` dispatch, `cmd_library_add()`, `cmd_library_remove()`, `cmd_library_list()`. | `src/main.rs` | (P with T-2.1) |\\\\n| T-2.3 | **Output formatting** — `format_library_list_json()` and `format_library_list_human()` in output.rs. JSON: `{\\\\\\\"libraries\\\\\\\": [{name, version, description, source, recipes, workflows}]}`. Human: colored table to stderr. | `src/output.rs` | (P with T-2.1) |\\\\n| T-2.4 | **Tests** — unit: install valid, install duplicate error, install no manifest, remove success, remove not found, list empty, list dedup. CLI integration: `jig library add ./path`, `jig library list --json`, `jig library remove name`. | `src/library/install.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-2.1-2.3) |\\\\n\\\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\\\n\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-2.1 | Event | WHEN `jig library add ./path` is run AND path has valid manifest, the system SHALL copy to `.jig/libraries/<name>/` |\\\\n| AC-2.2 | Unwanted | IF `./path/jig-library.yaml` does not exist, the system SHALL return exit 1 |\\\\n| AC-2.3 | Unwanted | IF library already installed at target, the system SHALL return exit 3 with hint to remove first |\\\\n| AC-2.4 | Event | WHEN `jig library add --global` is run, the system SHALL install to `~/.jig/libraries/<name>/` |\\\\n| AC-2.5 | Event | WHEN `jig library remove <name>` is run AND library exists, the system SHALL delete the directory |\\\\n| AC-2.6 | Event | WHEN `jig library list --json` is run, the system SHALL output JSON with name, version, source, recipe/workflow counts |\\\\n| AC-2.7 | Event | WHEN same library exists in both scopes, `list` SHALL show project-local and annotate as overriding global |\\\\n\\\\n### Gate\\\\nEnd-to-end local install, list, remove. JSON output matches schema. All pre-existing tests pass.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 3: Discovery & Namespaced Resolution\\\\n\\\\n**Goal:** `jig library recipes/workflows/info`. Make `jig run django/model/add-field` work.\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 2.\\\\n\\\\n### Tasks\\\\n\\\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\\\n| T-3.1 | **Create `src/library/discover.rs`** — `list_recipes(manifest, lib_dir) -> Vec<RecipeInfo>`, `list_workflows(manifest) -> Vec<WorkflowInfo>`, `recipe_info(manifest, lib_dir, path) -> Result<RecipeDetail, JigError>`. Load actual recipes for variable/operation details. | `src/library/discover.rs` | — |\\\\n| T-3.2 | **Create `src/library/resolve.rs`** — `resolve_recipe_path(input, storage) -> Result<ResolvedRecipe, JigError>` and `resolve_workflow_path(input, storage) -> Result<ResolvedWorkflow, JigError>`. Returns `FilePath(PathBuf)` for filesystem paths, `Library { library_name, recipe_path, resolved_path, library_dir }` for library refs. Filesystem paths take precedence (backward compat). | `src/library/resolve.rs` | (P with T-3.1) |\\\\n| T-3.3 | **Add discovery subcommands** — `Recipes { name }`, `Workflows { name }`, `Info { path }` to `LibraryCommands`. Output formatting in `output.rs`. | `src/main.rs`, `src/output.rs` | (P with T-3.1) |\\\\n| T-3.4 | **Integrate resolution into `cmd_run`, `cmd_workflow`, `cmd_validate`, `cmd_vars`** — Change `Commands::Run { recipe: PathBuf }` to `Commands::Run { recipe: String }`. In each handler, call `resolve_recipe_path()` first. If `ResolvedRecipe::Library`, use resolved path. Same for workflow. This is the highest-risk integration point. | `src/main.rs` | — (after T-3.2) |\\\\n| T-3.5 | **`load_library_workflow()`** — construct `Workflow` from inline manifest workflow definition, resolving recipe paths relative to library root (not workflow file). | `src/library/resolve.rs` or `src/workflow.rs` | — (after T-3.2) |\\\\n| T-3.6 | **Tests** — unit: resolve filesystem path (unchanged), resolve library recipe, resolve library workflow, library not found, recipe not found in library, ambiguous path (filesystem wins). Integration: install lib + run recipe, install lib + validate, install lib + list recipes. | `src/library/resolve.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-3.4) |\\\\n\\\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\\\n\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-3.1 | Event | WHEN `jig run django/model/add-field --vars '...'` is run AND `django` is installed with that recipe, the system SHALL execute the library recipe |\\\\n| AC-3.2 | Event | WHEN `jig run ./path/recipe.yaml` is run AND path exists, the system SHALL use filesystem resolution regardless of library names (backward compat) |\\\\n| AC-3.3 | Unwanted | IF `jig run nonexistent/recipe` is run AND no library `nonexistent` is installed, the system SHALL return exit 1 listing installed libraries |\\\\n| AC-3.4 | Event | WHEN `jig library recipes django --json` is run, the system SHALL output all recipes with path, description, qualified_name |\\\\n| AC-3.5 | Event | WHEN `jig library info django/model/add-field --json` is run, the system SHALL output variables, operations, description |\\\\n| AC-3.6 | Event | WHEN `jig workflow django/add-field --vars '...'` is run, the system SHALL resolve from manifest and execute via existing workflow engine |\\\\n| AC-3.7 | Event | WHEN `jig validate django/model/add-field` is run, the system SHALL validate the library recipe |\\\\n\\\\n### Gate\\\\nAll existing commands accept library-namespaced paths. Filesystem paths still work unchanged. Discovery commands produce correct output. All pre-existing tests pass.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 4: Convention System\\\\n\\\\n**Goal:** Parse `.jigrc.yaml`, inject conventions into library recipe execution.\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 3.\\\\n\\\\n### Tasks\\\\n\\\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\\\n| T-4.1 | **Create `src/library/conventions.rs`** — `ProjectConfig` struct for `.jigrc.yaml` (scoped to `libraries.<name>.conventions`). `load_project_config(root) -> Result<Option<ProjectConfig>, JigError>`. Missing file returns `None`, malformed returns exit 1. Unknown keys ignored. | `src/library/conventions.rs` | — |\\\\n| T-4.2 | **Convention resolution** — `resolve_conventions(manifest, overrides, vars) -> Result<Value, JigError>`. Start with manifest conventions, apply overrides, render each template with recipe vars using minijinja, return `{\\\\\\\"models\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"/rendered/path\\\\\\\", ...}`. Rendering errors map to exit 4. | `src/library/conventions.rs` | — (after T-4.1) |\\\\n| T-4.3 | **Inject conventions into execution** — Add `library_ctx: Option<&LibraryContext>` to `run_recipe()` signature. When present, resolve conventions and add `conventions` key to var context before rendering. `LibraryContext` holds `library_dir`, `manifest`, `overrides`. Non-library recipes pass `None` (zero behavioral change). | `src/workflow.rs`, `src/main.rs` | — (after T-4.2) |\\\\n| T-4.4 | **Tests** — unit: parse valid `.jigrc.yaml`, missing file returns None, malformed returns error, convention resolution with overrides, convention rendering with vars, missing var returns exit 4. Integration: library recipe using `{{ conventions.models }}`, `.jigrc.yaml` override applied. | `src/library/conventions.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-4.3) |\\\\n\\\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\\\n\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-4.1 | Event | WHEN a library recipe template uses `{{ conventions.models }}`, the system SHALL render the convention template with recipe vars and inject the result |\\\\n| AC-4.2 | Event | WHEN `.jigrc.yaml` overrides `libraries.django.conventions.models`, the system SHALL use the override instead of the manifest convention |\\\\n| AC-4.3 | State | WHILE `.jigrc.yaml` does not exist, the system SHALL use manifest conventions without error |\\\\n| AC-4.4 | Unwanted | IF a convention template references an unprovided variable, the system SHALL return exit 4 identifying the convention and missing variable |\\\\n| AC-4.5 | Ubiquitous | WHEN a non-library recipe is executed, the system SHALL NOT inject conventions (no behavioral change) |\\\\n\\\\n### Gate\\\\nConvention injection works end-to-end. Overrides apply. Non-library recipes unaffected. All pre-existing tests pass.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 5: Git Install & Update\\\\n\\\\n**Goal:** `jig library add <git-url>`, `jig library update <name>`.\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 2. **Can run in parallel with Phase 4.**\\\\n\\\\n### Tasks\\\\n\\\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\\\n| T-5.1 | **URL detection** — `is_git_url(source) -> bool` in `install.rs`. Matches `https://`, `git@`, `ssh://`, `.git` suffix. Everything else is local path. | `src/library/install.rs` | — |\\\\n| T-5.2 | **Git clone install** — `install_from_git(url, target_dir) -> Result<PathBuf, JigError>`. `git clone --depth 1` to tempdir, validate manifest, copy to target, store URL in `.jig-source` metadata file. Git not found -> exit 3 with hint. Clone failure -> exit 3 with git stderr. | `src/library/install.rs` | — (after T-5.1) |\\\\n| T-5.3 | **Update command** — `Update { name: String }` in `LibraryCommands`. Read `.jig-source` for git URL, clone latest, replace installed library. No `.jig-source` (local install) -> exit 1 with hint. | `src/main.rs`, `src/library/install.rs` | — (after T-5.2) |\\\\n| T-5.4 | **Tests** — unit: URL detection patterns, git not found error. Integration: init local git repo, `jig library add file:///...`, update after commit. | `src/library/install.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-5.3) |\\\\n\\\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\\\n\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-5.1 | Event | WHEN `jig library add https://...` is run AND git is available, the system SHALL clone and install |\\\\n| AC-5.2 | Unwanted | IF git is not in PATH, the system SHALL return exit 3 with hint \\\\\\\"git is required for URLs\\\\\\\" |\\\\n| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `jig library update django` is run AND django was git-installed, the system SHALL clone latest and replace |\\\\n| AC-5.4 | Unwanted | IF `jig library update django` is run AND django was locally installed, the system SHALL return exit 1 |\\\\n| AC-5.5 | Unwanted | IF cloned repo has no `jig-library.yaml`, the system SHALL return exit 1 |\\\\n\\\\n### Gate\\\\nGit install and update work with local `file://` repos. Clear errors when git unavailable. All pre-existing tests pass.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 6: Template Overrides & Extensions\\\\n\\\\n**Goal:** Project-local template overrides and recipe extensions.\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 3 + Phase 4.\\\\n\\\\n### Tasks\\\\n\\\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\\\n| T-6.1 | **Template override resolution** — When resolving library recipe templates, check `.jig/overrides/<lib>/<recipe>/templates/<template>` first. Override replaces individual templates, not the recipe. Add `override_dir: Option<PathBuf>` to template loading in `renderer.rs` or create `create_library_recipe_env()` variant. | `src/renderer.rs`, `src/library/resolve.rs` | — |\\\\n| T-6.2 | **Extension resolution** — In `resolve_recipe_path()`, check `.jig/extensions/<lib>/<recipe>/recipe.yaml` before library directory. Extensions are full recipe bundles. In `list_recipes()`, include extensions annotated as \\\\\\\"(extension)\\\\\\\". | `src/library/resolve.rs`, `src/library/discover.rs` | (P with T-6.1) |\\\\n| T-6.3 | **Tests** — unit: override replaces single template, other templates from library, extension found and executed, extension in recipe list. Integration: install lib + create override + run recipe, install lib + create extension + run. | `src/library/resolve.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-6.1-6.2) |\\\\n\\\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\\\n\\\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\\\n|----|------|-----------|\\\\n| AC-6.1 | Event | WHEN `.jig/overrides/django/model/add-field/templates/model_field.j2` exists, `jig run django/model/add-field` SHALL use the override template |\\\\n| AC-6.2 | Event | WHEN an override exists for one template, the system SHALL use library originals for all other templates |\\\\n| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN `.jig/extensions/django/model/add-audit-fields/recipe.yaml` exists, `jig library recipes django` SHALL include it as an extension |\\\\n| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `jig run django/model/add-audit-fields` matches an extension, the system SHALL execute the extension recipe |\\\\n| AC-6.5 | State | WHILE no `.jig/overrides/` or `.jig/extensions/` exist, the system SHALL operate identically to pre-override behavior |\\\\n\\\\n### Gate\\\\nOverrides and extensions work end-to-end. All pre-existing tests pass.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Execution Order\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nPhase 0 (tech debt) ─────────────────────────────┐\\\\n                                                  v\\\\nPhase 1 (manifest + storage) ────────────────────>│\\\\n                                                  v\\\\nPhase 2 (local install + CLI) ───────────────────>│\\\\n                                                  │\\\\n              ┌───────────────────────────────────>│\\\\n              │                                    │\\\\nPhase 3 (discovery + namespaced resolution) ──────┤\\\\n              │                                    │\\\\n              ├──> Phase 4 (conventions) ──────────┤\\\\n              │                                    │\\\\n              └──> Phase 5 (git install) ──────────┤  (4 & 5 parallel)\\\\n                                                   │\\\\nPhase 6 (overrides + extensions) ──────────────────┘\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n## New Files\\\\n\\\\n| File | Phase | Purpose |\\\\n|------|-------|---------|\\\\n| `src/library/mod.rs` | 1 | Module root, `LibraryPaths`, `LibraryContext` |\\\\n| `src/library/manifest.rs` | 1 | `LibraryManifest` parsing |\\\\n| `src/library/install.rs` | 2, 5 | Add, remove, update, list |\\\\n| `src/library/discover.rs` | 3 | Recipe/workflow enumeration |\\\\n| `src/library/resolve.rs` | 3, 6 | Namespaced resolution, overrides, extensions |\\\\n| `src/library/conventions.rs` | 4 | Convention resolution, `.jigrc.yaml` |\\\\n\\\\n## Modified Files\\\\n\\\\n| File | Phases | Changes |\\\\n|------|--------|---------|\\\\n| `Cargo.toml` | 1 | Add `dirs` |\\\\n| `src/main.rs` | 0, 2, 3, 4 | Consolidate `run_recipe`, add `Library` command, resolution integration, `Run.recipe: PathBuf` -> `String` |\\\\n| `src/workflow.rs` | 0, 4 | Fix C1, thread `LibraryContext` through `run_recipe` |\\\\n| `src/output.rs` | 0, 2, 3 | Fix M1, library output formatters |\\\\n| `src/renderer.rs` | 6 | Template override support |\\\\n| `tests/cli.rs` | 2, 3, 5 | Library CLI tests |\\\\n\\\\n## Risks\\\\n\\\\n| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |\\\\n|------|----------|------------|\\\\n| `Commands::Run { recipe: PathBuf }` -> `String` changes clap validation | HIGH | Resolution function checks filesystem first. Regression tests for all error paths. |\\\\n| Convention two-pass rendering cycle risk | MEDIUM | Conventions rendered with recipe vars only, never reference other conventions. Document restriction. |\\\\n| Library manifest workflows need different path resolution than `load_workflow()` | MEDIUM | `load_library_workflow()` constructs Workflow struct with paths relative to library root. |\\\\n| Integration test fixtures need installed libraries | MEDIUM | Test setup installs fixture library to temp `.jig/libraries/` before running commands. |\\\\n\\\\n## Verification\\\\n\\\\nAfter each phase:\\\\n1. `cargo test` — all existing + new tests pass\\\\n2. `cargo clippy` — clean\\\\n3. Manual smoke test of the phase's deliverable\\\\n\\\\nAfter all phases:\\\\n```bash\\\\n# Full lifecycle test\\\\njig library add ./test-fixtures/jig-django\\\\njig library list --json\\\\njig library recipes django\\\\njig library info django/model/add-field\\\\njig run django/model/add-field --vars '{\\\\\\\"app\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"hotels\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"model\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"Reservation\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"field_name\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"loyalty_tier\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"field_type\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"CharField\\\\\\\"}' --dry-run\\\\njig library remove django\\\\n```\\\\n\\\",\\\"planFilePath\\\":\\\"/Users/tylerobriant/.claude/plans/kind-floating-crescent.md\\\"}}],\\\"terminal_reason\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"fast_mode_state\\\":\\\"off\\\",\\\"uuid\\\":\\\"f2877490-4c7f-4a2f-a494-a32ec94f7304\\\"}\\ndocs/INVARIANTS.md:3:Project-wide constraints. Every workstream must respect these. If a change would violate an invariant, it requires explicit discussion and sign-off before proceeding.\\n./jig.md:781:    install.rs         # Add/remove/update libraries\\n./jig.md:936:jig-v1.0.0-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip\\n./jig.md:1213:libraries:\\n./jig.md:1260:# From a local directory (development / private libraries)\\n./jig.md:1263:# List installed libraries\\n./jig.md:1273:Libraries are installed to `~/.jig/libraries/` (global) or `.jig/libraries/` (project-local). Project-local takes precedence.\\n./jig.md:1290:jig ships with zero built-in libraries. The tool is framework-agnostic. Libraries are where framework opinions live:\\n./jig.md:1345:    libraries/\\n./jig.md:1372:3. **Claude Code plugins** wrap libraries with LLM intelligence — the skill reads code and decides what to do, the recipe handles the mechanical work\\n./jig.md:1373:4. **Project overrides** customize libraries for your team's conventions — no forks, no divergence\\n./jig.md:1431:Every one of these is a **workflow** — a predictable cascade of small, mechanical changes across files. jig libraries capture these patterns once. The LLM provides the judgment (what to call it, what type it should be, where it fits). jig provides the execution (render, patch, create, inject).\\n./jig.md:1726:Polyglot workflows span multiple libraries:\\n./jig.md:1958:- At least 3 community libraries (django, vue/react, rails or fastapi)\\n./jig.md:1986:- At least 3 community libraries (django, vue/react, rails or fastapi)\\n./jig.md:1995:- **Library registry** — a searchable index of community libraries (like crates.io or npm, but for jig libraries)\\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/exec/spec-population-20260404-145638.md:1:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"result\\\",\\\"subtype\\\":\\\"success\\\",\\\"is_error\\\":false,\\\"duration_ms\\\":1045359,\\\"duration_api_ms\\\":1043681,\\\"num_turns\\\":23,\\\"result\\\":\\\"All four workstream documents are populated. Here's what was written:\\\\n\\\\n**SPEC.md** — 8 functional requirements with 88 EARS acceptance criteria:\\\\n- FR-1: Recipe parsing extensions (19 ACs) — parse replace/patch into `FileOp` variants\\\\n- FR-2: Replace operation (14 ACs) — between markers, pattern match, 4 fallback modes\\\\n- FR-3: Indentation-based scope detection (10 ACs) — block, class_body, function_body\\\\n- FR-4: Delimiter-based scope detection (12 ACs) — braces, brackets, parens, function_signature\\\\n- FR-5: Semantic position resolution (11 ACs) — 7 position types with fallback behavior\\\\n- FR-6: Find narrowing (5 ACs) — sub-scope detection within larger scopes\\\\n- FR-7: Patch operation (12 ACs) — full anchor pipeline, skip_if, indentation matching\\\\n- FR-8: Verbose scope diagnostics (5 ACs) — scope boundaries in JSON and human output\\\\n- 4 NFRs: language-agnostic scopes, graceful degradation, idempotency, determinism\\\\n\\\\n**PLAN.md** — 5 phases with concrete milestones:\\\\n1. Recipe parsing extensions (7 milestones)\\\\n2. Replace operation (9 milestones)\\\\n3. Scope detection (9 milestones) — parallelizable with Phase 2\\\\n4. Patch operation (8 milestones)\\\\n5. Synthesized Execution Plan: replace-patch workstream\\\\n\\\\n> Generated: 2026-04-04\\\\n> Status: Ready for review\\\\n> Sources: Claude plan (Plan A), Codex plan (Plan B)\\\\n> Codebase state: 191 tests passing, 24 integration fixtures, `regex` crate present\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Execution Order\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nPhase 1 (recipe parsing)\\\\n   |\\\\n   +-----> Phase 2 (replace)  --------+\\\\n   |           (parallel)             |\\\\n   +-----> Phase 3 (scope)  -+       |\\\\n                              |       |\\\\n                              v       v\\\\n                          Phase 4 (patch)\\\\n                              |\\\\n                              v\\\\n                          Phase 5 (integration)\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Critical path:** Phase 1 → Phase 3 → Phase 4 → Phase 5\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 1: Recipe Parsing Extensions\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-1 (AC-1.1 through AC-1.19)\\\\n**Dependencies:** None\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~35\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.1: Define new types in `src/recipe.rs`\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` (after line 89, below existing `FileOp` enum)\\\\n\\\\nAdd types:\\\\n- `ReplaceSpec` enum — `Between { start: String, end: String }`, `Pattern(String)`\\\\n- `Fallback` enum — `Append`, `Prepend`, `Skip`, `Error` (default)\\\\n- `Anchor` struct — `pattern: String`, `scope: ScopeType`, `find: Option<String>`, `position: Position`\\\\n- `ScopeType` enum — 8 variants: `Line` (default), `Block`, `ClassBody`, `FunctionBody`, `FunctionSignature`, `Braces`, `Brackets`, `Parens`\\\\n- `Position` enum — 7 variants: `Before`, `After` (default), `BeforeClose`, `AfterLastField`, `AfterLastMethod`, `AfterLastImport`, `Sorted`\\\\n\\\\nExtend `FileOp` enum (line 76) with:\\\\n```rust\\\\nReplace { template: String, replace: String, spec: ReplaceSpec, fallback: Fallback },\\\\nPatch { template: String, patch: String, anchor: Anchor, skip_if: Option<String> },\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nUpdate `op_type_str()` (line 92) and `template()` (line 99) for new variants.\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL derive `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq` for all new types.\\\\n- Event: WHEN `FileOp::Replace` is constructed, the system SHALL expose `op_type_str() == \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"` and `template()` returning the template field.\\\\n- Event: WHEN `FileOp::Patch` is constructed, the system SHALL expose `op_type_str() == \\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\"` and `template()` returning the template field.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Nothing\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.2: Add `RawAnchor`/`RawBetween` serde structs, extend `RawFileOp`\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` (near line 118, alongside `RawFileOp`)\\\\n\\\\nAdd intermediate serde structs:\\\\n- `RawAnchor` — `pattern: Option<String>`, `scope: Option<String>`, `find: Option<String>`, `position: Option<String>`\\\\n- `RawBetween` — `start: Option<String>`, `end: Option<String>`\\\\n\\\\nModify `RawFileOp`:\\\\n- Change `replace`/`patch` from `Option<serde_yaml::Value>` to `Option<String>` (target path strings)\\\\n- Add fields: `between: Option<RawBetween>`, `pattern: Option<String>`, `fallback: Option<String>`, `anchor: Option<RawAnchor>`\\\\n- Update hint message (line 239) to list new valid field names\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN a recipe YAML contains `replace: \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\"` with `between:` or `pattern:`, the system SHALL deserialize without error.\\\\n- Event: WHEN a recipe YAML contains `patch: \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\"` with `anchor:`, the system SHALL deserialize without error.\\\\n- Event: WHEN unknown fields appear alongside replace/patch, the system SHALL report them via the `extra` catch-all.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.3: Implement `convert_file_op` logic for Replace and Patch\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` — replace rejection block at lines 276-291\\\\n\\\\n**Replace conversion:**\\\\n1. Validate exactly one of `between`/`pattern` is present (not both, not neither)\\\\n2. If `between`: validate both `start` and `end` present; compile-check both as regex via `validate_regex()`\\\\n3. If `pattern`: compile-check as regex\\\\n4. Parse `fallback` string (default: `Error`): `append` | `prepend` | `skip` | `error`\\\\n5. Reject invalid values with exit code 1, listing valid options\\\\n\\\\n**Patch conversion:**\\\\n1. Validate `anchor` present with required `pattern` field\\\\n2. Compile-check `anchor.pattern` as regex\\\\n3. Parse `anchor.scope` into `ScopeType` (default: `Line`); invalid → exit 1 listing valid types\\\\n4. Parse `anchor.position` into `Position` (default: `After`); invalid → exit 1 listing valid types\\\\n5. Pass through `anchor.find` as `Option<String>`\\\\n\\\\nAdd helpers: `parse_scope_type()`, `parse_position()`, `parse_fallback()`.\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN a replace op has `between` with valid `start`/`end`, the system SHALL parse as `ReplaceSpec::Between` (AC-1.1, AC-1.2).\\\\n- Event: WHEN a replace op has `pattern`, the system SHALL parse as `ReplaceSpec::Pattern` (AC-1.3).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `fallback` is omitted, the system SHALL default to `error` (AC-1.4).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF both `between` and `pattern` present, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting conflicting fields (AC-1.5).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF neither `between` nor `pattern`, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting missing match spec (AC-1.6).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `between` missing `start` or `end`, the system SHALL exit code 1 naming the missing field (AC-1.7).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF regex fails to compile, the system SHALL exit code 1 with pattern and compilation error (AC-1.8).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF invalid `fallback`/`scope`/`position` value, the system SHALL exit code 1 listing valid options (AC-1.9, AC-1.15, AC-1.16).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `anchor.scope` omitted, the system SHALL default to `Line` (AC-1.17).\\\\n- Event: WHEN `anchor.position` omitted, the system SHALL default to `After` (AC-1.18).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1, Task 1.2\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.4: Update `jig validate`/`jig vars` output\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — `build_validate_json()` at line 382\\\\n\\\\nExtend match on `FileOp` variants to include Replace and Patch in validation JSON output. Template existence validation already works via `op.template()` (extended in Task 1.1).\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `jig validate` runs on a recipe with replace/patch ops, the system SHALL report operation counts including replace/patch.\\\\n- Event: WHEN `jig vars` runs on a recipe with replace/patch ops, the system SHALL succeed and display variables.\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL validate template file existence for replace and patch operations.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.3\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.5: Unit tests for all Phase 1 parsing paths\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n**Valid parsing tests (~10):**\\\\n- `parse_replace_between` — between.start + between.end → `ReplaceSpec::Between`\\\\n- `parse_replace_pattern` — pattern → `ReplaceSpec::Pattern`\\\\n- `parse_replace_fallback_variants` — all four values parse correctly\\\\n- `parse_replace_fallback_default` — omitted → `Fallback::Error`\\\\n- `parse_patch_full_anchor` — all anchor fields → `FileOp::Patch`\\\\n- `parse_patch_minimal` — just anchor.pattern → defaults scope=Line, position=After\\\\n- `parse_patch_with_skip_if` — skip_if captured\\\\n- `parse_patch_all_scope_types` — all 8 scope type strings\\\\n- `parse_patch_all_position_types` — all 7 position type strings\\\\n- `parse_replace_path_is_string` — `replace:` field is a path string\\\\n\\\\n**Error case tests (~13, each asserts exit code 1):**\\\\n- `reject_replace_both_between_and_pattern` (AC-1.5)\\\\n- `reject_replace_neither_between_nor_pattern` (AC-1.6)\\\\n- `reject_replace_between_missing_start` (AC-1.7)\\\\n- `reject_replace_between_missing_end` (AC-1.7)\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_regex_start` (AC-1.8)\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_regex_end` (AC-1.8)\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_regex_pattern` (AC-1.8)\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_fallback` (AC-1.9)\\\\n- `reject_patch_missing_anchor` (AC-1.13)\\\\n- `reject_patch_missing_anchor_pattern` (AC-1.13)\\\\n- `reject_patch_invalid_anchor_regex` (AC-1.14)\\\\n- `reject_patch_invalid_scope_type` (AC-1.15)\\\\n- `reject_patch_invalid_position_type` (AC-1.16)\\\\n\\\\n**Regression:** `existing_create_inject_still_parse`. Update old v0.1 rejection tests (lines 627-655).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.3\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 1 Validation\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- [ ] All 13 error cases AC-1.5 through AC-1.16 produce exit 1 with clear messages listing valid options\\\\n- [ ] Regex patterns compile-checked at parse time\\\\n- [ ] `jig validate` reports replace/patch operation counts\\\\n- [ ] Existing 191 tests unaffected\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 2: Replace Operation\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-2 (AC-2.1 through AC-2.14), FR-8 (partial), NFR-2\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 1. **Can run in parallel with Phase 3.**\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~25\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.1: Create `src/operations/replace.rs` — executor skeleton (P with 2.5)\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\n\\\\nSignature follows inject.rs pattern:\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn execute(\\\\n    rendered_path: &str,\\\\n    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n    spec: &ReplaceSpec,\\\\n    fallback: &Fallback,\\\\n    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n    verbose: bool,\\\\n) -> OpResult\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nCore pipeline: resolve path → read file (virtual_files then disk) → dispatch to match mode → handle no-match via fallback → replace matched region → write back (virtual_files always; disk if not dry-run) → return `OpResult::Success` with `action: \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"`.\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN replace succeeds, the system SHALL report `action: \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"` with path, location description, and line count (AC-2.9).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF target file missing, the system SHALL exit code 3 with rendered content (AC-2.10).\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL resolve target path relative to `--base-dir` (AC-2.12).\\\\n- State: WHILE `--dry-run` is enabled, the system SHALL read/write through `virtual_files` for chaining (AC-2.13).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF write fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-2.14).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.2: Implement `match_between()` (P with 2.3)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn match_between(lines: &[&str], start_pattern: &str, end_pattern: &str) -> Result<(usize, usize), NoMatch>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n- Find first line matching `start` regex\\\\n- No start match → `NoMatch` (for fallback)\\\\n- Find first line matching `end` regex **after** start\\\\n- No end after start → `OpResult::Error` with \\\\\\\"end marker not found after line N\\\\\\\" (AC-2.11)\\\\n- Return `(start_idx, end_idx)` — content between is exclusive (markers preserved, AC-2.7)\\\\n- Adjacent markers (start_idx + 1 == end_idx) → empty range, content inserted between (AC-2.8)\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `between.start` and `between.end` match, the system SHALL replace content between markers exclusively, preserving marker lines (AC-2.1, AC-2.7).\\\\n- Event: WHEN markers are adjacent, the system SHALL insert content between them (AC-2.8).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `between.end` not found after start, the system SHALL exit code 3 with start line number (AC-2.11).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.3: Implement `match_pattern()` (P with 2.2)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn match_pattern(lines: &[&str], pattern: &str) -> Result<(usize, usize), NoMatch>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n- Find first contiguous block of matching lines\\\\n- Return `(first_match, last_match)` — inclusive range, matched lines replaced entirely (AC-2.2)\\\\n- No match → `NoMatch` (for fallback)\\\\n\\\\n**Consensus:** Both plans agree: first contiguous block, not all matches (predictable, documented in SPEC).\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `pattern` matches, the system SHALL replace all contiguous matched lines with rendered content (AC-2.2).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.4: Implement fallback dispatch\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn apply_fallback(content: &str, rendered: &str, fallback: &Fallback, ...) -> OpResult\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n- `Append` → append content to end, return Success with location `\\\\\\\"fallback:append\\\\\\\"` (AC-2.3)\\\\n- `Prepend` → prepend content to start, return Success with location `\\\\\\\"fallback:prepend\\\\\\\"` (AC-2.4)\\\\n- `Skip` → return `OpResult::Skip` with reason \\\\\\\"pattern not found\\\\\\\" (AC-2.5)\\\\n- `Error` → return `OpResult::Error` with rendered content included (AC-2.6)\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: append`, the system SHALL append content (AC-2.3).\\\\n- Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: prepend`, the system SHALL prepend content (AC-2.4).\\\\n- Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: skip`, the system SHALL skip with reason (AC-2.5).\\\\n- Unwanted: IF no match and `fallback: error`, the system SHALL exit code 3 with rendered content (AC-2.6).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.2, Task 2.3\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.5: Wire replace dispatch\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/mod.rs` — add `pub mod replace;` and match arm in `execute_operation()`.\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — add match arm for `FileOp::Replace` in rendered_path block to render target path as Jinja2.\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN a recipe with replace operations is run, the system SHALL render the replace target path as Jinja2 (AC-1.19).\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL render template content before execution (D-1: render-then-execute).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.4\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.6: Unit tests for replace operation (P with 2.1)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n**Between mode (5):** replaces_exclusively, adjacent_markers_inserts, multiline_content, end_not_found, start_not_found.\\\\n**Pattern mode (3):** replaces_matched_lines, single_line, no_match.\\\\n**Fallback (5):** append, prepend, skip, error, error_is_default.\\\\n**Edge (5):** missing_target_file, dry_run_virtual_files, dry_run_chains_with_create, success_reports_action, verbose_includes_content.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.4\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.7: Integration test fixtures\\\\n\\\\n**Directory:** `tests/fixtures/` (9 new subdirectories)\\\\n\\\\n| Fixture | Purpose | Key ACs |\\\\n|---------|---------|---------|\\\\n| `replace-between` | Replace content between `# START` and `# END` markers | AC-2.1, AC-2.7 |\\\\n| `replace-between-empty` | Adjacent markers (insert into empty region) | AC-2.8 |\\\\n| `replace-pattern` | Replace lines matching regex pattern | AC-2.2 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-append` | Pattern not found, fallback appends | AC-2.3 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-prepend` | Pattern not found, fallback prepends | AC-2.4 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-skip` | Pattern not found, fallback skips | AC-2.5 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-error` | Pattern not found, fallback errors | AC-2.6 |\\\\n| `error-replace-no-match` | Between start not found, default fallback | AC-2.6, AC-N2.2 |\\\\n| `error-replace-end-missing` | Start found, end not found | AC-2.11 |\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.5\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 2 Validation\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- [ ] `between` mode preserves markers, replaces content between\\\\n- [ ] `pattern` mode replaces first contiguous matched block\\\\n- [ ] All four fallback behaviors work correctly\\\\n- [ ] Missing target file exits 3 with rendered content\\\\n- [ ] Dry-run mode uses virtual_files correctly\\\\n- [ ] Replace works alongside create and inject\\\\n- [ ] All 9 integration fixtures pass\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 3: Scope Detection\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-3 (AC-3.1-3.10), FR-4 (AC-4.1-4.12), FR-5 (AC-5.1-5.11), FR-6 (AC-6.1-6.5)\\\\n**Dependencies:** Task 1.1 only (ScopeType enum). **Can run in parallel with Phase 2.**\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~60\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.1: Create `src/scope/mod.rs` — dispatch and types (P with Phase 2)\\\\n\\\\n**New directory:** `src/scope/`\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/mod.rs`\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — add `mod scope;`\\\\n\\\\nDefine types:\\\\n- `ScopeResult` — `start_line: usize`, `end_line: usize`, `closing_line: Option<usize>`, `is_empty: bool`\\\\n- `PositionResult` — `insertion_line: usize`, `indent: String`, `fallback: Option<(String, String)>`\\\\n- `FindResult` — `found_line: usize`, `sub_scope: Option<ScopeResult>`\\\\n\\\\nImplement:\\\\n- `detect_scope(lines, anchor_line, scope_type)` — dispatches by ScopeType: `Line` → single-line scope; `Block/ClassBody/FunctionBody` → indent.rs; `Braces/Brackets/Parens/FunctionSignature` → delimiter.rs\\\\n- `find_within_scope(lines, scope, find_str)` — search within scope, auto-detect sub-scopes\\\\n- `detect_sub_scope(lines, line_idx)` — heuristic: trailing `[`, `{`, `(` or assignment with delimiter\\\\n\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope_type` is `Line`, the system SHALL return a scope containing only the
