Criteria:**\\\\n- The system SHALL pass all new parsing tests\\\\n- The system SHALL pass all existing 191 tests without regression\\\\n- WHEN an invalid scope/position/fallback is provided, the error message SHALL list the valid options\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.3\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 1 Validation Criteria\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- [ ] Recipes with replace operations parse without error\\\\n- [ ] Recipes with patch operations parse without error\\\\n- [ ] All 13 error cases from AC-1.5 through AC-1.16 produce exit code 1 with clear messages\\\\n- [ ] Regex patterns in all fields are compile-checked at parse time\\\\n- [ ] `jig validate` reports replace/patch operation counts\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 2: Replace Operation\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-2 (AC-2.1 through AC-2.14), FR-8 (partial)\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 1\\\\n**Estimated tests added:** ~25\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.1: Create `src/operations/replace.rs` — core logic (P with 2.5)\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\n\\\\nFollow the exact signature pattern from `inject.rs:16`:\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn execute(\\\\n    rendered_path: &str,\\\\n    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n    spec: &ReplaceSpec,\\\\n    fallback: &Fallback,\\\\n    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n    verbose: bool,\\\\n) -> OpResult\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nImplementation steps (sequential within this function):\\\\n\\\\n1. **Resolve path:** `ctx.resolve_path(rendered_path)`\\\\n2. **Read target file:** Check `ctx.virtual_files` first, then disk. Missing file -> `OpResult::Error` with exit code 3, rendered content included (AC-2.10)\\\\n3. **Match region:** Dispatch to `match_between()` or `match_pattern()` based on `spec`\\\\n4. **Handle no-match:** Dispatch to fallback behavior (AC-2.3 through AC-2.6)\\\\n5. **Build new content:** Replace the matched region with `rendered_content`\\\\n6. **Write back:** `ctx.virtual_files.insert()` always; `fs::write()` if not dry-run (same pattern as inject.rs:99-116)\\\\n7. **Return `OpResult::Success`** with `action: \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"`, location description, line count\\\\n\\\\n**Subfunction `match_between()`:**\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn match_between(\\\\n    lines: &[&str],\\\\n    start_pattern: &str,\\\\n    end_pattern: &str,\\\\n    target: &Path,\\\\n    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n) -> Result<(usize, usize), OpResult>\\\\n```\\\\n- Compile `start` regex, find first matching line index\\\\n- If no start match -> return Err (for fallback handling)\\\\n- Compile `end` regex, find first matching line index **after** start\\\\n- If no end match after start -> `OpResult::Error` with \\\\\\\"end marker not found after line N\\\\\\\" (AC-2.11)\\\\n- Return `(start_idx, end_idx)` — exclusive range (markers preserved) (AC-2.7)\\\\n- Adjacent markers (start_idx + 1 == end_idx) -> empty range, content inserted between (AC-2.8)\\\\n\\\\n**Subfunction `match_pattern()`:**\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn match_pattern(\\\\n    lines: &[&str],\\\\n    pattern: &str,\\\\n    target: &Path,\\\\n    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n) -> Result<(usize, usize), OpResult>\\\\n```\\\\n- Compile regex, find first contiguous block of matching lines\\\\n- Return `(first_match, last_match)` — inclusive range (matched lines replaced entirely) (AC-2.2)\\\\n- If no match -> return Err (for fallback handling)\\\\n\\\\n**Fallback dispatch:**\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn apply_fallback(\\\\n    file_content: &str,\\\\n    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n    fallback: &Fallback,\\\\n    spec_desc: &str,\\\\n    target: &Path,\\\\n) -> OpResult\\\\n```\\\\n- `Append`: append content to end, return `OpResult::Success` with location \\\\\\\"fallback:append\\\\\\\" (AC-2.3)\\\\n- `Prepend`: prepend content to start, return `OpResult::Success` with location \\\\\\\"fallback:prepend\\\\\\\" (AC-2.4)\\\\n- `Skip`: return `OpResult::Skip` with reason \\\\\\\"pattern not found\\\\\\\" (AC-2.5)\\\\n- `Error`: return `OpResult::Error` with rendered content included (AC-2.6)\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `between.start` and `between.end` match, the system SHALL replace content between markers exclusively (AC-2.1, AC-2.7)\\\\n- WHEN markers are adjacent (empty region), the system SHALL insert content between them (AC-2.8)\\\\n- WHEN `pattern` matches, the system SHALL replace all contiguous matched lines (AC-2.2)\\\\n- WHEN `fallback: append` and no match, the system SHALL append content (AC-2.3)\\\\n- WHEN `fallback: prepend` and no match, the system SHALL prepend content (AC-2.4)\\\\n- WHEN `fallback: skip` and no match, the system SHALL skip with reason (AC-2.5)\\\\n- WHEN `fallback: error` and no match, the system SHALL error with rendered content (AC-2.6)\\\\n- WHEN replace succeeds, the system SHALL report `action: \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"` with location description (AC-2.9)\\\\n- IF target file missing, the system SHALL exit code 3 with rendered content (AC-2.10)\\\\n- IF `between.end` not found after start, the system SHALL exit code 3 with start line number (AC-2.11)\\\\n- The system SHALL resolve target path relative to `--base-dir` (AC-2.12)\\\\n- The system SHALL support dry-run via `virtual_files` (AC-2.13)\\\\n- IF write fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-2.14)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.2: Wire replace into `src/operations/mod.rs`\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/mod.rs`\\\\n\\\\n1. Add `pub mod replace;` at line 1 (alongside `pub mod create;` and `pub mod inject;`)\\\\n2. Add match arm in `execute_operation()` (after line 120):\\\\n```rust\\\\nFileOp::Replace { spec, fallback, .. } => {\\\\n    replace::execute(\\\\n        &prepared.rendered_path,\\\\n        &prepared.rendered_content,\\\\n        spec,\\\\n        fallback,\\\\n        ctx,\\\\n        verbose,\\\\n    )\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `execute_operation` receives a `PreparedOp` with `FileOp::Replace`, the system SHALL dispatch to `replace::execute`\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.3: Wire replace into `src/main.rs` — template preparation\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — the template preparation loop at lines 279-322\\\\n\\\\nAdd a new match arm for `FileOp::Replace` in the rendered_path block (after line 300):\\\\n```rust\\\\nrecipe::FileOp::Replace { replace, .. } => {\\\\n    match renderer::render_path_template(&env, replace, &vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].replace\\\\\\\", i)) {\\\\n        Ok(p) => p,\\\\n        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n    }\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThe `rendered_skip_if` block (lines 304-314) needs no change — Replace has no skip_if field, so it falls through to the `_ => None` arm.\\\\n\\\\nAlso update `build_validate_json()` (line 382) for the Replace variant — already covered in Task 1.4.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN a recipe with replace operations is run, the system SHALL render the replace target path as a Jinja2 template (AC-1.19)\\\\n- The system SHALL render the template content before execution (D-1: render-then-execute)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.2\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.4: Add verbose output for replace\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/output.rs` — no structural changes needed\\\\n\\\\nThe existing `format_json()` and `format_human()` already handle all `OpResult` variants generically. Replace operations return `OpResult::Success` with `action: \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"` and a location string — these format correctly with no output.rs changes.\\\\n\\\\nFor verbose scope diagnostics (FR-8 partial): add an optional `scope_diagnostics` field to `OpResult::Success`. **[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]**: Should we extend `OpResult::Success` with an optional diagnostics field now, or defer until Phase 4 when patch needs it? Recommendation: defer — keep OpResult unchanged for Phase 2, add the field in Phase 4.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `--verbose` is specified for a replace operation, the system SHALL include the match mode, matched region, and lines replaced (AC-8.3)\\\\n- The system SHALL display replace results in both JSON and human output modes\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.5: Unit tests for replace operation (P with 2.1)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block\\\\n\\\\nFollow the pattern from `inject.rs` tests (line 214+): use `TempDir`, write files, call `execute()` directly.\\\\n\\\\n**Between mode tests:**\\\\n- `between_replaces_content_exclusively` — markers preserved, content between swapped (AC-2.1, AC-2.7)\\\\n- `between_adjacent_markers_inserts` — empty region, content inserted (AC-2.8)\\\\n- `between_multiline_content` — multi-line rendered content between markers\\\\n- `between_end_not_found_after_start` — end marker missing -> error with start line number (AC-2.11)\\\\n- `between_start_not_found` — start marker missing -> triggers fallback\\\\n\\\\n**Pattern mode tests:**\\\\n- `pattern_replaces_matched_lines` — contiguous matched lines replaced (AC-2.2)\\\\n- `pattern_single_line_match` — single matching line replaced\\\\n- `pattern_no_match` — no matching lines -> triggers fallback\\\\n\\\\n**Fallback tests:**\\\\n- `fallback_append` — no match + append -> content at end (AC-2.3)\\\\n- `fallback_prepend` — no match + prepend -> content at start (AC-2.4)\\\\n- `fallback_skip` — no match + skip -> OpResult::Skip (AC-2.5)\\\\n- `fallback_error` — no match + error -> OpResult::Error with rendered_content (AC-2.6)\\\\n- `fallback_error_is_default` — verify Error is the default behavior\\\\n\\\\n**Edge case tests:**\\\\n- `missing_target_file` — exit code 3, rendered content included (AC-2.10)\\\\n- `dry_run_uses_virtual_files` — reads/writes virtual_files (AC-2.13)\\\\n- `dry_run_chains_with_prior_create` — create then replace on same file in dry-run\\\\n- `success_reports_replace_action` — action is \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\", location includes spec description (AC-2.9)\\\\n- `verbose_includes_content` — verbose=true -> rendered_content in Success\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- The system SHALL pass all replace unit tests\\\\n- Every AC from FR-2 SHALL be covered by at least one test\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.6: Integration test fixtures for replace\\\\n\\\\n**Directory:** `tests/fixtures/` (new subdirectories)\\\\n\\\\nEach fixture follows the established convention: `recipe.yaml`, `vars.json`, `templates/`, `existing/`, `expected/`, optionally `expected_output.json` and `expected_exit_code`.\\\\n\\\\n| Fixture | Purpose | Key ACs |\\\\n|---------|---------|---------|\\\\n| `replace-between` | Replace content between `# START` and `# END` markers | AC-2.1, AC-2.7 |\\\\n| `replace-between-empty` | Replace between adjacent markers (insert into empty region) | AC-2.8 |\\\\n| `replace-pattern` | Replace lines matching regex pattern | AC-2.2 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-append` | Pattern not found, fallback appends | AC-2.3 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-prepend` | Pattern not found, fallback prepends | AC-2.4 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-skip` | Pattern not found, fallback skips | AC-2.5 |\\\\n| `replace-fallback-error` | Pattern not found, fallback errors (exit 3) | AC-2.6 |\\\\n| `error-replace-no-match` | Between start not found, default fallback -> error | AC-2.6, AC-N2.2 |\\\\n| `error-replace-end-missing` | Between start found, end not found -> error | AC-2.11 |\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `cargo test` runs fixture_tests, all new replace fixtures SHALL pass\\\\n- WHEN error fixtures run, they SHALL produce exit code 3 with structured error fields\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.3 (wiring complete)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 2 Validation Criteria\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- [ ] `between` mode preserves marker lines, replaces content between\\\\n- [ ] `pattern` mode replaces all contiguous matched lines\\\\n- [ ] All four fallback behaviors work correctly\\\\n- [ ] Missing target file exits 3 with rendered content\\\\n- [ ] Dry-run mode uses virtual_files correctly\\\\n- [ ] Replace works in a recipe alongside create and inject\\\\n- [ ] All 9 integration fixtures pass\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 3: Scope Detection\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-3 (AC-3.1-3.10), FR-4 (AC-4.1-4.12), FR-5 (AC-5.1-5.11), FR-6 (AC-6.1-6.5)\\\\n**Dependencies:** None (independent of Phases 1-2 — can run in parallel after Task 1.1)\\\\n**Estimated tests added:** ~60\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.1: Create `src/scope/mod.rs` — dispatch and types (P with Phase 2)\\\\n\\\\n**New directory:** `src/scope/`\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/mod.rs`\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — add `mod scope;` at line 1\\\\n\\\\nDefine the public API:\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub mod indent;\\\\npub mod delimiter;\\\\npub mod position;\\\\n\\\\nuse crate::error::StructuredError;\\\\n\\\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\\\npub struct ScopeResult {\\\\n    /// First line of scope content (0-indexed).\\\\n    pub start_line: usize,\\\\n    /// Last line of scope content (0-indexed, inclusive).\\\\n    pub end_line: usize,\\\\n    /// Line of closing delimiter, if applicable.\\\\n    pub closing_line: Option<usize>,\\\\n    /// True if scope is empty (no content lines).\\\\n    pub is_empty: bool,\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\\\npub struct PositionResult {\\\\n    /// Line to insert before (0-indexed).\\\\n    pub insertion_line: usize,\\\\n    /// Indentation string for inserted content.\\\\n    pub indent: String,\\\\n    /// If a fallback was used: (original_position, fallback_position).\\\\n    pub fallback: Option<(String, String)>,\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\\\\npub struct FindResult {\\\\n    /// Line where find string was located (0-indexed).\\\\n    pub found_line: usize,\\\\n    /// Sub-scope if the found line opens a delimiter scope.\\\\n    pub sub_scope: Option<ScopeResult>,\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nImplement:\\\\n- `detect_scope(lines, anchor_line, scope_type) -> Result<ScopeResult, StructuredError>` — dispatches to indent or delimiter module based on ScopeType\\\\n- `find_within_scope(lines, scope, find_str) -> Result<FindResult, StructuredError>` — searches within scope boundaries, auto-detects sub-scopes\\\\n- `detect_sub_scope(lines, line_idx) -> Option<ScopeResult>` — heuristic: checks if line opens a delimiter scope (trailing `[`, `{`, `(` or assignment with delimiter like `list_display = [`)\\\\n\\\\nFor `ScopeType::Line`: return scope containing only the anchor line (no structural analysis).\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `scope_type` is `Line`, the system SHALL return a scope containing only the anchor line\\\\n- The system SHALL dispatch `Block`, `ClassBody`, `FunctionBody` to `indent.rs`\\\\n- The system SHALL dispatch `Braces`, `Brackets`, `Parens`, `FunctionSignature` to `delimiter.rs`\\\\n- The system SHALL not require any language identifier or configuration (AC-N1.3)\\\\n- WHEN `find_within_scope` finds the string, the system SHALL return the line index\\\\n- WHEN the found line opens a sub-scope, the system SHALL detect and return it (AC-6.2)\\\\n- IF the find string is not found, the system SHALL return a StructuredError (AC-6.3)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1 (for ScopeType enum)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.2: Create `src/scope/indent.rs` — indentation-based scope detection (P with 3.3)\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/indent.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn detect_indent_scope(\\\\n    lines: &[&str],\\\\n    anchor_line: usize,\\\\n    scope_type: &crate::recipe::ScopeType,\\\\n) -> Result<ScopeResult, StructuredError>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Algorithm:**\\\\n\\\\n1. Determine the anchor line's indentation level (count leading whitespace chars)\\\\n2. For `ClassBody`/`FunctionBody`: the body starts after the declaration line. Handle multi-line declarations by scanning forward until a line ends with `:` (Python). The anchor is the declaration; the body is everything after it at deeper indentation.\\\\n3. Walk forward from anchor+1:\\\\n   - Skip blank lines (they don't terminate the scope — AC-3.4)\\\\n   - If line indentation > anchor indentation: include in scope\\\\n   - If line indentation <= anchor indentation: scope ended (exclusive of this line)\\\\n4. If no lines are deeper than anchor (empty scope): return `ScopeResult { is_empty: true }` with start_line = end_line = anchor_line + 1 (AC-3.5)\\\\n5. Nested classes/functions within the scope are included (AC-3.6, AC-3.7)\\\\n\\\\n**Edge cases:**\\\\n- Mixed tabs and spaces: compare character-by-character raw prefix (AC-3.8)\\\\n- Multi-line class declaration (`class Foo(\\\\\\\\n    Bar,\\\\\\\\n):`) — scan for colon, body starts after (AC-3.9)\\\\n- Decorator before def/class: scope is from the def/class line's indentation, not decorator's (AC-3.10)\\\\n\\\\nHelper functions:\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn measure_indent(line: &str) -> usize {\\\\n    line.len() - line.trim_start().len()\\\\n}\\\\n\\\\nfn is_blank(line: &str) -> bool {\\\\n    line.trim().is_empty()\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `scope: block`, the system SHALL include all lines indented deeper than anchor (AC-3.1)\\\\n- WHEN `scope: class_body`, the system SHALL identify body from first indented line to dedent (AC-3.2)\\\\n- WHEN `scope: function_body`, the system SHALL identify body from first indented line to dedent (AC-3.3)\\\\n- WHEN blank lines appear within scope, the system SHALL include them (AC-3.4)\\\\n- WHEN scope is empty (immediate dedent), the system SHALL report `is_empty: true` (AC-3.5)\\\\n- WHEN nested classes appear, the system SHALL include them in outer scope (AC-3.6)\\\\n- WHEN nested functions appear, the system SHALL include them in outer scope (AC-3.7)\\\\n- The system SHALL measure indent by raw whitespace prefix length (AC-3.8)\\\\n- WHEN Python class has multi-line declaration, the system SHALL start body after colon (AC-3.9)\\\\n- WHEN decorator precedes def/class, the system SHALL use def/class indentation (AC-3.10)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.3: Create `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — delimiter-based scope detection (P with 3.2)\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/delimiter.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn detect_delimiter_scope(\\\\n    lines: &[&str],\\\\n    anchor_line: usize,\\\\n    open: char,\\\\n    close: char,\\\\n) -> Result<ScopeResult, StructuredError>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Algorithm:**\\\\n\\\\n1. Starting from `anchor_line`, scan for the first `open` delimiter (AC-4.11: use it if on anchor line)\\\\n2. If not found -> return error \\\\\\\"no {open} found on or after anchor\\\\\\\" (AC-4.8)\\\\n3. Track nesting depth (start at 1 after finding opener)\\\\n4. Walk forward character-by-character:\\\\n   - **String literals:** When encountering `\\\\\\\"`, `'`, or `` ` ``, skip until matching close quote. Handle escape sequences (`\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"`, `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\`). (AC-4.6)\\\\n   - **Escaped delimiters:** `\\\\\\\\{`, `\\\\\\\\}`, etc. — skip (AC-4.12)\\\\n   - **Single-line comments:** `//` or `#` — skip rest of line (AC-4.7)\\\\n   - **Multi-line comments:** `/*` — skip until `*/` (AC-4.7)\\\\n   - Open delimiter -> depth++\\\\n   - Close delimiter -> depth--\\\\n   - When depth reaches 0 -> found the closing delimiter\\\\n5. If depth never reaches 0 -> return error \\\\\\\"unbalanced {open} at line N\\\\\\\" (AC-4.9)\\\\n6. Scope content is everything between the open and close delimiters (exclusive) (AC-4.1-4.4)\\\\n7. Empty scope: delimiters adjacent or nothing between -> `is_empty: true` (AC-4.10)\\\\n\\\\nUse a `CharScanner` struct for clean character-by-character traversal:\\\\n```rust\\\\nstruct CharScanner<'a> {\\\\n    lines: &'a [&'a str],\\\\n    line: usize,\\\\n    col: usize,\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nWith methods: `advance()`, `peek()`, `skip_string_literal()`, `skip_line_comment()`, `skip_block_comment()`.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `scope: braces`, the system SHALL find `{` to `}` scope (AC-4.1)\\\\n- WHEN `scope: brackets`, the system SHALL find `[` to `]` scope (AC-4.2)\\\\n- WHEN `scope: parens`, the system SHALL find `(` to `)` scope (AC-4.3)\\\\n- WHEN `scope: function_signature`, the system SHALL find `(` to `)` (AC-4.4)\\\\n- WHEN nested delimiters appear, the system SHALL track depth correctly (AC-4.5)\\\\n- WHEN delimiters appear in strings, the system SHALL ignore them (AC-4.6)\\\\n- WHEN delimiters appear in comments, the system SHALL ignore them (AC-4.7)\\\\n- IF opening delimiter not found, the system SHALL return error (AC-4.8)\\\\n- IF closing delimiter never found, the system SHALL return error with line number (AC-4.9)\\\\n- WHEN scope is empty, the system SHALL report `is_empty: true` (AC-4.10)\\\\n- WHEN opener is on anchor line, the system SHALL use it (AC-4.11)\\\\n- WHEN escaped delimiters appear, the system SHALL ignore them (AC-4.12)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.4: Create `src/scope/position.rs` — semantic position resolution (P with 3.2, 3.3)\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/position.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn resolve_position(\\\\n    lines: &[&str],\\\\n    scope: &ScopeResult,\\\\n    position: &crate::recipe::Position,\\\\n) -> Result<PositionResult, StructuredError>\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Implementation for each position:**\\\\n\\\\n| Position | Logic | Fallback |\\\\n|----------|-------|----------|\\\\n| `Before` | `insertion_line = scope.start_line` | N/A |\\\\n| `After` | `insertion_line = scope.end_line + 1` | N/A |\\\\n| `BeforeClose` | If `closing_line` exists: `closing_line`. Else: `end_line + 1` (last line of indented block) | N/A |\\\\n| `AfterLastField` | Scan scope for last line matching `^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]`. Insert after it. | Falls back to `BeforeClose` (AC-5.8) |\\\\n| `AfterLastMethod` | Scan scope for last line matching `^\\\\\\\\s+def \\\\\\\\w+` or `^\\\\\\\\s+(pub\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn\\\\\\\\|async fn) \\\\\\\\w+` or `^\\\\\\\\s+(public\\\\\\\\|private\\\\\\\\|protected\\\\\\\\|static\\\\\\\\|async)?\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*\\\\\\\\(`. Find that method's body end (recursive scope detection). Insert after body. | Falls back to `BeforeClose` (AC-5.9) |\\\\n| `AfterLastImport` | Scan scope for last line matching `^\\\\\\\\s*(from\\\\\\\\|import) `. Insert after it. | Falls back to `Before` (AC-5.10) |\\\\n| `Sorted` | Find alphabetically correct insertion point among sibling lines at same indent level. Compare trimmed content. | N/A |\\\\n\\\\n**Indentation detection:** Determine indent from the line at or near insertion point. If scope is empty, use anchor indentation + one level (detected from file context or default to 4 spaces).\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn detect_indent_at(lines: &[&str], scope: &ScopeResult) -> String\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `position: before`, the system SHALL insert before scope start (AC-5.1)\\\\n- WHEN `position: after`, the system SHALL insert after scope end (AC-5.2)\\\\n- WHEN `position: before_close`, the system SHALL insert before closing delimiter (AC-5.3)\\\\n- WHEN `position: after_last_field`, the system SHALL find `^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` (AC-5.4)\\\\n- WHEN `position: after_last_method`, the system SHALL find complete method body end (AC-5.5, AC-5.11)\\\\n- WHEN `position: after_last_import`, the system SHALL find `^\\\\\\\\s*(from\\\\\\\\|import) ` (AC-5.6)\\\\n- WHEN `position: sorted`, the system SHALL insert alphabetically (AC-5.7)\\\\n- IF `after_last_field` finds no fields, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` (AC-5.8)\\\\n- IF `after_last_method` finds no methods, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` (AC-5.9)\\\\n- IF `after_last_import` finds no imports, the system SHALL fall back to `before` (AC-5.10)\\\\n- WHEN `after_last_method` resolves, the system SHALL find the method's complete body end, not just the def line (AC-5.11)\\\\n- The system SHALL return the detected indentation for the insertion context\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.5: Unit tests for indentation scope detection (P with 3.6, 3.7)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/indent.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\nTest with inline string snippets (no disk I/O needed):\\\\n\\\\n- `python_class_body` — `class Foo:\\\\\\\\n    a = 1\\\\\\\\n    b = 2\\\\\\\\nother` -> scope is lines with `a = 1`, `b = 2`\\\\n- `python_function_body` — `def bar():\\\\\\\\n    return 1\\\\\\\\nx = 2` -> scope is `return 1`\\\\n- `blank_lines_within_scope` — blank line between two indented lines -> included (AC-3.4)\\\\n- `empty_scope` — `class Empty:\\\\\\\\nclass Other:` -> is_empty (AC-3.5)\\\\n- `nested_class` — class within class -> outer scope includes inner (AC-3.6)\\\\n- `nested_function` — def within def -> outer scope includes inner (AC-3.7)\\\\n- `mixed_tabs_spaces` — verify raw prefix comparison (AC-3.8)\\\\n- `multiline_class_declaration` — `class Foo(\\\\\\\\n    Bar,\\\\\\\\n):\\\\\\\\n    pass` -> body starts after `:` (AC-3.9)\\\\n- `decorator_before_class` — `@deco\\\\\\\\nclass X:\\\\\\\\n    pass` -> anchor is class line, scope is body (AC-3.10)\\\\n- `yaml_nested_block` — YAML indentation scope detection\\\\n- `deeply_nested_indent` — 3+ levels of nesting\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- The system SHALL pass all indentation scope tests\\\\n- The system SHALL produce identical results on identical inputs (AC-N4.1)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.2\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.6: Unit tests for delimiter scope detection (P with 3.5, 3.7)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n- `rust_struct_braces` — `struct Foo {\\\\\\\\n    bar: i32,\\\\\\\\n}` -> scope between braces\\\\n- `typescript_interface_braces` — `interface Props {\\\\\\\\n    name: string;\\\\\\\\n}` -> scope between braces\\\\n- `go_function_braces` — `func main() {\\\\\\\\n    fmt.Println()\\\\\\\\n}` -> scope between braces\\\\n- `python_list_brackets` — `items = [\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\"b\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n]` -> scope between brackets\\\\n- `function_signature_parens` — `fn foo(a: i32, b: i32) {` -> scope between parens\\\\n- `nested_braces` — `{ { inner } }` -> outer scope includes inner (AC-4.5)\\\\n- `delimiters_in_double_quotes` — `\\\\\\\"{ not a scope }\\\\\\\"` -> ignored (AC-4.6)\\\\n- `delimiters_in_single_quotes` — `'{ not a scope }'` -> ignored (AC-4.6)\\\\n- `delimiters_in_backticks` — `` `{ not a scope }` `` -> ignored (AC-4.6)\\\\n- `delimiters_in_line_comments` — `// { comment }` -> ignored (AC-4.7)\\\\n- `delimiters_in_block_comments` — `/* { comment } */` -> ignored (AC-4.7)\\\\n- `escaped_delimiters` — `\\\\\\\\{ \\\\\\\\}` -> ignored (AC-4.12)\\\\n- `missing_opening_delimiter` — error with message (AC-4.8)\\\\n- `unbalanced_delimiter` — error with line number (AC-4.9)\\\\n- `empty_braces` — `{}` -> is_empty (AC-4.10)\\\\n- `opener_on_anchor_line` — `fn main() {` -> uses the `{` on this line (AC-4.11)\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- The system SHALL pass all delimiter scope tests\\\\n- The system SHALL correctly handle strings, comments, and escapes across Rust, TS, Go, JSON\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.3\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.7: Unit tests for position resolution (P with 3.5, 3.6)\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/position.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n- `position_before` — inserts at scope start (AC-5.1)\\\\n- `position_after` — inserts after scope end (AC-5.2)\\\\n- `position_before_close` — inserts before closing delimiter (AC-5.3)\\\\n- `after_last_field_python` — `class Foo:\\\\\\\\n    name = \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n    age = 0\\\\\\\\n    def method(self):` -> after `age = 0` (AC-5.4)\\\\n- `after_last_field_rust` — `struct Foo {\\\\\\\\n    name: String,\\\\\\\\n    age: u32,\\\\\\\\n}` -> after `age: u32,`\\\\n- `after_last_method_python` — `class Foo:\\\\\\\\n    def a(self):\\\\\\\\n        pass\\\\\\\\n    def b(self):\\\\\\\\n        pass` -> after `b`'s body (AC-5.5, AC-5.11)\\\\n- `after_last_method_rust` — `impl Foo {\\\\\\\\n    fn a() {}\\\\\\\\n    fn b() {}\\\\\\\\n}` -> after `b`'s body\\\\n- `after_last_import` — `from x import y\\\\\\\\nfrom z import w\\\\\\\\nclass Foo:` -> after second import (AC-5.6)\\\\n- `sorted_insertion` — `\\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\"c\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\"d\\\\\\\"` + inserting `\\\\\\\"b\\\\\\\"` -> between a and c (AC-5.7)\\\\n- `after_last_field_fallback` — no fields in scope -> falls back to before_close (AC-5.8)\\\\n- `after_last_method_fallback` — no methods -> falls back to before_close (AC-5.9)\\\\n- `after_last_import_fallback` — no imports -> falls back to before (AC-5.10)\\\\n- `indent_detection` — correct indentation string returned\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- The system SHALL pass all position resolution tests\\\\n- WHEN a position falls back, the `PositionResult.fallback` field SHALL contain `(original, fallback_to)` names\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.4\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.8: Unit tests for find narrowing\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/mod.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n- `find_within_scope_basic` — find \\\\\\\"list_display\\\\\\\" in a class body -> returns line index (AC-6.1)\\\\n- `find_detects_bracket_sub_scope` — `list_display = [\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\"a\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n]` -> sub_scope is brackets scope (AC-6.2)\\\\n- `find_detects_brace_sub_scope` — `config = {\\\\\\\\n    \\\\\\\"key\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"val\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\\n}` -> sub_scope is braces scope\\\\n- `find_not_found` — StructuredError with scope range in message (AC-6.3)\\\\n- `find_before_close_on_sub_scope` — `before_close` on sub-scope inserts before `]` (AC-6.4)\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN find string is found, the system SHALL return the line index\\\\n- WHEN found line opens a sub-scope, the system SHALL detect and return it\\\\n- IF find string not found, the system SHALL return error with scope boundaries\\\\n- The system SHALL apply find AFTER scope detection but BEFORE position resolution (AC-6.5)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 3 Validation Criteria\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new scope tests\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- [ ] Python class body correctly identified through dedent\\\\n- [ ] Python function body correctly identified through dedent\\\\n- [ ] Blank lines don't terminate indentation scope\\\\n- [ ] Nested delimiters tracked correctly\\\\n- [ ] String literals, comments, escapes don't affect delimiter counting\\\\n- [ ] All 7 position types tested with realistic code\\\\n- [ ] Position fallbacks work correctly\\\\n- [ ] Find narrowing detects sub-scopes\\\\n- [ ] Same input always produces same scope boundaries (determinism)\\\\n- [ ] Scope detection works across Python, Rust, TypeScript, Go without configuration\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 4: Patch Operation\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-6, FR-7 (AC-7.1-7.12), FR-8 (complete)\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 1 + Phase 3\\\\n**Estimated tests added:** ~30\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.1: Create `src/operations/patch.rs` — core pipeline\\\\n\\\\n**New file:** `src/operations/patch.rs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\npub fn execute(\\\\n    rendered_path: &str,\\\\n    rendered_content: &str,\\\\n    rendered_skip_if: Option<&str>,\\\\n    anchor: &Anchor,\\\\n    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\n    verbose: bool,\\\\n) -> OpResult\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Pipeline** (sequential — each step depends on the previous):\\\\n\\\\n1. **Resolve path:** `ctx.resolve_path(rendered_path)`\\\\n2. **Read target file:** virtual_files first, then disk. Missing -> error (AC-7.10)\\\\n3. **Check skip_if:** If `rendered_skip_if` found in file content -> `OpResult::Skip` (AC-7.2). Search entire file, not just scope (AC-N3.3)\\\\n4. **Find anchor:** Compile `anchor.pattern` as regex, find first matching line (AC-7.6). No match -> error (AC-7.4)\\\\n5. **Detect scope:** Call `scope::detect_scope(lines, anchor_idx, &anchor.scope)`. Failure -> error with rendered content (AC-7.5)\\\\n6. **Apply find narrowing** (if `anchor.find` is Some): Call `scope::find_within_scope()`. If sub_scope detected, use it for position resolution. Failure -> error (AC-6.3)\\\\n7. **Resolve position:** Call `scope::position::resolve_position()`. Get `PositionResult` with insertion line and indent.\\\\n8. **Match indentation:** Adjust rendered content's base indentation to match insertion context (AC-7.7). Preserve relative indentation within the template.\\\\n9. **Insert content:** Build new file content with rendered content inserted at `insertion_line`\\\\n10. **Write back:** virtual_files + disk (same pattern as inject/replace)\\\\n11. **Return `OpResult::Success`** with `action: \\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\"`, location `\\\\\\\"{scope_type}({pattern}):{position}\\\\\\\"` (AC-7.3)\\\\n\\\\n**Indentation matching (step 8):**\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn adjust_indentation(rendered_content: &str, target_indent: &str) -> String {\\\\n    let lines: Vec<&str> = rendered_content.lines().collect();\\\\n    if lines.is_empty() { return rendered_content.to_string(); }\\\\n\\\\n    // Detect base indent of rendered content (indent of first non-empty line)\\\\n    let base_indent = lines.iter()\\\\n        .find(|l| !l.trim().is_empty())\\\\n        .map(|l| l.len() - l.trim_start().len())\\\\n        .unwrap_or(0);\\\\n\\\\n    // Adjust each line: remove base indent, add target indent\\\\n    lines.iter().map(|line| {\\\\n        if line.trim().is_empty() {\\\\n            String::new()\\\\n        } else {\\\\n            let stripped = if line.len() >= base_indent {\\\\n                &line[base_indent..]\\\\n            } else {\\\\n                line.trim_start()\\\\n            };\\\\n            format!(\\\\\\\"{}{}\\\\\\\", target_indent, stripped)\\\\n        }\\\\n    }).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\")\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**`scope: line` degenerate case (AC-7.12):**\\\\nWhen scope is `Line`, skip scope detection, find narrowing, and position resolution. Just insert after the anchor line (identical to inject's `after` mode). This is the simplest behavior.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN a patch operation executes, the system SHALL perform anchor->scope->find->position->insert (AC-7.1)\\\\n- WHEN `skip_if` matches, the system SHALL skip and report `action: \\\\\\\"skip\\\\\\\"` (AC-7.2)\\\\n- WHEN patch succeeds, the system SHALL report `action: \\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\"` with location description (AC-7.3)\\\\n- IF anchor pattern matches zero lines, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-7.4)\\\\n- IF scope detection fails, the system SHALL exit code 3 with rendered content (AC-7.5)\\\\n- WHEN anchor matches multiple lines, the system SHALL use first match (AC-7.6)\\\\n- WHEN inserting content, the system SHALL match surrounding indentation (AC-7.7)\\\\n- The system SHALL resolve path relative to `--base-dir` (AC-7.8)\\\\n- The system SHALL support dry-run via virtual_files (AC-7.9)\\\\n- IF target file missing, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-7.10)\\\\n- IF write fails, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-7.11)\\\\n- WHEN `scope: line`, the system SHALL behave like inject after (AC-7.12)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1 (FileOp::Patch), Phase 3 (scope module)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.2: Wire patch into `src/operations/mod.rs`\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/mod.rs`\\\\n\\\\n1. Add `pub mod patch;` at line 1\\\\n2. Add match arm in `execute_operation()`:\\\\n```rust\\\\nFileOp::Patch { anchor, .. } => {\\\\n    patch::execute(\\\\n        &prepared.rendered_path,\\\\n        &prepared.rendered_content,\\\\n        prepared.rendered_skip_if.as_deref(),\\\\n        anchor,\\\\n        ctx,\\\\n        verbose,\\\\n    )\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.3: Wire patch into `src/main.rs` — template preparation\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — template preparation loop\\\\n\\\\nAdd match arm for `FileOp::Patch` in the rendered_path block:\\\\n```rust\\\\nrecipe::FileOp::Patch { patch, .. } => {\\\\n    match renderer::render_path_template(&env, patch, &vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].patch\\\\\\\", i)) {\\\\n        Ok(p) => p,\\\\n        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n    }\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nAdd match arm for rendered_skip_if (alongside inject's):\\\\n```rust\\\\nrecipe::FileOp::Patch { skip_if: Some(skip_if_expr), .. } => {\\\\n    match renderer::render_path_template(\\\\n        &env, skip_if_expr, &vars, &format!(\\\\\\\"files[{}].skip_if\\\\\\\", i),\\\\n    ) {\\\\n        Ok(s) => Some(s),\\\\n        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\\\\n    }\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN a recipe with patch operations is run, the system SHALL render the patch target path as Jinja2 (AC-1.19)\\\\n- WHEN `skip_if` contains Jinja2 expressions, the system SHALL render them with recipe variables (AC-7.2)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.2\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.4: Complete verbose scope diagnostics\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/patch.rs` — build diagnostics during execution\\\\n**File:** `src/output.rs` — format diagnostics in both JSON and human modes\\\\n\\\\n**[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]**: How to pass diagnostics to output.\\\\n\\\\n**Option A (recommended):** Add an optional `diagnostics: Option<serde_json::Value>` field to `OpResult::Success`. When verbose=true and the operation is patch/replace, populate it with scope diagnostic details. Output.rs includes it in JSON output under `scope_diagnostics` and in human output as indented detail lines. Update all existing `OpResult::Success` constructions in create.rs and inject.rs to add `diagnostics: None`.\\\\n\\\\n**Option B:** Use a separate channel (e.g., verbose messages to stderr) outside OpResult. This avoids touching OpResult but loses JSON structure.\\\\n\\\\nGoing with Option A (recommended):\\\\n\\\\nIn `OpResult::Success`, add:\\\\n```rust\\\\ndiagnostics: Option<serde_json::Value>,\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nIn patch.rs, when verbose=true:\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet diagnostics = if verbose {\\\\n    Some(serde_json::json!({\\\\n        \\\\\\\"anchor_line\\\\\\\": anchor_idx + 1,\\\\n        \\\\\\\"scope_start\\\\\\\": scope.start_line + 1,\\\\n        \\\\\\\"scope_end\\\\\\\": scope.end_line + 1,\\\\n        \\\\\\\"insertion_line\\\\\\\": pos.insertion_line + 1,\\\\n        \\\\\\\"find_match_line\\\\\\\": find_result.as_ref().map(|f| f.found_line + 1),\\\\n        \\\\\\\"sub_scope\\\\\\\": find_result.as_ref().and_then(|f| f.sub_scope.as_ref().map(|s|\\\\n            serde_json::json!({\\\\\\\"start\\\\\\\": s.start_line + 1, \\\\\\\"end\\\\\\\": s.end_line + 1})\\\\n        )),\\\\n        \\\\\\\"position_fallback\\\\\\\": pos.fallback.as_ref().map(|(from, to)| format!(\\\\\\\"{} -> {}\\\\\\\", from, to)),\\\\n    }))\\\\n} else {\\\\n    None\\\\n};\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nUpdate `format_json()` in output.rs to include `scope_diagnostics` when present.\\\\nUpdate `format_human()` to print diagnostic details as indented lines.\\\\nUpdate all existing `OpResult::Success` constructions in create.rs and inject.rs to add `diagnostics: None`.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `--verbose` for patch, output SHALL include anchor line, scope range, insertion line (AC-8.1)\\\\n- WHEN `--verbose` with find, output SHALL include find line and sub-scope (AC-8.2)\\\\n- WHEN `--verbose` for replace, output SHALL include match mode and region (AC-8.3)\\\\n- WHEN position falls back, output SHALL note the fallback (AC-8.4)\\\\n- Diagnostics SHALL appear in both JSON and human output (AC-8.5)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.1, Task 2.1 (update replace.rs too)\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.5: Unit tests for patch operation\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/patch.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n- `patch_class_body_after_last_field` — Python class, insert field after last field\\\\n- `patch_function_body_before_close` — Python function, insert before return\\\\n- `patch_braces_before_close` — Rust struct, insert before `}`\\\\n- `patch_brackets_before_close` — insert before `]` in a list\\\\n- `patch_function_signature` — insert parameter in function args\\\\n- `patch_find_narrowing_brackets` — find `list_display` -> insert in its brackets\\\\n- `patch_skip_if_matched` — skip_if string found -> skip\\\\n- `patch_skip_if_not_matched` — skip_if string not found -> proceed\\\\n- `patch_scope_line` — scope: line -> insert after anchor (like inject)\\\\n- `patch_indent_matching` — template indent adjusted to match context\\\\n- `patch_sorted_insertion` — sorted position within a list\\\\n- `patch_anchor_not_found` — error with rendered content\\\\n- `patch_scope_detection_failure` — unbalanced delimiters -> error with rendered content\\\\n- `patch_find_not_found` — find string missing -> error with rendered content\\\\n- `patch_dry_run_virtual_files` — dry-run mode\\\\n- `patch_chains_with_prior_create` — create + patch on same file\\\\n- `patch_first_match_used` — multiple anchor matches, first used\\\\n- `patch_missing_target_file` — error with rendered content\\\\n- `patch_verbose_diagnostics` — verbose=true -> diagnostics populated\\\\n- `patch_idempotency_with_skip_if` — run twice, second skips\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- The system SHALL pass all patch unit tests\\\\n- Every AC from FR-7 SHALL be covered by at least one test\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.1\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.6: Integration test fixtures for patch\\\\n\\\\n**Directory:** `tests/fixtures/` (new subdirectories)\\\\n\\\\n| Fixture | Purpose | Key ACs |\\\\n|---------|---------|---------|\\\\n| `patch-class-body-field` | Add field to Python class after last field | AC-7.1, AC-3.2, AC-5.4 |\\\\n| `patch-function-body` | Insert into Python function body | AC-3.3, AC-7.1 |\\\\n| `patch-braces` | Insert into Rust struct (braces scope) | AC-4.1, AC-7.1 |\\\\n| `patch-brackets` | Insert into Python list (brackets scope) | AC-4.2 |\\\\n| `patch-function-signature` | Add parameter to function signature | AC-4.4 |\\\\n| `patch-find-narrowing` | Find + before_close on list_display | AC-6.1, AC-6.2, AC-6.4 |\\\\n| `patch-skip-if` | Skip when content already present | AC-7.2, AC-N3.1 |\\\\n| `patch-sorted` | Sorted insertion in alphabetical list | AC-5.7 |\\\\n| `patch-scope-line` | Default scope (line) = inject after | AC-7.12 |\\\\n| `patch-indent-matching` | Auto-adjust indentation on insert | AC-7.7 |\\\\n| `patch-nested-delimiters` | Scope with nested braces | AC-4.5 |\\\\n| `patch-string-delimiters` | Delimiters in strings ignored | AC-4.6 |\\\\n| `error-patch-no-anchor` | Anchor pattern not found | AC-7.4 |\\\\n| `error-patch-unbalanced` | Unbalanced delimiters | AC-4.9, AC-7.5 |\\\\n| `error-patch-find-missing` | Find string not in scope | AC-6.3 |\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `cargo test` runs fixture_tests, all new patch fixtures SHALL pass\\\\n- Error fixtures SHALL produce exit code 3 with structured error fields and rendered content\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.3\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 4 Validation Criteria\\\\n\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- [ ] Full patch pipeline works end-to-end: anchor->scope->position->insert\\\\n- [ ] skip_if prevents duplicate content\\\\n- [ ] Indentation matching adjusts inserted content correctly\\\\n- [ ] scope: line behaves like inject after\\\\n- [ ] Scope detection errors include rendered content\\\\n- [ ] Find narrowing works with sub-scopes\\\\n- [ ] Verbose output shows complete scope diagnostics\\\\n- [ ] All 15 integration fixtures pass\\\\n- [ ] No regressions in existing tests\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 5: Integration Testing + Full Validation\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** All FRs and NFRs\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 2 + Phase 4\\\\n**Estimated tests added:** ~15\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.1: `combined-all-ops` Second run should show all skips (AC-N3.1, AC-N3.3).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 2, Phase 4\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.3: Cross-language scope detection fixtures (P)\\\\n\\\\nAdd fixtures that test scope detection against real-world code patterns:\\\\n\\\\n| Fixture | Language | Scope Type |\\\\n|---------|----------|------------|\\\\n| `patch-python-class` | Python | class_body, after_last_field |\\\\n| `patch-rust-struct` | Rust | braces, before_close |\\\\n| `patch-typescript-interface` | TypeScript | braces, after_last_field |\\\\n| `patch-go-function` | Go | braces, before_close |\\\\n| `patch-yaml-block` | YAML | block, after |\\\\n\\\\nEach fixture has a realistic `existing/` file and verifies correct scope detection + insertion.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- The system SHALL detect scopes correctly across Python, Rust, TypeScript, Go, YAML (AC-N1.1, AC-N1.2)\\\\n- No language identifier or configuration SHALL be required (AC-N1.3)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 4\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.4: Determinism test for patch/replace\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `tests/integration.rs` — add test alongside existing `determinism_identical_output_across_runs`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\n#[test]\\\\nfn determinism_patch_replace() {\\\\n    // Run a patch+replace recipe 3 times, assert byte-identical output (AC-N4.1, AC-N4.2)\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 2, Phase 4\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.5: Error recovery coverage\\\\n\\\\nVerify every row in the SPEC.md Error Handling table has a test. +--------------------->\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Parallel opportunities:**\\\\n- Phase 2 (replace) and Phase 3 (scope) can run in parallel after Phase 1\\\\n- Within Phase 3: Tasks 3.2 (indent), 3.3 (delimiter), 3.4 (position) are parallel\\\\n- Within Phase 3: Tasks 3.5, 3.6, 3.7, 3.8 (tests) are parallel\\\\n- Within Phase 5: Tasks 5.3, 5.4, 5.5 are parallel\\\\n\\\\n**Critical path:** Phase 1 -> Phase 3 -> Phase 4 -> Phase 5\\\\n\\\\n## Task Summary\\\\n\\\\n| Phase | Tasks | New Files | Tests Added |\\\\n|-------|-------|-----------|-------------|\\\\n| 1: Recipe Parsing | 5 | 0 | ~35 |\\\\n| 2: Replace Op | 6 | 1 (`operations/replace.rs`) + 9 fixture dirs | ~25 |\\\\n| 3: Scope Detection | 8 | 4 (`scope/mod.rs`, `indent.rs`, `delimiter.rs`, `position.rs`) | ~60 |\\\\n| 4: Patch Op | 6 | 1 (`operations/patch.rs`) + 15 fixture dirs | ~30 |\\\\n| 5: Integration | 7 | 7+ fixture dirs | ~15 |\\\\n| **Total** | **32** | **6 source + 31 fixture dirs** | **~165** |\\\\n\\\\nProjected test count: 191 (existing) + 165 (new) = **~356 tests**\\\\n\\\\n## Open Decisions\\\\n\\\\n| # | Decision | Recommendation | Status |\\\\n|---|----------|----------------|--------|\\\\n| 1 | Extend `OpResult::Success` with `diagnostics: Option<Value>` for verbose scope output | Add the field in Phase 4, backfill existing ops with `None` | [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED] |\\\\n| 2 | `pattern` mode: match first contiguous block or all matches? | First contiguous block (predictable, documented in SPEC) | Decided |\\\\n| 3 | Indentation matching: detect from file-wide convention or immediate context? | Immediate context (insertion point line) | Decided |\\\\n| 4 | Should `after_last_method` handle Rust `impl` blocks with `pub fn`? | Yes — regex `^\\\\\\\\s+(pub\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn\\\\\\\\|async fn) \\\\\\\\w+` covers it | Decided Execution Plan: replace-patch workstream\\\\n\\\\n> Generated: 2026-04-04\\\\n> Status: Ready for review\\\\n> Codebase state: 191 tests passing, 24 integration fixtures\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 1: Recipe Parsing Extensions\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-1 (AC-1.1 through AC-1.19)\\\\n**Dependencies:** None\\\\n**Estimated tests added:** ~35\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.1: Define new types in `src/recipe.rs`\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` (after line 89, below existing `FileOp` enum)\\\\n\\\\nAdd types: `ReplaceSpec` (Between/Pattern), `Fallback` (Append/Prepend/Skip/Error), `Anchor` struct (pattern, scope, find, position), `ScopeType` enum (8 variants: Line, Block, ClassBody, FunctionBody, FunctionSignature, Braces, Brackets, Parens), `Position` enum (7 variants: Before, After, BeforeClose, AfterLastField, AfterLastMethod, AfterLastImport, Sorted).\\\\n\\\\nExtend `FileOp` enum (line 76) with `Replace` and `Patch` variants. Update `op_type_str()` (line 92) and `template()` (line 99).\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `FileOp::Replace` is constructed, the system SHALL expose `op_type_str() == \\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\"` and `template()` returning the template field\\\\n- WHEN `FileOp::Patch` is constructed, the system SHALL expose `op_type_str() == \\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\"` and `template()` returning the template field\\\\n- The system SHALL derive `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq` for all new types\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Nothing\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.2: Add `RawAnchor` serde struct and extend `RawFileOp`\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` (near line 118)\\\\n\\\\nAdd `RawAnchor` (pattern, scope, find, position) and `RawBetween` (start, end) deserialization structs.\\\\n\\\\nModify `RawFileOp`: change `replace`/`patch` from `Option<serde_yaml::Value>` to `Option<String>`. Add fields: `between: Option<RawBetween>`, `pattern: Option<String>`, `fallback: Option<String>`, `anchor: Option<RawAnchor>`. Update hint message (line 239) to list new valid field names.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN a recipe YAML contains `replace: \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\"` with `between:` or `pattern:`, the system SHALL deserialize without error\\\\n- WHEN a recipe YAML contains `patch: \\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\"` with `anchor:`, the system SHALL deserialize without error\\\\n- The system SHALL accept `anchor` as a nested object with `pattern`, `scope`, `find`, `position` subfields\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.3: Implement `convert_file_op` logic for Replace and Patch\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` — replace rejection block at lines 276-291\\\\n\\\\n**Replace conversion:** Validate exactly one of `between`/`pattern`. If `between`: validate `start`+`end` present, compile-check both as regex. If `pattern`: compile-check regex. Parse `fallback` (default: Error). Reject invalid values with exit 1.\\\\n\\\\n**Patch conversion:** Validate `anchor` present with required `pattern` field. Compile-check anchor pattern. Parse `scope` string into `ScopeType` (default: Line). Parse `position` string into `Position` (default: After). Pass through `find` as Option. Invalid scope/position/fallback exits 1 listing valid options.\\\\n\\\\nAdd helpers: `parse_scope_type()`, `parse_position()`, `parse_fallback()`.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN a replace op has `between` with valid `start`/`end`, the system SHALL parse as `ReplaceSpec::Between` (AC-1.1, AC-1.2)\\\\n- WHEN a replace op has `pattern`, the system SHALL parse as `ReplaceSpec::Pattern` (AC-1.3)\\\\n- IF both `between` and `pattern` present, the system SHALL exit code 1 (AC-1.5)\\\\n- IF neither `between` nor `pattern`, the system SHALL exit code 1 (AC-1.6)\\\\n- IF `between` missing `start`/`end`, the system SHALL exit code 1 naming field (AC-1.7)\\\\n- IF regex fails compile, the system SHALL exit code 1 with pattern and error (AC-1.8)\\\\n- IF invalid `fallback`/`scope`/`position`, the system SHALL exit code 1 listing valid options (AC-1.9, AC-1.15, AC-1.16)\\\\n- WHEN `anchor.scope` omitted, the system SHALL default to `Line` (AC-1.17)\\\\n- WHEN `anchor.position` omitted, the system SHALL default to `After` (AC-1.18)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1, Task 1.2\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.4: Update `jig validate`/`jig vars` output\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — `build_validate_json()` at line 382\\\\n\\\\nExtend match on `FileOp` to include Replace and Patch variants in the JSON output.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.3\\\\n\\\\n### Task 1.5: Unit tests for all Phase 1 parsing paths\\\\n\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\n\\\\n**Valid:** parse_replace_between, parse_replace_pattern, parse_replace_fallback_variants, parse_replace_fallback_default, parse_patch_full_anchor, parse_patch_minimal, parse_patch_with_skip_if, parse_patch_all_scope_types, parse_patch_all_position_types.\\\\n\\\\n**Error (each asserts exit 1):** reject_replace_both_between_and_pattern, reject_replace_neither, reject_replace_between_missing_start, reject_replace_between_missing_end, reject_replace_invalid_regex_*, reject_replace_invalid_fallback, reject_patch_missing_anchor, reject_patch_missing_anchor_pattern, reject_patch_invalid_anchor_regex, reject_patch_invalid_scope_type, reject_patch_invalid_position_type.\\\\n\\\\n**Regression:** existing_create_inject_still_parse. Update old v0.1 rejection tests (lines 627-655).\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.3\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 1 Validation\\\\n- `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests; `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- All error cases AC-1.5 through AC-1.16 produce exit 1 with clear messages\\\\n- Regex compile-checked at parse time; `jig validate` reports replace/patch ops\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 2: Replace Operation\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-2 (AC-2.1 through AC-2.14), FR-8 (partial)\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 1\\\\n**Estimated tests added:** ~25\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.1: Create `src/operations/replace.rs` (P with 2.5)\\\\n\\\\nFollow inject.rs signature: `execute(rendered_path, rendered_content, spec, fallback, ctx, verbose) -> OpResult`.\\\\n\\\\n**Core logic:** resolve path, read file (virtual_files then disk), match region (between or pattern), handle no-match via fallback, replace region, write back.\\\\n\\\\n**`match_between`:** Find first start regex match, then first end match after start. Markers preserved (exclusive). Adjacent markers = empty region insert. Missing end after start = error with line number.\\\\n\\\\n**`match_pattern`:** Find first contiguous block of matching lines. Replace entirely.\\\\n\\\\n**`apply_fallback`:** Append/Prepend = write content, return Success with fallback location. Skip = OpResult::Skip. Error = OpResult::Error with rendered content.\\\\n\\\\n**Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\n- WHEN `between` markers match, the system SHALL replace content exclusively (AC-2.1, AC-2.7, AC-2.8)\\\\n- WHEN `pattern` matches, the system SHALL replace contiguous lines (AC-2.2)\\\\n- WHEN fallback triggers, the system SHALL dispatch correctly (AC-2.3-2.6)\\\\n- IF target missing, exit 3 with rendered content (AC-2.10)\\\\n- IF end not found after start, exit 3 with start line (AC-2.11)\\\\n- The system SHALL support dry-run via virtual_files (AC-2.13)\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.2: Wire replace into `src/operations/mod.rs`\\\\n\\\\nAdd `pub mod replace;` and match arm in `execute_operation()`.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.3: Wire replace into `src/main.rs`\\\\n\\\\nAdd match arm for `FileOp::Replace` in rendered_path block. Render replace target path as Jinja2.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.2\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.4: Verbose output for replace\\\\n\\\\nExisting output.rs handles all OpResult variants generically. No changes needed for Phase 2. Verbose scope diagnostics deferred to Phase 4.\\\\n\\\\n**[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]**: Extend `OpResult::Success` with `diagnostics: Option<Value>` now or in Phase 4? Recommendation: Phase 4.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.5: Unit tests for replace (P with 2.1)\\\\n\\\\n**Between:** replaces_exclusively, adjacent_markers_inserts, multiline, end_not_found, start_not_found.\\\\n**Pattern:** replaces_matched, single_line, no_match.\\\\n**Fallback:** append, prepend, skip, error, error_is_default.\\\\n**Edge:** missing_file, dry_run_virtual_files, chains_with_create, reports_replace_action, verbose.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 2.6: Integration test fixtures\\\\n\\\\n9 fixtures: replace-between, replace-between-empty, replace-pattern, replace-fallback-append, replace-fallback-prepend, replace-fallback-skip, replace-fallback-error, error-replace-no-match, error-replace-end-missing.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.3\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 2 Validation\\\\n- `cargo test` + `cargo clippy` clean; between/pattern/fallback all work\\\\n- Missing file exits 3; dry-run works; replace alongside create/inject; all 9 fixtures pass\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 3: Scope Detection\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-3, FR-4, FR-5, FR-6\\\\n**Dependencies:** Task 1.1 only (ScopeType enum). Can run in parallel with Phase 2.\\\\n**Estimated tests added:** ~60\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.1: Create `src/scope/mod.rs` (P with Phase 2)\\\\n\\\\n**New directory:** `src/scope/`. Add `mod scope;` in `src/main.rs`.\\\\n\\\\nDefine: `ScopeResult` (start_line, end_line, closing_line, is_empty), `PositionResult` (insertion_line, indent, fallback), `FindResult` (found_line, sub_scope).\\\\n\\\\nImplement: `detect_scope()` dispatching to indent/delimiter by ScopeType. `find_within_scope()` searching within scope, auto-detecting sub-scopes. `detect_sub_scope()` heuristic for trailing delimiters and assignment patterns.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.2: Create `src/scope/indent.rs` (P with 3.3, 3.4)\\\\n\\\\n`detect_indent_scope(lines, anchor_line, scope_type) -> Result<ScopeResult>`.\\\\n\\\\nAlgorithm: measure anchor indent, walk forward including deeper-indented lines, stop at same/shallower. Blank lines don't terminate. Handle ClassBody/FunctionBody (body starts after colon). Handle multi-line class declarations, decorators, mixed tabs/spaces.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.3: Create `src/scope/delimiter.rs` (P with 3.2, 3.4)\\\\n\\\\n`detect_delimiter_scope(lines, anchor_line, open, close) -> Result<ScopeResult>`.\\\\n\\\\nAlgorithm: find first opener on/after anchor, track nesting depth with string/comment/escape awareness, close when depth=0. CharScanner struct for character-by-character traversal. Handle nested delimiters, strings (single/double/backtick), comments (// # /* */), escapes.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.4: Create `src/scope/position.rs` (P with 3.2, 3.3)\\\\n\\\\n`resolve_position(lines, scope, position) -> Result<PositionResult>`.\\\\n\\\\n7 positions: Before (scope start), After (scope end+1), BeforeClose (closing_line or end+1), AfterLastField (`^\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\s*[:=]`, fallback: BeforeClose), AfterLastMethod (`^\\\\\\\\s+(pub\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn|async fn) \\\\\\\\w+` etc, find body end recursively, fallback: BeforeClose), AfterLastImport (`^\\\\\\\\s*(from|import) `, fallback: Before), Sorted (alphabetical among siblings). Detect indent from context.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.5: Unit tests for indent scope (P with 3.6, 3.7, 3.8)\\\\n\\\\n11 tests: python_class_body, python_function_body, blank_lines, empty_scope, nested_class, nested_function, mixed_tabs_spaces, multiline_class_declaration, decorator, yaml_block, deeply_nested.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.2\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.6: Unit tests for delimiter scope (P with 3.5, 3.7, 3.8)\\\\n\\\\n16 tests: rust_struct, ts_interface, go_function, python_list, function_signature, nested, strings (3 quote types), comments (2 types), escapes, missing_opener, unbalanced, empty, opener_on_anchor.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.3\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.7: Unit tests for position resolution (P with 3.5, 3.6, 3.8)\\\\n\\\\n13 tests: before, after, before_close, after_last_field (Python + Rust), after_last_method (Python + Rust), after_last_import, sorted, field_fallback, method_fallback, import_fallback, indent_detection.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.4\\\\n\\\\n### Task 3.8: Unit tests for find narrowing (P with 3.5, 3.6, 3.7)\\\\n\\\\n5 tests: find_basic, find_bracket_sub_scope, find_brace_sub_scope, find_not_found, find_before_close_sub_scope.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 3 Validation\\\\n- `cargo test` + `cargo clippy` clean\\\\n- Python/Rust/TS/Go/YAML scope detection works without language config\\\\n- Blank lines, nested delimiters, strings, comments, escapes all handled\\\\n- All 7 positions + 3 fallbacks tested; find narrowing + sub-scopes work\\\\n- Deterministic: same input = same boundaries\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 4: Patch Operation\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-6, FR-7, FR-8\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 1 + Phase 3\\\\n**Estimated tests added:** ~30\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.1: Create `src/operations/patch.rs`\\\\n\\\\n`execute(rendered_path, rendered_content, rendered_skip_if, anchor, ctx, verbose) -> OpResult`.\\\\n\\\\nPipeline: resolve path -> read file -> check skip_if (whole file, not just scope) -> find anchor (first match) -> detect scope -> apply find narrowing -> resolve position -> adjust indentation -> insert content -> write back.\\\\n\\\\n**Indentation matching:** Detect base indent of rendered content, detect target indent from insertion context, adjust each line (remove base, add target, preserve relative).\\\\n\\\\n**scope: line degenerate case:** Skip scope/find/position, just insert after anchor line.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1, Phase 3\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.2: Wire patch into `src/operations/mod.rs`\\\\n\\\\nAdd `pub mod patch;` and match arm.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.3: Wire patch into `src/main.rs`\\\\n\\\\nAdd match arms for rendered_path and rendered_skip_if.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.2\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.4: Complete verbose scope diagnostics\\\\n\\\\n**[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]**: Add `diagnostics: Option<serde_json::Value>` to `OpResult::Success`.\\\\n\\\\nPopulate for patch (anchor_line, scope_start, scope_end, insertion_line, find_match_line, sub_scope, position_fallback) and replace (match_mode, region, lines_replaced). Update output.rs for JSON and human display. Backfill create.rs/inject.rs with `diagnostics: None`.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.1, Task 2.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.5: Unit tests for patch\\\\n\\\\n20 tests covering full pipeline, skip_if, scope:line, indent matching, sorted, all error cases, dry-run, chaining, first match, verbose diagnostics, idempotency.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.1\\\\n\\\\n### Task 4.6: Integration test fixtures\\\\n\\\\n15 fixtures: patch-class-body-field, patch-function-body, patch-braces, patch-brackets, patch-function-signature, patch-find-narrowing, patch-skip-if, patch-sorted, patch-scope-line, patch-indent-matching, patch-nested-delimiters, patch-string-delimiters, error-patch-no-anchor, error-patch-unbalanced, error-patch-find-missing.\\\\n\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.3\\\\n\\\\n### Phase 4 Validation\\\\n- Full pipeline anchor->scope->position->insert works; skip_if prevents duplicates\\\\n- Indentation matching correct; scope:line = inject after; errors include rendered content\\\\n- Find narrowing with sub-scopes works; verbose diagnostics complete; all 15 fixtures pass\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Phase 5: Integration Testing + Full Validation\\\\n\\\\n**Traces to:** All FRs and NFRs\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 2 + Phase 4\\\\n**Estimated tests added:** ~15\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.1: `combined-all-ops` fixture\\\\n\\\\nRecipe with create + inject + replace + patch. Tests all four ops with ordered execution.\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.2: `combined-patch-idempotent` fixture\\\\n\\\\nPatch-heavy recipe run twice. Second run = all skips.\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.3: Cross-language scope fixtures (P)\\\\n\\\\n5 fixtures: patch-python-class, patch-rust-struct, patch-typescript-interface, patch-go-function, patch-yaml-block.\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.4: Determinism test (P with 5.3)\\\\n\\\\nRun patch+replace recipe 3 times, assert byte-identical output.\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.5: Error recovery coverage (P with 5.3)\\\\n\\\\nVerify every SPEC.md error table row has a test.\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.6: Snapshot tests\\\\n\\\\n4 snapshots: replace JSON, patch JSON, combined JSON, patch error message.\\\\n\\\\n### Task 5.7: Final validation\\\\n\\\\n`cargo test` + `cargo clippy` clean. 300+ tests. All fixture tests pass. All four ops in one recipe. Idempotency. Determinism. Full error coverage. Cross-language scope detection.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Execution Order\\\\n\\\\n```\\\\nPhase 1 -----> Phase 2 (replace) --------+\\\\n   |                                      |\\\\n   +-----> Phase 3 (scope)  ----> Phase 4 (patch) ----> Phase 5\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\n**Parallel:** Phase 2 || Phase 3 after Phase 1. Within Phase 3: indent || delimiter || position. Within Phase 5: 5.3 || 5.4 || 5.5.\\\\n\\\\n**Critical path:** Phase 1 -> Phase 3 -> Phase 4 -> Phase 5\\\\n\\\\n## Summary\\\\n\\\\n| Phase | Tasks | New Source Files | New Fixture Dirs | Tests |\\\\n|-------|-------|-----------------|------------------|-------|\\\\n| 1 | 5 | 0 | 0 | ~35 |\\\\n| 2 | 6 | 1 | 9 | ~25 |\\\\n| 3 | 8 | 4 | 0 | ~60 |\\\\n| 4 | 6 | 1 | 15 | ~30 |\\\\n| 5 | 7 | 0 | 7+ | ~15 |\\\\n| **Total** | **32** | **6** | **31** | **~165** |\\\\n\\\\n**Projected:** 191 (existing) + 165 (new) = ~356 tests\\\\n\\\\n## Open Decisions\\\\n\\\\n| # | Decision | Recommendation | Status |\\\\n|---|----------|----------------|--------|\\\\n| 1 | Add `diagnostics: Option<Value>` to `OpResult::Success` | Add in Phase 4, backfill with None | [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED] |\\\\n| 2 | `pattern` mode: first contiguous block vs all | First contiguous block | Decided |\\\\n| 3 | Indentation: file-wide vs immediate context | Immediate context | Decided |\\\\n| 4 | `after_last_method` for Rust `pub fn` | Yes, covered by regex | Decided |\\\\n\\\"}}],\\\"terminal_reason\\\":\\\"completed\\\",\\\"fast_mode_state\\\":\\\"off\\\",\\\"uuid\\\":\\\"76abe750-0bea-4f0e-82d9-352d4abc932e\\\"}\\ndocs/ARCHITECTURE.md:3:Target architecture for jig, the iterative build strategy to get there, and the evaluation criteria at each gate. This document grounds all workstream planning.\\ndocs/ARCHITECTURE.md:620:These are explicitly out of scope for the architecture and will be addressed in later workstreams:\\n./AGENTS.md:13:1. `/ws-init <name>` - Initialize workstream docs\\n./AGENTS.md:14:2. `/ws-plan <name>` - Dual-agent planning (Claude + Codex), synthesize\\n./AGENTS.md:15:3. `/ws-plan-review <name>` - Adversarial review of plan/specs\\n./AGENTS.md:16:4. `/ws-execute <name> [task]` - Iterative execution with fresh-context retries\\n./AGENTS.md:17:5. `/ws-validate` - Check task readiness (tests + VALIDATION.md)\\n./AGENTS.md:18:6. `/ws-review <name> [task]` - Adversarial code review\\n./AGENTS.md:19:7. `/ws-consolidate` - Capture learnings, update durable docs\\n./AGENTS.md:23:- EARS format for all acceptance criteria in SPEC.md\\n./AGENTS.md:24:- PLAN.md is source of truth for status/phases\\n./AGENTS.md:25:- SPEC.md is canonical for requirements and interfaces\\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/SPEC.md:1:# SPEC.md\\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/SPEC.md:9:The replace-patch workstream extends jig from greenfield-only (create, inject) to brownfield operations — modifying existing code structurally. Two new operations are added:\\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/SPEC.md:16:Out of scope for this workstream: workflows (v0.3), libraries (v0.4), scan/infer/check (v0.7+), custom filters via shell, .jigrc.yaml config.\\ndocs/workstreams/workflows/reviews/code-review-claude-20260404-200636.md:1:{\\\"type\\\":\\\"result\\\",\\\"subtype\\\":\\\"success\\\",\\\"is_error\\\":false,\\\"duration_ms\\\":613439,\\\"duration_api_ms\\\":682224,\\\"num_turns\\\":29,\\\"result\\\":\\\"Good — `Replace` does NOT have a `skip_if` field, so `run_recipe` correctly only handles skip_if for Inject and Patch.\\\\n\\\\nI now have everything I need. Here's the review:\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n**Verdict: Request Changes**\\\\n\\\\n## Critical (must fix before merge)\\\\n\\\\n**C1: `extract_rendered_from_error` returns the error description, not the rendered template content**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:621-628`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nfn extract_rendered_from_error(err: &JigError) -> Option<String> {\\\\n    match err {\\\\n        JigError::TemplateRendering(se) | JigError::FileOperation(se) => {\\\\n            Some(se.what.clone())  // ← BUG: \\\\\\\"what\\\\\\\" is \\\\\\\"anchor pattern not found\\\\\\\", not the rendered content\\\\n        }\\\\n        _ => None,\\\\n    }\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis is called at line 519 to populate `StepResult::Error::rendered_content`, which propagates to the JSON output's `rendered_content` field. The spec says (AC-8.4, AC-N3.2): rendered_content should contain the actual rendered template so an LLM caller can fall back to manual editing (I-4, I-10). Instead, it contains the error description string.\\\\n\\\\nThe fix: extract from the last `OpResult::Error` in `partial_results` instead:\\\\n```rust\\\\nErr((e, partial_results)) => {\\\\n    let rendered = partial_results.last().and_then(|r| match r {\\\\n        OpResult::Error { rendered_content, .. } if !rendered_content.is_empty() => {\\\\n            Some(rendered_content.clone())\\\\n        }\\\\n        _ => None,\\\\n    });\\\\n    // ...\\\\n}\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nFor non-operation errors (variable validation, when-template failure), `rendered_content: None` is correct — there is no rendered content to extract. The current code incorrectly returns `Some(\\\\\\\"missing required variable 'name'\\\\\\\")` as rendered_content for those cases too.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Major (should fix)\\\\n\\\\n**M1: `format_workflow_json` status logic breaks with per-step `on_error` overrides**\\\\n`src/output.rs:300-306`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet status = if !has_errors || exit_code == 0 {\\\\n    \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\"\\\\n} else if exit_code == 3 && result.on_error == workflow::OnError::Report {\\\\n    \\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\"\\\\n} else {\\\\n    \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"\\\\n};\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis checks `result.on_error` (the **workflow-level** default). When a step uses `on_error: report` as a per-step override but the workflow default is `stop`, `compute_workflow_exit_code` correctly returns 3, but the status incorrectly becomes `\\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\"` instead of `\\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\"`. The LLM caller sees exit code 3 + status \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\" — a contradiction since \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\" implies the workflow halted but exit 3 means report-mode partial failure.\\\\n\\\\nFix: don't rely on `result.on_error` for the \\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\" check:\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet status = if !has_errors || exit_code == 0 {\\\\n    \\\\\\\"success\\\\\\\"\\\\n} else {\\\\n    // Determine if any errored step used report mode\\\\n    let any_report = result.steps.iter().any(|s| matches!(s, StepResult::Error { .. }))\\\\n        && exit_code == 3;\\\\n    // But also check if we stopped early (stop mode with file-op error also gives exit 3)\\\\n    let stopped_early = result.steps.iter().last().map_or(false, |s| s.is_error())\\\\n        && result.steps.len() < total_steps;  // need total from workflow\\\\n    if any_report && !stopped_early { \\\\\\\"partial\\\\\\\" } else { \\\\\\\"error\\\\\\\" }\\\\n};\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nOr simpler: pass a flag from `compute_workflow_exit_code` indicating whether report-mode errors were present.\\\\n\\\\n**M2: `resolve_step_variables` panics on non-object JSON via `.unwrap()`**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:369` and `src/workflow.rs:394`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet obj = vars.as_object_mut().unwrap();\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nIf `vars` is ever not a JSON object (e.g., `null`, array, string), this panics with no context. This is a public function. While `variables::collect_vars` should always return an object, defensive code here avoids an opaque panic. Return a `JigError::VariableValidation` instead.\\\\n\\\\n**M3: `cmd_run` and `run_recipe` are divergent copies of the same logic**\\\\n`src/main.rs:335-427` vs `src/workflow.rs:551-618`\\\\n\\\\nThe rendering pipeline (create env → render templates → render paths → render skip_if → execute ops) is duplicated. They're already subtly different:\\\\n- `cmd_run` returns early via `handle_early_error` for template failures (no partial results)\\\\n- `run_recipe` collects into a vec and returns `Err((error, vec![]))` for template failures\\\\n\\\\nIf a new operation type or rendering phase is added (e.g., for libraries v0.4), the change must be made in both places. Refactor `cmd_run` to call `run_recipe` and handle early errors at the CLI layer.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Minor (nice to fix)\\\\n\\\\n**m1: Determinism unit test is trivially weak**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:1215-1233`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nresults.push(format!(\\\\\\\"{:?}\\\\\\\", result.steps.len()));\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis only compares the Debug representation of a `usize` (e.g., `\\\\\\\"1\\\\\\\"` == `\\\\\\\"1\\\\\\\"`). It doesn't compare actual output content, file content, or operation results. A real determinism test should compare the `StepResult` variants and their contents across runs (or use the integration-level determinism test which is much stronger).\\\\n\\\\n**m2: `load_workflow` collects all validation errors but only reports the first**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:303-306`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nlet (_, _, err) = validation_errors.into_iter().next().unwrap();\\\\nreturn Err(JigError::RecipeValidation(err));\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nIf steps 2 and 5 both reference invalid recipes, the user only sees the error for step 2. They fix it, re-run, and then see step 5. Reporting all errors at once would be more LLM-friendly. The errors are already collected — just use `JigError::VariableValidation(errors)` style or a new multi-error variant.\\\\n\\\\n**m3: No integration test for AC-7.8 (recipe passed to `jig workflow`)**\\\\nThe spec requires (AC-7.8): passing a recipe file to `jig workflow` should exit 1 with a message pointing to `jig run`. The code handles this correctly (`main.rs:510-519`), but there's no test fixture for it. Add an `error-workflow-is-recipe` fixture with a recipe.yaml and expected_exit_code=1.\\\\n\\\\n**m4: Early error handler creates misleading JSON**\\\\n`src/main.rs:473-507`\\\\n\\\\nPre-execution errors produce JSON with `\\\\\\\"steps\\\\\\\": [{\\\\\\\"recipe\\\\\\\": \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"}]` — an empty recipe string and a fake step entry. An LLM caller might try to interpret this. For pre-execution errors (file not found, bad YAML, variable validation), consider `\\\\\\\"steps\\\\\\\": []` instead, with the error at the top level.\\\\n\\\\n**m5: `validate_workflow` sets `valid: true` for all steps unconditionally**\\\\n`src/workflow.rs:342-344`\\\\n\\\\n```rust\\\\nvalid: true, // already validated in load_workflow\\\\n```\\\\n\\\\nThis is correct (load_workflow would have returned an error if any step was invalid), but it means `jig validate` never produces a per-step error report — if any recipe is invalid, the whole command fails before reaching the validation output. AC-7.5/AC-7.6 only require per-step output when validation succeeds, so this is spec-compliant, but a validation command that shows \\\\\\\"steps 1, 3 valid / step 2 invalid\\\\\\\" would be more useful.\\\\n\\\\n---\\\\n\\\\n## Strengths\\\\n\\\\n- **Clean architecture**: Parsing → variable resolution → condition evaluation → execution is well-separated. Each concern is testable in isolation.\\\\n- **Simultaneous `vars_map` renaming**: The snapshot-then-remove-then-insert approach at lines 372-389 correctly implements non-chaining rename semantics. The test at line 887 nails the tricky case.\\\\n- **ExecutionContext sharing across steps**: Single context for virtual_files, base_dir, and force flag is the right design. Dry-run chaining works correctly (tested at line 1140).\\\\n- **File type auto-detection**: The `detect_file_type` function cleanly handles all four cases (workflow, recipe, both, neither) and integrates smoothly into `jig validate` and `jig vars`.\\\\n- **Error handling structure**: Every error path through the step execution loop respects the effective `on_error` mode (step override or workflow default). The pattern at lines 440-453 is repeated consistently for all five failure points.\\\\n- **35 unit tests + 25 integration fixtures**: Good coverage of core logic. The fixture auto-discovery pattern is elegant — no test registration needed.\\\\n- **Spec traceability**: The test names and comments reference acceptance criteria consistently.\\\",\\\"stop_reason\\\":\\\"end_turn\\\",\\\"session_id\\\":\\\"8f910b25-063b-4054-a75d-988d5e1469d4\\\",\\\"total_cost_usd\\\":2.344686099999999,\\\"usage\\\":{\\\"input_tokens\\\":20,\\\"cache_creation_input_tokens\\\":111076,\\\"cache_read_input_tokens\\\":1279858,\\\"output_tokens\\\":24985,\\\"server_tool_use\\\":{\\\"web_search_requests\\\":0,\\\"web_fetch_requests\\\":0},\\\"service_tier\\\":\\\"standard\\\",\\\"cache_creation\\\":{\\\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\\\":111076,\\\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\\\":0},\\\"inference_geo\\\":\\\"\\\",\\\"iterations\\\":[],\\\"speed\\\":\\\"standard\\\"},\\\"modelUsage\\\":{\\\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":209,\\\"outputTokens\\\":16306,\\\"cacheReadInputTokens\\\":1611206,\\\"cacheCreationInputTokens\\\":114358,\\\"webSearchRequests\\\":0,\\\"costUSD\\\":0.38580709999999996,\\\"contextWindow\\\":200000,\\\"maxOutputTokens\\\":32000},\\\"claude-opus-4-6[1m]\\\":{\\\"inputTokens\\\":20,\\\"outputTokens\\\":24985,\\\"cacheReadInputTokens\\\":1279858,\\\"cacheCreationInputTokens\\\":111076,\\\"webSearchRequests\\\":0,\\\"costUSD\\\":1.958879,\\\"contextWindow\\\":1000000,\\\"maxOutputTokens\\\":64000}},\\\"permission_denials\\\":[{\\\"tool_name\\\":\\\"Bash\\\",\\\"tool_use_id\\\":\\\"toolu_01Buj5C7L5RAzHW4Ejikn4va\\\",\\\"tool_input\\\":{\\\"command\\\":\\\"for dir in /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/error-workflow-* /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures/workflow-*; do echo \\\\\\\"=== $(basename $dir) ===\\\\\\\"; ls -la \\\\\\\"$dir\\\\\\\" | grep -E \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\\.yaml|\\\\\\\\.yml|recipe\\\\\\\"; done\\\"}},{\\\"tool_name\\\":\\\"Bash\\\",\\\"tool_use_id\\\":\\\"toolu_013zGsGm3sSwYKf76P1oVF11\\\",\\\"tool_input\\\":{\\\"command\\\":\\\"cd /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures && for dir in error-workflow-* workflow-*; do if [ -d \\\\\\\"$dir\\\\\\\" ]; then echo \\\\\\\"=== $dir ===\\\\\\\"; ls -1 \\\\\\\"$dir\\\\\\\"; echo \\\\\\\"\\\\\\\"; fi; done\\\"}},{\\\"tool_name\\\":\\\"Bash\\\",\\\"tool_use_id\\\":\\\"toolu_018dEk75WpEnBud2PEaz7nc8\\\",\\\"tool_input\\\":{\\\"command\\\":\\\"find /Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/fixtures -type d \\\\\\\\( -name \\\\\\\"error-workflow*\\\\\\\" -o -name \\\\\\\"workflow-*\\\\\\\" \\\\\\\\) -exec sh -c 'echo \\\\\\\"=== $(basename \\\\\\\"$1\\\\\\\") ===\\\\\\\"; Specifically: recipe.rs parsing infrastructure, operations/mod.rs dispatch, output.rs formatting, error.rs types, the existing integration test harness.\\n./src/library/discover.rs:243:        let lib_dir = project_dir.join(\\\".jig/libraries/testlib\\\");\\ndocs/workstreams/replace-patch/NARRATIVE.md:8:Adds two new file operations to jig — **replace** and **patch** — that let recipes modify existing code, not just create new files or inject at regex anchors.
