|\\\\\\\\n|-------|-------|-----------------|------------------|-----------|\\\\\\\\n| 1: Recipe Parsing | 5 | 0 | 0 | ~35 |\\\\\\\\n| 2: Replace Op | 7 | 1 (`operations/replace.rs`) | 9 | ~25 |\\\\\\\\n| 3: Scope Detection | 8 | 4 (`scope/mod.rs`, `indent.rs`, `delimiter.rs`, `position.rs`) | 0 | ~60 |\\\\\\\\n| 4: Patch Op | 5 | 1 (`operations/patch.rs`) | 15 | ~30 |\\\\\\\\n| 5: Integration | 7 | 0 | 7+ | ~15 |\\\\\\\\n| **Total** | **32** | **6** | **31** | **~165** |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Projected:** 191 (existing) + 165 (new) = **~356 tests**\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Agreements (consensus between both plans)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nBoth plans agree on:\\\\\\\\n1. **5-phase structure** with identical execution order and critical path\\\\\\\\n2. **Type definitions:** `ReplaceSpec`, `Fallback`, `Anchor`, `ScopeType` (8 variants), `Position` (7 variants)\\\\\\\\n3. **Replace semantics:** between mode (markers preserved, exclusive), pattern mode (first contiguous block)\\\\\\\\n4. **Fallback default:** `error`\\\\\\\\n5. **Scope detection architecture:** dispatch by ScopeType, indent vs delimiter modules, CharScanner for delimiter parsing\\\\\\\\n6. **Patch pipeline:** anchor→scope→find→position→insert with indentation matching\\\\\\\\n7. **scope: line degenerate case** — skip structural analysis, insert after anchor\\\\\\\\n8. **skip_if searches entire file**, not just scope\\\\\\\\n9. **Diagnostics via `Option<serde_json::Value>` on `OpResult::Success`** (both recommend, flagged for human decision)\\\\\\\\n10. **~165 new tests** across ~31 fixture directories + unit tests\\\\\\\\n11. **Parallel opportunities:** Phase 2 || Phase 3; within Phase 3: indent || delimiter || position; test tasks parallel with each other\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Open Decisions\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| # | Decision | Plan A | Plan B | Status |\\\\\\\\n|---|----------|--------|--------|--------|\\\\\\\\n| 1 | Add `diagnostics: Option<Value>` to `OpResult::Success` | Add in Phase 4, backfill with None | Same | **[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]** — consensus, needs confirmation |\\\\\\\\n| 2 | `pattern` mode: first contiguous block vs all matches | First contiguous block | Same | **Decided** — consensus |\\\\\\\\n| 3 | Indentation matching: file-wide vs immediate context | Immediate context | Same | **Decided** — consensus |\\\\\\\\n| 4 | `after_last_method` handles Rust `pub fn`/`async fn` | Yes, via regex | Same | **Decided** — consensus |\\\\\\\\n| 5 | Scope diagnostics on skip/error results too? | Not addressed | Raised as question | **[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]** — Plan B unique |\\\\\\\\n| 6 | Delimiter parser: JS template literals `${}`, Rust raw strings | Not addressed | Raised: v0.2 scope vs documented limitation | **[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]** — Plan B unique |\\\\\\\\n| 7 | Mixed tab/space strictness | Literal prefix comparison (per SPEC AC-3.8) | Raised: literal prefix vs conversion | **[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]** — SPEC says literal prefix, confirm |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Disagreements\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Test organization strategy\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Plan A:** All parser tests in `src/recipe.rs` `mod tests` under `mod replace_patch_parsing`. Integration tests in `tests/integration.rs`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Plan B:** Separate `spec::fr1::*`, `spec::fr2::*` etc. test modules in `tests/` for formal traceability.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]:** Plan A is simpler (co-located). Plan B gives spec-to-test audit trail. Could combine: unit tests co-located, integration tests organized by FR.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Replace task granularity\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Plan A:** Single task implements entire replace executor.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Plan B:** 4 tasks: skeleton, between mode, pattern mode, fallback.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Merged plan uses Plan B's granularity** (Tasks 2.1-2.4) for better progress tracking and parallel between/pattern implementation.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Unique Insights\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### From Plan A (not in Plan B)\\\\\\\\n- **Detailed `adjust_indentation()` algorithm** — line-by-line base indent detection and adjustment preserving relative offsets\\\\\\\\n- **Specific `detect_sub_scope()` heuristic** — trailing delimiters and assignment patterns like `list_display = [`\\\\\\\\n- **Exact line numbers** in existing codebase for surgical edits (lines 76, 89, 92, 99, 118, 197, 239, 276-291, etc.)\\\\\\\\n- **Existing `output.rs` handles replace generically** — no output.rs changes needed for Phase 2 (only Phase 4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### From Plan B (not in Plan A)\\\\\\\\n- **Spec traceability test naming** (`spec::frN::*`) for audit trail\\\\\\\\n- **Explicit separation of \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"find narrowing + sub-scope rebasing\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"** as its own implementation task\\\\\\\\n- **JS template literal `${}` nesting** — delimiter parser edge case to scope for v0.2\\\\\\\\n- **Rust raw strings** — potential delimiter parser blind spot\\\\\\\\n- **Diagnostics on skip/error** — useful for debugging failed operations, not just successful ones\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\"}},{\\\\\\\"tool_name\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"Write\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"tool_use_id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"toolu_01JWWh1NmevU8ux6Xyh6KF7T\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"tool_input\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"file_path\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/workstreams/replace-patch/exec/synthesized-20260404-151404.md\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"# Synthesized Execution Plan: replace-patch workstream\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n> Generated: 2026-04-04\\\\\\\\n> Status: Ready for review\\\\\\\\n> Sources: Claude plan (Plan A), Codex plan (Plan B)\\\\\\\\n> Codebase state: 191 tests passing, 24 integration fixtures, `regex` crate present\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Execution Order\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\nPhase 1 (recipe parsing)\\\\\\\\n   |\\\\\\\\n   +-----> Phase 2 (replace)  --------+\\\\\\\\n   |           (parallel)             |\\\\\\\\n   +-----> Phase 3 (scope)  -+       |\\\\\\\\n                              |       |\\\\\\\\n                              v       v\\\\\\\\n                          Phase 4 (patch)\\\\\\\\n                              |\\\\\\\\n                              v\\\\\\\\n                          Phase 5 (integration)\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Critical path:** Phase 1 → Phase 3 → Phase 4 → Phase 5\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Phase 1: Recipe Parsing Extensions\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-1 (AC-1.1 through AC-1.19)\\\\\\\\n**Dependencies:** None\\\\\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~35\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 1.1: Define new types in `src/recipe.rs`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` (after line 89, below existing `FileOp` enum)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAdd types:\\\\\\\\n- `ReplaceSpec` enum — `Between { start: String, end: String }`, `Pattern(String)`\\\\\\\\n- `Fallback` enum — `Append`, `Prepend`, `Skip`, `Error` (default)\\\\\\\\n- `Anchor` struct — `pattern: String`, `scope: ScopeType`, `find: Option<String>`, `position: Position`\\\\\\\\n- `ScopeType` enum — 8 variants: `Line` (default), `Block`, `ClassBody`, `FunctionBody`, `FunctionSignature`, `Braces`, `Brackets`, `Parens`\\\\\\\\n- `Position` enum — 7 variants: `Before`, `After` (default), `BeforeClose`, `AfterLastField`, `AfterLastMethod`, `AfterLastImport`, `Sorted`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExtend `FileOp` enum (line 76) with:\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\nReplace { template: String, replace: String, spec: ReplaceSpec, fallback: Fallback },\\\\\\\\nPatch { template: String, patch: String, anchor: Anchor, skip_if: Option<String> },\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nUpdate `op_type_str()` (line 92) and `template()` (line 99) for new variants.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL derive `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq` for all new types.\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `FileOp::Replace` is constructed, the system SHALL expose `op_type_str() == \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` and `template()` returning the template field.\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `FileOp::Patch` is constructed, the system SHALL expose `op_type_str() == \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` and `template()` returning the template field.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Nothing\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 1.2: Add `RawAnchor`/`RawBetween` serde structs, extend `RawFileOp`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` (near line 118, alongside `RawFileOp`)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAdd intermediate serde structs:\\\\\\\\n- `RawAnchor` — `pattern: Option<String>`, `scope: Option<String>`, `find: Option<String>`, `position: Option<String>`\\\\\\\\n- `RawBetween` — `start: Option<String>`, `end: Option<String>`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nModify `RawFileOp`:\\\\\\\\n- Change `replace`/`patch` from `Option<serde_yaml::Value>` to `Option<String>` (target path strings)\\\\\\\\n- Add fields: `between: Option<RawBetween>`, `pattern: Option<String>`, `fallback: Option<String>`, `anchor: Option<RawAnchor>`\\\\\\\\n- Update hint message (line 239) to list new valid field names\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN a recipe YAML contains `replace: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with `between:` or `pattern:`, the system SHALL deserialize without error.\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN a recipe YAML contains `patch: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"path\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with `anchor:`, the system SHALL deserialize without error.\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN unknown fields appear alongside replace/patch, the system SHALL report them via the `extra` catch-all.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 1.3: Implement `convert_file_op` logic for Replace and Patch\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` — replace rejection block at lines 276-291\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Replace conversion:**\\\\\\\\n1. Validate exactly one of `between`/`pattern` is present (not both, not neither)\\\\\\\\n2. If `between`: validate both `start` and `end` present; compile-check both as regex via `validate_regex()`\\\\\\\\n3. If `pattern`: compile-check as regex\\\\\\\\n4. Parse `fallback` string (default: `Error`): `append` | `prepend` | `skip` | `error`\\\\\\\\n5. Reject invalid values with exit code 1, listing valid options\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Patch conversion:**\\\\\\\\n1. Validate `anchor` present with required `pattern` field\\\\\\\\n2. Compile-check `anchor.pattern` as regex\\\\\\\\n3. Parse `anchor.scope` into `ScopeType` (default: `Line`); invalid → exit 1 listing valid types\\\\\\\\n4. Parse `anchor.position` into `Position` (default: `After`); invalid → exit 1 listing valid types\\\\\\\\n5. Pass through `anchor.find` as `Option<String>`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nAdd helpers: `parse_scope_type()`, `parse_position()`, `parse_fallback()`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN a replace op has `between` with valid `start`/`end`, the system SHALL parse as `ReplaceSpec::Between` (AC-1.1, AC-1.2).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN a replace op has `pattern`, the system SHALL parse as `ReplaceSpec::Pattern` (AC-1.3).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `fallback` is omitted, the system SHALL default to `error` (AC-1.4).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF both `between` and `pattern` present, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting conflicting fields (AC-1.5).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF neither `between` nor `pattern`, the system SHALL exit code 1 reporting missing match spec (AC-1.6).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `between` missing `start` or `end`, the system SHALL exit code 1 naming the missing field (AC-1.7).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF regex fails to compile, the system SHALL exit code 1 with pattern and compilation error (AC-1.8).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF invalid `fallback`/`scope`/`position` value, the system SHALL exit code 1 listing valid options (AC-1.9, AC-1.15, AC-1.16).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `anchor.scope` omitted, the system SHALL default to `Line` (AC-1.17).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `anchor.position` omitted, the system SHALL default to `After` (AC-1.18).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1, Task 1.2\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 1.4: Update `jig validate`/`jig vars` output\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — `build_validate_json()` at line 382\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nExtend match on `FileOp` variants to include Replace and Patch in validation JSON output. Template existence validation already works via `op.template()` (extended in Task 1.1).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `jig validate` runs on a recipe with replace/patch ops, the system SHALL report operation counts including replace/patch.\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `jig vars` runs on a recipe with replace/patch ops, the system SHALL succeed and display variables.\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL validate template file existence for replace and patch operations.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.3\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 1.5: Unit tests for all Phase 1 parsing paths\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/recipe.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Valid parsing tests (~10):**\\\\\\\\n- `parse_replace_between` — between.start + between.end → `ReplaceSpec::Between`\\\\\\\\n- `parse_replace_pattern` — pattern → `ReplaceSpec::Pattern`\\\\\\\\n- `parse_replace_fallback_variants` — all four values parse correctly\\\\\\\\n- `parse_replace_fallback_default` — omitted → `Fallback::Error`\\\\\\\\n- `parse_patch_full_anchor` — all anchor fields → `FileOp::Patch`\\\\\\\\n- `parse_patch_minimal` — just anchor.pattern → defaults scope=Line, position=After\\\\\\\\n- `parse_patch_with_skip_if` — skip_if captured\\\\\\\\n- `parse_patch_all_scope_types` — all 8 scope type strings\\\\\\\\n- `parse_patch_all_position_types` — all 7 position type strings\\\\\\\\n- `parse_replace_path_is_string` — `replace:` field is a path string\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Error case tests (~13, each asserts exit code 1):**\\\\\\\\n- `reject_replace_both_between_and_pattern` (AC-1.5)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_replace_neither_between_nor_pattern` (AC-1.6)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_replace_between_missing_start` (AC-1.7)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_replace_between_missing_end` (AC-1.7)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_regex_start` (AC-1.8)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_regex_end` (AC-1.8)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_regex_pattern` (AC-1.8)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_replace_invalid_fallback` (AC-1.9)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_patch_missing_anchor` (AC-1.13)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_patch_missing_anchor_pattern` (AC-1.13)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_patch_invalid_anchor_regex` (AC-1.14)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_patch_invalid_scope_type` (AC-1.15)\\\\\\\\n- `reject_patch_invalid_position_type` (AC-1.16)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Regression:** `existing_create_inject_still_parse`. Update old v0.1 rejection tests (lines 627-655).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.3\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase 1 Validation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\\\\\n- [ ] All 13 error cases AC-1.5 through AC-1.16 produce exit 1 with clear messages listing valid options\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Regex patterns compile-checked at parse time\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `jig validate` reports replace/patch operation counts\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Existing 191 tests unaffected\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Phase 2: Replace Operation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-2 (AC-2.1 through AC-2.14), FR-8 (partial), NFR-2\\\\\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 1. **Can run in parallel with Phase 3.**\\\\\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~25\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 2.1: Create `src/operations/replace.rs` — executor skeleton (P with 2.6)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**New file:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSignature follows inject.rs pattern:\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\npub fn execute(\\\\\\\\n    rendered_path: &str,\\\\\\\\n    rendered_content: &str,\\\\\\\\n    spec: &ReplaceSpec,\\\\\\\\n    fallback: &Fallback,\\\\\\\\n    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\\\\\n    verbose: bool,\\\\\\\\n) -> OpResult\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nCore pipeline: resolve path → read file (virtual_files then disk) → dispatch to match mode → handle no-match via fallback → replace matched region → write back (virtual_files always; disk if not dry-run) → return `OpResult::Success` with `action: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN replace succeeds, the system SHALL report `action: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"replace\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with path, location description, and line count (AC-2.9).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF target file missing, the system SHALL exit code 3 with rendered content (AC-2.10).\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL resolve target path relative to `--base-dir` (AC-2.12).\\\\\\\\n- State: WHILE `--dry-run` is enabled, the system SHALL read/write through `virtual_files` for chaining (AC-2.13).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF write fails due to permissions, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-2.14).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 2.2: Implement `match_between()` (P with 2.3)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\nfn match_between(lines: &[&str], start_pattern: &str, end_pattern: &str) -> Result<(usize, usize), NoMatch>\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Find first line matching `start` regex\\\\\\\\n- No start match → `NoMatch` (for fallback)\\\\\\\\n- Find first line matching `end` regex **after** start\\\\\\\\n- No end after start → `OpResult::Error` with \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"end marker not found after line N\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" (AC-2.11)\\\\\\\\n- Return `(start_idx, end_idx)` — content between is exclusive (markers preserved, AC-2.7)\\\\\\\\n- Adjacent markers (start_idx + 1 == end_idx) → empty range, content inserted between (AC-2.8)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `between.start` and `between.end` match, the system SHALL replace content between markers exclusively, preserving marker lines (AC-2.1, AC-2.7).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN markers are adjacent, the system SHALL insert content between them (AC-2.8).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `between.end` not found after start, the system SHALL exit code 3 with start line number (AC-2.11).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 2.3: Implement `match_pattern()` (P with 2.2)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\nfn match_pattern(lines: &[&str], pattern: &str) -> Result<(usize, usize), NoMatch>\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- Find first contiguous block of matching lines\\\\\\\\n- Return `(first_match, last_match)` — inclusive range, matched lines replaced entirely (AC-2.2)\\\\\\\\n- No match → `NoMatch` (for fallback)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `pattern` matches, the system SHALL replace all contiguous matched lines with rendered content (AC-2.2).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 2.4: Implement fallback dispatch\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\nfn apply_fallback(content: &str, rendered: &str, fallback: &Fallback, ...) -> OpResult\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- `Append` → append content to end, return Success with location `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"fallback:append\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` (AC-2.3)\\\\\\\\n- `Prepend` → prepend content to start, return Success with location `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"fallback:prepend\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` (AC-2.4)\\\\\\\\n- `Skip` → return `OpResult::Skip` with reason \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"pattern not found\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\" (AC-2.5)\\\\\\\\n- `Error` → return `OpResult::Error` with rendered content included (AC-2.6)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: append`, the system SHALL append content (AC-2.3).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: prepend`, the system SHALL prepend content (AC-2.4).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN no match and `fallback: skip`, the system SHALL skip with reason (AC-2.5).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF no match and `fallback: error`, the system SHALL exit code 3 with rendered content (AC-2.6).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.2, Task 2.3\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 2.5: Wire replace dispatch\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/mod.rs` — add `pub mod replace;` and match arm in `execute_operation()`.\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — add match arm for `FileOp::Replace` in rendered_path block to render target path as Jinja2.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN a recipe with replace operations is run, the system SHALL render the replace target path as Jinja2 (AC-1.19).\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL render template content before execution (D-1: render-then-execute).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.4\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 2.6: Unit tests for replace operation (P with 2.1)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/replace.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Between mode (5):** replaces_exclusively, adjacent_markers_inserts, multiline_content, end_not_found, start_not_found.\\\\\\\\n**Pattern mode (3):** replaces_matched_lines, single_line, no_match.\\\\\\\\n**Fallback (5):** append, prepend, skip, error, error_is_default.\\\\\\\\n**Edge (5):** missing_target_file, dry_run_virtual_files, dry_run_chains_with_create, success_reports_action, verbose_includes_content.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.4\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 2.7: Integration test fixtures\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Directory:** `tests/fixtures/` (9 new subdirectories)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Fixture | Purpose | Key ACs |\\\\\\\\n|---------|---------|---------|\\\\\\\\n| `replace-between` | Replace content between `# START` and `# END` markers | AC-2.1, AC-2.7 |\\\\\\\\n| `replace-between-empty` | Adjacent markers (insert into empty region) | AC-2.8 |\\\\\\\\n| `replace-pattern` | Replace lines matching regex pattern | AC-2.2 |\\\\\\\\n| `replace-fallback-append` | Pattern not found, fallback appends | AC-2.3 |\\\\\\\\n| `replace-fallback-prepend` | Pattern not found, fallback prepends | AC-2.4 |\\\\\\\\n| `replace-fallback-skip` | Pattern not found, fallback skips | AC-2.5 |\\\\\\\\n| `replace-fallback-error` | Pattern not found, fallback errors | AC-2.6 |\\\\\\\\n| `error-replace-no-match` | Between start not found, default fallback | AC-2.6, AC-N2.2 |\\\\\\\\n| `error-replace-end-missing` | Start found, end not found | AC-2.11 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 2.5\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase 2 Validation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new tests\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `between` mode preserves markers, replaces content between\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `pattern` mode replaces first contiguous matched block\\\\\\\\n- [ ] All four fallback behaviors work correctly\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Missing target file exits 3 with rendered content\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Dry-run mode uses virtual_files correctly\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Replace works alongside create and inject\\\\\\\\n- [ ] All 9 integration fixtures pass\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Phase 3: Scope Detection\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-3 (AC-3.1-3.10), FR-4 (AC-4.1-4.12), FR-5 (AC-5.1-5.11), FR-6 (AC-6.1-6.5)\\\\\\\\n**Dependencies:** Task 1.1 only (ScopeType enum). **Can run in parallel with Phase 2.**\\\\\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~60\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 3.1: Create `src/scope/mod.rs` — dispatch and types (P with Phase 2)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**New directory:** `src/scope/`\\\\\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/mod.rs`\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — add `mod scope;`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nDefine types:\\\\\\\\n- `ScopeResult` — `start_line: usize`, `end_line: usize`, `closing_line: Option<usize>`, `is_empty: bool`\\\\\\\\n- `PositionResult` — `insertion_line: usize`, `indent: String`, `fallback: Option<(String, String)>`\\\\\\\\n- `FindResult` — `found_line: usize`, `sub_scope: Option<ScopeResult>`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nImplement:\\\\\\\\n- `detect_scope(lines, anchor_line, scope_type)` — dispatches by ScopeType: `Line` → single-line scope; `Block/ClassBody/FunctionBody` → indent.rs; `Braces/Brackets/Parens/FunctionSignature` → delimiter.rs\\\\\\\\n- `find_within_scope(lines, scope, find_str)` — search within scope, auto-detect sub-scopes\\\\\\\\n- `detect_sub_scope(lines, line_idx)` — heuristic: trailing `[`, `{`, `(` or assignment with delimiter (e.g. `list_display = [`)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope_type` is `Line`, the system SHALL return a scope containing only the anchor line.\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL dispatch to indent or delimiter module based on scope type without language identifiers (AC-N1.3).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `find_within_scope` finds the string, the system SHALL return the line index (AC-6.1).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN the found line opens a sub-scope, the system SHALL detect and return it (AC-6.2).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF find string not found in scope, the system SHALL return structured error with scope boundaries (AC-6.3).\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL apply find AFTER scope detection but BEFORE position resolution (AC-6.5).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 1.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 3.2: Create `src/scope/indent.rs` — indentation-based scope (P with 3.3, 3.4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/indent.rs`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\npub fn detect_indent_scope(lines: &[&str], anchor_line: usize, scope_type: &ScopeType) -> Result<ScopeResult, StructuredError>\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Algorithm:**\\\\\\\\n1. Measure anchor line indentation (raw whitespace prefix length)\\\\\\\\n2. For `ClassBody`/`FunctionBody`: body starts after declaration. Handle multi-line class declarations (scan for colon).\\\\\\\\n3. Walk forward from anchor+1: skip blank lines (AC-3.4), include deeper-indented lines, stop at same/shallower indentation\\\\\\\\n4. Empty scope (immediate dedent): `is_empty: true` (AC-3.5)\\\\\\\\n5. Nested classes/functions included in outer scope (AC-3.6, AC-3.7)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Edge cases:** mixed tabs/spaces (literal prefix comparison, AC-3.8), multi-line class declaration (AC-3.9), decorator before def/class (AC-3.10).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: block`, the system SHALL include all lines indented deeper than anchor (AC-3.1).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: class_body`, the system SHALL identify body from first indented line to dedent (AC-3.2).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: function_body`, the system SHALL identify body from first indented line to dedent (AC-3.3).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN blank lines appear within scope, the system SHALL include them (AC-3.4).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN scope is empty, the system SHALL report `is_empty: true` (AC-3.5).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN nested classes/functions appear, the system SHALL include them in outer scope (AC-3.6, AC-3.7).\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL measure indent by raw whitespace prefix length (AC-3.8).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN Python class has multi-line declaration, the system SHALL start body after colon (AC-3.9).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN decorator precedes def/class, the system SHALL use def/class indentation (AC-3.10).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 3.3: Create `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — delimiter-based scope (P with 3.2, 3.4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/delimiter.rs`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\npub fn detect_delimiter_scope(lines: &[&str], anchor_line: usize, open: char, close: char) -> Result<ScopeResult, StructuredError>\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Algorithm:**\\\\\\\\n1. Scan from `anchor_line` for first `open` delimiter (use it if on anchor line, AC-4.11)\\\\\\\\n2. Not found → error (AC-4.8)\\\\\\\\n3. Track nesting depth (start at 1 after opener)\\\\\\\\n4. Walk forward character-by-character via `CharScanner`:\\\\\\\\n   - **String literals:** `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"`, `'`, `` ` `` — skip to matching close, handle escape sequences (AC-4.6)\\\\\\\\n   - **Escaped delimiters:** `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\{`, `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\}`, etc. — skip (AC-4.12)\\\\\\\\n   - **Single-line comments:** `//` or `#` — skip rest of line (AC-4.7)\\\\\\\\n   - **Multi-line comments:** `/*` → skip until `*/` (AC-4.7)\\\\\\\\n   - Open → depth++; Close → depth--; depth=0 → found closing\\\\\\\\n5. Depth never reaches 0 → error with line number (AC-4.9)\\\\\\\\n6. Scope content between delimiters (exclusive, AC-4.1-4.4)\\\\\\\\n7. Empty scope (adjacent delimiters): `is_empty: true` (AC-4.10)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**CharScanner struct:**\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\nstruct CharScanner<'a> { lines: &'a [&'a str], line: usize, col: usize }\\\\\\\\n// Methods: advance(), peek(), skip_string_literal(), skip_line_comment(), skip_block_comment()\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: braces`, the system SHALL find `{`…`}` scope (AC-4.1).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: brackets`, the system SHALL find `[`…`]` scope (AC-4.2).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: parens`/`function_signature`, the system SHALL find `(`…`)` scope (AC-4.3, AC-4.4).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN nested delimiters appear, the system SHALL track depth correctly (AC-4.5).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN delimiters appear in strings, the system SHALL ignore them (AC-4.6).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN delimiters appear in comments, the system SHALL ignore them (AC-4.7).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF opening delimiter not found, the system SHALL return error (AC-4.8).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF closing delimiter never found, the system SHALL return error with line number (AC-4.9).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN scope is empty, the system SHALL report `is_empty: true` (AC-4.10).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN opener is on anchor line, the system SHALL use it (AC-4.11).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN escaped delimiters appear, the system SHALL ignore them (AC-4.12).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 3.4: Create `src/scope/position.rs` — semantic position resolution (P with 3.2, 3.3)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**New file:** `src/scope/position.rs`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\npub fn resolve_position(lines: &[&str], scope: &ScopeResult, position: &Position) -> Result<PositionResult, StructuredError>\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Position | Heuristic | Fallback |\\\\\\\\n|----------|-----------|----------|\\\\\\\\n| `Before` | insertion_line = scope.start_line | N/A |\\\\\\\\n| `After` | insertion_line = scope.end_line + 1 | N/A |\\\\\\\\n| `BeforeClose` | closing_line if exists, else end_line + 1 | N/A |\\\\\\\\n| `AfterLastField` | Last line matching `^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` | → `BeforeClose` (AC-5.8) |\\\\\\\\n| `AfterLastMethod` | Last line matching `^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s+(pub\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s+)?(fn\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\|async fn) \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\w+` or `^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s+def \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\w+` etc., find complete body end via recursive scope detection | → `BeforeClose` (AC-5.9) |\\\\\\\\n| `AfterLastImport` | Last line matching `^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s*(from\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\|import) ` | → `Before` (AC-5.10) |\\\\\\\\n| `Sorted` | Alphabetically correct insertion among siblings at same indent | N/A |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nDetect indent from non-blank line within scope. Empty scope: anchor indent + one level (default 4 spaces).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: before`, the system SHALL insert before scope start (AC-5.1).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: after`, the system SHALL insert after scope end (AC-5.2).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: before_close`, the system SHALL insert before closing delimiter (AC-5.3).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: after_last_field`, the system SHALL find last `^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\w+\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s*[:=]` (AC-5.4).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: after_last_method`, the system SHALL find method's complete body end (AC-5.5, AC-5.11).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: after_last_import`, the system SHALL find last `^\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\s*(from\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\|import) ` (AC-5.6).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `position: sorted`, the system SHALL insert alphabetically (AC-5.7).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `after_last_field` finds no fields, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` with fallback note (AC-5.8).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `after_last_method` finds no methods, the system SHALL fall back to `before_close` with fallback note (AC-5.9).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF `after_last_import` finds no imports, the system SHALL fall back to `before` with fallback note (AC-5.10).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 3.5: Unit tests for indentation scope (P with 3.6, 3.7, 3.8)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/indent.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n11 tests: `python_class_body`, `python_function_body`, `blank_lines_within_scope`, `empty_scope`, `nested_class`, `nested_function`, `mixed_tabs_spaces`, `multiline_class_declaration`, `decorator_before_class`, `yaml_nested_block`, `deeply_nested_indent`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.2\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 3.6: Unit tests for delimiter scope (P with 3.5, 3.7, 3.8)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/delimiter.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n16 tests: `rust_struct_braces`, `typescript_interface_braces`, `go_function_braces`, `python_list_brackets`, `function_signature_parens`, `nested_braces`, `delimiters_in_double_quotes`, `delimiters_in_single_quotes`, `delimiters_in_backticks`, `delimiters_in_line_comments`, `delimiters_in_block_comments`, `escaped_delimiters`, `missing_opening_delimiter`, `unbalanced_delimiter`, `empty_braces`, `opener_on_anchor_line`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.3\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 3.7: Unit tests for position resolution (P with 3.5, 3.6, 3.8)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/position.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n13 tests: `position_before`, `position_after`, `position_before_close`, `after_last_field_python`, `after_last_field_rust`, `after_last_method_python`, `after_last_method_rust`, `after_last_import`, `sorted_insertion`, `after_last_field_fallback`, `after_last_method_fallback`, `after_last_import_fallback`, `indent_detection`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.4\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 3.8: Unit tests for find narrowing (P with 3.5, 3.6, 3.7)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/scope/mod.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n5 tests: `find_within_scope_basic`, `find_detects_bracket_sub_scope`, `find_detects_brace_sub_scope`, `find_not_found`, `find_before_close_on_sub_scope`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 3.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase 3 Validation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all existing + new scope tests\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `cargo clippy` clean\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Python class body / function body correctly identified through dedent\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Blank lines don't terminate indentation scope\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Nested delimiters tracked correctly\\\\\\\\n- [ ] String literals, comments, escapes don't affect delimiter counting\\\\\\\\n- [ ] All 7 position types tested with realistic code\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Position fallbacks work and record fallback note\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Find narrowing detects sub-scopes\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Deterministic: same input = same boundaries (AC-N4.1)\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Works across Python, Rust, TypeScript, Go, YAML without configuration (AC-N1.1, AC-N1.2)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Phase 4: Patch Operation + Verbose Diagnostics\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Traces to:** FR-6, FR-7 (AC-7.1-7.12), FR-8 (AC-8.1-8.5)\\\\\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 1 + Phase 3\\\\\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~30\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 4.1: Create `src/operations/patch.rs` — core pipeline\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**New file:** `src/operations/patch.rs`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\npub fn execute(\\\\\\\\n    rendered_path: &str,\\\\\\\\n    rendered_content: &str,\\\\\\\\n    rendered_skip_if: Option<&str>,\\\\\\\\n    anchor: &Anchor,\\\\\\\\n    ctx: &mut ExecutionContext,\\\\\\\\n    verbose: bool,\\\\\\\\n) -> OpResult\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Pipeline** (each step depends on the previous):\\\\\\\\n1. **Resolve path** via `ctx.resolve_path()`\\\\\\\\n2. **Read target file** — virtual_files first, then disk. Missing → error (AC-7.10)\\\\\\\\n3. **Check skip_if** — if found in file content → `OpResult::Skip` (AC-7.2). Search entire file, not just scope (AC-N3.3)\\\\\\\\n4. **Find anchor** — compile regex, find first matching line (AC-7.6). No match → error (AC-7.4)\\\\\\\\n5. **Detect scope** — call `scope::detect_scope()`. Failure → error with rendered content (AC-7.5)\\\\\\\\n6. **Apply find narrowing** (if `anchor.find` is Some) — call `scope::find_within_scope()`. Sub-scope detected → use it for position resolution. Failure → error (AC-6.3)\\\\\\\\n7. **Resolve position** — call `scope::position::resolve_position()`. Get insertion line and indent.\\\\\\\\n8. **Adjust indentation** — match rendered content's base indent to insertion context. Preserve relative indentation within template (AC-7.7).\\\\\\\\n9. **Insert content** at resolved insertion line\\\\\\\\n10. **Write back** — virtual_files + disk (same pattern as inject/replace)\\\\\\\\n11. **Return `OpResult::Success`** with `action: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"`, location `\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"{scope_type}({pattern}):{position}\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` (AC-7.3)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Indentation matching (step 8):**\\\\\\\\n```rust\\\\\\\\nfn adjust_indentation(rendered_content: &str, target_indent: &str) -> String\\\\\\\\n```\\\\\\\\nDetect base indent of rendered content (first non-empty line). For each line: remove base indent, add target indent, preserve relative offset. Blank lines → empty string.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**`scope: line` degenerate case (AC-7.12):**\\\\\\\\nSkip scope detection, find narrowing, and position resolution. Insert after anchor line (= inject after).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN a patch operation executes, the system SHALL perform anchor→scope→find→position→insert (AC-7.1).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `skip_if` matches, the system SHALL skip (AC-7.2).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN patch succeeds, the system SHALL report `action: \\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"patch\\\\\\\\\\\\\\\"` with location (AC-7.3).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF anchor matches zero lines, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-7.4).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF scope detection fails, the system SHALL exit code 3 with rendered content (AC-7.5).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN anchor matches multiple lines, the system SHALL use first match (AC-7.6).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN inserting, the system SHALL match surrounding indentation (AC-7.7).\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL resolve path relative to `--base-dir` (AC-7.8).\\\\\\\\n- State: WHILE `--dry-run`, the system SHALL update virtual_files (AC-7.9).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF target file missing, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-7.10).\\\\\\\\n- Unwanted: IF write fails, the system SHALL exit code 3 (AC-7.11).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `scope: line`, the system SHALL behave like inject after (AC-7.12).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1, Phase 3\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 4.2: Wire patch dispatch\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/mod.rs` — add `pub mod patch;` and match arm.\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/main.rs` — add match arms for `FileOp::Patch` in rendered_path block and rendered_skip_if block.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 4.3: Complete verbose scope diagnostics\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED #1]**: Add `diagnostics: Option<serde_json::Value>` to `OpResult::Success`?\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nBoth plans recommend **Option A**: add the field, populate for patch/replace when verbose=true, backfill create.rs/inject.rs with `diagnostics: None`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nPatch diagnostics: `anchor_line`, `scope_start`, `scope_end`, `insertion_line`, `find_match_line`, `sub_scope`, `position_fallback`.\\\\\\\\nReplace diagnostics: `match_mode`, `region_start`, `region_end`, `lines_replaced`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nUpdate `format_json()` and `format_human()` in output.rs.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**[HUMAN DECISION NEEDED #2] (Plan B unique):** Should diagnostics be emitted on `skip` and `error` results too, or only on `success`?\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `--verbose` for patch, output SHALL include anchor line, scope range, insertion line (AC-8.1).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `--verbose` with find, output SHALL include find line and sub-scope (AC-8.2).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN `--verbose` for replace, output SHALL include match mode and region (AC-8.3).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN position falls back, output SHALL note the fallback (AC-8.4).\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: Diagnostics SHALL appear in both JSON and human output (AC-8.5).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.1, Task 2.4 (update replace.rs too)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 4.4: Unit tests for patch operation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**File:** `src/operations/patch.rs` — `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n20 tests: `patch_class_body_after_last_field`, `patch_function_body_before_close`, `patch_braces_before_close`, `patch_brackets_before_close`, `patch_function_signature`, `patch_find_narrowing_brackets`, `patch_skip_if_matched`, `patch_skip_if_not_matched`, `patch_scope_line`, `patch_indent_matching`, `patch_sorted_insertion`, `patch_anchor_not_found`, `patch_scope_detection_failure`, `patch_find_not_found`, `patch_dry_run_virtual_files`, `patch_chains_with_prior_create`, `patch_first_match_used`, `patch_missing_target_file`, `patch_verbose_diagnostics`, `patch_idempotency_with_skip_if`.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.1\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 4.5: Integration test fixtures\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Directory:** `tests/fixtures/` (15 new subdirectories)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Fixture | Purpose | Key ACs |\\\\\\\\n|---------|---------|---------|\\\\\\\\n| `patch-class-body-field` | Add field to Python class after last field | AC-7.1, AC-3.2, AC-5.4 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-function-body` | Insert into Python function body | AC-3.3, AC-7.1 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-braces` | Insert into Rust struct (braces scope) | AC-4.1, AC-7.1 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-brackets` | Insert into Python list (brackets scope) | AC-4.2 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-function-signature` | Add parameter to function signature | AC-4.4 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-find-narrowing` | Find + before_close on list_display | AC-6.1, AC-6.2, AC-6.4 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-skip-if` | Skip when content already present | AC-7.2, AC-N3.1 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-sorted` | Sorted insertion in alphabetical list | AC-5.7 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-scope-line` | Default scope (line) = inject after | AC-7.12 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-indent-matching` | Auto-adjust indentation on insert | AC-7.7 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-nested-delimiters` | Scope with nested braces | AC-4.5 |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-string-delimiters` | Delimiters in strings ignored | AC-4.6 |\\\\\\\\n| `error-patch-no-anchor` | Anchor pattern not found | AC-7.4 |\\\\\\\\n| `error-patch-unbalanced` | Unbalanced delimiters | AC-4.9, AC-7.5 |\\\\\\\\n| `error-patch-find-missing` | Find string not in scope | AC-6.3 |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Task 4.2\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Phase 4 Validation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n- [ ] `cargo test` passes all works with sub-scopes\\\\\\\\n- [ ] Verbose output shows complete scope diagnostics\\\\\\\\n- [ ] All 15 integration fixtures pass\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n---\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n## Phase 5: Integration Testing + Full Validation\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Traces to:** All FRs and NFRs\\\\\\\\n**Dependencies:** Phase 2 + Phase 4\\\\\\\\n**Estimated new tests:** ~15\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 5.1: `combined-all-ops` fixture\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nSingle recipe using all four operation types: create → inject → replace → patch. Shared variables. Tests ordered execution (I-9).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL preserve operation declaration order across mixed operation recipes.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 2, Phase 4\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 5.2: `combined-patch-idempotent` fixture\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nRecipe with: 2 creates (`skip_if_exists`), 1 inject (`skip_if`), 1 replace (`between`, inherently idempotent), 2 patches (`skip_if`). Run twice — second run = all skips.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN an idempotent recipe is run twice with same inputs, the system SHALL produce skips and no file changes on second run (AC-N3.1, AC-N3.3).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 2, Phase 4\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 5.3: Cross-language scope detection fixtures (P with 5.4, 5.5)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n| Fixture | Language | Scope Type |\\\\\\\\n|---------|----------|------------|\\\\\\\\n| `patch-python-class` | Python | class_body, after_last_field |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-rust-struct` | Rust | braces, before_close |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-typescript-interface` | TypeScript | braces, after_last_field |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-go-function` | Go | braces, before_close |\\\\\\\\n| `patch-yaml-block` | YAML | block, after |\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: The system SHALL detect scopes correctly across Python, Rust, TypeScript, Go, YAML without language config (AC-N1.1, AC-N1.2, AC-N1.3).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 4\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 5.4: Determinism test (P with 5.3, 5.5)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nRun patch+replace recipe 3 times, assert byte-identical output.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- State: WHILE inputs are unchanged, the system SHALL produce byte-identical outputs across repeated runs (AC-N4.1, AC-N4.2).\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**Depends on:** Phase 2, Phase 4\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n### Task 5.5: Error recovery coverage (P with 5.3, 5.4)\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\nVerify every row in SPEC.md Error Handling table has a test.\\\\\\\\n\\\\\\\\n**EARS Acceptance Criteria:**\\\\\\\\n- Ubiquitous: Every patch/replace error SHALL include what, where, why, hint, and rendered content (AC-N2.3).\\\\\\\\n- Event: WHEN
