36\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n    37\tpub struct WorkflowStepInfo {\n    38\t    pub recipe: String,\n    39\t    pub conditional: bool,\n    40\t}\n    41\t\n    42\t/// List all recipes in an installed library.\n    43\tpub fn list_recipes(library_name: &str, base_dir: &Path) -> Result<Vec<(String, String)>, JigError> {\n    44\t    let manifest = install::load_installed_manifest(library_name, base_dir)?;\n    45\t    Ok(manifest\n    46\t        .recipes\n    47\t        .into_iter()\n    48\t        .collect())\n    49\t}\n    50\t\n    51\t/// Get detailed info about a specific recipe in a library.\n    52\tpub fn recipe_info(\n    53\t    library_name: &str,\n    54\t    recipe_path: &str,\n    55\t    base_dir: &Path,\n    56\t) -> Result<RecipeInfo, JigError> {\n    57\t    let manifest = install::load_installed_manifest(library_name, base_dir)?;\n    58\t\n    59\t    let description = manifest\n    60\t        .recipes\n    61\t        .get(recipe_path)\n    62\t        .ok_or_else(|| {\n    63\t            JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n    64\t                what: format!(\n    65\t                    \"recipe '{recipe_path}' not found in library '{library_name}'\"\n    66\t                ),\n    67\t                where_: library_name.to_string(),\n    68\t                why: format!(\"the library does not declare a recipe at '{recipe_path}'\"),\n    69\t                hint: format!(\n    70\t                    \"use 'jig library recipes {library_name}' to see available recipes\"\n    71\t                ),\n    72\t            })\n    73\t        })?\n    74\t        .clone();\n    75\t\n    76\t    // Try to load the actual recipe.yaml for detailed info.\n    77\t    let recipe_yaml_path = manifest\n    78\t        .resolve_recipe_path(recipe_path)\n    79\t        .unwrap();\n    80\t\n    81\t    let (variables, operations) = if recipe_yaml_path.exists() {\n    82\t        match Recipe::load(&recipe_yaml_path) {\n    83\t            Ok(recipe) => {\n    84\t                let vars: Vec<RecipeVarInfo> = recipe\n    85\t                    .variables\n    86\t                    .iter()\n    87\t                    .map(|(name, decl)| RecipeVarInfo {\n    88\t                        name: name.clone(),\n    89\t                        var_type: decl.var_type.to_string(),\n    90\t                        required: decl.required,\n    91\t                        description: decl.description.clone(),\n    92\t                    })\n    93\t                    .collect();\n    94\t                let ops: Vec<String> = recipe\n    95\t                    .files\n    96\t                    .iter()\n    97\t                    .map(|op| op.op_type_str().to_string())\n    98\t                    .collect();\n    99\t                (vars, ops)\n   100\t            }\n   101\t            Err(_) => (Vec::new(), Vec::new()),\n   102\t        }\n   103\t    } else {\n   104\t        (Vec::new(), Vec::new())\n   105\t    };\n   106\t\n   107\t    Ok(RecipeInfo {\n   108\t        library: library_name.to_string(),\n   109\t        path: recipe_path.to_string(),\n   110\t        description,\n   111\t        variables,\n   112\t        operations,\n   113\t    })\n   114\t}\n   115\t\n   116\t/// List all workflows in an installed library.\n   117\tpub fn list_workflows(\n   118\t    library_name: &str,\n   119\t    base_dir: &Path,\n   120\t) -> Result<Vec<WorkflowInfo>, JigError> {\n   121\t    let manifest = install::load_installed_manifest(library_name, base_dir)?;\n   122\t\n   123\t    Ok(manifest\n   124\t        .workflows\n   125\t        .iter()\n   126\t        .map(|(name, wf)| WorkflowInfo {\n   127\t            library: library_name.to_string(),\n   128\t            name: name.clone(),\n   129\t            description: wf.description.clone(),\n   130\t            steps: wf\n   131\t                .steps\n   132\t                .iter()\n   133\t                .map(|s| WorkflowStepInfo {\n   134\t                    recipe: s.recipe.clone(),\n   135\t                    conditional: s.when.is_some(),\n   136\t                })\n   137\t                .collect(),\n   138\t        })\n   139\t        .collect())\n   140\t}\n   141\t\n   142\t/// Resolve a library-qualified recipe path (e.g., \"django/model/add-field\")\n   143\t/// to the absolute path of the recipe.yaml file.\n   144\t///\n   145\t/// Returns (library_name, recipe_path_within_library, absolute_recipe_yaml_path).\n   146\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Used in tests and future library-aware run command\n   147\tpub fn resolve_library_recipe(\n   148\t    qualified_path: &str,\n   149\t    base_dir: &Path,\n   150\t) -> Result<(String, String, std::path::PathBuf), JigError> {\n   151\t    // Split \"library_name/recipe/path\" — the first segment is the library name.\n   152\t    let slash_pos = qualified_path.find('/').ok_or_else(|| {\n   153\t        JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n   154\t            what: format!(\"invalid library recipe path '{qualified_path}'\"),\n   155\t            where_: qualified_path.to_string(),\n   156\t            why: \"expected format: <library>/<recipe-path>\".into(),\n   157\t            hint: \"example: django/model/add-field\".into(),\n   158\t        })\n   159\t    })?;\n   160\t\n   161\t    let library_name = &qualified_path[..slash_pos];\n   162\t    let recipe_path = &qualified_path[slash_pos + 1..];\n   163\t\n   164\t    let manifest = install::load_installed_manifest(library_name, base_dir)?;\n   165\t\n   166\t    let resolved = manifest.resolve_recipe_path(recipe_path).ok_or_else(|| {\n   167\t        let available: Vec<&str> = manifest.recipes.keys().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();\n   168\t        let hint = if available.is_empty() {\n   169\t            format!(\"library '{library_name}' has no recipes\")\n   170\t        } else {\n   171\t            format!(\n   172\t                \"available recipes: {}\",\n   173\t                available.join(\", \")\n   174\t            )\n   175\t        };\n   176\t        JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n   177\t            what: format!(\n   178\t                \"recipe '{recipe_path}' not found in library '{library_name}'\"\n   179\t            ),\n   180\t            where_: qualified_path.to_string(),\n   181\t            why: format!(\"the library does not declare recipe '{recipe_path}'\"),\n   182\t            hint,\n   183\t        })\n   184\t    })?;\n   185\t\n   186\t    Ok((library_name.to_string(), recipe_path.to_string(), resolved))\n   187\t}\n   188\t\n   189\t/// Resolve a library-qualified workflow name (e.g., \"django/add-field\").\n   190\t///\n   191\t/// Returns the manifest so the caller can use workflow metadata.\n   192\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Used in tests and future library-aware workflow command\n   193\tpub fn resolve_library_workflow(\n   194\t    qualified_name: &str,\n   195\t    base_dir: &Path,\n   196\t) -> Result<(String, String, LibraryManifest), JigError> {\n   197\t    let slash_pos = qualified_name.find('/').ok_or_else(|| {\n   198\t        JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n   199\t            what: format!(\"invalid library workflow path '{qualified_name}'\"),\n   200\t            where_: qualified_name.to_string(),\n   201\t            why: \"expected format: <library>/<workflow-name>\".into(),\n   202\t            hint: \"example: django/add-field\".into(),\n   203\t        })\n   204\t    })?;\n   205\t\n   206\t    let library_name = &qualified_name[..slash_pos];\n   207\t    let workflow_name = &qualified_name[slash_pos + 1..];\n   208\t\n   209\t    let manifest = install::load_installed_manifest(library_name, base_dir)?;\n   210\t\n   211\t    if !manifest.has_workflow(workflow_name) {\n   212\t        let available: Vec<&str> = manifest.workflows.keys().map(|s| s.as_str()).collect();\n   213\t        let hint = if available.is_empty() {\n   214\t            format!(\"library '{library_name}' has no workflows\")\n   215\t        } else {\n   216\t            format!(\"available workflows: {}\", available.join(\", \"))\n   217\t        };\n   218\t        return Err(JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n   219\t            what: format!(\n   220\t                \"workflow '{workflow_name}' not found in library '{library_name}'\"\n   221\t            ),\n   222\t            where_: qualified_name.to_string(),\n   223\t            why: format!(\"the library does not declare workflow '{workflow_name}'\"),\n   224\t            hint,\n   225\t        }));\n   226\t    }\n   227\t\n   228\t    Ok((\n   229\t        library_name.to_string(),\n   230\t        workflow_name.to_string(),\n   231\t        manifest,\n   232\t    ))\n   233\t}\n   234\t\n   235\t#[cfg(test)]\n   236\tmod tests {\n   237\t    use super::*;\n   238\t    use std::fs;\n   239\t    use tempfile::TempDir;\n   240\t\n   241\t    fn setup_library(tmp: &TempDir) -> std::path::PathBuf {\n   242\t        let project_dir = tmp.path().join(\"project\");\n   243\t        let lib_dir = project_dir.join(\".jig/libraries/testlib\");\n   244\t        fs::create_dir_all(&lib_dir).unwrap();\n   245\t\n   246\t        let manifest = r#\"\n   247\tname: testlib\n   248\tversion: 0.1.0\n   249\tdescription: Test library\n   250\t\n   251\tconventions:\n   252\t  models: \"{{ app }}/models.py\"\n   253\t\n   254\trecipes:\n   255\t  model/add-field: \"Add a field\"\n   256\t  model/add-model: \"Add a model\"\n   257\t\n   258\tworkflows:\n   259\t  add-field:\n   260\t    description: \"Full stack field addition\"\n   261\t    steps:\n   262\t      - recipe: model/add-field\n   263\t      - recipe: model/add-model\n   264\t        when: \"{{ create_model }}\"\n   265\t\"#;\n   266\t        fs::write(lib_dir.join(\"jig-library.yaml\"), manifest).unwrap();\n   267\t\n   268\t        // Create a recipe.yaml for model/add-field.\n   269\t        let recipe_dir = lib_dir.join(\"model/add-field\");\n   270\t        fs::create_dir_all(&recipe_dir).unwrap();\n   271\t        let recipe = r#\"\n   272\tname: add-field\n   273\tvariables:\n   274\t  field_name:\n   275\t    type: string\n   276\t    required: true\n   277\t  field_type:\n   278\t    type: string\n   279\t    required: true\n   280\t    description: \"The Django field type\"\n   281\tfiles: []\n   282\t\"#;\n   283\t        fs::write(recipe_dir.join(\"recipe.yaml\"), recipe).unwrap();\n   284\t\n   285\t        project_dir\n   286\t    }\n   287\t\n   288\t    #[test]\n   289\t    fn list_recipes_from_library() {\n   290\t        let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n   291\t        let project_dir = setup_library(&tmp);\n   292\t\n   293\t        let recipes = list_recipes(\"testlib\", &project_dir).unwrap();\n   294\t        assert_eq!(recipes.len(), 2);\n   295\t        assert_eq!(recipes[0].0, \"model/add-field\");\n   296\t        assert_eq!(recipes[0].1, \"Add a field\");\n   297\t    }\n   298\t\n   299\t    #[test]\n   300\t    fn get_recipe_info() {\n   301\t        let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n   302\t        let project_dir = setup_library(&tmp);\n   303\t\n   304\t        let info = recipe_info(\"testlib\", \"model/add-field\", &project_dir).unwrap();\n   305\t        assert_eq!(info.library, \"testlib\");\n   306\t        assert_eq!(info.path, \"model/add-field\");\n   307\t        assert_eq!(info.description, \"Add a field\");\n   308\t        assert_eq!(info.variables.len(), 2);\n   309\t        assert_eq!(info.variables[0].name, \"field_name\");\n   310\t        assert!(info.variables[0].required);\n   311\t    }\n   312\t\n   313\t    #[test]\n   314\t    fn recipe_info_not_found() {\n   315\t        let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n   316\t        let project_dir = setup_library(&tmp);\n   317\t\n   318\t        let err = recipe_info(\"testlib\", \"nonexistent\", &project_dir).unwrap_err();\n   319\t        let msg = format!(\"{err}\");\n   320\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"not found\"));\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_17","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba src/library/manifest.rs | sed -n '1,260p'\"","aggregated_output":"     1\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n     2\t\n     3\tuse indexmap::IndexMap;\n     4\tuse serde::Deserialize;\n     5\t\n     6\tuse crate::error::{JigError, StructuredError};\n     7\t\n     8\t/// A parsed jig library manifest (`jig-library.yaml`).\n     9\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n    10\tpub struct LibraryManifest {\n    11\t    pub name: String,\n    12\t    pub version: String,\n    13\t    pub description: Option<String>,\n    14\t    pub framework: Option<String>,\n    15\t    pub language: Option<String>,\n    16\t    pub conventions: IndexMap<String, String>,\n    17\t    /// Recipe paths → descriptions.\n    18\t    pub recipes: IndexMap<String, String>,\n    19\t    /// Workflow definitions embedded in the manifest.\n    20\t    pub workflows: IndexMap<String, ManifestWorkflow>,\n    21\t    /// Directory containing the manifest file.\n    22\t    pub library_dir: PathBuf,\n    23\t}\n    24\t\n    25\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\n    26\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Fields used in tests and future workflow generation\n    27\tpub struct ManifestWorkflow {\n    28\t    pub description: Option<String>,\n    29\t    pub steps: Vec<ManifestWorkflowStep>,\n    30\t    #[serde(default)]\n    31\t    pub on_error: Option<String>,\n    32\t}\n    33\t\n    34\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Deserialize)]\n    35\t#[allow(dead_code)] // Fields used in tests and future workflow generation\n    36\tpub struct ManifestWorkflowStep {\n    37\t    pub recipe: String,\n    38\t    pub when: Option<String>,\n    39\t    pub vars_map: Option<IndexMap<String, String>>,\n    40\t    pub vars: Option<IndexMap<String, serde_json::Value>>,\n    41\t    pub on_error: Option<String>,\n    42\t}\n    43\t\n    44\t// ── Raw deserialization ────────────────────────────────────────────\n    45\t\n    46\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize)]\n    47\tstruct RawManifest {\n    48\t    name: String,\n    49\t    version: String,\n    50\t    description: Option<String>,\n    51\t    framework: Option<String>,\n    52\t    language: Option<String>,\n    53\t    #[serde(default)]\n    54\t    conventions: IndexMap<String, String>,\n    55\t    #[serde(default)]\n    56\t    recipes: IndexMap<String, String>,\n    57\t    #[serde(default)]\n    58\t    workflows: IndexMap<String, ManifestWorkflow>,\n    59\t}\n    60\t\n    61\timpl LibraryManifest {\n    62\t    /// Load and validate a library manifest from a `jig-library.yaml` file.\n    63\t    pub fn load(path: &Path) -> Result<Self, JigError> {\n    64\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path).map_err(|e| {\n    65\t            JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n    66\t                what: format!(\"cannot read library manifest '{}'\", path.display()),\n    67\t                where_: path.display().to_string(),\n    68\t                why: e.to_string(),\n    69\t                hint: \"check the file path and permissions\".into(),\n    70\t            })\n    71\t        })?;\n    72\t\n    73\t        Self::parse(&content, path)\n    74\t    }\n    75\t\n    76\t    /// Parse manifest from YAML content with a source path for error messages.\n    77\t    pub fn parse(content: &str, source_path: &Path) -> Result<Self, JigError> {\n    78\t        let raw: RawManifest = serde_yaml::from_str(content).map_err(|e| {\n    79\t            JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n    80\t                what: \"invalid library manifest YAML\".into(),\n    81\t                where_: source_path.display().to_string(),\n    82\t                why: e.to_string(),\n    83\t                hint: \"check the jig-library.yaml format\".into(),\n    84\t            })\n    85\t        })?;\n    86\t\n    87\t        // Validate required fields.\n    88\t        if raw.name.is_empty() {\n    89\t            return Err(JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n    90\t                what: \"library name is required\".into(),\n    91\t                where_: source_path.display().to_string(),\n    92\t                why: \"the 'name' field is empty\".into(),\n    93\t                hint: \"add a non-empty 'name' field to jig-library.yaml\".into(),\n    94\t            }));\n    95\t        }\n    96\t\n    97\t        if raw.version.is_empty() {\n    98\t            return Err(JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n    99\t                what: \"library version is required\".into(),\n   100\t                where_: source_path.display().to_string(),\n   101\t                why: \"the 'version' field is empty\".into(),\n   102\t                hint: \"add a non-empty 'version' field to jig-library.yaml\".into(),\n   103\t            }));\n   104\t        }\n   105\t\n   106\t        // Validate that recipe paths in workflows reference declared recipes.\n   107\t        for (wf_name, wf) in &raw.workflows {\n   108\t            for step in &wf.steps {\n   109\t                if !raw.recipes.contains_key(&step.recipe) {\n   110\t                    return Err(JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n   111\t                        what: format!(\n   112\t                            \"workflow '{}' references undeclared recipe '{}'\",\n   113\t                            wf_name, step.recipe\n   114\t                        ),\n   115\t                        where_: source_path.display().to_string(),\n   116\t                        why: format!(\n   117\t                            \"recipe '{}' is not listed in the 'recipes' block\",\n   118\t                            step.recipe\n   119\t                        ),\n   120\t                        hint: \"add the recipe to the 'recipes' block or fix the reference\".into(),\n   121\t                    }));\n   122\t                }\n   123\t            }\n   124\t        }\n   125\t\n   126\t        let library_dir = source_path\n   127\t            .parent()\n   128\t            .unwrap_or(Path::new(\".\"))\n   129\t            .to_path_buf();\n   130\t\n   131\t        Ok(LibraryManifest {\n   132\t            name: raw.name,\n   133\t            version: raw.version,\n   134\t            description: raw.description,\n   135\t            framework: raw.framework,\n   136\t            language: raw.language,\n   137\t            conventions: raw.conventions,\n   138\t            recipes: raw.recipes,\n   139\t            workflows: raw.workflows,\n   140\t            library_dir,\n   141\t        })\n   142\t    }\n   143\t\n   144\t    /// Resolve a recipe path (e.g., \"model/add-field\") to the absolute path\n   145\t    /// of the recipe.yaml file within this library.\n   146\t    pub fn resolve_recipe_path(&self, recipe_path: &str) -> Option<PathBuf> {\n   147\t        if self.recipes.contains_key(recipe_path) {\n   148\t            let full_path = self.library_dir.join(recipe_path).join(\"recipe.yaml\");\n   149\t            Some(full_path)\n   150\t        } else {\n   151\t            None\n   152\t        }\n   153\t    }\n   154\t\n   155\t    /// Check if a recipe exists in this library.\n   156\t    #[allow(dead_code)] // Used in tests\n   157\t    pub fn has_recipe(&self, recipe_path: &str) -> bool {\n   158\t        self.recipes.contains_key(recipe_path)\n   159\t    }\n   160\t\n   161\t    /// Check if a workflow exists in this library.\n   162\t    pub fn has_workflow(&self, workflow_name: &str) -> bool {\n   163\t        self.workflows.contains_key(workflow_name)\n   164\t    }\n   165\t}\n   166\t\n   167\t#[cfg(test)]\n   168\tmod tests {\n   169\t    use super::*;\n   170\t\n   171\t    fn dummy_path() -> PathBuf {\n   172\t        PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-lib/jig-library.yaml\")\n   173\t    }\n   174\t\n   175\t    #[test]\n   176\t    fn parse_minimal_manifest() {\n   177\t        let yaml = r#\"\n   178\tname: django\n   179\tversion: 0.1.0\n   180\trecipes: {}\n   181\t\"#;\n   182\t        let m = LibraryManifest::parse(yaml, &dummy_path()).unwrap();\n   183\t        assert_eq!(m.name, \"django\");\n   184\t        assert_eq!(m.version, \"0.1.0\");\n   185\t        assert!(m.description.is_none());\n   186\t        assert!(m.framework.is_none());\n   187\t        assert!(m.language.is_none());\n   188\t        assert!(m.conventions.is_empty());\n   189\t        assert!(m.recipes.is_empty());\n   190\t        assert!(m.workflows.is_empty());\n   191\t    }\n   192\t\n   193\t    #[test]\n   194\t    fn parse_full_manifest() {\n   195\t        let yaml = r#\"\n   196\tname: django\n   197\tversion: 0.3.0\n   198\tdescription: Recipes for Django development\n   199\tframework: django\n   200\tlanguage: python\n   201\t\n   202\tconventions:\n   203\t  models: \"{{ app }}/models/{{ model | snakecase }}.py\"\n   204\t  services: \"{{ app }}/services/{{ model | snakecase }}_service.py\"\n   205\t\n   206\trecipes:\n   207\t  model/add-field: \"Add a field to an existing Django model\"\n   208\t  model/add-model: \"Scaffold a new Django model\"\n   209\t  service/add-method: \"Add a method to a service\"\n   210\t\n   211\tworkflows:\n   212\t  add-field:\n   213\t    description: \"Add a field across the full stack\"\n   214\t    steps:\n   215\t      - recipe: model/add-field\n   216\t      - recipe: service/add-method\n   217\t        when: \"{{ update_service }}\"\n   218\t\"#;\n   219\t        let m = LibraryManifest::parse(yaml, &dummy_path()).unwrap();\n   220\t        assert_eq!(m.name, \"django\");\n   221\t        assert_eq!(m.version, \"0.3.0\");\n   222\t        assert_eq!(m.description.as_deref(), Some(\"Recipes for Django development\"));\n   223\t        assert_eq!(m.framework.as_deref(), Some(\"django\"));\n   224\t        assert_eq!(m.language.as_deref(), Some(\"python\"));\n   225\t        assert_eq!(m.conventions.len(), 2);\n   226\t        assert_eq!(m.recipes.len(), 3);\n   227\t        assert_eq!(m.workflows.len(), 1);\n   228\t\n   229\t        let wf = &m.workflows[\"add-field\"];\n   230\t        assert_eq!(wf.steps.len(), 2);\n   231\t        assert_eq!(wf.steps[0].recipe, \"model/add-field\");\n   232\t        assert!(wf.steps[0].when.is_none());\n   233\t        assert_eq!(wf.steps[1].recipe, \"service/add-method\");\n   234\t        assert!(wf.steps[1].when.is_some());\n   235\t    }\n   236\t\n   237\t    #[test]\n   238\t    fn empty_name_errors() {\n   239\t        let yaml = r#\"\n   240\tname: \"\"\n   241\tversion: 0.1.0\n   242\t\"#;\n   243\t        let err = LibraryManifest::parse(yaml, &dummy_path()).unwrap_err();\n   244\t        assert_eq!(err.exit_code(), 1);\n   245\t        let msg = format!(\"{err}\");\n   246\t        assert!(msg.contains(\"library name is required\"));\n   247\t    }\n   248\t\n   249\t    #[test]\n   250\t    fn empty_version_errors() {\n   251\t        let yaml = r#\"\n   252\tname: django\n   253\tversion: \"\"\n   254\t\"#;\n   255\t        let err = LibraryManifest::parse(yaml, &dummy_path()).unwrap_err();\n   256\t        assert_eq!(err.exit_code(), 1);\n   257\t        let msg = format!(\"{err}\");\n   258\t 23\t#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Default)]\n    24\tstruct RawProjectConfig {\n    25\t    #[serde(default)]\n    26\t    libraries: IndexMap<String, RawLibraryOverrides>,\n    27\t}\n    28\t\n ProjectConfig {\n    36\t    /// Load project config from `.jigrc.yaml` in the given directory.\n    37\t    /// Returns a default (empty) config if the file doesn't exist.\n    38\t    pub fn load(base_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self, JigError> {\n    39\t        let config_path = base_dir.join(\".jigrc.yaml\");\n    40\t        if !config_path.exists() {\n    41\t            return Ok(Self::default());\n    42\t        }\n    43\t\n    44\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&config_path).map_err(|e| {\n    45\t            JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n e.to_string(),\n    49\t                hint: \"check the .jigrc.yaml file\".into(),\n    50\t            })\n    51\t        })?;\n    52\t\n    53\t        let raw: RawProjectConfig = serde_yaml::from_str(&content).map_err(|e| {\n    54\t            JigError::RecipeValidation(StructuredError {\n    55\t                what: \"invalid .jigrc.yaml\".into(),\n    56\t                where_: config_path.display().to_string(),\n    57\t                why: e.to_string(),\n    58\t                hint: \"check the YAML syntax\".into(),\n    59\t            })\n    60\t        })?;\n overrides)| {\n    67\t                    (\n    68\t                        name,\n    69\t                        LibraryOverrides {\n    70\t                            conventions: 77\t}\n    78\t\n    79\t/// Resolve conventions for a library, applying project-level overrides.\n    80\t///\n    81\t/// Project overrides replace individual convention entries. Conventions\n    82\t/// not overridden keep their library defaults.\n    83\tpub fn resolve_conventions(\n    84\t    manifest: &LibraryManifest,\n    85\t    project_config: &ProjectConfig,\n 96\t}\n    97\t\n    98\t#[cfg(test)]\n    99\tmod tests {\n   100\t    use super::*;\n   101\t    use std::fs;\n   102\t    use tempfile::TempDir;\n   103\t\n   104\t    #[test]\n   105\t    fn load_missing_config_returns_default() {\n   106\t        let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n   107\t        let config = ProjectConfig::load(tmp.path()).unwrap();\n   108\t        assert!(config.libraries.is_empty());\n   109\t    }\n   110\t\n   111\t    #[test]\n   112\t    fn load_project_config() {\n   113\t        let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n   114\t        let 19\t\n    20\t#[derive(Parser)]\n    21\t#[command(name = \"jig\", version, about = \"Template rendering CLI for LLM code generation workflows\")]\n    22\tstruct Cli {\n    23\t    /// Inline variables as JSON string\n    24\t    #[arg(long, global = true)]\n    25\t    vars: Option<String>,\n    26\t\n    27\t    /// Variables from a JSON file\n    28\t    #[arg(long, global = true)]\n #[arg(long, global = true)]\n    49\t    force: bool,\n    50\t\n    51\t    /// Resolve output paths from this directory\n    52\t    #[arg(long, global = true)]\n    53\t    base_dir: 60\t    command: Commands,\n    61\t}\n    62\t\n    63\t#[derive(Subcommand)]\n    64\tenum Commands {\n    65\t    /// Parse a recipe and report whether it is valid\n    66\t    Validate {\n    67\t        /// Path to the recipe YAML file\n    68\t        recipe: PathBuf,\n    69\t    },\n    70\t    /// Show expected variables for a recipe as JSON\n    71\t    Vars {\n    72\t        /// Path to the recipe YAML file\n    73\t        recipe: PathBuf,\n    74\t    },\n    75\t    /// Render a template with variables\n    76\t    Render {\n    77\t        /// Path to the template file\n    78\t file operations in a recipe\n    84\t    Run {\n    85\t        /// Path to the recipe YAML file\n    86\t        recipe: PathBuf,\n    87\t    },\n    88\t    /// Execute a multi-recipe workflow\n    89\t    Workflow {\n    90\t        /// Path to the workflow YAML file\n    91\t        path: PathBuf,\n    92\t    },\n    93\t    /// Manage recipe libraries\n    94\t    Library {\n    95\t        #[command(subcommand)]\n    96\t        action: LibraryAction,\n    97\t    },\n    98\t}\n    99\t\n   100\t#[derive(Subcommand)]\n   101\tenum LibraryAction {\n   102\t    /// Install a library from a local directory\n   103\t    Add {\n   104\t        /// Path to the library directory\n   105\t        path: PathBuf,\n   106\t        /// Install globally instead of project-local\n   107\t        #[arg(long)]\n   108\t        global: bool,\n   109\t    },\n   110\t    /// Remove an installed library\n   111\t    Remove {\n   112\t        /// Library name\n   113\t        name: String,\n   114\t    },\n   115\t    /// Update an installed library from a source path\n   116\t    Update {\n   117\t        /// Library name\n   118\t        name: String,\n   119\t        /// Path to the updated library directory\n   120\t        path: PathBuf,\n   121\t    },\n   122\t    /// List installed libraries\n   123\t    List,\n   124\t    /// List all recipes in a library\n   125\t    Recipes {\n   126\t        /// Library name\n   127\t        name: String,\n   128\t    },\n   129\t    /// Show details for a specific recipe\n   130\t    Info {\n   131\t        /// Library-qualified recipe path (e.g., django/model/add-field)\n   132\t        path: String,\n   133\t    },\n   134\t    /// List all workflows in a library\n   135\t    Workflows {\n   136\t        /// Library name\n   137\t        name: String,\n   138\t    },\n   139\t}\n   140\t\n   141\tfn main() {\n   142\t    let cli = Cli::parse();\n   143\t    let result = run(cli);\n   144\t    match result {\n   145\t        Ok(code) => process::exit(code),\n   146\t        Err(e) => {\n   147\t            eprintln!(\"{e}\");\n   148\t            process::exit(e.exit_code());\n   149\t        }\n   150\t    }\n   151\t}\n   152\t\n   153\tfn run(cli: Cli) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\n   154\t    match cli.command {\n   155\t        Commands::Validate { recipe } => cmd_validate(&recipe, cli.json),\n   156\t        Commands::Vars { recipe } => cmd_vars(&recipe),\n   157\t        Commands::Render { template, to } => {\n   158\t            cmd_render(&template, to.as_deref(), cli.vars.as_deref(), cli.vars_file.as_deref(), cli.vars_stdin)\n   159\t        }\n   160\t        Commands::Run { recipe } => {\n   161\t            cmd_run(\n   162\t                &recipe,\n   163\t                cli.vars.as_deref(),\n   164\t                cli.vars_file.as_deref(),\n   165\t                cli.vars_stdin,\n   166\t                cli.dry_run,\n   167\t                cli.json,\n   168\t                cli.quiet,\n   169\t                cli.force,\n   170\t                cli.base_dir.as_deref(),\n   171\t                cli.verbose,\n   172\t            )\n   173\t        }\n   174\t        Commands::Workflow { path } => {\n   175\t            cmd_workflow(\n   176\t                &path,\n   177\t                cli.vars.as_deref(),\n   178\t                cli.vars_file.as_deref(),\n   179\t                cli.vars_stdin,\n   180\t                cli.dry_run,\n   181\t                cli.json,\n   182\t                cli.quiet,\n   183\t                cli.force,\n   184\t                cli.base_dir.as_deref(),\n   185\t                cli.verbose,\n   186\t            )\n   187\t        }\n   188\t        Commands::Library { action } => {\n   189\t            let base_dir = cli.base_dir.as_deref()\n   190\t                .map(|p| p.to_path_buf())\n   191\t                .unwrap_or_else(|| std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(\".\")));\n   192\t            cmd_library(action, &base_dir, cli.json, cli.quiet)\n   193\t        }\n   194\t    }\n   195\t}\n   196\t\n   197\tfn cmd_validate(path: &std::path::Path, json: bool) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\n   198\t    match workflow::detect_file_type(path)? {\n   199\t        workflow::FileType::Recipe => {\n   200\t            let recipe = Recipe::load(path)?;\n   201\t            if json {\n   202\t                let output = build_validate_json(&recipe);\n   203\t                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&output).unwrap());\n   204\t            } else {\n   205\t                eprintln!(\"Recipe is valid: {}\", path.display());\n   206\t                eprintln!(\"  Variables: {}\", recipe.variables.len());\n   207\t                if !recipe.variables.is_empty() {\n   208\t                    for name in recipe.variables.keys() {\n   209\t                        eprintln!(\"    - {name}\");\n   210\t                    }\n   211\t                }\n   212\t                let op_types = summarize_op_types(&recipe);\n   213\t                eprintln!(\"  Operations: {} ({})\", recipe.files.len(), op_types);\n   214\t            }\n   215\t        }\n   216\t        workflow::FileType::Workflow => {\n   217\t            let validation = workflow::validate_workflow(path)?;\n   218\t            if json {\n   219\t                let output = output::build_workflow_validate_json(&validation);\n   220\t                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&output).unwrap());\n   221\t            } else {\n   222\t                eprintln!(\"Workflow is valid: {}\", path.display());\n   223\t                if let Some(ref name) = validation.name {\n   224\t                    eprintln!(\"  Name: {name}\");\n   225\t                }\n   226\t                eprintln!(\"  Variables: {}\", validation.variables.len());\n   227\t                if !validation.variables.is_empty() {\n   228\t                    for name in validation.variables.keys() {\n   229\t                        eprintln!(\"    - {name}\");\n   230\t                    }\n   231\t                }\n   232\t                let unconditional = validation.steps.iter().filter(|s| !s.conditional).count();\n   233\t                let conditional = validation.steps.iter().filter(|s| s.conditional).count();\n   234\t                eprintln!(\n   235\t                    \"  Steps: {} ({} unconditional, {} conditional)\",\n   236\t                    validation.steps.len(),\n   237\t                    unconditional,\n   238\t                    conditional\n   239\t                );\n   240\t                for (i, step) in validation.steps.iter().enumerate() {\n   241\t                    let cond = if step.conditional { \" (conditional)\" } else { \"\" };\n   242\t                    let status = if step.valid { \"valid\" } else { \"INVALID\" };\n   243\t                    eprintln!(\"    {}. {} — {}{}\", i + 1, step.recipe, status, cond);\n   244\t                }\n   245\t            }\n   246\t        }\n   247\t    }\n   248\t\n   249\t    Ok(0)\n   250\t}\n   251\t\n   252\tfn cmd_vars(path: &std::path::Path) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\n   253\t    let vars = match workflow::detect_file_type(path)? {\n   254\t        workflow::FileType::Recipe => {\n   255\t            let recipe = Recipe::load(path)?;\n   256\t            variables::vars_json(&recipe.variables)\n   257\t        }\n   258\t        workflow::FileType::Workflow => {\n   259\t            let wf = workflow::load_workflow(path)?;\n   260\t            variables::vars_json(&wf.variables)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

cmd_render(\n   268\t    template_path: &std::path::Path,\n   269\t    to: Option<&std::path::Path>,\n   270\t    inline_vars: Option<&str>,\n   271\t    vars_file: Option<&std::path::Path>,\n   272\t    vars_stdin: bool,\n   273\t) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\n   274\t    // Read the template file\n   275\t    let source = std::fs::read_to_string(template_path).map_err(|e| {\n   276\t        JigError::TemplateRendering(crate::error::StructuredError {\n   277\t            what: format!(\"cannot read template file '{}'\", template_path.display()),\n   278\t            where_: template_path.display().to_string(),\n   279\t            why: e.to_string(),\n   280\t            hint: \"check the template file path\".into(),\n   281\t        })\n   282\t    })?;\n   283\t\n   284\t    // Collect variables from all sources (no recipe context → no type validation)\n   285\t    let vars = variables::collect_vars(inline_vars, vars_file, vars_stdin)?;\n   286\t\n   287\t    // Create standalone environment and render\n   288\t    let env = renderer::create_standalone_env();\n   289\t    let source_name = template_path.display().to_string();\n   290\t    let output = renderer::render_string(&env, &source, &vars, &source_name)?;\n   291\t\n   292\t    // Write to file or stdout\n   293\t    match to {\n   294\t        Some(path) => {\n   295\t            if let Some(parent) = path.parent().filter(|p| !p.as_os_str().is_empty()) {\n   296\t                std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).map_err(|e| {\n   297\t                    JigError::FileOperation(crate::error::StructuredError {\n   298\t                        what: format!(\"cannot create output directory '{}'\", parent.display()),\n   299\t                        where_: path.display().to_string(),\n   300\t                        why: e.to_string(),\n   301\t                        hint: \"check directory permissions\".into(),\n   302\t                    })\n   303\t                })?;\n   304\t            }\n   305\t            std::fs::write(path, &output).map_err(|e| {\n   306\t                JigError::FileOperation(crate::error::StructuredError {\n   307\t                    what: format!(\"cannot write output file '{}'\", path.display()),\n   308\t                    where_: path.display().to_string(),\n   309\t                    why: e.to_string(),\n   310\t                    hint: \"check file permissions\".into(),\n   311\t                })\n   312\t            })?;\n   313\t        }\n   314\t        None => {\n   315\t            print!(\"{output}\");\n   316\t        }\n   317\t    }\n   318\t\n   319\t    Ok(0)\n   320\t}\n   321\t\n   322\t#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n   323\tfn cmd_run(\n   324\t    recipe_path: &std::path::Path,\n   325\t    inline_vars: Option<&str>,\n   326\t    vars_file: Option<&std::path::Path>,\n   327\t    vars_stdin: bool,\n   328\t    dry_run: bool,\n   329\t    force_json: bool,\n   330\t    quiet: bool,\n   331\t    force: bool,\n   332\t    base_dir: Option<&std::path::Path>,\n   333\t    verbose: bool,\n   334\t) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\n   335\t    let mode = output::detect_mode(force_json);\n   336\t\n   337\t    // Helper: handle pre-operation errors respecting --json and --quiet.\n   338\t    // In JSON/piped mode, emit a JSON error envelope to stdout.\n   339\t    // In human mode with --quiet, suppress stderr.\n   340\t    // Returns Ok(exit_code) so main() doesn't double-print.\n   341\t    let handle_early_error = |e: JigError| -> Result<i32, JigError> {\n   342\t        let code = e.exit_code();\n   343\t        match mode {\n   344\t            output::OutputMode::Json => {\n   345\t                let se = e.structured_error().clone();\n   346\t                let json = serde_json::json!({\n   347\t                    \"dry_run\": dry_run,\n   348\t                    \"operations\": [{\n   349\t                        \"action\": \"error\",\n   350\t                        \"path\": \"\",\n   351\t                        \"what\": se.what,\n   352\t                        \"where\": se.where_,\n   353\t                        \"why\": se.why,\n   354\t                        \"hint\": se.hint,\n   355\t                        \"rendered_content\": \"\",\n   356\t                    }],\n   357\t                    \"files_written\": [],\n   358\t                    \"files_skipped\": [],\n   359\t                });\n   360\t                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap());\n   361\t                Ok(code)\n   362\t            }\n   363\t            output::OutputMode::Human => {\n   364\t                if !quiet {\n   365\t                    eprintln!(\"{e}\");\n   366\t                }\n   367\t                Ok(code)\n   368\t            }\n   369\t        }\n   370\t    };\n   371\t\n   372\t    // 1. Load and validate recipe (exit 1 on failure).\n   373\t    let recipe = match Recipe::load(recipe_path) {\n   374\t        Ok(r) => r,\n   375\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   376\t    };\n   377\t\n   378\t    // 2. Collect and validate variables (exit 4 on failure).\n   379\t    let provided = match variables::collect_vars(inline_vars, vars_file, vars_stdin) {\n   380\t        Ok(v) => v,\n   381\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   382\t    };\n   383\t    let vars = match variables::validate_variables(&recipe.variables, &provided) {\n   384\t        Ok(v) => v,\n   385\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   386\t    };\n   387\t\n   388\t    // 3. Create recipe-aware environment and render ALL templates upfront (exit 2 on failure).\n   389\t    let env = match renderer::create_recipe_env(&recipe) {\n   390\t        Ok(e) => e,\n   391\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   392\t    };\n   393\t\n   394\t    let mut prepared_ops = Vec::with_capacity(recipe.files.len());\n   395\t    for (i, file_op) in recipe.files.iter().enumerate() {\n   396\t        // Render the template content.\n   397\t        let rendered_content = match renderer::render_template(&env, file_op.template(), &vars) {\n   398\t            Ok(c) => c,\n   399\t            Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   400\t        };\n   401\t\n   402\t        // Render templated path fields.\n   403\t        let rendered_path = match file_op {\n   404\t            recipe::FileOp::Create { to, .. } => {\n   405\t                match renderer::render_path_template(&env, to, &vars, &format!(\"files[{}].to\", i)) {\n   406\t                    Ok(p) => p,\n   407\t                    Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   408\t                }\n   409\t            }\n   410\t            recipe::FileOp::Inject { inject, .. } => {\n   411\t                match renderer::render_path_template(&env, inject, &vars, &format!(\"files[{}].inject\", i)) {\n   412\t                    Ok(p) => p,\n   413\t                    Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   414\t                }\n   415\t            }\n   416\t            recipe::FileOp::Replace { replace, .. } => {\n   417\t                match renderer::render_path_template(&env, replace, &vars, &format!(\"files[{}].replace\", i)) {\n   418\t                    Ok(p) => p,\n   419\t                    Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   420\t                }\n   421\t            }\n   422\t            recipe::FileOp::Patch { patch, .. } => {\n   423\t                match renderer::render_path_template(&env, patch, &vars, &format!(\"files[{}].patch\", i)) {\n   424\t                    Ok(p) => p,\n   425\t                    Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   426\t                }\n   427\t            }\n   428\t        };\n   429\t\n   430\t        // Render skip_if for inject and patch operations.\n   431\t        let rendered_skip_if = match file_op {\n   432\t            recipe::FileOp::Inject { skip_if: Some(skip_if_expr), .. }\n   433\t            | recipe::FileOp::Patch { skip_if: Some(skip_if_expr), .. } => {\n   434\t                match renderer::render_path_template(\n   435\t                    &env, skip_if_expr, &vars, &format!(\"files[{}].skip_if\", i),\n   436\t                ) {\n   437\t                    Ok(s) => Some(s),\n   438\t                    Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   439\t                }\n   440\t            }\n   441\t            _ => None,\n   442\t        };\n   443\t\n   444\t        prepared_ops.push(operations::PreparedOp {\n   445\t            file_op: file_op.clone(),\n   446\t            rendered_content,\n   447\t            rendered_path,\n   448\t            rendered_skip_if,\n   449\t        });\n   450\t    }\n   451\t\n   452\t    // 4. Validate base_dir exists (AC-4.10). Done after rendering to respect\n   453\t    // pipeline stage ordering: recipe(1) → variables(4) → rendering(2) → file ops(3) (AC-N5.2).\n   454\t    let resolved_base = match base_dir {\n   455\t        Some(bd) => {\n   456\t            if !bd.is_dir() {\n   457\t                return handle_early_error(JigError::FileOperation(crate::error::StructuredError {\n   458\t                    what: format!(\"base directory does not exist: '{}'\", bd.display()),\n   459\t                    where_: bd.display().to_string(),\n   460\t                    why: \"the specified --base-dir path is not an existing directory\".into(),\n   461\t                    hint: \"create the directory first, or omit --base-dir to use the current directory\".into(),\n   462\t                }));\n   463\t            }\n   464\t            bd.to_path_buf()\n   465\t        }\n   466\t        None => std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(\".\")),\n   467\t    };\n   468\t\n   469\t    // 5. Execute operations in declaration order, fail-fast on error.\n   470\t    let mut ctx = operations::ExecutionContext::new(resolved_base, dry_run, force);\n   471\t    let mut results: Vec<operations::OpResult> = Vec::with_capacity(prepared_ops.len());\n   472\t\n   473\t    for prepared in &prepared_ops {\n   474\t        let result = operations::execute_operation(prepared, &mut ctx, verbose);\n   475\t        let is_err = result.is_error();\n   476\t        results.push(result);\n   477\t        if is_err {\n   478\t            break; // Fail-fast: stop on first error (AC-6.10).\n   479\t        }\n   480\t    }\n   481\t\n   482\t    // 6. Format and emit output.\n   483\t    let has_error = results.iter().any(|r| r.is_error());\n   484\t\n   485\t    match mode {\n   486\t        output::OutputMode::Json => {\n   487\t            let json = output::format_json(&results, dry_run, verbose);\n   488\t            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap());\n   489\t        }\n   490\t        output::OutputMode::Human => {\n   491\t            if !quiet {\n   492\t                output::format_human(&results, dry_run, verbose);\n   493\t            }\n   494\t        }\n   495\t    }\n   496\t\n   497\t    // 7. Return appropriate exit code.\n   498\t    // Error details are already in the JSON/human output above, so return Ok\n   499\t    // with the exit code to avoid duplicate stderr output from main().\n   500\t    if has_error {\n   501\t        if let Some(last) = results.last()\n   502\t            && let Some(jig_err) = operations::op_error_to_jig_error(last) {\n   503\t                return Ok(jig_err.exit_code());\n   504\t            }\n   505\t    }\n   506\t\n   507\t    Ok(0)\n   508\t}\n   509\t\n   510\t#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n   511\tfn cmd_workflow(\n   512\t    workflow_path: &std::path::Path,\n   513\t    inline_vars: Option<&str>,\n   514\t    vars_file: Option<&std::path::Path>,\n   515\t    vars_stdin: bool,\n   516\t    dry_run: bool,\n   517\t    force_json: bool,\n   518\t    quiet: bool,\n   519\t    force: bool,\n   520\t    base_dir: Option<&std::path::Path>,\n   521\t    verbose: bool,\n   522\t) -> Result<i32, JigError> {\n   523\t    let mode = output::detect_mode(force_json);\n   524\t\n   525\t    // Helper for early errors.\n   526\t    let handle_early_error = |e: JigError| -> Result<i32, JigError> {\n   527\t        let code = e.exit_code();\n   528\t        match mode {\n   529\t            output::OutputMode::Json => {\n   530\t                let se = e.structured_error().clone();\n   531\t                let json = serde_json::json!({\n   532\t                    \"dry_run\": dry_run,\n   533\t                    \"workflow\": serde_json::Value::Null,\n   534\t                    \"on_error\": \"stop\",\n   535\t                    \"status\": \"error\",\n   536\t                    \"steps\": [{\n   537\t                        \"recipe\": \"\",\n   538\t                        \"status\": \"error\",\n   539\t                        \"error\": {\n   540\t                            \"what\": se.what,\n   541\t                            \"where\": se.where_,\n   542\t                            \"why\": se.why,\n   543\t                            \"hint\": se.hint,\n   544\t                        },\n   545\t                        \"operations\": [],\n   546\t                    }],\n   547\t                    \"files_written\": [],\n   548\t                    \"files_skipped\": [],\n   549\t                });\n   550\t                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap());\n   551\t                Ok(code)\n   552\t            }\n   553\t            output::OutputMode::Human => {\n   554\t                if !quiet {\n   555\t                    eprintln!(\"{e}\");\n   556\t                }\n   557\t                Ok(code)\n   558\t            }\n   559\t        }\n   560\t    };\n   561\t\n   562\t    // Guard: check if file is a recipe instead of a workflow.\n   563\t    match workflow::detect_file_type(workflow_path) {\n   564\t        Ok(workflow::FileType::Recipe) => {\n   565\t            return handle_early_error(JigError::RecipeValidation(\n   566\t                crate::error::StructuredError {\n   567\t                    what: \"expected a workflow file, got a recipe file\".into(),\n   568\t                    where_: workflow_path.display().to_string(),\n   569\t                    why: \"file has 'files' (recipe) instead of 'steps' (workflow)\".into(),\n   570\t                    hint: \"use 'jig run' to execute recipes, or provide a workflow file with 'steps'\".into(),\n   571\t                },\n   572\t            ));\n   573\t        }\n   574\t        Ok(workflow::FileType::Workflow) => {} // proceed\n   575\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   576\t    }\n   577\t\n   578\t    // Load workflow.\n   579\t    let wf = match workflow::load_workflow(workflow_path) {\n   580\t        Ok(w) => w,\n   581\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   582\t    };\n   583\t\n   584\t    // Collect and validate workflow-level variables.\n   585\t    let provided = match variables::collect_vars(inline_vars, vars_file, vars_stdin) {\n   586\t        Ok(v) => v,\n   587\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   588\t    };\n   589\t    let vars = match variables::validate_variables(&wf.variables, &provided) {\n   590\t        Ok(v) => v,\n   591\t        Err(e) => return handle_early_error(e),\n   592\t    };\n   593\t\n   594\t    // Validate base_dir exists.\n   595\t    let resolved_base = match base_dir {\n   596\t        Some(bd) => {\n   597\t            if !bd.is_dir() {\n   598\t                return handle_early_error(JigError::FileOperation(crate::error::StructuredError {\n   599\t                    what: format!(\"base directory does not exist: '{}'\", bd.display()),\n   600\t                    where_: bd.display().to_string(),\n   601\t                    why: \"the specified --base-dir path is not an existing directory\".into(),\n   602\t                    hint: \"create the directory first, or omit --base-dir to use the current directory\".into(),\n   603\t                }));\n   604\t            }\n   605\t            bd.to_path_buf()\n   606\t        }\n   607\t        None => std::env::current_dir().unwrap_or_else(|_| PathBuf::from(\".\")),\n   608\t    };\n   609\t\n   610\t    // Execute workflow.\n   611\t    let mut ctx = operations::ExecutionContext::new(resolved_base, dry_run, force);\n   612\t    let result = workflow::execute_workflow(&wf, vars, &mut ctx, verbose);\n   613\t\n   614\t    // Determine exit code based on step results and their effective on_error modes.\n   615\t    let exit_code = compute_workflow_exit_code(&wf, &result);\n   616\t\n   617\t    // Format output.\n   618\t    match mode {\n   619\t        output::OutputMode::Json => {\n   620\t            let json = output::format_workflow_json(&result, dry_run, verbose, exit_code);\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

output::format_workflow_json(&result, dry_run, verbose, exit_code);\n   621\t            println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&json).unwrap());\n   622\t        }\n   623\t        output::OutputMode::Human => {\n   624\t            if !quiet {\n   625\t                output::format_workflow_human(&result, dry_run, verbose);\n   626\t            }\n   627\t        }\n   628\t    }\n   629\t\n   630\t    Ok(exit_code)\n   631\t}\n   632\t\n   633\t/// Compute the exit code for a completed workflow based on step results\n   634\t/// and their effective on_error modes.\n   635\tfn compute_workflow_exit_code(\n   636\t    wf: &workflow::Workflow,\n   637\t    result: &workflow::WorkflowResult,\n   638\t) -> i32 {\n   639\t    let mut has_report_error = false;\n   640\t\n   641\t    for (i, step_result) in result.steps.iter().enumerate() {\n   642\t        if let workflow::StepResult::Error { error, .. } = step_result {\n   643\t            let effective_mode = wf\n   644\t                .steps\n   645\t                .get(i)\n   646\t                .and_then(|s| s.on_error)\n   647\t                .unwrap_or(wf.on_error);\n   648\t\n   649\t            match effective_mode {\n   650\t                workflow::OnError::Stop => {\n   651\t                    // This step halted execution — return its error code.\n   652\t                    return error.exit_code();\n   653\t                }\n   654\t                workflow::OnError::Continue => {\n   655\t                    // Tolerated — no effect on exit code.\n   656\t                }\n   657\t                workflow::OnError::Report => {\n   658\t                    has_report_error = true;\n   659\t                }\n   660\t            }\n   661\t        }\n   662\t    }\n   663\t\n   664\t    if has_report_error {\n   665\t        3\n   666\t    } else {\n   667\t        0\n   668\t    }\n   669\t}\n   670\t\n   671\tfn cmd_library(\n   672\t    action: LibraryAction,\n path.display().to_string(),\n   685\t                    why: e.to_string(),\n   686\t                    hint: \"check the path exists\".into(),\n   687\t                })\n   688\t            })?;\n   689\t            let location = if global {\n   690\t                library::install::InstallLocation::Global\n   691\t            } else {\n   692\t                library::install::InstallLocation::ProjectLocal\n   693\t            };\n   694\t            let installed = library::install::add_from_path(&source, location, base_dir)?;\n   695\t            match mode {\n   696\t                output::OutputMode::Json => {\n   697\t                    let json = serde_json::json!({\n   698\t                        \"action\": \"add\",\n   699\t           .description\n   951\t                                    .as_deref()\n   952\t                                    .map(|d| format!(\" — {d}\"))\n   953\t                                    .unwrap_or_default();\n   954\t                                let steps_info = format!(\"{} steps\", wf.steps.len());\n   955\t                                eprintln!(\"  {} ({steps_info}){desc}\", wf.name);\n   956\t                            }\n   957\t                        }\n   958\t                    }\n   959\t                }\n   960\t            }\n   961\t            Ok(0)\n   962\t        }\n   963\t    }\n   964\t}\n   965\t\n   966\tfn build_validate_json(recipe: &Recipe) -> serde_json::Value {\n   967\t    let vars = variables::vars_json(&recipe.variables);\n   968\t\n   969\t    let ops: Vec<serde_json::Value> = recipe.files.iter().map(|op| {\n   970\t        let mut m = serde_json::Map::new();\n   971\t        m.insert(\"type\".into(), serde_json::Value::String(op.op_type_str().into()));\n   972\t        match op {\n   973\t            recipe::FileOp::Create { to, .. } => {\n   974\t                m.insert(\"to\".into(), serde_json::Value::String(to.clone()));\n   975\t            }\n   976\t            recipe::FileOp::Inject { inject, .. } => {\n   977\t                m.insert(\"inject\".into(), serde_json::Value::String(inject.clone()));\n   978\t            }\n   979\t            recipe::FileOp::Replace { replace, .. } => {\n   980\t                m.insert(\"replace\".into(), serde_json::Value::String(replace.clone()));\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_27","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba tests/library.rs | sed -n '1,360p'\"","aggregated_output":"     1\t//! Integration tests for `jig library` subcommands.\n     2\t//!\n     3\t//! These tests exercise the CLI end-to-end: create temp directories with\n     4\t//! library fixtures, run `jig library <action>`, and verify output.\n     5\t\n     6\tuse std::fs;\n     7\tuse std::path::Path;\n     8\tuse std::process::Command;\n     9\t\n    10\tuse tempfile::TempDir;\n    11\t\n    12\tfn jig_bin() -> String {\n    13\t    env!(\"CARGO_BIN_EXE_jig\").to_string()\n    14\t}\n    15\t\n    16\t/// Create a minimal library source directory for testing.\n    17\tfn create_library_source(dir: &Path, name: &str, version: &str) {\n    18\t    fs::create_dir_all(dir).unwrap();\n    19\t    let manifest = format!(\n    20\t        r#\"name: {name}\n    21\tversion: {version}\n    22\tdescription: Test library for {name}\n    23\tframework: test\n    24\tlanguage: rust\n    25\t\n    26\tconventions:\n    27\t  models: \"src/models/{{{{ model | snakecase }}}}.rs\"\n    28\t\n    29\trecipes:\n    30\t  model/add-field: \"Add a field to a model\"\n    31\t  model/add-model: \"Create a new model\"\n    32\t\n    33\tworkflows:\n    34\t  add-field:\n    35\t    description: \"Add a field across the stack\"\n    36\t    steps:\n    37\t      - recipe: model/add-field\n    38\t      - recipe: model/add-model\n    39\t        when: \"{{{{ create_model }}}}\"\n    40\t\"#\n    41\t    );\n    42\t    fs::write(dir.join(\"jig-library.yaml\"), manifest).unwrap();\n    43\t\n    44\t    // Create recipe directories with recipe.yaml files.\n    45\t    let add_field_dir = dir.join(\"model/add-field\");\n    46\t    fs::create_dir_all(add_field_dir.join(\"templates\")).unwrap();\n    47\t    fs::write(\n    48\t        add_field_dir.join(\"recipe.yaml\"),\n    49\t        r#\"name: add-field\n    50\tdescription: Add a field to a model\n    51\tvariables:\n    52\t  field_name:\n    53\t    type: string\n    54\t    required: true\n    55\t    description: \"Name of the field\"\n    56\t  field_type:\n    57\t    type: string\n    58\t    required: true\n    59\tfiles: []\n    60\t\"#,\n    61\t    )\n    62\t    .unwrap();\n    63\t    fs::write(\n    64\t        add_field_dir.join(\"templates/field.rs.j2\"),\n    65\t        \"pub {{ field_name }}: {{ field_type }},\\n\",\n    66\t    )\n    67\t    .unwrap();\n    68\t\n    69\t    let add_model_dir = dir.join(\"model/add-model\");\n    70\t    fs::create_dir_all(add_model_dir.join(\"templates\")).unwrap();\n    71\t    fs::write(\n    72\t        add_model_dir.join(\"recipe.yaml\"),\n    73\t        \"name: add-model\\nfiles: []\\n\",\n    74\t    )\n    75\t    .unwrap();\n    76\t}\n    77\t\n    78\t// ── jig library add ─────────────────────────────────────────────\n    79\t\n    80\t#[test]\n    81\tfn library_add_from_local_path() {\n    82\t    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n    83\t    let source = tmp.path().join(\"mylib-source\");\n    84\t    create_library_source(&source, \"mylib\", \"0.1.0\");\n    85\t\n    86\t    let project = tmp.path().join(\"project\");\n    87\t    fs::create_dir_all(&project).unwrap();\n    88\t\n    89\t    let output = Command::new(jig_bin())\n    90\t        .args([\"library\", \"add\", &source.display().to_string()])\n    91\t        .args([\"--base-dir\", &project.display().to_string()])\n    92\t        .args([\"--json\"])\n    93\t        .output()\n    94\t        .expect(\"failed to run jig\");\n    95\t\n    96\t    assert!(\n    97\t        output.status.success(),\n    98\t        \"jig library add failed: {}\",\n    99\t        String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr)\n   100\t    );\n   101\t\n   102\t    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&output.stdout).unwrap();\n   103\t    assert_eq!(json[\"action\"], \"add\");\n   104\t    assert_eq!(json[\"library\"], \"mylib\");\n   105\t    assert_eq!(json[\"version\"], \"0.1.0\");\n   106\t    assert_eq!(json[\"location\"], \"project\");\n   107\t\n   108\t    // Verify library is installed.\n   109\t    assert!(project.join(\".jig/libraries/mylib/jig-library.yaml\").exists());\n   110\t}\n   111\t\n   112\t#[test]\n   113\tfn library_add_already_installed_errors() {\n   114\t    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n   115\t    let source = tmp.path().join(\"mylib-source\");\n   116\t    create_library_source(&source, \"mylib\", \"0.1.0\");\n   117\t\n   118\t    let project = tmp.path().join(\"project\");\n   119\t    fs::create_dir_all(&project).unwrap();\n   120\t\n   121\t    // assert!(!steps[0][\"conditional\"].as_bool().unwrap());\n   454\t    assert_eq!(steps[1][\"recipe\"], \"model/add-model\");\n   455\t    assert!(steps[1][\"conditional\"].as_bool().unwrap());\n   456\t}\n   457\t\n   458\t// ── Full lifecycle ──────────────────────────────────────────────\n   459\t\n   460\t#[test]\n   461\tfn library_full_lifecycle() {\n   462\t    let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n   463\t    let project = tmp.path().join(\"project\");\n   464\t    fs::create_dir_all(&project).unwrap();\n   465\t\n   466\t    // 1. List — empty.\n   467\t    let out = Command::new(jig_bin())\n   468\t        .args([\"library\", \"list\", \"--json\"])\n   469\t        .args([\"--base-dir\", &project.display().to_string()])\n   470\t        .output()\n   471\t        .unwrap();\n   472\t    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&out.stdout).unwrap();\n   473\t    assert_eq!(json[\"libraries\"].as_array().unwrap().len(), 0);\n   474\t\n   475\t    // 2. Add.\n   476\t    let source = tmp.path().join(\"lib-source\");\n   477\t    create_library_source(&source, \"testlib\", \"1.0.0\");\n   478\t    let out = Command::new(jig_bin())\n   479\t        .args([\"library\", \"add\", &source.display().to_string()])\n   480\t        .args([\"--base-dir\", &project.display().to_string()])\n   481\t        .args([\"--json\"])\n   482\t        .output()\n   483\t        .unwrap();\n   484\t    assert!(out.status.success());\n   485\t\n   486\t    // 3. List — should show 1 library.\n   487\t    let out = Command::new(jig_bin())\n   488\t        .args([\"library\", \"list\", \"--json\"])\n   489\t        .args([\"--base-dir\", &project.display().to_string()])\n   490\t        .output()\n   491\t        .unwrap();\n   492\t    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&out.stdout).unwrap();\n   493\t    assert_eq!(json[\"libraries\"].as_array().unwrap().len(), 1);\n   494\t    assert_eq!(json[\"libraries\"][0][\"name\"], \"testlib\");\n   495\t\n   496\t    // 4. Recipes.\n   497\t    let out = Command::new(jig_bin())\n   498\t        .args([\"library\", \"recipes\", \"testlib\", \"--json\"])\n   499\t        .args([\"--base-dir\", &project.display().to_string()])\n   500\t        .output()\n   501\t        .unwrap();\n   502\t    assert!(out.status.success());\n   503\t    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&out.stdout).unwrap();\n   504\t    assert_eq!(json[\"recipes\"].as_array().unwrap().len(), 2);\n   505\t\n   506\t    // 5. Info.\n   507\t    let out = Command::new(jig_bin())\n   508\t        .args([\"library\", \"info\", \"testlib/model/add-field\", \"--json\"])\n   509\t        .args([\"--base-dir\", &project.display().to_string()])\n   510\t        .output()\n   511\t        .unwrap();\n   512\t    assert!(out.status.success());\n   513\t    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&out.stdout).unwrap();\n   514\t    assert_eq!(json[\"recipe\"], \"model/add-field\");\n   515\t\n   516\t    // 6. Update.\n   517\t    let source_v2 = tmp.path().join(\"lib-source-v2\");\n   518\t    create_library_source(&source_v2, \"testlib\", \"2.0.0\");\n   519\t    let out = Command::new(jig_bin())\n   520\t        .args([\n   521\t            \"library\",\n   522\t            \"update\",\n   523\t            \"testlib\",\n   524\t            &source_v2.display().to_string(),\n   525\t            \"--json\",\n   526\t        ])\n   527\t        .args([\"--base-dir\", &project.display().to_string()])\n   528\t        .output()\n   529\t        .unwrap();\n   530\t    assert!(out.status.success());\n   531\t    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&out.stdout).unwrap();\n   532\t    assert_eq!(json[\"version\"], \"2.0.0\");\n   533\t\n   534\t    // 7. Remove.\n   535\t    let out = Command::new(jig_bin())\n   536\t        .args([\"library\", \"remove\", \"testlib\", \"--json\"])\n   537\t        .args([\"--base-dir\", &project.display().to_string()])\n   538\t        .output()\n   539\t        .unwrap();\n   540\t    assert!(out.status.success());\n   541\t\n   542\t    // 8. List — empty again.\n   543\t    let out = Command::new(jig_bin())\n   544\t        .args([\"library\", \"list\", \"--json\"])\n   545\t        .args([\"--base-dir\", &project.display().to_string()])\n   546\t        .output()\n   547\t        .unwrap();\n   548\t    let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_slice(&out.stdout).unwrap();\n   549\t    assert_eq!(json[\"libraries\"].as_array().unwrap().len(), 0);\n   550\t}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_30","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba docs/ARCHITECTURE.md | sed -n '320,390p'\"","aggregated_output":"   320\tfn detect_file_type(path) -> Result<FileType>       // auto-detect workflow vs recipe\n   321\tfn load_workflow(path) -> Result<Workflow>            // parse + validate + resolve paths\n   322\tfn resolve_step_variables(vars, step) -> Value        // vars_map rename + vars override\n   323\tfn evaluate_when(expr, vars) -> Result<bool>          // Jinja2 render + truthiness check\n   324\tfn execute_workflow(wf, vars, ctx, verbose) -> WorkflowResult\n   325\tfn run_recipe(recipe, vars, ctx, verbose) -> Result<Vec<OpResult>>\n   326\t```\n   327\t\n   328\tNote: `run_recipe()` duplicates the rendering pipeline from `cmd_run` in `main.rs` (review finding M4). Should be consolidated before v0.4.\n   329\t\n   330\t### `src/library/` — Library Management (v0.4+)\n   331\t\n   332\tOwns: Library manifest parsing, installation, discovery, convention mapping.\n   333\tDepends on: recipe, filesystem, git (for remote installs).\n   334\t\n   335\tNot built until v0.4. Included here for architectural completeness.\n   336\t\n   337\t## Dependency Map\n   338\t\n   339\t```\n   340\t                     main.rs\n   341\t                    /   |   \\\n   342\t                   /    |    \\\n   343\t              recipe  variables  output\n   344\t                |       |         |\n   345\t                v       v         v\n   346\t             renderer  (merges)  error\n   347\t                |\n   348\t                v\n   349\t           operations/mod\n   350\t           /    |    \\    \\\n   351\t       create inject replace patch\n   352\t                              |\n   353\t                              v\n   354\t                          scope/mod\n   355\t                         /    |    \\\n   356\t                    indent  delim  position\n   357\t```\n   358\t\n   359\tExternal crate usage:\n   360\t- clap: main.rs only\n   361\t- serde + serde_yaml: recipe.rs\n   362\t- serde_json: variables.rs, output.rs\n   363\t- minijinja: renderer.rs\n   364\t- regex: inject.rs, replace.rs, patch.rs, scope/*.rs\n   365\t- heck: filters.rs\n   366\t- owo-colors: output.rs\n   367\t- thiserror: error.rs\n   368\t- indexmap: recipe.rs, variables.rs\n   369\t- pluralizer: filters.rs\n   370\t- strsim: renderer.rs (did-you-mean hints)\n   371\t- insta: tests (snapshot testing)\n   372\t\n   373\t## Key Design Decisions\n   374\t\n   375\t### D-1: Render-Then-Execute\n   376\t\n   377\tAll templates are rendered before any operation executes. The pipeline renders every template in the recipe upfront, then executes operations sequentially. This means:\n   378\t- A rendering error in any template prevents all file writes\n   379\t- The rendered content is always available for error messages (I-4)\n   380\t- Operations are simpler — they just deal with strings and files\n   381\t- Note: execution failures (e.g., inject target missing) can still produce partial writes if earlier operations succeeded. Recovery: re-run with `--force` or use rendered content from error output for manual editing.\n   382\t\n   383\t### D-2: Recipe-Relative Template Resolution\n   384\t\n   385\tTemplate paths in recipes are relative to the recipe file, not the working directory. This keeps recipes self-contained (I-7). The `--base-dir` flag only affects output paths (where files are written), not template paths (where templates are read from).\n   386\t\n   387\t### D-3: Regex for Anchoring, Not Parsing\n   388\t\n   389\tAnchors use regex, not AST parsing. This is deliberate:\n   390\t- Language-agnostic (same tool works for Python, TypeScript, Go, Rust)\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

overrides\n  1932\t- Library recipe discovery (`jig library recipes <name>`)\n  1933\t- Project-local extensions directory\n  1934\t- Template overrides (replace a single template without forking)\n  1935\t\n  1936\t### v0.5 — Distribution\n  1937\t- GitHub Actions release pipeline (cross-compile all targets)\n  1938\t- Homebrew tap formula\n  1939\t- `cargo install jig-cli`\n  1940\t- Nix flake\n  1941\t- npm binary wrapper (platform-specific postinstall, like esbuild)\n  1942\t- Shell installer script\n  1943\t- README with usage examples\n  1944\t\n  1945\t### v0.6 — Claude Code Plugin\n  1946\t- Plugin with `/jig:init` skill (scaffold recipe + templates in a skill directory)\n  1947\t- Plugin with `/jig:doctor` skill (validate all recipes in a plugin)\n  1948\t- Reference documentation for skill authors\n  1949\t- `jig-django` library as first community library + Claude Code plugin dual-publish\n  1950\t- Example workflows: add-field, add-endpoint, scaffold-resource\n  1951\t\n  1952\t### v1.0 — Stable\n  1953\t- Semver stability guarantee on recipe format, anchor/scope system, and library manifest\n  1954\t- Semver stability guarantee on CLI interface and JSON output\n  1955\t- Semver stability guarantee on exit codes\n  1956\t- Comprehensive documentation site\n  1957\t- Published to homebrew-core\n  1958\t- At least 3 community libraries (django, vue/react, rails or fastapi)\n  1959\t\n  1960\t### v0.7 — Scan and Check\n  1961\t- `jig scan` — reverse a recipe to extract variables from existing code\n  1962\t- Directory-level scan for project mapping\n  1963\t- `jig check` — conformance verification against recipes\n  1964\t- `fix_recipe` references in check output\n  1965\t- `--strict` and `--exit-on-failure` for CI gating\n  1966\t\n  1967\t### v0.8 — Infer\n  1968\t- `jig infer` from before/after file pairs\n  1969\t- Multi-example inference with variable detection\n  1970\t- `jig infer --from-git` to learn from commit history\n  1971\t- Multi-file workflow inference from single commits\n  1972\t- Draft review and promote workflow\n  1973\t\n  1974\t### v0.9 — Polyglot and Schema-First\n  1975\t- Cross-library workflows (django + vue in a single workflow)\n  1976\t- `jig from-schema openapi|sql|proto|graphql` command\n  1977\t- Type mapping definitions in library manifests\n  1978\t- Schema-to-variables resolution\n  1979\t\n  1980\t### v1.0 — Stable\n  1981\t- Semver stability guarantee on recipe format, anchor/scope system, and library manifest\n  1982\t- Semver stability guarantee on CLI interface and JSON output\n  1983\t- Semver stability guarantee on exit codes\n  1984\t- Comprehensive documentation site\n  1985\t- Published to homebrew-core\n  1986\t- At least 3 community libraries (django, vue/react, rails or fastapi)\n  1987\t\n  1988\t### Future (Post-1.0)\n  1989\t\n  1990\t- **Observation engine** — Claude Code hook that logs edit patterns and proposes recipes from repeated behavior\n  1991\t- **Hooks** — `pre_run` and `post_run` shell commands in recipes (e.g., run `ruff format` on patched files, `prettier --write` on generated TypeScript)\n  1992\t- **Interactive mode** — for human users who want to be prompted for variables (not the priority, but nice to have)\n  1993\t- **Watch mode** — re-render when templates change (useful when authoring templates)\n  1994\t- **Tree-sitter integration** — optional, for projects that need precise AST-aware scoping beyond indentation/delimiter heuristics\n  1995\t- **Library registry** — a searchable index of community libraries (like crates.io or npm, but for jig libraries)\n  1996\t- **Diff preview** — `--diff` flag that outputs unified diffs instead of writing files, for review before applying\n  1997\t- **Undo** — `jig undo` to revert the last run (backed by a `.jig/history/` log of operations and original file contents)\n  1998\t- **Template linting** — `jig lint` to catch common template mistakes (unused variables, unreachable conditionals, missing skip_if for patches)\n  1999\t\n  2000\t---\n  2001\t\n  2002\t## Cross-Compatibility: Working With Every Agentic Coding Tool\n  2003\t\n  2004\tjig is not Claude Code-specific. It's designed to work with every LLM-powered coding agent — Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, Windsurf, Aider, Continue, Cline, GitHub Copilot, Zed AI, Amp, and whatever comes next.\n  2005\t\n  2006\t### The Universal Interface: CLI + JSON stdout\n  2007\t\n  2008\tEvery agentic coding tool in 2026 can invoke a CLI via a shell/bash tool and read its stdout. This is the lowest common denominator. It works today, on all 11 major tools, with zero integration effort.\n  2009\t\n  2010\t| Tool | Shell/Bash | MCP Client | Plugin System |\n  2011\t|------|-----------|------------|---------------|\n  2012\t| Claude Code | Yes | Yes | Skills, Hooks |\n  2013\t| Codex CLI (OpenAI) | Yes (sandboxed) | Yes | AGENTS.md |\n  2014\t| OpenCode | Yes | Yes | Agents, Skills |\n  2015\t| Cursor | Yes | Yes | .cursorrules |\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
