{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_31","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba jig.md | sed -n '1040,1345p'\"","aggregated_output":"  1040\t\n  1041\tBeyond the built-in filters, users can register custom filters as shell commands:\n  1042\t\n  1043\t```yaml\n  1044\t# In .jigrc.yaml or recipe.yaml\n  1045\tfilters:\n  1046\t  license_header: \"cat ./HEADER.txt\"\n  1047\t  format_date: \"date -d '{{ value }}' '+%Y-%m-%d'\"\n  1048\t```\n  1049\t\n  1050\tIn a template:\n  1051\t```jinja2\n  1052\t{{ \"2024-01-15\" | format_date }}\n  1053\t```\n  1054\t\n  1055\tThis runs the shell command with `{{ value }}` replaced by the filter input. Simple, composable, no plugin API to learn.\n  1056\t\n  1057\t---\n  1058\t\n  1059\t## Libraries: Reusable Recipe Collections\n  1060\t\n  1061\tIndividual recipes are useful. But the real leverage comes when someone packages a set of recipes for a specific framework and shares them. That's a **jig library** — a versioned collection of recipes, templates, and patch definitions for a domain.\n  1062\t\n  1063\t### What a Library Is\n  1064\t\n  1065\tA library is a directory (or published package) of recipes organized by concern:\n  1066\t\n  1067\t```\n  1068\tjig-django/\n  1069\t  jig-library.yaml            # library manifest\n  1070\t  model/\n  1071\t    add-field/\n  1072\t      recipe.yaml\n  1073\t      templates/\n  1074\t        model_field.j2\n  1075\t        migration_hint.j2\n  1076\t    add-model/\n  1077\t      recipe.yaml\n  1078\t      templates/\n  1079\t        model.py.j2\n  1080\t        admin.py.j2\n  1081\t        factories.py.j2\n  1082\t    add-index/\n  1083\t      recipe.yaml\n  1084\t      templates/\n  1085\t        index_migration.j2\n  1086\t  service/\n  1087\t    add-method/\n  1088\t      recipe.yaml\n  1089\t      templates/\n  1090\t        service_method.j2\n  1091\t        service_test.j2\n  1092\t    add-service/\n  1093\t      recipe.yaml\n  1094\t      templates/\n  1095\t        service.py.j2\n  1096\t        service_test.py.j2\n  1097\t  view/\n  1098\t    add-endpoint/\n  1099\t      recipe.yaml\n  1100\t      templates/\n  1101\t        view_method.j2\n  1102\t        url_pattern.j2\n  1103\t        schema_request.j2\n  1104\t        schema_response.j2\n  1105\t    add-view/\n  1106\t      recipe.yaml\n  1107\t      templates/\n  1108\t        view.py.j2\n  1109\t        urls.py.j2\n  1110\t        schemas.py.j2\n  1111\t  schema/\n  1112\t    add-field/\n  1113\t      recipe.yaml\n  1114\t      templates/\n  1115\t        msgspec_field.j2\n  1116\t        marshmallow_field.j2\n  1117\t  test/\n  1118\t    add-unit-test/\n  1119\t      recipe.yaml\n  1120\t      templates/ ...\n  1121\t    add-integration-test/\n  1122\t      recipe.yaml\n  1123\t      templates/ ...\n  1124\t    add-factory-field/\n  1125\t      recipe.yaml\n  1126\t      templates/ ...\n  1127\t  admin/\n  1128\t    add-field/\n  1129\t      recipe.yaml\n  1130\t      templates/ ...\n  1131\t    add-admin/\n  1132\t      recipe.yaml\n  1133\t      templates/ ...\n  1134\t```\n  1135\t\n  1136\t### Library Manifest\n  1137\t\n  1138\t```yaml\n  1139\t# jig-library.yaml\n  1140\tname: django\n  1141\tversion: 0.3.0\n  1142\tdescription: Recipes for Django model/service/view development\n  1143\tframework: django\n  1144\tlanguage: python\n  1145\t\n  1146\t# Conventions this library assumes — the LLM reads these to map a project's\n  1147\t# actual layout to the library's expectations\n  1148\tconventions:\n  1149\t  models: \"{{ app }}/models/{{ model | snakecase }}.py\"\n  1150\t  services: \"{{ app }}/services/{{ model | snakecase }}_service.py\"\n  1151\t  views: \"{{ app }}/views/{{ model | snakecase }}_view.py\"\n  1152\t  schemas: \"{{ app }}/schemas/{{ model | snakecase }}.py\"\n  1153\t  tests: \"{{ app }}/tests/test_{{ model | snakecase }}.py\"\n  1154\t  factories: \"{{ app }}/tests/factories.py\"\n  1155\t  admin: \"{{ app }}/admin.py\"\n  1156\t  urls: \"{{ app }}/urls.py\"\n  1157\t\n  1158\t# Recipes in this library\n  1159\trecipes:\n  1160\t  model/add-field: \"Add a field to an existing Django model\"\n  1161\t  model/add-model: \"Scaffold a new Django model with admin, factory, and tests\"\n  1162\t  model/add-index: \"Add a database index to an existing model\"\n  1163\t  service/add-method: \"Add a method to an existing service class\"\n  1164\t  service/add-service: \"Scaffold a new service class with tests\"\n  1165\t  view/add-endpoint: \"Add an endpoint to an existing view\"\n  1166\t  view/add-view: \"Scaffold a new view with URL config and schemas\"\n  1167\t  schema/add-field: \"Add a field to an existing request/response schema\"\n  1168\t  test/add-unit-test: \"Generate a unit test for a service method\"\n  1169\t  test/add-integration-test: \"Generate an integration test for an endpoint\"\n  1170\t  test/add-factory-field: \"Add a field to a factory class\"\n  1171\t  admin/add-field: \"Add a field to admin list_display/fieldsets\"\n  1172\t  admin/add-admin: \"Scaffold admin registration for a model\"\n  1173\t\n  1174\t# Composable workflows — multi-recipe operations\n  1175\tworkflows:\n  1176\t  add-field:\n  1177\t    description: \"Add a field across the full stack (model → service → schema → view → admin → tests)\"\n  1178\t    steps:\n  1179\t      - recipe: model/add-field\n  1180\t      - recipe: service/add-method\n  1181\t        when: \"{{ update_service }}\"\n  1182\t      - recipe: schema/add-field\n  1183\t      - recipe: view/add-endpoint\n  1184\t        when: \"{{ update_view }}\"\n  1185\t      - recipe: test/add-factory-field\n  1186\t      - recipe: admin/add-field\n  1187\t\n  1188\t  add-endpoint:\n  1189\t    description: \"Add a new API endpoint (view → URL → schema → test)\"\n  1190\t    steps:\n  1191\t      - recipe: schema/add-field\n  1192\t        vars_map: { field_name: \"request_field\" }\n  1193\t      - recipe: view/add-endpoint\n  1194\t      - recipe: test/add-integration-test\n  1195\t\n  1196\t  scaffold-resource:\n  1197\t    description: \"Create a new model + service + view + admin from scratch\"\n  1198\t    steps:\n  1199\t      - recipe: model/add-model\n  1200\t      - recipe: service/add-service\n  1201\t      - recipe: view/add-view\n  1202\t      - recipe: admin/add-admin\n  1203\t```\n  1204\t\n  1205\t### Convention Mapping\n  1206\t\n  1207\tThe `conventions` block is crucial. It tells jig (and the LLM) where this library expects files to live. But real projects aren't always organized exactly the same way.\n  1208\t\n  1209\tA project can override conventions in its `.jigrc.yaml`:\n  1210\t\n  1211\t```yaml\n  1212\t# .jigrc.yaml\n  1213\tlibraries:\n  1214\t  django:\n  1215\t    conventions:\n  1216\t      models: \"{{ app }}/models.py\"              # single-file models, not per-model files\n  1217\t      services: \"{{ app }}/domain/services.py\"    # different service location\n  1218\t      schemas: \"{{ app }}/api/schemas.py\"\n  1219\t```\n  1220\t\n  1221\tThis lets the same library work across projects with different directory structures. The override is simple key-value — remap the path pattern, keep everything else.\n  1222\t\n  1223\t### Workflows: Multi-Recipe Operations\n  1224\t\n  1225\tA workflow chains multiple recipes together. This is the answer to \"I want to add a field and have it cascade through the whole stack.\"\n  1226\t\n  1227\t```bash\n  1228\t# Run a full-stack field addition\n  1229\tjig workflow django/add-field \\\n  1230\t  --vars '{\"app\": \"hotels\", \"model\": \"Reservation\", \"field_name\": \"loyalty_tier\", \"field_type\": \"CharField\", \"field_args\": \"max_length=50\", \"nullable\": true, \"update_service\": true, \"update_view\": false}'\n  1231\t```\n  1232\t\n  1233\tWorkflows support:\n  1234\t\n  1235\t| Feature | Syntax | Behavior |\n  1236\t|---------|--------|----------|\n  1237\t| **Conditional steps** | `when: \"{{ expr }}\"` | Skip this recipe if the expression is falsy |\n  1238\t| **Variable mapping** | `vars_map: {a: b}` | Rename variables when passing to a sub-recipe |\n  1239\t| **Variable overrides** | `vars: {key: value}` | Override specific variables for a sub-recipe |\n  1240\t| **Shared variables** | (default) | All top-level variables are passed through to every step |\n  1241\t| **Stop on error** | `on_error: stop` (default) | Stop the workflow if any recipe fails |\n  1242\t| **Continue on error** | `on_error: continue` | Skip the failed recipe and continue |\n  1243\t| **Report partial** | `on_error: report` | Continue but mark the workflow as partial in output |\n  1244\t\n  1245\t### Workflows vs. Includes\n  1246\t\n  1247\tThe earlier `includes` concept (in the Patches section) is now subsumed by workflows. The difference:\n  1248\t\n  1249\t- **`includes`** was static — always runs all sub-recipes\n  1250\t- **Workflows** are conditional, configurable, and report per-step results\n  1251\t\n  1252\tWorkflows are the right abstraction. `includes` is removed.\n  1253\t\n  1254\t### Installing Libraries\n  1255\t\n  1256\t```bash\n  1257\t# From a git repository\n  1258\tjig library add https://github.com/someone/jig-django\n  1259\t\n  1260\t# From a local directory (development / private libraries)\n  1261\tjig library add ./path/to/jig-django\n  1262\t\n  1263\t# List installed libraries\n  1264\tjig library list\n  1265\t\n  1266\t# Update a library\n  1267\tjig library update django\n  1268\t\n  1269\t# Remove a library\n  1270\tjig library remove django\n  1271\t```\n  1272\t\n  1273\tLibraries are installed to `~/.jig/libraries/` (global) or `.jig/libraries/` (project-local). Project-local takes precedence.\n  1274\t\n  1275\t### Library Discovery\n  1276\t\n  1277\t```bash\n  1278\t# List all recipes in an installed library\n  1279\tjig library recipes django\n  1280\t\n  1281\t# Show details for a specific recipe\n  1282\tjig library info django/model/add-field\n  1283\t\n  1284\t# List all workflows\n  1285\tjig library workflows django\n  1286\t```\n  1287\t\n  1288\t### Built-In vs. Community Libraries\n  1289\t\n  1290\tjig ships with zero built-in libraries. The tool is framework-agnostic. Libraries are where framework opinions live:\n  1291\t\n  1292\t| Library | Domain |\n  1293\t|---------|--------|\n  1294\t| `jig-django` | Django models, views, services, admin, DRF, msgspec |\n  1295\t| `jig-fastapi` | FastAPI routes, Pydantic models, dependency injection |\n  1296\t| `jig-rails` | Rails models, controllers, views, migrations, RSpec |\n  1297\t| `jig-nextjs` | Next.js pages, API routes, components, server actions |\n  1298\t| `jig-vue` | Vue 3 components, composables, Pinia stores, Vitest |\n  1299\t| `jig-react` | React components, hooks, tests, Storybook stories |\n  1300\t| `jig-express` | Express routes, middleware, controllers, Joi schemas |\n  1301\t| `jig-rust` | Rust structs, traits, impls, tests, Cargo features |\n  1302\t| `jig-go` | Go structs, handlers, middleware, table-driven tests |\n  1303\t| `jig-flutter` | Dart widgets, BLoC providers, repository pattern |\n  1304\t\n  1305\tAnyone can publish a library. There's no registry — it's just a git repo with a `jig-library.yaml`.\n  1306\t\n  1307\t### Libraries as Claude Code Plugins\n  1308\t\n  1309\tA jig library can simultaneously be a Claude Code plugin. The plugin provides skills that know how to use the library's recipes:\n  1310\t\n  1311\t```\n  1312\tjig-django/\n  1313\t  jig-library.yaml              # jig library manifest\n  1314\t  .claude-plugin/\n  1315\t    plugin.json                  # Claude Code plugin manifest\n  1316\t  skills/\n  1317\t    add-field/\n  1318\t      SKILL.md                   # \"Read the model, extract context, run jig workflow django/add-field\"\n  1319\t    add-endpoint/\n  1320\t      SKILL.md                   # \"Read the view, extract context, run jig workflow django/add-endpoint\"\n  1321\t    scaffold-resource/\n  1322\t      SKILL.md                   # \"Ask the user for model name + fields, run jig workflow django/scaffold-resource\"\n  1323\t  model/\n  1324\t    add-field/\n  1325\t      recipe.yaml\n  1326\t      templates/ ...\n  1327\t  service/ ...\n  1328\t  view/ ...\n  1329\t```\n  1330\t\n  1331\tThe skills wrap the recipes with LLM intelligence:\n  1332\t- The **skill** reads code, understands context, extracts variables, and decides which workflow to run\n  1333\t- The **recipe/workflow** does the deterministic file generation and patching\n  1334\t- The **library** provides the full collection, installable as one unit\n  1335\t\n  1336\tThis means a team can `jig library add jig-django` to get the CLI recipes, AND install the Claude Code plugin to get the LLM-powered skills that invoke them.\n  1337\t\n  1338\t### Project-Specific Overrides and Extensions\n  1339\t\n  1340\tTeams can extend a library with their own recipes without forking it:\n  1341\t\n  1342\t```\n  1343\tmy-project/\n  1344\t  .jig/\n  1345\t    libraries/\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

This is where framework opinions live — jig itself stays agnostic.\n    80\t\n    81\t**Scope (from jig.md):**\n    82\t\n    83\t*Library manifest:*\n    84\t- `jig-library.yaml` format: name, version, description, framework, language\n    85\t- `conventions` block: path templates mapping concerns to file locations\n    86\t- `recipes` block: flat list of recipe paths + descriptions\n    87\t- `workflows` block: multi-recipe workflow definitions (uses v0.3 workflow format)\n    88\t\n    89\t*CLI commands:*\n    90\t- `jig library add <url|path>` — install from git repo or local directory\n    91\t- `jig library remove <name>` — uninstall\n    92\t- `jig library update <name>` — pull latest\n    93\t- `jig library list` — show installed libraries\n    94\t- `jig library recipes <name>` — list all recipes in a library\n    95\t- `jig library info <name>/<recipe>` — show recipe details\n    96\t- `jig library workflows <name>` — list workflows\n    97\t\n    98\t*Installation:*\n    99\t- Global: `~/.jig/libraries/`\n   100\t- Project-local: `.jig/libraries/` (takes precedence)\n   101\t\n   102\t*Convention overrides:*\n   103\t- `.jigrc.yaml` per-project convention remapping\n   104\t- Override just the path patterns, keep everything else\n   105\t\n   106\t*Project extensions:*\n   107\t- `.jig/overrides/<library>/<recipe>/templates/` — replace a single template without forking\n   108\t- `.jig/extensions/<library>/<recipe>/` — add new recipes namespaced under a library\n   109\t\n   110\t**What this unlocks:** Libraries make jig useful beyond a single project. They're also a prerequisite for the MCP server (which needs `jig_library_recipes` to work), the Claude Code plugin (which wraps a library), and meaningful agent evals (which need a library like jig-django to test against).\n   111\t\n   112\t**Open questions for SPEC:**\n   113\t- Does `jig library add` from git clone the whole repo or just the jig-library.yaml + recipe dirs?\n   114\t- Versioning strategy: git tags? semver in manifest? lock file?\n   115\t- How does `jig run` resolve a library recipe path (e.g., `jig run django/model/add-field`)? Is it `jig run --library django model/add-field` or path-based?\n   116\t- Does `jig workflow` accept library-qualified workflow names directly?\n   117\t\n   118\t---\n   119\t\n   120\t### v0.5 — Distribution\n   121\t\n   122\t**Workstream docs:** None (needs PLAN)\n   123\t**Depends on:** v0.4 (distribution should include library install capability)\n   124\t**jig.md reference:** lines 806–950, roadmap line 1937\n   125\t\n   126\tPackage and ship jig so others can install it.\n   127\t\n   128\t**Scope (from jig.md):**\n   129\t\n   130\t| Channel | Mechanism |\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

clippy\"},{\"tool\":\"Bash\",\"prompt\":\"run git commands for status and diff\"},{\"tool\":\"Bash\",\"prompt\":\"list directory contents with ls\"}],\"plan\":\"# Libraries (v0.4) Implementation Plan\\n\\n> Workstream: libraries\\n> Status: Planning\\n> Depends on: workflows (v0.3, complete — 343 tests passing)\\n\\n## Context\\n\\njig is a template rendering CLI for LLM code generation. v0.1-v0.3 built the core engine (recipes, operations, workflows). v0.4 adds **libraries**: versioned recipe collections for a framework (e.g., `jig-django`), installable globally or per-project, with convention overrides and template customization. Libraries are the ecosystem layer — they're where framework opinions live, separate from the framework-agnostic engine.\\n\\nThe spec defines libraries in `jig.md` lines 1059-1376. The architecture slots them into `src/library/` (ARCHITECTURE.md Phase I).\\n\\n## Scope\\n\\n**In:** Manifest parsing, local + git install, remove, update, list, recipe/workflow discovery, namespaced resolution in `jig run`/`jig workflow`/`jig validate`/`jig vars`, convention system with `.jigrc.yaml` overrides, template overrides, project-local extensions.\\n\\n**Out:** Claude Code plugin integration (v0.6), cross-library workflows (v0.9), custom filters in `.jigrc.yaml` (future), `jig library link` for dev mode (future).\\n\\n## Key Decisions\\n\\n| ID | Decision | Rationale |\\n|----|----------|-----------|\\n| D-L1 | Shell out to `git` CLI for clone/pull | No new binary deps; git is ubiquitous on dev machines; only needed at install-time, not runtime. Clear error when git is absent. |\\n| D-L2 | Local install copies directory (not symlink) | Immutable after install — no surprise mutations. Symlink dev mode can be added later as `jig library link`. |\\n| D-L3 | Conventions injected as `{{ conventions.models }}` variable namespace | Reuses existing variable/template pipeline. Two-pass: render convention templates with recipe vars, then inject rendered paths into template context. |\\n| D-L4 | Library-namespaced resolution via slash syntax | `jig run django/model/add-field` — first segment is library name, rest is recipe path. Filesystem paths take precedence for backward compat. |\\n| D-L5 | No new error types or exit codes | Library errors map to existing JigError: exit 1 (manifest/validation), exit 3 (install/remove I/O), exit 4 (convention variable errors). Respects I-5. |\\n| D-L6 | `src/library/` module with submodules | Follows `src/operations/` and `src/scope/` patterns. Self-contained surface area. |\\n| D-L7 | `dirs` crate for home directory resolution | Tiny crate, zero transitive deps on macOS/Linux, handles platform differences. |\\n| D-L8 | `.jigrc.yaml` scoped to convention overrides only in v0.4 | Only feature libraries need. `base_dir`, `vars_file`, `filters` deferred. Unknown keys ignored. |\\n| D-L9 | Do NOT extend `detect_file_type()` for manifests | Library manifests are only processed by `jig library` commands, never by `jig run`/`workflow`. Different concept. |\\n| D-L10 | Inline manifest workflows reuse `Workflow` struct | Maximum reuse. Only difference is path resolution (relative to library root, not workflow file). |\\n\\n### [HUMAN DECISION NEEDED]\\n\\n1. **Phase 0 scope**: The v0.3 review found critical/major bugs (C1, M1-M4). M4 (duplicated `run_recipe`) directly impacts v0.4 integration. Should Phase 0 fix all of C1+M1-M4, or just M4?\\n2. **`--global` flag on add/remove**: Spec says project-local by default. Should `jig library add --global` install to `~/.jig/libraries/`? Or should default be global (like npm -g pattern)?\\n3. **Convention rendering**: Two-pass (render `{{ app }}/models/{{ model }}.py` with vars, inject result as string) vs pass-through (inject raw template string, let recipe template render it inline)? Two-pass is cleaner for template authors but adds complexity.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 0: Tech Debt Consolidation\\n\\n**Goal:** Fix v0.3 review findings that impact library development. Clean foundation.\\n**Blocks:** All subsequent phases.\\n\\n### Tasks\\n\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\n| T-0.1 | **Consolidate `run_recipe()`** — refactor `cmd_run()` (`main.rs:269-455`) to call `workflow::run_recipe()` (`workflow.rs:551-619`) instead of duplicating the rendering pipeline. `cmd_run` keeps CLI concerns (var collection, output formatting); rendering moves entirely to `run_recipe`. | `src/main.rs`, `src/workflow.rs` | — |\\n| T-0.2 | **Fix `extract_rendered_from_error`** (C1) — `workflow.rs:621-628` returns `se.what.clone()` (error description) instead of actual rendered template content. Extract from `OpResult::Error.rendered_content` in partial results instead. | `src/workflow.rs` | (P) |\\n| T-0.3 | **Fix `format_workflow_json` status logic** (M1) — `output.rs:300-302` derives status from `result.on_error` but ignores step-level overrides. Derive from `exit_code` parameter instead. | `src/output.rs` | (P) |\\n| T-0.4 | **Fix workflow early-error JSON** (M3) — `main.rs:477` calls `e.structured_error()` losing all but first validation error. Use `e.structured_errors()` to emit all. | `src/main.rs` | (P) |\\n\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\n|----|------|-----------|\\n| AC-0.1 | Ubiquitous | The system SHALL produce byte-identical output for all 343 existing tests after `run_recipe` consolidation |\\n| AC-0.2 | Event | WHEN a workflow step fails with a rendering error AND partial results contain rendered content, the JSON `rendered_content` field SHALL contain the actual rendered template, not the error description |\\n| AC-0.3 | Event | WHEN a workflow step has `on_error: report` overriding workflow-level `stop`, AND the step fails, JSON status SHALL be `\\\"partial\\\"` (not `\\\"error\\\"`) |\\n| AC-0.4 | Event | WHEN workflow variable validation produces N errors, the JSON error output SHALL include all N errors |\\n\\n### Gate\\nAll 343 tests pass. `cargo clippy` clean. No behavioral changes visible through existing fixtures.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 1: Library Manifest & Storage\\n\\n**Goal:** Parse `jig-library.yaml`, define storage paths, resolve home dir.\\n**Depends on:** Phase 0.\\n\\n### Tasks\\n\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\n| T-1.1 | Add `dirs = \\\"6\\\"` to `Cargo.toml` | `Cargo.toml` | (P) |\\n| T-1.2 | **Create `src/library/mod.rs`** — module root with `LibraryPaths` struct: `global_dir` (`~/.jig/libraries/`), `project_dir` (`.jig/libraries/`). `LibraryPaths::resolve(project_root)` finds both. `LibraryPaths::find_library(name) -> Option<PathBuf>` checks project-local first, then global. Register `mod library` in `main.rs`. | `src/library/mod.rs`, `src/main.rs` | — |\\n| T-1.3 | **Create `src/library/manifest.rs`** — `LibraryManifest` struct with serde: name (required), version (required), description, framework, language, conventions (`IndexMap<String, String>`), recipes (`IndexMap<String, String>`), workflows (`IndexMap<String, RawLibraryWorkflow>`). `LibraryManifest::load(path) -> Result<Self, JigError>`. Validates required fields, checks declared recipe directories exist. | `src/library/manifest.rs` | — (after T-1.2) |\\n| T-1.4 | **Unit tests** — valid manifest, minimal manifest, missing name/version, invalid YAML, missing recipe dir, path resolution precedence, `find_library` returns None when not installed | `src/library/mod.rs`, `src/library/manifest.rs` | — (after T-1.3) |\\n\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\n|----|------|-----------|\\n| AC-1.1 | Event | WHEN a valid `jig-library.yaml` is loaded, the system SHALL parse all fields into `LibraryManifest` |\\n| AC-1.2 | Unwanted | IF `jig-library.yaml` is missing `name`, the system SHALL return exit 1 with hint \\\"add a 'name' field\\\" |\\n| AC-1.3 | Unwanted | IF `jig-library.yaml` is missing `version`, the system SHALL return exit 1 with hint \\\"add a 'version' field\\\" |\\n| AC-1.4 | Unwanted | IF a declared recipe path does not exist as a subdirectory with `recipe.yaml`, the system SHALL return exit 1 identifying the missing recipe |\\n| AC-1.5 | Event | WHEN a library exists in both project-local and global, `find_library` SHALL return the project-local path |\\n| AC-1.6 | Unwanted | IF `dirs::home_dir()` returns None, the system SHALL skip global search without error |\\n\\n### Gate\\n`cargo test` passes with ~12 new unit tests. Manifest parses all spec-defined fields.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 2: Local Install & Management CLI\\n\\n**Goal:** `jig library add <path>`, `jig library remove <name>`, `jig library list`.\\n**Depends on:** Phase 1.\\n\\n### Tasks\\n\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\n| T-2.1 | **Create `src/library/install.rs`** — `install_from_path(source, target_dir) -> Result<PathBuf, JigError>`: validate manifest, copy directory recursively, error if already exists. `remove_library(name, storage)`: find and delete. `list_libraries(storage) -> Vec<InstalledLibrary>`: scan both dirs, parse manifests, dedup (project-local wins). | `src/library/install.rs` | — |\\n| T-2.2 | **Add `Library` subcommand** — nested `LibraryCommands` enum with `Add { source: String, #[arg(long)] global: bool }`, `Remove { name: String }`, `List`. Implement `cmd_library()` dispatch, `cmd_library_add()`, `cmd_library_remove()`, `cmd_library_list()`. | `src/main.rs` | (P with T-2.1) |\\n| T-2.3 | **Output formatting** — `format_library_list_json()` and `format_library_list_human()` in output.rs. JSON: `{\\\"libraries\\\": [{name, version, description, source, recipes, workflows}]}`. Human: colored table to stderr. | `src/output.rs` | (P with T-2.1) |\\n| T-2.4 | **Tests** — unit: install valid, install duplicate error, install no manifest, remove success, remove not found, list empty, list dedup. CLI integration: `jig library add ./path`, `jig library list --json`, `jig library remove name`. | `src/library/install.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-2.1-2.3) |\\n\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\n|----|------|-----------|\\n| AC-2.1 | Event | WHEN `jig library add ./path` is run AND path has valid manifest, the system SHALL copy to `.jig/libraries/<name>/` |\\n| AC-2.2 | Unwanted | IF `./path/jig-library.yaml` does not exist, the system SHALL return exit 1 |\\n| AC-2.3 | Unwanted | IF library already installed at target, the system SHALL return exit 3 with hint to remove first |\\n| AC-2.4 | Event | WHEN `jig library add --global` is run, the system SHALL install to `~/.jig/libraries/<name>/` |\\n| AC-2.5 | Event | WHEN `jig library remove <name>` is run AND library exists, the system SHALL delete the directory |\\n| AC-2.6 | Event | WHEN `jig library list --json` is run, the system SHALL output JSON with name, version, source, recipe/workflow counts |\\n| AC-2.7 | Event | WHEN same library exists in both scopes, `list` SHALL show project-local and annotate as overriding global |\\n\\n### Gate\\nEnd-to-end local install, list, remove. JSON output matches schema. All pre-existing tests pass.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 3: Discovery & Namespaced Resolution\\n\\n**Goal:** `jig library recipes/workflows/info`. Make `jig run django/model/add-field` work.\\n**Depends on:** Phase 2.\\n\\n### Tasks\\n\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\n| T-3.1 | **Create `src/library/discover.rs`** — `list_recipes(manifest, lib_dir) -> Vec<RecipeInfo>`, `list_workflows(manifest) -> Vec<WorkflowInfo>`, `recipe_info(manifest, lib_dir, path) -> Result<RecipeDetail, JigError>`. Load actual recipes for variable/operation details. | `src/library/discover.rs` | — |\\n| T-3.2 | **Create `src/library/resolve.rs`** — `resolve_recipe_path(input, storage) -> Result<ResolvedRecipe, JigError>` and `resolve_workflow_path(input, storage) -> Result<ResolvedWorkflow, JigError>`. Returns `FilePath(PathBuf)` for filesystem paths, `Library { library_name, recipe_path, resolved_path, library_dir }` for library refs. Filesystem paths take precedence (backward compat). | `src/library/resolve.rs` | (P with T-3.1) |\\n| T-3.3 | **Add discovery subcommands** — `Recipes { name }`, `Workflows { name }`, `Info { path }` to `LibraryCommands`. Output formatting in `output.rs`. | `src/main.rs`, `src/output.rs` | (P with T-3.1) |\\n| T-3.4 | **Integrate resolution into `cmd_run`, `cmd_workflow`, `cmd_validate`, `cmd_vars`** — Change `Commands::Run { recipe: PathBuf }` to `Commands::Run { recipe: String }`. In each handler, call `resolve_recipe_path()` first. If `ResolvedRecipe::Library`, use resolved path. Same for workflow. This is the highest-risk integration point. | `src/main.rs` | — (after T-3.2) |\\n| T-3.5 | **`load_library_workflow()`** — construct `Workflow` from inline manifest workflow definition, resolving recipe paths relative to library root (not workflow file). | `src/library/resolve.rs` or `src/workflow.rs` | — (after T-3.2) |\\n| T-3.6 | **Tests** — unit: resolve filesystem path (unchanged), resolve library recipe, resolve library workflow, library not found, recipe not found in library, ambiguous path (filesystem wins). Integration: install lib + run recipe, install lib + validate, install lib + list recipes. | `src/library/resolve.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-3.4) |\\n\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\n|----|------|-----------|\\n| AC-3.1 | Event | WHEN `jig run django/model/add-field --vars '...'` is run AND `django` is installed with that recipe, the system SHALL execute the library recipe |\\n| AC-3.2 | Event | WHEN `jig run ./path/recipe.yaml` is run AND path exists, the system SHALL use filesystem resolution regardless of library names (backward compat) |\\n| AC-3.3 | Unwanted | IF `jig run nonexistent/recipe` is run AND no library `nonexistent` is installed, the system SHALL return exit 1 listing installed libraries |\\n| AC-3.4 | Event | WHEN `jig library recipes django --json` is run, the system SHALL output all recipes with path, description, qualified_name |\\n| AC-3.5 | Event | WHEN `jig library info django/model/add-field --json` is run, the system SHALL output variables, operations, description |\\n| AC-3.6 | Event | WHEN `jig workflow django/add-field --vars '...'` is run, the system SHALL resolve from manifest and execute via existing workflow engine |\\n| AC-3.7 | Event | WHEN `jig validate django/model/add-field` is run, the system SHALL validate the library recipe |\\n\\n### Gate\\nAll existing commands accept library-namespaced paths. Filesystem paths still work unchanged. Discovery commands produce correct output. All pre-existing tests pass.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 4: Convention System\\n\\n**Goal:** Parse `.jigrc.yaml`, inject conventions into library recipe execution.\\n**Depends on:** Phase 3.\\n\\n### Tasks\\n\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\n| T-4.1 | **Create `src/library/conventions.rs`** — `ProjectConfig` struct for `.jigrc.yaml` (scoped to `libraries.<name>.conventions`). `load_project_config(root) -> Result<Option<ProjectConfig>, JigError>`. Missing file returns `None`, malformed returns exit 1. Unknown keys ignored. | `src/library/conventions.rs` | — |\\n| T-4.2 | **Convention resolution** — `resolve_conventions(manifest, overrides, vars) -> Result<Value, JigError>`. Start with manifest conventions, apply overrides, render each template with recipe vars using minijinja, return `{\\\"models\\\": \\\"/rendered/path\\\", ...}`. Rendering errors map to exit 4. | `src/library/conventions.rs` | — (after T-4.1) |\\n| T-4.3 | **Inject conventions into execution** — Add `library_ctx: Option<&LibraryContext>` to `run_recipe()` signature. When present, resolve conventions and add `conventions` key to var context before rendering. `LibraryContext` holds `library_dir`, `manifest`, `overrides`. Non-library recipes pass `None` (zero behavioral change). | `src/workflow.rs`, `src/main.rs` | — (after T-4.2) |\\n| T-4.4 | **Tests** — unit: parse valid `.jigrc.yaml`, missing file returns None, malformed returns error, convention resolution with overrides, convention rendering with vars, missing var returns exit 4. Integration: library recipe using `{{ conventions.models }}`, `.jigrc.yaml` override applied. | `src/library/conventions.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-4.3) |\\n\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\n|----|------|-----------|\\n| AC-4.1 | Event | WHEN a library recipe template uses `{{ conventions.models }}`, the system SHALL render the convention template with recipe vars and inject the result |\\n| AC-4.2 | Event | WHEN `.jigrc.yaml` overrides `libraries.django.conventions.models`, the system SHALL use the override instead of the manifest convention |\\n| AC-4.3 | State | WHILE `.jigrc.yaml` does not exist, the system SHALL use manifest conventions without error |\\n| AC-4.4 | Unwanted | IF a convention template references an unprovided variable, the system SHALL return exit 4 identifying the convention and missing variable |\\n| AC-4.5 | Ubiquitous | WHEN a non-library recipe is executed, the system SHALL NOT inject conventions (no behavioral change) |\\n\\n### Gate\\nConvention injection works end-to-end. Overrides apply. Non-library recipes unaffected. All pre-existing tests pass.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 5: Git Install & Update\\n\\n**Goal:** `jig library add <git-url>`, `jig library update <name>`.\\n**Depends on:** Phase 2. **Can run in parallel with Phase 4.**\\n\\n### Tasks\\n\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\n| T-5.1 | **URL detection** — `is_git_url(source) -> bool` in `install.rs`. Matches `https://`, `git@`, `ssh://`, `.git` suffix. Everything else is local path. | `src/library/install.rs` | — |\\n| T-5.2 | **Git clone install** — `install_from_git(url, target_dir) -> Result<PathBuf, JigError>`. `git clone --depth 1` to tempdir, validate manifest, copy to target, store URL in `.jig-source` metadata file. Git not found -> exit 3 with hint. Clone failure -> exit 3 with git stderr. | `src/library/install.rs` | — (after T-5.1) |\\n| T-5.3 | **Update command** — `Update { name: String }` in `LibraryCommands`. Read `.jig-source` for git URL, clone latest, replace installed library. No `.jig-source` (local install) -> exit 1 with hint. | `src/main.rs`, `src/library/install.rs` | — (after T-5.2) |\\n| T-5.4 | **Tests** — unit: URL detection patterns, git not found error. Integration: init local git repo, `jig library add file:///...`, update after commit. | `src/library/install.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-5.3) |\\n\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\n|----|------|-----------|\\n| AC-5.1 | Event | WHEN `jig library add https://...` is run AND git is available, the system SHALL clone and install |\\n| AC-5.2 | Unwanted | IF git is not in PATH, the system SHALL return exit 3 with hint \\\"git is required for URLs\\\" |\\n| AC-5.3 | Event | WHEN `jig library update django` is run AND django was git-installed, the system SHALL clone latest and replace |\\n| AC-5.4 | Unwanted | IF `jig library update django` is run AND django was locally installed, the system SHALL return exit 1 |\\n| AC-5.5 | Unwanted | IF cloned repo has no `jig-library.yaml`, the system SHALL return exit 1 |\\n\\n### Gate\\nGit install and update work with local `file://` repos. Clear errors when git unavailable. All pre-existing tests pass.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Phase 6: Template Overrides & Extensions\\n\\n**Goal:** Project-local template overrides and recipe extensions.\\n**Depends on:** Phase 3 + Phase 4.\\n\\n### Tasks\\n\\n| Task | Description | Files | Parallel |\\n|------|-------------|-------|----------|\\n| T-6.1 | **Template override resolution** — When resolving library recipe templates, check `.jig/overrides/<lib>/<recipe>/templates/<template>` first. Override replaces individual templates, not the recipe. Add `override_dir: Option<PathBuf>` to template loading in `renderer.rs` or create `create_library_recipe_env()` variant. | `src/renderer.rs`, `src/library/resolve.rs` | — |\\n| T-6.2 | **Extension resolution** — In `resolve_recipe_path()`, check `.jig/extensions/<lib>/<recipe>/recipe.yaml` before library directory. Extensions are full recipe bundles. In `list_recipes()`, include extensions annotated as \\\"(extension)\\\". | `src/library/resolve.rs`, `src/library/discover.rs` | (P with T-6.1) |\\n| T-6.3 | **Tests** — unit: override replaces single template, other templates from library, extension found and executed, extension in recipe list. Integration: install lib + create override + run recipe, install lib + create extension + run. | `src/library/resolve.rs`, `tests/cli.rs` | — (after T-6.1-6.2) |\\n\\n### Acceptance Criteria\\n\\n| ID | Type | Criterion |\\n|----|------|-----------|\\n| AC-6.1 | Event | WHEN `.jig/overrides/django/model/add-field/templates/model_field.j2` exists, `jig run django/model/add-field` SHALL use the override template |\\n| AC-6.2 | Event | WHEN an override exists for one template, the system SHALL use library originals for all other templates |\\n| AC-6.3 | Event | WHEN `.jig/extensions/django/model/add-audit-fields/recipe.yaml` exists, `jig library recipes django` SHALL include it as an extension |\\n| AC-6.4 | Event | WHEN `jig run django/model/add-audit-fields` matches an extension, the system SHALL execute the extension recipe |\\n| AC-6.5 | State | WHILE no `.jig/overrides/` or `.jig/extensions/` exist, the system SHALL operate identically to pre-override behavior |\\n\\n### Gate\\nOverrides and extensions work end-to-end. All pre-existing tests pass.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Execution Order\\n\\n```\\nPhase 0 (tech debt) ─────────────────────────────┐\\n                                                  v\\nPhase 1 (manifest + storage) ────────────────────>│\\n                                                  v\\nPhase 2 (local install + CLI) ───────────────────>│\\n                                                  │\\n              ┌───────────────────────────────────>│\\n              │                                    │\\nPhase 3 (discovery + namespaced resolution) ──────┤\\n              │                                    │\\n              ├──> Phase 4 (conventions) ──────────┤\\n              │                                    │\\n              └──> Phase 5 (git install) ──────────┤  (4 & 5 parallel)\\n                                                   │\\nPhase 6 (overrides + extensions) ──────────────────┘\\n```\\n\\n## New Files\\n\\n| File | Phase | Purpose |\\n|------|-------|---------|\\n| `src/library/mod.rs` | 1 | Module root, `LibraryPaths`, `LibraryContext` |\\n| `src/library/manifest.rs` | 1 | `LibraryManifest` parsing |\\n| `src/library/install.rs` | 2, 5 | Add, remove, update, list |\\n| `src/library/discover.rs` | 3 | Recipe/workflow enumeration |\\n| `src/library/resolve.rs` | 3, 6 | Namespaced resolution, overrides, extensions |\\n| `src/library/conventions.rs` | 4 | Convention resolution, `.jigrc.yaml` |\\n\\n## Modified Files\\n\\n| File | Phases | Changes |\\n|------|--------|---------|\\n| `Cargo.toml` | 1 | Add `dirs` |\\n| `src/main.rs` | 0, 2, 3, 4 | Consolidate `run_recipe`, add `Library` command, resolution integration, `Run.recipe: PathBuf` -> `String` |\\n| `src/workflow.rs` | 0, 4 | Fix C1, thread `LibraryContext` through `run_recipe` |\\n| `src/output.rs` | 0, 2, 3 | Fix M1, library output formatters |\\n| `src/renderer.rs` | 6 | Template override support |\\n| `tests/cli.rs` | 2, 3, 5 | Library CLI tests |\\n\\n## Risks\\n\\n| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |\\n|------|----------|------------|\\n| `Commands::Run { recipe: PathBuf }` -> `String` changes clap validation | HIGH | Resolution function checks filesystem first. Regression tests for all error paths. |\\n| Convention two-pass rendering cycle risk | MEDIUM | Conventions rendered with recipe vars only, never reference other conventions. Document restriction. |\\n| Library manifest workflows need different path resolution than `load_workflow()` | MEDIUM | `load_library_workflow()` constructs Workflow struct with paths relative to library root. |\\n| Integration test fixtures need installed libraries | MEDIUM | Test setup installs fixture library to temp `.jig/libraries/` before running commands. |\\n\\n## Verification\\n\\nAfter each phase:\\n1. `cargo test` — all existing + new tests pass\\n2. `cargo clippy` — clean\\n3. Manual smoke test of the phase's deliverable\\n\\nAfter all phases:\\n```bash\\n# Full lifecycle test\\njig library add ./test-fixtures/jig-django\\njig library list --json\\njig library recipes django\\njig library info django/model/add-field\\njig run django/model/add-field --vars '{\\\"app\\\":\\\"hotels\\\",\\\"model\\\":\\\"Reservation\\\",\\\"field_name\\\":\\\"loyalty_tier\\\",\\\"field_type\\\":\\\"CharField\\\"}' --dry-run\\njig library remove django\\n```\\n\",\"planFilePath\":\"/Users/tylerobriant/.claude/plans/kind-floating-crescent.md\"}}],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"f2877490-4c7f-4a2f-a494-a32ec94f7304\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_52","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba AGENTS.md | sed -n '1,120p'\"","aggregated_output":"     1\t# Mentat\n     2\t\n     3\tSpec-driven development workflow with dual-agent planning and iterative execution.\n     4\t\n     5\t## Key Documents\n     6\t\n     7\t- [docs/INVARIANTS.md](docs/INVARIANTS.md) - Project-wide constraints\n     8\t- [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md) - System architecture\n     9\t- [docs/development/](docs/development/) - Methodology and philosophy\n    10\t\n    11\t## Workflow\n    12\t\n    13\t1. `/ws-init <name>` - Initialize workstream docs\n    14\t2. `/ws-plan <name>` - Dual-agent planning (Claude + Codex), synthesize\n    15\t3. `/ws-plan-review <name>` - Adversarial review of plan/specs\n    16\t4. `/ws-execute <name> [task]` - Iterative execution with fresh-context retries\n    17\t5. `/ws-validate` - Check task readiness (tests + VALIDATION.md)\n    18\t6. `/ws-review <name> [task]` - Adversarial code review\n    19\t7. `/ws-consolidate` - Capture learnings, update durable docs\n    20\t\n    21\t## Conventions\n    22\t\n    23\t- EARS format for all acceptance criteria in SPEC.md\n    24\t- PLAN.md is source of truth for status/phases\n    25\t- SPEC.md is canonical for requirements and interfaces\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_56","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/zsh -lc \"nl -ba 'docs/workstreams/libraries/exec/iteration-(workstream)-1-20260404-215011.md' | sed -n '1,260p'\"","aggregated_output":"     1\t{\"type\":\"result\",\"subtype\":\"success\",\"is_error\":false,\"duration_ms\":3716791,\"duration_api_ms\":3700784,\"num_turns\":60,\"result\":\"All **386 tests pass** (359 unit + 2 CLI + 12 integration + 13 library integration). Zero clippy warnings from library code.\\n\\n## Summary of what was implemented\\n\\n### New files\\n- **`src/library/mod.rs`** — Module root exposing manifest, install, discover, conventions\\n- **`src/library/manifest.rs`** — `jig-library.yaml` parsing and validation (name, version, description, framework, language, conventions, recipes, workflows) with 7 unit tests\\n- **`src/library/install.rs`** — Add/remove/update/list operations with project-local (`<project>/.jig/libraries/`) and global (`~/.jig/libraries/`) install locations, project-local takes precedence — 7 unit tests\\n- **`src/library/discover.rs`** — Recipe listing, recipe info (loads actual `recipe.yaml` for variable/operation details), workflow listing, library-qualified path resolution — 7 unit tests\\n- **`src/library/conventions.rs`** — `.jigrc.yaml` project config parsing with per-library convention overrides that merge with library defaults — 6 unit tests\\n- **`tests/library.rs`** — 13 CLI integration tests covering all 7 subcommands + error cases + full lifecycle\\n\\n### Modified files\\n- **`src/main.rs`** — Added `library` module, `Library` command variant with 7 `LibraryAction` subcommands, `cmd_library` handler with JSON and human-readable output for each action\\n\\n### CLI commands added\\n```\\njig library add <path> [--global]     # Install from local directory\\njig library remove <name>             # Uninstall\\njig library update <name> <path>      # Replace with updated source\\njig library list                      # Show installed libraries\\njig library recipes <name>            # List recipes in a library\\njig library info <lib>/<recipe>       # Show recipe details + variables\\njig library workflows <name>          # List workflows in a library\\n```\\n\\nAll commands support `--json` and `--quiet` flags consistent with existing CLI patterns.\\n\\nCOMPLETE\",\"stop_reason\":\"end_turn\",\"session_id\":\"17dd53f2-c239-461c-a519-8660819edf3f\",\"total_cost_usd\":3.16323865,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":56,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":80950,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":3173034,\"output_tokens\":28410,\"server_tool_use\":{\"web_search_requests\":0,\"web_fetch_requests\":0},\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":80950,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"},\"modelUsage\":{\"claude-opus-4-6[1m]\":{\"inputTokens\":57,\"outputTokens\":33812,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":3222236,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":80950,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":2.962640499999999,\"contextWindow\":1000000,\"maxOutputTokens\":64000},\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\":{\"inputTokens\":71,\"outputTokens\":6311,\"cacheReadInputTokens\":955384,\"cacheCreationInputTokens\":58747,\"webSearchRequests\":0,\"costUSD\":0.20059815,\"contextWindow\":200000,\"maxOutputTokens\":32000}},\"permission_denials\":[],\"terminal_reason\":\"completed\",\"fast_mode_state\":\"off\",\"uuid\":\"fa605e12-0f90-49c2-bc88-b854f69b2d9b\"}\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_58","type":"agent_message","text":"**Verdict:** Request Changes\n\n**Critical** (must fix before merge):\n- No canonical workstream spec exists to review against: `SPEC.md` is required/canonical by process, but `libraries` still has none, so EARS AC traceability is impossible ([AGENTS.md#L23](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/AGENTS.md#L23), [docs/ROADMAP.md#L75](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ROADMAP.md#L75)).\n- `library add` is vulnerable to path traversal via manifest `name` (`../../...` or absolute paths) because only non-empty is validated, then directly joined into install path ([src/library/manifest.rs#L88](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/manifest.rs#L88), [src/library/install.rs#L81](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L81)).\n- `library update` can corrupt library identity: it does not verify source manifest name matches the target `<name>`, leading to list/remove inconsistencies ([src/library/install.rs#L162](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L162), [src/library/install.rs#L184](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L184), [src/library/install.rs#L220](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L220), [src/library/install.rs#L295](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L295)).\n- `library info` silently returns success with empty metadata when `recipe.yaml` is invalid/missing parseable content, violating graceful-fail expectations for LLM callers ([src/library/discover.rs#L82](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/discover.rs#L82), [src/library/discover.rs#L101](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/discover.rs#L101)).\n\n**Major** (should fix):\n- Spec mismatch: `jig library add <url|path>` and `jig library update <name>` are specified, but implementation is path-only add plus `update <name> <path>` ([jig.md#L1258](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/jig.md#L1258), [jig.md#L1267](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/jig.md#L1267), [src/main.rs#L103](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/main.rs#L103), [src/main.rs#L116](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/main.rs#L116), [src/main.rs#L681](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/main.rs#L681)).\n- Library-qualified execution is not integrated (`jig run django/...`, `jig workflow django/...` examples fail because handlers treat input as filesystem paths) ([jig.md#L1229](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/jig.md#L1229), [jig.md#L1487](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/jig.md#L1487), [src/main.rs#L323](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/main.rs#L323), [src/main.rs#L562](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/main.rs#L562), [src/library/discover.rs#L146](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/discover.rs#L146)).\n- Convention overrides are implemented as a module but not wired into execution paths, so v0.4 convention behavior is not delivered end-to-end ([docs/ROADMAP.md#L102](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ROADMAP.md#L102), [src/library/conventions.rs#L83](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/conventions.rs#L83), [src/main.rs#L323](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/main.rs#L323)).\n- Required extension/override features are missing (`.jig/overrides/...`, `.jig/extensions/...`) ([docs/ROADMAP.md#L107](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ROADMAP.md#L107), [docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L583](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md#L583), [src/library/mod.rs#L1](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/mod.rs#L1)).\n- `library list` silently drops malformed installed libraries instead of surfacing actionable errors ([src/library/install.rs#L284](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L284)).\n- Test coverage is strong for implemented local-path lifecycle, but missing for spec-critical behaviors above (URL add, name-safe update, library-qualified run/workflow, overrides/extensions) ([tests/library.rs#L80](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/library.rs#L80)).\n\n**Minor** (nice to fix):\n- Listing order is based on raw `read_dir` iteration and not sorted, so output ordering may be non-deterministic across environments ([src/library/install.rs#L255](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L255), [src/library/install.rs#L264](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L264)).\n\n**Strengths** (what's done well):\n- Structured error shape is consistent and generally high quality (`what/where/why/hint`) across library operations ([src/library/install.rs#L65](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L65)).\n- Project-local precedence over global is implemented clearly for find/list paths ([src/library/install.rs#L200](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L200), [src/library/install.rs#L218](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/src/library/install.rs#L218)).\n- Good CLI integration coverage for the currently implemented subcommands and lifecycle flow ([tests/library.rs#L460](/Users/tylerobriant/code/tetra/jig/tests/library.rs#L460))."}}
